Round 367 — render Step 2b: warm increme.decision_summary: The warm MCP host now owns GENERATED.md regeneration: every successful mutate incrementally reconciles the render projection (R340 analogue — only changed units re-render) and writes the recomposed doc, superseding the cold full-render auto_regenerate; the cold CLI/CI keeps auto_regenerate.
changes_bullets: RenderProjectionService::reload is now incremental (reconcile_render_index, the render analogue of R340 reconcile_branch_index): Section keyed by section_id, ChangelogEntry by entry_id; unchanged Salsa input handles are reused and only changed fields are set (Salsa bumps an input revision on every set, so compare-then-set is required for incrementality), and the sections/entries Vec is reset only on a membership change. A single-field mutate re-runs only that unit's Tier-1 render plus the cheap Tier-2 concat, not all N — size-independent invalidation.

MCP mutate tools run the primitive with regenerate=false (the cold CLI/CI keeps auto_regenerate). New MnemosyneServer::sync_read_models_after_mutate recomposes and atomic-writes GENERATED.md through the warm render projection (fail-loud — the cascade-output contract the cold auto_regenerate held), then re-syncs the validation projection from the same in-memory store snapshot (best-effort cache, infallible on the already-loaded store). Store is loaded once and shared by both read models.

notify_projection removed (superseded by sync_read_models_after_mutate); finish_mutate, finish_inventory_mutate, and the redact_term handler all route through it and set regenerated=true (redact only when not dry_run). render-projection field doc and the render_projection tool description updated from 2a read-only to 2b write-path-owning.
verification_bullets: 688 workspace tests green (+5 this round): 4 projection reconcile tests (single-field edit re-runs exactly +2 bodies proving size-independence, no-op reload fully memoized, section removal byte-identical, plus the pre-existing membership-change byte-identity test); 1 MCP integration test.

New MCP integration test builds a warm MnemosyneServer over a temp-workspace store and asserts the 2b write path (load to incremental reconcile to recompose to atomic-write) produces GENERATED.md byte-identical to the cold render_atomic_store_to_md the validate gates compare against.
clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings plus fmt clean; cold verify-generated still OK (sync); validate-workspace green (T3 reject 0).
carry_forward_bullets: The order:u64 render-input field is intentionally NOT re-added (R366 removed it as premature 2b substrate); reconcile keys by section_id/entry_id and ordering is carried by BTreeMap Vec position, stable across re-syncs since a field-only edit never changes a key.
32 dormant #[ignore] tests still carry (pre-R252 corpus): re-verify the non-re-anchorable claim per-test and obtain explicit user confirmation before any deletion.

A single-field mutate re-runs only that unit's Tier-1 render plus the cheap Tier-2 concat, not all N — size-independent invalidation.

MCP mutate tools run the primitive with regenerate=false (the cold CLI/CI keeps auto_regenerate). New MnemosyneServer::sync_read_models_after_mutate recomposes and atomic-writes GENERATED.md through the warm render projection (fail-loud — the cascade-output contract the cold auto_regenerate held), then re-syncs the validation projection from the same in-memory store snapshot (best-effort cache, infallible on the already-loaded store). Store is loaded once and shared by both read models.

notify_projection removed (superseded by sync_read_models_after_mutate); finish_mutate, finish_inventory_mutate, and the redact_term handler all route through it and set regenerated=true (redact only when not dry_run). render-projection field doc and the render_projection tool description updated from 2a read-only to 2b write-path-owning.
publishable_verification_bullets: 688 workspace tests green (+4 this round): 3 new projection reconcile tests (single-field edit re-runs exactly +2 tracked bodies, proving size-independent invalidation; no-op reload fully memoized; section removal byte-identical) joining the pre-existing membership-change byte-identity test, plus 1 new MCP integration test.

New MCP integration test builds a warm MnemosyneServer over a temp-workspace store and asserts the 2b write path (load to incremental reconcile to recompose to atomic-write) produces GENERATED.md byte-identical to the cold render_atomic_store_to_md the validate gates compare against.
clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings plus fmt clean; cold verify-generated still OK (sync); validate-workspace green (T3 reject 0).
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: The order:u64 render-input field is intentionally NOT re-added (R366 removed it as premature 2b substrate); reconcile keys by section_id/entry_id and ordering is carried by BTreeMap Vec position, stable across re-syncs since a field-only edit never changes a key.
32 dormant #[ignore] tests still carry (pre-R252 corpus): re-verify the non-re-anchorable claim per-test and obtain explicit user confirmation before any deletion.

Round 368 — render Step 2b review fixes:.decision_summary: Three adversarial reviewers confirmed R367 structurally textbook-clean; this round closes the verification gaps they surfaced — the CLAUDE.md-mandated field-parity test for the two RenderSectionInput write paths, a real change-through-host integration cycle (the prior test was a no-op reconcile), a fail-loud write-failure test, and a count-stable membership test — plus one stale refresh-arg doc the R367 sweep missed.
changes_bullets: Fixed a stale doc leftover the R367 sweep missed: RenderProjectionArgs::refresh still said "2a does NOT wire the warm render projection ... until 2b wires the write path", contradicting the already-updated tool description. R367 is 2b; the default now reflects the current log after a mutate, and refresh=true is only for out-of-band edits. Updated to match.

No production-logic change: 3 fresh adversarial reviewers (told to distrust the carry) re-derived R367 from code and confirmed it textbook-clean — every mutate regenerates (25/25 route through a regenerating finisher), single GENERATED.md write-path, warm render byte-identical to cold by the shared compose_generated_md builder, fail-loud regenerate contract preserved, projection/cascade still RocksDB-free at link, incrementality real (compare-then-set necessary and correct), and the add+remove-net-to-same-count membership edge correctly caught by the new-key arm.
verification_bullets: Field-invariant parity test (CLAUDE.md multi-write-path rule): reconcile_every_renderable_field_change_propagates edits every renderable Section and ChangelogEntry field, reloads, and asserts byte-identity to a cold compose. project_section_input (compiler-checked positional ::new) and reconcile_sections (per-field sync!) are two write paths to one RenderSectionInput; a field added to one and forgotten in the other would compile clean and silently serve stale bytes — this test fires every sync! set-arm so the omission fails in CI, not production.

Count-stable membership test: remove one section and add a different one in the same reload (unit count unchanged), caught only by the None-arm rather than the len() guard — pins the Vec rebuild and byte-identity.

MCP integration test strengthened: it was a no-op reconcile (build and sync loaded the same store, proving only the write seam); added a second real change-through-host cycle asserting the incremental warm write picks up the change AND stays byte-identical to cold render of the mutated store.

Fail-loud test: with the GENERATED.md output path made a directory, a post-mutate write fails and sync_read_models_after_mutate returns Err (the 2b cascade-output safety contract, never a silent desync).
691 workspace tests green (+3 this round); clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings plus fmt clean; validate-workspace green (T3 reject 0, GENERATED.md sync).
carry_forward_bullets: Field parity is now TEST-enforced but not COMPILER-enforced (Salsa generates independent per-field setters; a custom derive to force the field list into both write paths is YAGNI for 13+5 fields — the parity test is the proportionate guard).

redact_term dry_run/non-dry MCP branching and finish_inventory_mutate's sync remain integration-untested; the write seam they share is now covered by the strengthened integration test and the branching is a simple !dry_run guard, so deferring is acceptable (not a correctness risk).

Round 369 — strip_section_marker canonic.decision_summary: strip_section_marker canonical in mnemosyne-core — DRY 5 verbatim §-marker normalize sites (two private helpers + 3 inline copies), the R353/R357/R358 canonical-converter class
changes_bullets: Added `mnemosyne-core::strip_section_marker(&str) -> &str` — the canonical normalizer that strips a section id's optional leading `§` reference marker (`§43` -> `43`, bare ids pass through). The marker-strip operation and its `'§'` literal now live in one L0 place instead of being re-derived per call site.

Three crates had independently re-derived the identical idiom `s.strip_prefix('§').unwrap_or(s)`: `mnemosyne-mcp::strip_section` (23 callers) and `mnemosyne-cli::strip_section_prefix` (22 callers) were verbatim private helpers, plus inline copies in ops/query.rs, cli/main.rs arg-parse, and parser/lib.rs split_section_number. Two crates writing the same private helper is the canonical-converter DRY class of Round 353 / Round 357 / Round 358.
Deleted mcp's `strip_section` wrapper as pure redundancy — every one of its 23 callers was a `.to_string()` closure with no fn-pointer use — and routed them to the imported canonical via rename.

Kept cli's `strip_section_prefix` as a thin owned-`String` adapter that delegates to the canonical: it has genuine ergonomics an `&str`-returning fn lacks (its result is stored in arg structs and it is used as a `.map(strip_section_prefix)` fn pointer in four places). The strip logic is single-sourced; only the owned-return adaptation is local.
The 3 inline normalize sites (ops query, cli main arg-parse, parser split_section_number) now call the canonical directly.

Deliberately left untouched the 3 conditional-parse sites that branch on marker presence — parser:338 (`strip_prefix('§')?`), parser:577 (`if let Some` + `'§'.len_utf8()` byte advance), query:331 (`if let Some`): those test or extract conditioned on the marker, a different operation than normalize, and a `&str -> &str` helper cannot express the presence test. Also left the ~30 `format!("§{}")` display/error sites: prepending the sigil in human-facing strings is not the strip operation, and routing them through a const would harm readability for no behavior benefit.
verification_bullets: 5 new core unit tests for strip_section_marker: leading marker stripped, bare id passthrough, empty string, marker-only -> empty, doubled marker strips only the first.
696 workspace tests pass / 0 fail / 47 ignored (+5 vs the prior 691, all in core).
clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean.

Behavior byte-identical: the canonical body is the exact idiom it replaced, so GENERATED.md is unchanged and validate-workspace round-trip 1/1 + verify-generated stay green (T3 reject 0, GENERATED.md=sync).
carry_forward_bullets: `AtomicSection.normative_excerpt` is write-only today (CLI + MCP setter, zero read consumers) — deferred RFC-002 spec-citation substrate. A persisted schema field with a frozen-once-set invariant; NOT autonomously deletable (data-loss + frozen-ledger risk). Surfaced by the SSOT audit; flagged for user decision, not removed.

`mnemosyne-index` and `mnemosyne-server` remain built-but-unwired into the live cli/mcp path (convergence B index-materialization / D write-side txn service). Intentional convergence substrate per ARCHITECTURE.md §3; YAGNI re-justification candidates only if those stages stay deferred — never delete.
47 dormant `#[ignore]` tests (pre-Round 252 corpus) — re-verify non-re-anchorable per-test and obtain explicit user confirmation before any deletion.

Round 370 — spec-revision drift scan (va.decision_summary: Add validate-spec-drift: flag Active Sections whose normative_excerpt.source_revision trails the workspace spec_source.revision; configurable severity (default warn), Superseded/Removed exempt.
changes_bullets: New mnemosyne-validate::spec_drift — scan_spec_drift(&AtomicStore, workspace_rev) pure offline label-diff; only Active/unset Sections with a normative_excerpt drift, Superseded/Removed exempt (partial-migration pattern); BTreeMap-key-ordered output; SpecDriftViolation::to_cli_json flat shape.
config [spec_drift].severity (default warn, validated reject|warn|info at config-load fail-loud); the fetched_sha256 ^[0-9a-f]{64}$ config-load enforcement pre-existed as RFC-002 substrate.

CLI validate-spec-drift subcommand — --json + --severity override (flag > [spec_drift].severity > warn); skip exit 0 when [workspace.spec_source] absent (mirrors validate-code-refs skip-when-unconfigured); reject => exit 1 on any drift; bare section_id in TTY output (no literal section sigil the R255 hook scans).
MCP tool deliberately omitted — sibling axis validate-code-refs is CLI-only and SCE consumes via CI CLI; no warm-host drift consumer exists yet (YAGNI, no speculative substrate).
verification_bullets: 8 validate unit tests (matching/active-stale/unset/superseded/removed/no-excerpt/ordering/json) + 2 config tests (default-warn / invalid-severity reject) + 7 CLI smoke tests (skip / clean / warn-json / reject-flag / superseded-exempt / config-severity-reject / no-excerpt) all green.
full workspace suite 0 fail; clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings + fmt clean; validate-workspace docs 1/1 round-trip 1/1 T3 reject 0 GENERATED.md=sync orphan 0+0 divergence 10/10.
dogfood: own workspace (no spec_source) => skip exit 0 in both --json and TTY form.
carry_forward_bullets: SCE adoption sequencing: B2 done; next A2 (persistence-boundary refactor — separate mutate from save so bulk = N in-memory mutate + 1 save_with_receipt, one section-write-path), then C2 (route dispatch through PluginRegistry + ext->lang config-driven, landed WITH SCE cpp-resolver PR). Both still PoC-gated discipline; build each its own round on byte-identity + validate-workspace green.

Open decision raised by SCE PoC §4: normative_excerpt has no read-path (query/projection/ops = 0 reads, write-only frozen anchor) — B2 does not need it (rev-label diff only), but the compliance-ledger "show the reviewer the normative text" half is unmet; recommend Mnemosyne-side getter/render (atomic store is SSOT, consumer-side raw JSON read = 2nd read-path) as a separate round, user decision pending.

Round 372 — ban vN version-postfix namin.decision_summary: Broaden the no-postfix-versioning rule to ALL identifiers (production + test fixtures/names/data) and enforce it with a pre-commit gate that scans staged .rs added lines for foo_v2 / BarV3 patterns.
changes_bullets: CLAUDE.md anti-pattern broadened: the vN version-postfix ban now explicitly covers EVERY identifier (production fn/struct/enum AND test fixtures, test function names, test data labels, locals, modules), not just production API. The right name describes what differs (section_alt), never which iteration (section_v2). Carve-out spelled out: real version numbers (schema_version, CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION, upstream spec revisions) are not identifier postfixes and are not banned.

.githooks/pre-commit Gate 6 added: scans staged .rs added lines for the [A-Za-z0-9]_v[0-9] and [a-z0-9]V[0-9] patterns and rejects the commit on a hit; the _v[0-9] form deliberately does not match _version, so schema_version and friends pass.

swept the remaining vN test names in code_refs.rs (four scan_v4_/scan_v5_ helpers renamed to their descriptive form); R371 already renamed the projection parity fixtures (section_alt/entry_alt) and one generate_docs test in the files it touched.
verification_bullets: Gate 6 regex validated by hand: rejects section_v2 / fooV2 / BarV3 / scan_v4_test; passes schema_version / CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION / a "/v2/" URL literal / source_revision / fetched_sha256. Workspace-wide vN identifier scan = 0 remaining.
full workspace suite 0 fail; clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings + fmt clean; the gate fired live on the R371 commit (passed, clean fixtures).
carry_forward_bullets: Gate 6 scans .rs only by design — GENERATED.md and changelog prose may legitimately mention v2/v3 when discussing the rule itself, so the audit trail is not falsely tripped. Comment text in .rs containing a literal vN token would trip it; reword such mentions (the gate treats strictness over comment convenience as correct).

publishable_verification_bullets: Gate 6 regex validated by hand: rejects section_v2 / fooV2 / BarV3 / scan_v4_test; passes schema_version / CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION / a "/v2/" URL literal / source_revision / fetched_sha256. Workspace-wide vN identifier scan = 0 remaining.
full workspace suite 0 fail; clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings + fmt clean; the gate fired live on the R371 commit (passed, clean fixtures).

Round 373 — import-sections bulk section.decision_summary: Add import_sections: bulk section-create from a JSON manifest as one atomic transaction (3-way idempotency: absent=create / identical=no-op / divergent=reject), reusing add_section's extracted in-memory core so there is exactly one section-create write-path.
changes_bullets: Extracted add_section's validate+construct into build_candidate_section (shared in-memory core, no insert/no save); add_section now = build + dup-reject + insert + 1 save, and the bulk import_sections = N build/classify + 1 save. Both shape a new Section through the one core → a single section-create write-path, NOT a bespoke second writer (CLAUDE.md half-enforced-invariant rule).

Extracted normative_excerpt validation into build_normative_excerpt, now shared by the post-create setter AND the inline-at-create path; manifest entries anchor the excerpt frozen-at-create (RFC-002 FR-1). Added a field-invariant parity test that feeds the same edge inputs through both write paths and asserts identical accept/reject verdicts.

import_sections runs a 3-way classification per entry: absent->create / byte-identical->no-op (idempotent re-run) / divergent->reject the WHOLE manifest. Atomicity is free via run_atomic_mutate: any reject returns Err before the single save, so the freshly-loaded store is discarded and the disk is untouched; an all-no-op manifest writes nothing (written_bytes 0). Manifest order = parents before children (a child resolves a parent inserted earlier in the same batch).

CLI import-sections --manifest <path.json> wired (dispatch + help + usage). MCP tool deliberately deferred: the immediate named consumer is SCE's CI via the CLI; the fiction-adapter warm path is the YAGNI-gated future consumer (same discipline as B2 CLI-only).
verification_bullets: +7 atomic unit tests (create-absent / idempotent-no-op-no-save / divergent-reject / inline-excerpt-at-create / in-manifest-parent-ordered / invalid-excerpt-reject / intra-manifest-divergent-no-save) + the write-path parity test + 3 CLI smoke tests (create+excerpt-render / idempotent re-run / divergent exit-1). Full workspace suite 0 fail.
clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings + fmt clean; validate-workspace docs 1/1 round-trip 1/1 T3 reject 0 GENERATED.md=sync; validate-code-refs 0 violations.
carry_forward_bullets: SCOPE: A2 landed as a SECTION-scoped mutate-from-save extraction (build_candidate_section), NOT the full ~28-primitive Unit-of-Work the S17 plan sketched — verified that is all bulk-section-import needs to avoid a second write-path. The full UoW (move save to the run_atomic_mutate boundary for every primitive) and the redact_term 1-5 multi-save fix stay deferred: no named consumer beyond a hypothetical second bulk path = speculative substrate.

Next SCE-scoped Mnemosyne round = C2 (route resolver dispatch through PluginRegistry + ext->lang config-driven), which lands WITH SCE's tree-sitter-cpp plugin PR (not before — speculative otherwise). A1 (W3C TOC->manifest) and B1 (CI fetch->sha256) are SCE-side, no Mnemosyne land.

Also drop the retired install-hooks.sh reference. Resolves SCE PoC #1 §6.
changes_bullets: SCHEMA_GUIDE.md: 13 `[code_refs]` table headers + prose field references rewritten to `[plugins.set_equality_validator]` — the R306 in-place rename of that table, which parses NO `[code_refs]` alias. The "External-spec mirror" example SCE cited in its PoC #1 §6 now shows the working key.

Mnemosyne's internal hook installer was never an external-adopter step.

Round 375 — strip § sigil in import-sect.decision_summary: Fix import_sections leaking the § citation sigil into store keys (a §-form manifest rendered §§ double-sigil, silently): strip § in the shared build_candidate_section so add_section and import_sections normalize section_id + parent_section identically for every caller.
changes_bullets: build_candidate_section (the one shared section-create core) now strips a leading `§` citation sigil from section_id AND parent_section via mnemosyne_core::strip_section_marker (trim-strip-trim). add_section and import_sections both route through it, so a citation-form manifest entry (`"§scxml-1"`) stores the bare key `scxml-1` regardless of caller — symmetric with the CLI/MCP add_section paths that already stripped. Also fixes parent_section lookup for §-form manifests (now matches the bare stored keys).

The bug (SCE-found, R373 regression): import_sections' CLI handler did not strip §, unlike cmd_add_section, so a §-form manifest stored `§scxml-1` and generate-docs rendered `### §§scxml-1` (double sigil). validate-workspace round-trips fine (store internally self-consistent) so it was SILENT corruption. Fixing in the shared core (not just the CLI handler) closes the footgun for every caller — direct primitive, CLI, and any future MCP import tool.
Chose strip (lenient, accept §-or-bare) over reject-at-parse, to match add_section's established normalization behavior.
verification_bullets: +2 atomic unit tests (import strips section + parent sigil with parent-ref resolution; add_section vs import_sections store the identical bare key for a §-form id) + 1 CLI smoke (a §-form manifest renders no `§§` in GENERATED.md). The add_section behavior change (now strips via the core) broke no existing test — full atomic suite 116 pass.
full workspace suite 0 fail; clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings + fmt clean.
carry_forward_bullets: Normalization for the section-create path now lives in one place (build_candidate_section). The CLI/MCP § strips for the set_section_* primitives remain — those do not route through build_candidate_section (they call section_mut_strict directly), so the CLI boundary is still their input-normalization point; for add_section the CLI strip is now redundant-but-idempotent, not divergent. SCE's PoC #1 also flagged the SCHEMA_GUIDE [code_refs] drift (closed by R374).

Round 376.decision_summary: Add section_namespace scope to set_equality_validator so a file may cite multiple specs, each gated by its own workspace
changes_bullets: Add SetEqualityValidatorConfig.section_namespace: when set, only §<id> whose pre-hyphen segment equals it are validated; foreign-namespace cites skip (no SectionMissing, no binding).
Empty section_namespace rejected at config load (fetched_sha256 strictness precedent); unset preserves prior behaviour byte-for-byte.
Independent of R277/R284 external-prefix axes; lets one source file cite multiple specs, each gated by its own workspace ledger.
verification_bullets: +6 validate tests (namespace scope: foreign skip, in-scope fire, unset back-compat, exact-segment not prefix, no-hyphen foreign) + 2 config tests (empty reject / accepted).
Full workspace suite 0 fail; clippy --workspace -D warnings + fmt clean. Pure addition (no deletions).
carry_forward_bullets: SCE (named consumer) is the live driver: docs/sce-ledger/{mesh,wire} workspaces will set section_namespace=mesh/wire so §mesh-* and §wire-* cites gate against their own ledgers while §scxml-* stays under docs/spec/scxml.
Test helper uses a default-delegating wrapper (scan_paths_no_resolvers -> _ns) so existing call sites are untouched; matches the per-site too_many_arguments allow already noted in pre-commit gate 5.

Round 377 — commit↔ledger gate multi-wor.decision_summary: commit↔ledger drift gate (R293/R301) assumed a single-workspace repo: it scanned repo-global commit subjects for (R<n>) labels and diffed against one workspace ledger, so in a multi-workspace mono-repo a sibling workspace round label false-flagged every workspace (SCE adoption report). Two-part fix, same class as R376 citation-namespace. D1: path-scope the git-log scan to the workspace subtree (git log -- .) so a sibling-only label no longer bleeds in. D2: [commit_ledger].severity (default reject, preserving the R301 dogfood hard-reject) lets a consumer workspace whose (R<n>) labels are not Mnemosyne changelog rounds downgrade to warn/info — the drift line still prints, the exit code is not gated. Round-namespace relabeling deferred YAGNI: the gate is a Mnemosyne self-development invariant that does not apply to a pure consumer ledger.

print_commit_ledger_drift_surface: [commit_ledger].severity gates exit — reject bails (R301), warn/info print-only
config: CommitLedgerSection / [commit_ledger].severity, default reject, validated reject|warn|info at load
dogfood unaffected: workspace root = git root, so `-- .` = whole repo (cited=35, drift still 0)
verification_bullets: mnemosyne-config: 3 unit tests (default reject / warn opt-out / invalid severity rejected)
mnemosyne-cli: 3 commit_ledger_gate smoke tests — default reject gates, warn opts out (drift line still printed), path-scope no sibling bleed (ws_b passes, ws_a gated)
clippy --all-targets clean, fmt clean, dogfood validate-workspace green (cited=35 / missing=0)
carry_forward_bullets: D2 round-namespace ([commit_ledger] round_label, R376-parallel) deferred YAGNI — add only if a consumer needs an active drift gate under a foreign label
SCE consumer mitigation: set [commit_ledger] severity = "warn" in each of its 3 workspaces' mnemosyne.toml

Round 379 — external code-ref matcher: m.decision_summary: pinion (W3C-citing Rust GUI, MNEMO-GAP-001) adopted validate-code-refs (Mnemosyne change 0, confirming un-adopted-not-un-served) and PoC-measured the residual false-positives. The external-standard skip matcher (is_external_section_cite, R277/281/284) only covered single-token prefix + pure-numeric document token, so W3C forms fell through: UAX #9 (# breaks numeric detection) and CSS Color 4 (multi-word name; only Color was read). R379 widens the matcher in-paradigm — document-number recognizer accepts leading # and trailing letters; prefix match becomes a multi-word token-boundary suffix. Verbatim-prefix gate preserved, so over-skip stays bounded to registered prefixes (no internal cite silently skipped). Config schema unchanged. Closes (a)+(b) (~38 of pinion measured cases); the pinion-discovered (c) multi-cite chaining + (d) comment-block-scope shapes are deferred to a follow-up round (extraction context-model change, separate over-skip surface).
changes_bullets: is_external_section_cite: new is_document_number_token (leading # + trailing-letter doc numbers: UAX #9, 802.11ax) + prose_ends_with_prefix (multi-word prefix matched as token-boundary suffix)
covers UAX/UTS/UTR #N, CSS Color 4 (numeric multi-word), Unicode Standard (bare multi-word); config schema unchanged (Vec<String> values may now contain spaces)
SCHEMA_GUIDE external-standard-prefix section: multi-word + #N documented; stale "register trailing token" workaround removed
verification_bullets: +3 code_refs tests (hash doc-number / multi-word / e2e W3C skip) + all 113 code_refs tests green, zero regression on RFC/IEEE/ISO/WCAG/WAI-ARIA paths
negatives lock no over-skip: SCSS != CSS, "random Color 4" != "CSS Color", unregistered #9 not skipped, internal 5.16 still extracted
clippy --all-targets clean, fmt clean
carry_forward_bullets: follow-up round = (c) multi-cite chaining + (d) comment-block-scope wrapped prefix (pinion-measured ~19 cases); extraction context-model change, over-skip risk needs care; pinion holds severity=warn until both land
LOW unbatched: cli/main.rs cmd_validate_workspace comment wrongly says commit-ledger surface never gates the exit code (R377 made it gate at severity=reject)
pinion-side (not Mnemosyne): adjective forms (5.13-style) + bare prose cites = pinion authoring cleanup

Round 380 — external code-ref matcher: s.decision_summary: pinion MNEMO-GAP-001 PoC measured two citation-SCOPE shapes R379 token-shape fix did not cover: (c) same-line multi-cite chaining (UAX #9 sectionA / sectionB — only the first sigil has the prefix immediately before it) and (d) comment-block wrap (a standard-name prefix on one /// line, the sigil on the next). R380 widens the scope inside extract_section_citations: a line-local chain inherits external context across /+whitespace separators from a prior external cite, and a marker-only sigil inherits when the immediately previous comment line ends with the prefix. Over-skip (silently not validating a real internal cite) is the dangerous direction, so it is bounded four ways: the verbatim-prefix gate is preserved, a comma or word breaks the chain, only the immediately previous line carries, and the prefix must be that line trailing content. Closes pinion (c)+(d); with R379 (a)+(b) all four measured false-positive classes are covered, so pinion can promote severity=reject and wire the pre-commit gate.

Round 381 — adopter docs: validate-code-.decision_summary: SCE (named live consumer) asked how an external consumer runs validate-code-refs as a CI gate — Mnemosyne ships no prebuilt binaries and had no consumer CI docs. Decision: cargo install --git --rev <pinned-sha> --locked mnemosyne-cli — lightest, reproducible (rev-pin freezes the citation grammar until a visible bump, which a compliance ledger wants), zero new release infra for a pre-release project whose consumers already run cargo. Prebuilt release binaries and a packaged GitHub Action are deferred until a non-Rust consumer or a measured CI-compile cost appears. Documented with a per-workspace consumer CI job. Same round also documents the citation-token id-boundary grammar edges SCE reported (hyphen-glue over-extension; dot continues only between digits), reconciles the now-stale self-contained-citation-rule section with R379/380, and fixes the dogfood workflow stale [code_refs]/DESIGN references (the R374-class silent-skip footgun in a copyable example). Doc and comment only.

Round 382 — C2: in-tree tree-sitter-cpp.decision_summary: C2 (resolver wiring) was gated on SCE contributing an in-tree tree-sitter-cpp plugin; the PR arrived. Landed SCE the in-tree mnemosyne-plugin-tree-sitter-cpp crate (TreesitterCppResolver implementing the core SymbolResolver port via tree-sitter-cpp, smallest-covering-declaration like the rust backend, with C++ declarator-nesting handled: out-of-line definitions resolve to the qualified Foo::bar form, inline members to the bare name) and wired it into build_symbol_resolver_map (cpp BACKEND_KEY branch) + lang_for_file (C/C++ header+source extensions). RFC-002 FR-3 symbol-level enforcement (the SymbolMismatch axis) now works for C++. Reviewed and verified before land (build/tests/clippy/fmt). Kept the existing hardcoded dispatch + ext map, consistent with the rust backend; the C2 contract broader refactor (route through PluginRegistry + config-driven ext->lang) is deferred as YAGNI at two in-process backends, to revisit when a 3rd or out-of-process resolver appears.
changes_bullets: new crate mnemosyne-plugin-tree-sitter-cpp: TreesitterCppResolver (SymbolResolver via tree-sitter-cpp 0.23); query captures declaration nodes, bounded declarator descent extracts the name; qualified Foo::bar out-of-line, bare member inline; mirrors the rust plugin shape
wiring: build_symbol_resolver_map cpp BACKEND_KEY branch (cli/main.rs); lang_for_file += h/hh/hpp/hxx/cpp/cc/cxx -> cpp (code_refs.rs); validate + cli dep the crate
tests: 10 plugin unit + symbol_enforcement_cpp_smoke (happy / mismatch / opt-out, mirrors the rust FR-3 smoke)

verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace clean; cpp 10 unit + cpp-smoke 3 + rust-smoke 4 regression all green (no regression); clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings clean; fmt clean
reviewed: structure parity with rust plugin, C++ declarator-descent correctness, fail-loud (no swallow), bounded 64-depth descent guard, no vN
carry_forward_bullets: DEFERRED refactor (C2 contract): route build_symbol_resolver_map through PluginRegistry + make lang_for_file ext->lang config-driven; YAGNI at 2 in-process backends, trigger = a 3rd / external / out-of-process (Transport) resolver
HEADS-UP: lang_for_file maps .h but not .c (C++-only; confirm for any C consumer); template<...> parameter line resolves to None (best-effort)
NEXT (consumer): SCE reinstalls mnemosyne-cli at the R382 rev so cpp symbol citations enforce

Round 383 — set-membership symbol bindin.decision_summary: RFC-002 FR-3 symbol enforcement under-represented the store. The validator indexed Implementation.symbol as section->file->symbol via an iterator .collect() into BTreeMap, so when a section recorded more than one symbol in the same file (which add_section_implementation explicitly allows — set semantics, only exact (file,symbol) dups rejected) the collect kept only the last, and a legitimate cite inside any earlier symbol falsely surfaced as SymbolMismatch. R383 makes the index set-valued (section->file->{symbols}); a cite is bound at symbol granularity iff its resolved enclosing symbol is a MEMBER of the registered set. Backward compatible (a set of one reproduces the prior single-symbol check). This is a correctness fix aligning the validator index with the store invariant, not a new capability.
changes_bullets: impl symbol index changed section->file->symbol (last-wins map) to section->file->{symbols} (BTreeSet); a cite binds iff its resolved enclosing symbol is a member
old .collect() into BTreeMap silently dropped all-but-last symbol on duplicate file keys -> false SymbolMismatch when a section had >1 symbol in one file; the store already allowed it (set semantics)
SymbolMismatch docstring + binding-site comments updated to set-membership semantics
verification_bullets: new smoke set_membership_multiple_symbols_one_file: alpha/beta bound, unregistered gamma = exactly 1 mismatch (line 8)

carry_forward_bullets: prerequisite confirmed: add_section_implementation permits multiple (file, symbol) entries (rejects only exact dup); index now matches that store invariant

Round 384 — Path B curation tooling (pro.decision_summary: Path B (code->section binding) adoption needs a curation path: an external consumer with thousands of existing citations cannot hand-author every section->file->symbol implementation entry. R384 adds propose-implementations, a read-only tool that scans the cited code, resolves each citations enclosing symbol via the language SymbolResolver, and emits the proposed implementation sets (plus ready-to-run add-section-implementation commands) for maintainer ratification — the review doubles as an audit of where each section is cited. Paired with it, the cpp resolver gains documented-symbol semantics: a section citation in source is conventionally a doc comment ABOVE the declaration it documents, but tree-sitters smallest-enclosing-node would bind it to the outer namespace/class; documented_symbol detects a comment that (with no intervening blank line) immediately precedes a declaration and binds to that declaration instead, falling through to enclosing scope for file-header/taxonomy comments. Both additive and read-only; no severity or scan behaviour changes.
changes_bullets: new propose-implementations CLI command + SetEqualityValidator::propose_implementations: scan cites, resolve each enclosing symbol, emit proposed section->file->{symbols} sets + add-section-implementation commands (read-only; maintainer ratifies)

no regression: R382 cpp smoke 3 + rust smoke 5 green (body-comment cite still resolves to enclosing fn — next sibling is a statement, not a decl)

Round 385 — split severity_coverage from.decision_summary: Round 269 added the ImplementationMissing edge but bucketed it under severity_binding (C1, YAGNI) and carried the severity_coverage split decision pending empirical evidence from external workspace adoption. SCE spec-mirror adoption is that evidence: spec-mirror sections are mostly normative prose that is legitimately uncited, so coverage (an Active section having zero implementations) must be downgradable independently of binding (the per-edge cite/symbol checks). R385 realizes the split: ImplementationMissing moves from binding_count to its own coverage_count gated by a new severity_coverage axis; the binding bucket is now CitationUnbound + ImplementationUnbacked + SymbolMismatch. severity_coverage inherits severity_binding when unset (flag > config > binding), so the dogfood and every pre-split config are unchanged. Validated at the CLI use site like the sibling severities. A consumer downgrades coverage to warn/info while keeping binding reject — previously impossible.

Round 386 — complete [workspace] root: o.decision_summary: The [workspace] root override was only half-wired: repo_root() returned the RESOLVED root, then ops called discover_config(resolved_root), which walks UP and misses a subdir-located mnemosyne.toml — so a ledger rooted in a subdirectory (the SCE 3-workspace layout) silently fell back to the default sidecar, loading an empty store (section_missing 58). R386 completes it: ops take an anchor (the toml dir) and resolve the declared [workspace] root themselves via workspace_root_from; workspace-relative sidecar/output/doc/code paths join the declared root, while an explicit --sidecar/--output override short-circuits discovery and joins the anchor. The CLI splits repo_root() into workspace_anchor() (passed to every ops/mutate call) and workspace_root() (the resolved root, for abs->repo-relative normalization). A subdir ledger now resolves code paths repo-relative with no .. traversal. Dogfood unchanged: with [workspace] root unset the anchor equals the resolved root.

Round 389 — coverage applicability class.decision_summary: Adds AtomicSection.coverage_expectation (normative | informative), a per-section classification that makes the Round 269 coverage axiom precise. Normative (default) keeps the axiom; Informative exempts prose-only sections (terminology / overview / references) that have nothing to implement here, so an external-spec ledger's meta sections no longer drown real gaps in false impl_missing. The enum is canonical in mnemosyne-core beside BindingKind (not the medium-neutral skeleton); the field is adapter-local on AtomicSection beside bindings, skipped from JSON when default so unclassified stores stay byte-identical. schema_version 5 to 6 migrates declaratively (serde default, no load arm); the default is the conservative no-op so no migration report is needed. Two write paths (setter + import-sections field) enforce one closed value set, pinned by a parity test. Rendered deviation-only in GENERATED.md and surfaced on the query read view. The positive coverage report is deferred to R390.

Round 390 — report-coverage positive pro.decision_summary: Adds the read-only report-coverage CLI verb (TTY + --json): the positive 3-way coverage breakdown — implemented / normative-gap / informative-exempt — plus the Removed tombstones excluded from the denominator and a coverage ratio over the applicable (Normative, non-Removed) set. Round 391 — North Star clarified: spec i.decision_summary: Clarifies the ARCHITECTURE North Star (section 1) so its model is not re-derived. A specification is one instance of a narrative (spec subset narrative), not a coordinate second pillar: spec-compliance is the tractable beachhead, a game-engine narrative manager is the encompassing target, and the North Star does not change as the medium widens. Facts are multi-axis: the actual/historical fact and each agent's understood fact are distinct facts on distinct axes, both true; frames are never cross-validated for agreement, and an actor's behaviour derives from its own frame. branch is therefore the epistemic-frame mechanism (coexisting perspectival truths, never merged), which is the deep reason the bitemporal/branch foundation is load-bearing, not speculative. Validation is frame-scoped: strict within a frame, cross-frame disagreement is data not a violation. Adds section 1.1 and tweaks the North Star blockquote to mark the subset relation. Doc-only; ARCHITECTURE is hand-maintained (not round-trip).

Round 392 — report-spec-map unified spec.decision_summary: Adds the read-only report-spec-map CLI verb (TTY + --json): a unified per-section spec-fact-code projection joining the coverage class (single-sourced through classify_coverage so the map never drifts from report-coverage / validate-code-refs), the external-spec provenance (normative_excerpt anchor_url + source_revision), the Path B bindings, the spec-revision drift flag (validate-spec-drift), and a reverse citation count from a new SetEqualityValidator::citation_index aggregation (section to citing code sites). No authoritative state of its own — every field is projected from the atomic store plus a code-citation scan, an L3 view mirroring report-coverage / report-binding-migration. Citation density requires the set_equality_validator plugin; when absent the rest of the map still projects with zero counts. Feeds future spec-map visualization (ToC overlay / coverage / citation density / drift in one graph). CLI-only; MCP tool deferred (SCE CI consumes JSON, YAGNI). Backfilled entry: the feature shipped at commit f78f458 as an un-numbered feature commit with no atomic-store entry; this restores the round to the single source of truth.

Round 396 — store-direct cross-ref orpha.decision_summary: Move the T1 cross-ref orphan check off parsed markdown onto the atomic store. scan_store_prose_cross_ref_orphans scans each section's synthesized prose for numeric section references and resolves them against the store (intra-doc set, last-segment alias, atomic) — the same resolution chain as the parsed-markdown path, but store-direct. The numeric scanner is single-sourced as mnemosyne_core::numeric_section_refs, reused by both the markdown parser and the new validator so the two cannot diverge. Structured references stay validated by the atomic referential closure. The parsed-markdown orphan fn and its tests remain as the generic markdown-doc capability until the markdown model is torn out. Dogfood orphan stays 0.

Round 403 — normative_excerpt becomes EP.decision_summary: Flip normative_excerpt.text from a frozen hand-authored value to a derived cache projected from the committed EPUB. The frozen-once-set rejection is removed so the cache can be refreshed; build_normative_excerpt now verifies the declared text_sha256 against sha256(stored text) when present. The new import_epub_excerpts primitive (sibling of import_epub_anchors) refreshes text + text_sha256 from a medium-forge epub-anchor-map/v2 while preserving the authored anchor_url + source_revision (store-side identity, not EPUB-projected). The hand-authoring set_section_normative_excerpt (atomic fn + CLI verb + MCP tool) is deleted per no-legacy-carry, leaving import_epub_excerpts and import_sections-inline as the two write paths, both routing through one build_normative_excerpt validator pinned by a field-invariant parity test.
changes_bullets: Removed the frozen-once-set rejection on normative_excerpt; text is now an EPUB-projected cache (overwrite allowed), no longer an immutable authored value.
build_normative_excerpt now takes text_sha256 and verifies sha256(stored text) equals the declared hash when non-empty (empty hash = unrevalidatable, hand-authored).

Round 404 — scan_content_drift: offline.decision_summary: Add the offline revalidation gate that makes the EPUB-as-content-SSOT claim trustworthy. scan_content_drift re-hashes each excerpt's stored text against its declared text_sha256 entirely offline (no EPUB, no Python); a non-empty hash that no longer equals sha256(text) is a HashMismatch finding — the cache was edited out-of-band (direct sidecar JSON edit, bypassing the mutate API which guarantees the hash at write time). NormativeExcerpt owns the predicate (recompute_text_sha256 + text_sha256_matches). The [content_drift] severity config defaults to reject (unlike [spec_drift]'s warn) because a cache diverging from its own hash is corruption, never a legitimate intermediate state. validate-content-drift composes the mismatch list (severity-gated) with the R402 backfill report's unrevalidatable count (informational), single-sourcing each concern. EPUB-file provenance (epub_sha256/epub_path) + commit-EPUB + SCE runbook are a separate axis deferred to R405.
changes_bullets: New mnemosyne-validate::scan_content_drift (sibling of scan_spec_drift): offline re-hash of each excerpt's stored text vs its declared text_sha256; a non-empty hash that no longer matches sha256(text) is a HashMismatch finding (out-of-band tamper / direct-JSON edit of the cache).
NormativeExcerpt gains recompute_text_sha256() + text_sha256_matches() (None when empty = unrevalidatable), so the type owns its content-integrity predicate.
New [content_drift].severity config (default reject; mirror of [spec_drift]) validated at config load; a cache that diverges from its hash is never a legitimate intermediate state.

New validate-content-drift CLI verb: prints HashMismatch findings (severity-gated) plus the unrevalidatable count sourced from the R402 excerpt_hash_backfill_report (informational, never gates) — each concern single-sourced.
SCHEMA_GUIDE.md documents [content_drift] + the offline revalidation model.
EPUB-file provenance (SpecSource.epub_sha256 / epub_path) + commit-EPUB convention + SCE runbook deferred to R405 (independent axis: EPUB-file identity vs cached-text integrity).

Round 405 — EPUB-file provenance: SpecSo.decision_summary: Close the EPUB-as-content-SSOT loop with the EPUB-file provenance anchor. SpecSource gains epub_path + epub_sha256 pinning the committed content-SSOT EPUB. validate-content-drift, when those are set, re-hashes the committed EPUB file offline and compares to the pin — a mismatch (the EPUB was swapped/updated) or a missing file is gated under [content_drift].severity alongside the per-excerpt cache scan. This is the more fundamental gate: if the EPUB file drifted, every excerpt is suspect even when each still matches its own now-stale hash. The mismatch is also the trigger for the Layer B remediation (re-run medium-forge, import-epub-excerpts, re-pin epub_sha256), which stays Python/CI-side per the hexagonal boundary. docs/EPUB_SSOT_RUNBOOK.md documents the full external-consumer adoption path so SCE can formally switch its 196-section W3C ledger.
changes_bullets: SpecSource gains epub_path + epub_sha256 (the committed content-SSOT EPUB and its pinned hash), validated at config load: 64-char lowercase hex, and a both-or-neither pairing (a path without a hash cannot be checked; a hash without a path has nothing to check).

validate-content-drift now also re-hashes the committed EPUB file at epub_path vs epub_sha256 (offline file hash, no Python); a swapped/updated or missing EPUB is gated under [content_drift].severity alongside the per-excerpt content scan, so one green run means EPUB-file pinned AND every cache matches its hash.

Round 406 — seal-excerpt-hashes: seal co.decision_summary: SCE's RFC reply showed that EPUB-as-content-SSOT is one model, not the only one: a consumer whose excerpt text is authored by its own extractor (SCE's direct-body scope is finer than the EPUB div granularity — 0/196 byte-identical, projecting EPUB text would collapse 196 precise excerpts into 167 heading-polluted overlapping blobs) treats its own text as SSOT, with the EPUB file pin as source provenance only. R403 deleted the hand-authoring setter assuming EPUB is the sole text source, leaving no way to fill text_sha256 from already-stored text (report-excerpt-hash-backfill reports only; import-epub-excerpts is EPUB-sourced). seal_excerpt_hashes fills the gap: for each excerpt with an empty text_sha256, set text_sha256 = sha256(text), sealing the authored text as its own revalidation baseline — never overwriting text and never re-sealing a populated hash (a wrong populated hash stays drift for validate-content-drift). This unblocks SCE's 196 excerpts without regression and generalizes the model: excerpt text may be EPUB-projected or consumer-authored, unified by the integrity hash and the EPUB-file provenance pin.
changes_bullets: New seal_excerpt_hashes primitive + seal-excerpt-hashes CLI verb: stamp text_sha256 = sha256(text) on every excerpt whose hash is empty, sealing the already-stored (consumer-authored) text as its own revalidation baseline. Never overwrites text or a populated hash; idempotent (a second run seals 0).

Generalizes the content-SSOT model after SCE's RFC: excerpt text may be EPUB-projected (import-epub-excerpts) OR consumer-authored (import-sections then seal). The EPUB-file pin (R405) is source provenance either way; text_sha256 is the integrity anchor over whichever text is authoritative. SCE's direct-body extractor is finer than the EPUB div granularity (0/196 byte-identical), so projecting EPUB text would regress 196 precise excerpts into 167 collapsed blobs.
report-excerpt-hash-backfill hint now names both fills (seal from stored text, or import-epub-excerpts from EPUB).
SCHEMA_GUIDE.md + EPUB_SSOT_RUNBOOK.md document the two models (EPUB-projected vs consumer-authored-sealed).

Build when a consumer wants EPUB projection below div scope.

Round 407 — medium-forge heading-scope e.decision_summary: SCE adopted the full EPUB-as-content-SSOT model (committed EPUB = authoritative excerpt text), un-holding ask 2. medium-forge must extract at HEADING granularity, not container subtree: W3C SCXML section ids are finer than div1..div4 (Appendix D has ~15 h4 function sections under one div1), so container.text_content() collapsed 29 distinct ids onto one blob (0/196 byte-identical). New --text-scope {container,heading}: heading mode emits each section's direct body (heading to next heading, descendants excluded) with the heading text excluded (the body-only canonical rule — the heading already lives in AtomicSection.title), porting SCE's proven heading-to-next-heading extractor for cutover diff fidelity. Container-only sections emit the locator only (no text), so import-epub-excerpts skips them while import-epub-anchors keeps the pointer. An anchor-collapse guard signals distinct ids that resolve to identical text via needs_review + confidence 0.0 rather than emitting a duplicate sha. EPUB rendering is unchanged; no Rust or schema change (import-epub-excerpts already overwrites text+text_sha256 unconditionally). Resolves the gating dependency for SCE's full EPUB-SSOT cutover.
changes_bullets: medium-forge convert.py: new --text-scope {container,heading} (default container). heading mode extracts each anchored section's DIRECT body (its heading up to the next heading of any level, descendants excluded), with the heading text itself excluded (body-only canonical rule — the heading lives in AtomicSection.title). Ports SCE's proven heading-to-next-heading BodyExtractor (the algorithm that produced the correct 196), whitespace-collapsed, script/style dropped.
Container-only sections (blank direct body) emit the locator only (text + text_sha256 keys omitted) so import-epub-excerpts skips them while import-epub-anchors keeps the EPUB pointer.
Anchor-collapse guard (both scopes): distinct ids resolving to identical text get needs_review = true + confidence 0.0 instead of silently emitting a duplicate text + sha.

EPUB rendering is unchanged (still publishes full container subtrees); only the v2 text/text_sha256 field changes scope. No Rust or schema change — import-epub-excerpts already overwrites text + text_sha256 unconditionally.
README documents the two scopes + the body-only rule.
verification_bullets: Ran convert.py on a fixture HTML (nested h2/h3/h4 plus an Appendix-style h4-under-one-div1): heading scope emits each heading's own direct body, heading text excluded, zero collapse; container scope reproduces the prior single-blob collapse. Fixture removed after.
Anchor-collapse guard verified: two ids forced onto identical text both surface needs_review true + confidence 0.0.
Rust workspace unaffected (Python-only change); validate-workspace green, validate-content-drift unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: heading set = h1-h6 (generic tool); SCE's proven extractor used h2-h4. No difference expected for W3C SCXML (title is h1 with no anchored section, body sections are h2-h4, no h5/h6); SCE's step-3 diff-review gate will catch any h1/h5/h6 edge. Flagged to SCE.

R406 seal stays the interim anchor; OPEN in-place authored-text setter stays YAGNI per SCE.

publishable_changes_bullets: medium-forge convert.py: new --text-scope {container,heading} (default container). heading mode extracts each anchored section's DIRECT body (its heading up to the next heading of any level, descendants excluded), with the heading text itself excluded (body-only canonical rule — the heading lives in AtomicSection.title). Ports SCE's proven heading-to-next-heading BodyExtractor (the algorithm that produced the correct 196), whitespace-collapsed, script/style dropped.
Container-only sections (blank direct body) emit the locator only (text + text_sha256 keys omitted) so import-epub-excerpts skips them while import-epub-anchors keeps the EPUB pointer.
Anchor-collapse guard (both scopes): distinct ids resolving to identical text get needs_review = true + confidence 0.0 instead of silently emitting a duplicate text + sha.

EPUB rendering is unchanged (still publishes full container subtrees); only the v2 text/text_sha256 field changes scope. No Rust or schema change — import-epub-excerpts already overwrites text + text_sha256 unconditionally.
README documents the two scopes + the body-only rule.
publishable_verification_bullets: Ran convert.py on a fixture HTML (nested h2/h3/h4 plus an Appendix-style h4-under-one-div1): heading scope emits each heading's own direct body, heading text excluded, zero collapse; container scope reproduces the prior single-blob collapse. Fixture removed after.
Anchor-collapse guard verified: two ids forced onto identical text both surface needs_review true + confidence 0.0.
Rust workspace unaffected (Python-only change); validate-workspace green, validate-content-drift unchanged.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: heading set = h1-h6 (generic tool); SCE's proven extractor used h2-h4. No difference expected for W3C SCXML (title is h1 with no anchored section, body sections are h2-h4, no h5/h6); SCE's step-3 diff-review gate will catch any h1/h5/h6 edge. Flagged to SCE.

ask 2 resolved -> SCE unblocked for the full EPUB-SSOT cutover (vendor fixed medium-forge -> regenerate spec.epub + v2 anchors -> diff-review -> import-epub-excerpts -> pin epub_path/epub_sha256 -> validate-content-drift in CI). R406 seal stays the interim anchor; OPEN in-place authored-text setter stays YAGNI per SCE.

Round 408 — retire seal-excerpt-hashes (.decision_summary: SCE completed the full EPUB-as-content-SSOT cutover (origin/main 8f1c36786, store v6 to v8, 197 sections, import-epub-excerpts hash-mismatch 0, epub_path/epub_sha256 pinned, validate-content-drift CI green) and confirmed seal-excerpt-hashes was the fallback path, UNUSED — they took the EPUB-projected path exclusively, with no demand for consumer-authored text or in-place arbitrary text update. The consumer-authored text model's only concrete consumer is gone, so keeping seal_excerpt_hashes for a hypothetical consumer violates no-legacy-carry / YAGNI — the same basis on which R403 deleted set-section-normative-excerpt once EPUB + import-sections absorbed its need. Remove the seal-specific surface (primitive + CLI verb + tests/smoke + the report hint mention + the consumer-authored doc sections) while keeping the shared EPUB-projected revalidation foundation. The R406 entry stays as the frozen historical record that seal existed and why (audit lives in the store, code lives in code); the model can be re-added from it if a future consumer needs store-authored sealing.

Kept the shared revalidation foundation untouched: text_sha256, build_normative_excerpt hash verify, NormativeExcerpt recompute/matches, scan_content_drift + validate-content-drift, report-excerpt-hash-backfill (empty-hash list), epub_path/epub_sha256, import-epub-excerpts.

Round 409 — parent_doc canonical post-R4.decision_summary: SCE asked the canonical value of AtomicSection.parent_doc after R400 removed the render path. Investigation: parent_doc is a mandatory non-empty L0 SectionSkeleton field (medium-neutral; surfaced in query / facts / schema / cascade index; mutable via set_section_parent_doc) and is NOT validated against any file post-R400 (the file-path interpretation died with the markdown-doc parser and render). CANONICAL: parent_doc is a free-form LOGICAL owning-document / namespace label, not a filesystem path and not provenance (provenance lives in normative_excerpt.anchor_url + [workspace.spec_source]). Of SCE's three options: option 2 (clear/null) is structurally impossible (mandatory non-empty); option 1 (keep docs/GENERATED.md) is a harmless-but-stale render-era leftover, not canonical; option 3 (a meaningful logical doc id) is correct, with the caveat that the target is a grouping label, never a source URL. Re-binding is cosmetic (no functional consumer breaks) and safe (set_section_parent_doc is a dedicated mutable setter, not frozen-ledger). Aligned the dogfood's sections to the logical label mnemosyne-design so adopters can follow the canonical without diverging from upstream. parent_doc is not on a deprecation roadmap.
changes_bullets: Documented the post-R400 canonical for AtomicSection.parent_doc in SCHEMA_GUIDE: a free-form LOGICAL owning-document / namespace label (medium-neutral L0), NOT a filesystem path, NOT validated against any file (the file-path reading died with the markdown-doc parser/render in R400), and NOT provenance (provenance lives in normative_excerpt.anchor_url + [workspace.spec_source]).

Aligned the dogfood store: re-bound all sections' parent_doc from the stale render-era "docs/GENERATED.md" to the logical label "mnemosyne-design" via set-section-parent-doc, so the canonical is exemplified upstream and adopters do not diverge.
verification_bullets: validate-workspace green: parent_doc is not file-validated, so the re-bind touches no gate (T1/T3/frozen/orphan unaffected); ledger entries reference sections by section_id, not parent_doc.
No Rust change (store mutate + doc only); workspace build/test/clippy/fmt unaffected.
carry_forward_bullets: parent_doc is NOT on a deprecation/removal roadmap; it stays a live L0 grouping field. Its semantics narrowed from file-path (markdown-doc era) to logical owning-document label. Clearing/nulling is structurally impossible (mandatory non-empty); option 2 is off the table without a schema change that is not planned.

Adopters: set parent_doc to a logical document id (e.g. scxml, mesh, wire) at leisure via set-section-parent-doc. This is cosmetic — nothing cross-validates parent_doc between stores, so there is no functional divergence either way; render-era leftover values are harmless and may be grandfathered.
parent_doc != provenance: do not point it at an EPUB path or spec URL; that conflates grouping with source. Source provenance is normative_excerpt.anchor_url + [workspace.spec_source].epub_path/url.

Round 412 — activate BindingKind::verifi.decision_summary: Activate the reserved Verifies binding kind. A verifies binding records a test/evidence artifact for a section; it counts as neither implements-coverage nor a code↔spec citation edge, so it is excluded from the bidirectional citation set-equality — a verifies test file is externally mapped (e.g. a conformance manifest) and is not required to cite the section. Unblocks SCE registering verification bindings ahead of the VerificationMissing coverage gate.

code_refs: counts_as_coverage(Verifies)=false, plus a new is_citation_edge predicate that excludes verifies from the citation set-equality (citation_unbound / binding_unbacked / symbol_mismatch)
CLI and MCP add-section-binding + set-section-binding-kind now accept --kind verifies

binding_kind_write_path_parity extended to all three variants; workspace build + all-targets clippy + fmt all green
non-mutating CLI smoke confirms --kind verifies is accepted (fails only on the missing section, not on kind)

Round 413 — verify axis: VerificationExp.decision_summary: Add the verify axis. A VerificationExpectation {Dedicated, ByConstruction} classification, orthogonal to coverage_expectation, plus the VerificationMissing gate: a Normative + Dedicated, non-Removed section with zero verifies bindings is the gap. Opt-in via [plugins.set_equality_validator].severity_verification — None disables the axis entirely, so a workspace that registers no verifies bindings (e.g. the dogfood) is unaffected. ByConstruction sections (no per-unit oracle) and Informative sections are exempt. The classification is consumer-supplied; the ledger only enforces it. SCE-confirmed shape.

Round 414 — cleanup: exhaustive sort_key.decision_summary: Session-review cleanup of two R413 structural smells. (1) Remove the sort_violations (a,b) wildcard tiebreaker that defeated the exhaustive-match discipline — it compiled with a missing same-rank arm and panicked at runtime (the R413 verify-axis bug, caught only by live e2e); replace with a single exhaustive sort_key match so a new variant is a compile error. (2) Extract is_verification_gap as the single-source predicate mirroring classify_section_coverage, so the verify gate and any future positive projection cannot drift. Add a CLI smoke test locking the opt-in/gate/exempt wiring.
changes_bullets: sort_violations: the (a,b) wildcard tiebreaker became an exhaustive ViolationSortKey computed in one match, sorted via sort_by_cached_key; a new variant is now a compile error here, not a runtime panic
is_verification_gap extracted as the single-source predicate (mirrors classify_section_coverage); scan Step 5 calls it instead of inlining the gap definition
new verification_axis_smoke.rs CLI regression: axis off by default, --severity-verification reject gates the Dedicated gap, by_construction exempts it
verification_bullets: full workspace test + all-targets clippy + fmt green; sort and gate behavior preserved across 157 validate tests
new CLI smoke locks the opt-in/gate/exempt wiring that was previously only manually e2e-checked
carry_forward_bullets: session-review finding: the exhaustive-match guarantee was applied inconsistently — counts_as_coverage and is_citation_edge enforced it, but the sort wildcard silently lost it (compiled with a missing arm, panicked at runtime on the R413 multi-gap case). A wildcard cross-variant tiebreaker defeats the compile-time variant check; avoid it
no further verify-axis work queued Mnemosyne-side; v2 verifies-link freshness + report-coverage surface remain deferred
Round 414 — cleanup: exhaustive sort_key.publishable_decision_summary: Session-review cleanup of two R413 structural smells. (1) Remove the sort_violations (a,b) wildcard tiebreaker that defeated the exhaustive-match discipline — it compiled with a missing same-rank arm and panicked at runtime (the R413 verify-axis bug, caught only by live e2e); replace with a single exhaustive sort_key match so a new variant is a compile error. (2) Extract is_verification_gap as the single-source predicate mirroring classify_section_coverage, so the verify gate and any future positive projection cannot drift. Add a CLI smoke test locking the opt-in/gate/exempt wiring.
publishable_changes_bullets: sort_violations: the (a,b) wildcard tiebreaker became an exhaustive ViolationSortKey computed in one match, sorted via sort_by_cached_key; a new variant is now a compile error here, not a runtime panic
is_verification_gap extracted as the single-source predicate (mirrors classify_section_coverage); scan Step 5 calls it instead of inlining the gap definition
new verification_axis_smoke.rs CLI regression: axis off by default, --severity-verification reject gates the Dedicated gap, by_construction exempts it
publishable_verification_bullets: full workspace test + all-targets clippy + fmt green; sort and gate behavior preserved across 157 validate tests
new CLI smoke locks the opt-in/gate/exempt wiring that was previously only manually e2e-checked
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: session-review finding: the exhaustive-match guarantee was applied inconsistently — counts_as_coverage and is_citation_edge enforced it, but the sort wildcard silently lost it (compiled with a missing arm, panicked at runtime on the R413 multi-gap case). A wildcard cross-variant tiebreaker defeats the compile-time variant check; avoid it
no further verify-axis work queued Mnemosyne-side; v2 verifies-link freshness + report-coverage surface remain deferred

Round 415 — Severity enum: replace strin.decision_summary: Replace the stringly-typed severity model (String + scattered matches!(reject|warn|info)) with a parsed Severity {Reject, Warn, Info} enum in mnemosyne-config. Validation now happens once: serde at config load, and Severity::from_tag at the CLI --severity-* boundary. All 8 reject-class knobs convert (5 set-equality severities + the spec_drift/commit_ledger/content_drift gates). The TOML format is unchanged (still the reject/warn/info strings) — a pure internal-type refactor with no migration. Severity lives in config, not core, because it is config policy, never stored.
changes_bullets: new Severity {Reject, Warn, Info} enum in mnemosyne-config (as_str/from_tag/is_reject, default Reject, serde lowercase); the 8 reject-class config knobs (5 set-equality + spec_drift/commit_ledger/content_drift) + the 2 default fns are typed to it

CLI resolves each --severity-* through one Severity::from_tag (the single validation point) and decides reject via .is_reject() / == Some(Severity::Reject); the scattered matches!("reject"|"warn"|"info") checks are gone
removed the 3 redundant config-load severity validations (serde rejects invalid values at deserialization) and reworked the 3 invalid-severity tests to assert serde's unknown-variant rejection
verification_bullets: full workspace test + all-targets clippy + fmt green; dogfood validate-code-refs output and reject behavior preserved end-to-end
TOML severity strings are unchanged (still reject/warn/info) so the dogfood and SCE configs deserialize identically — a pure internal-type refactor, no migration
carry_forward_bullets: session-review debt closure: this was the inherited stringly-typed severity debt flagged after R414. Severity lives in mnemosyne-config (not core) because it is a config-policy concept, never stored in the atomic store, unlike BindingKind/CoverageExpectation. The style-tier StyleSeverity (warn|info, no reject) is a distinct vocabulary, intentionally left alone

NOT unified: severity_coverage (None=inherit binding) vs severity_verification (None=off) keep divergent Option<Severity> None semantics — a genuine domain difference (universal axiom vs opt-in axis), documented on the field, not a smell to collapse

Round 429 — Phase 1 entry ratified: fict.decision_summary: Phase 1 entry is declared and the build order is ratified: fictional medium adapter first. The pre-reset Round 172 priority audit chose the fictional adapter (score 6.00, 3.00x domination over branch) but its audit ledger predates the reset and is absent from this store, so this entry is the post-reset ratification record. A delta-audit against the current substrate confirms the ordering holds and the margin widened: SCE field-proved the spec instance of the North Star (verify axis R412-R428 live at reject), leaving the narrative half (R391 spec-subset-narrative: frame-driven behaviour, frame-scoped validation, prose-to-fact extraction) validatable only by a narrative consumer — which only the fictional adapter provides; its prerequisites are landed (R306 FR-1/FR-2 plugin substrate, R401-R408 EPUB content SSOT), so unmet_deps stays 0, while every competitor is either dependent on the adapter (cascade-narrative, studio-ui), consumer-shape-gated by ARCHITECTURE section 6.3 (branch, bi-temporal), or both plus frozen-ledger conflicted (saga). Phase 0 hardening met its termination conditions (T3 reject 0, T1 orphan 0, atomic orphan refs 0 outside the known-stale ledger) — the substrate is consumer-proven by four live stores. Phase 1A begins with the fictional adapter, Novel medium first dogfood.

Round 430 — narrative fact entity: frame.decision_summary: Phase 1A build Round A (of the A/B/C decomposition ratified with R429): the narrative fact entity lands. NarrativeFact = a claim held in exactly one epistemic frame over a canon-time extent in structure-section coordinates, with mandatory evidence provenance, recorded conflict assertion edges, and in-frame succession for time-indexed belief change — the storage half of the North Star sentence that frames are sparse non-privileged axes (a believed-fact and the ground-truth fact are distinct facts, both true, never cross-validated; absence in a frame = unrecorded, not false). Frame mechanism per the design round: frames-as-data at the SSOT log layer (a registry + an axis field), no inheritance or overlay (the multi-axis model leaves nothing to overlay); branch-KV remains the read-side projection realization when scale materializes the index. Canon closure is DERIVED: a superseded belief's effective end comes from its successor's canon_from, never written back (append-only, frozen-ledger genre). Both write paths route one shared builder (R305 parity); quote hashes are computed at write so a stored hash can never start out wrong (R404). Schema v11 to v12 is a declarative default — all four consumer stores load unchanged. Proven against the real corpus: the 27-chapter Dracula PoC store took 4 frames + 10 hand-extracted facts (3 cross-frame conflict clusters, 2 in-frame belief changes) in one atomic transaction with forward succession refs, idempotent on re-run. Round B (continuity gate) and Round C (frame-at-T projection) follow.
changes_bullets: NarrativeFact + Frame structs land in mnemosyne-core (L0: spec-subset-narrative makes fact/frame/canon-time substrate vocabulary, the SectionSkeleton placement logic; nothing fiction-shaped in the struct)

AtomicStore gains top-level frames registry + narrative_facts maps (R416 confirmation_events placement: never nested on sections, no frozen-ledger contact), schema v11 to v12 via declarative serde default — every existing consumer store loads unchanged with empty maps, no migration arm

one shared build path build_candidate_fact + validate_fact_refs for BOTH write paths (R305 field-invariant parity): frame must exist in registry, canon_from/canon_to/evidence refs must name existing sections, evidence >= 1, no self refs, in-frame-only succession (cross-frame disagreement is data, never succession), quote_sha256 computed at write never caller-supplied (R404 drift pattern)

new primitives import_facts (bulk frames+facts, one atomic transaction, forward succession refs within a manifest legal, divergent rejects whole manifest before save) / add_frame / add_fact / add_fact_conflict (recorded assertion edge, either-direction duplicate rejects as already-recorded)
CLI verbs import-facts / add-frame / add-fact / add-fact-conflict + usage; SCHEMA_GUIDE narrative-facts section + what-stays-fixed destale (entity list was 5-strong and pre-R400 parser-vocabulary)
verification_bullets: 8 new atomic unit tests (round-trip with forward succession + computed quote_sha256, unknown-frame/missing-ref/empty-evidence/canon rejects before any save, cross-frame succession reject, divergent-manifest atomicity with disk-untouched assert, add/import write-path parity over 6 edge cases, conflict-edge append + either-direction duplicate reject, pre-v12 store loads with empty maps)

4 new CLI smoke tests (import_facts_smoke.rs: bulk create + idempotent re-run no-op, unknown-frame reject leaves store byte-identical, add-frame/add-fact/add-fact-conflict end-to-end, cross-frame succession reject)
workspace: all test suites green, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean

acceptance on the real corpus: 27-chapter Dracula PoC store imported 4 frames + 10 hand-extracted facts (3 conflict clusters, 2 in-frame belief changes) in one transaction, idempotent re-run 14 no-op, store v12
carry_forward_bullets: Round B next: continuity gate — effective-extent derivation from succession + frame-scoped conflict scan (same-frame overlap = violation, cross-frame = data) + opt-in severity_continuity; needs a canon-order rule for overlap (natural sort of section ids vs explicit order — decide at build)
Round C after: read projection "facts of frame F at canon point T" (report-* verb pattern)
MCP tools for the fact primitives deferred until an agent session needs them (R127 mutate-gate parity then)
MediumAdapter L1 field-composition debt (spec-shaped fields hard-welded on AtomicSection, surfaced by the novel medium) stays flagged, separate round
consumer note: schema v12 is load-transparent (empty maps); global cli+mcp need reinstall at next sync

Round 431 — continuity gate: frame-scope.decision_summary: Phase 1A build Round B: the continuity gate lands — the validation half of the North Star sentence whose storage half was R430. scan_continuity evaluates the RECORDED conflict assertion edges frame-scoped: a same-frame pair whose DERIVED canon extents co-hold at any point is a violation; a cross-frame pair is data, counted and never gated. Effective extents are derived at scan exactly as designed (stored canon_to, cut by any in-frame successor's canon_from — nothing written back); a stored end that outlives its successor's start is itself flagged. Canon order obeys guardrail B-1: it is a DECLARED partial order (canon-order/v1 edge JSON, consumer/medium-adapter generated, cycles reject, optional sha256 pin per the R428 authority-input rule) — section-id spelling is never consulted, non-comparable pairs surface as a count and never gate, and equality needs no declaration so the gate already catches same-coordinate contradictions with no order file. Guardrail B-2 is structural: conflict scoping lives in one same_scope predicate that widens to frame-x-branch when the world-line axis lands. Opt-in via the [continuity] table (severity default reject, the content-drift rationale: a same-frame simultaneous contradiction is wrong data, never a legitimate intermediate state). Proven on the real corpus: the Dracula store's 4 recorded conflict pairs are all cross-frame = 0 violations under reject (the multi-axis thesis executing), and a fabricated same-frame overlap was caught at its exact canon point with exit 1. Round C (frame-at-T projection) remains.
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-validate continuity.rs: CanonOrder (declared partial order from canon-order/v1 edge JSON, transitive closure, cycle reject) + load_canon_order with optional sha256 pin (R428 authority-input symmetry) + scan_continuity

guardrail B-1 enforced structurally: order is consumer/medium-adapter DECLARED (R426 catalog contract pattern), section-id spelling never consulted; non-comparable pairs surface as unordered_pairs count and never gate; equal coordinates need no declaration so the gate works orderless
guardrail B-2: conflict scoping isolated in one same_scope predicate (today frame; widens to frame-x-branch as key-widening when the world-line axis lands)

effective extent DERIVED at scan: stored canon_to, cut by any in-frame successor canon_from; violations FrameConflictOverlap / SuccessionContradiction (stored end outlives successor start) / SuccessionCrossFrame + ConflictTargetMissing / SuccessionTargetMissing fail-loud re-checks

[continuity] config table (canon_order_path optional, severity default reject, canon_order_sha256 requires path + hex-validated) + validate-continuity CLI verb (--order bypasses pin by design, --severity flag over config over disabled, --sidecar for non-dogfood stores, TTY+--json)
SCHEMA_GUIDE continuity-gate section with the canon-order contract and toml example
verification_bullets: 9 continuity unit tests: same-frame overlap violation, cross-frame = data (acceptance 1 shape), derived closure prevents overlap with control (acceptance 3), succession contradiction, undeclared order = unordered not violation, equality needs no declaration, DAG incomparable arms do not overlap (B-1 quest shape), cycle reject, order node must be a section

2 config unit tests (defaults + sha-requires-path/hex) + 5 CLI smoke tests (validate_continuity_smoke.rs: cross-frame exit 0 with JSON contract keys, same-frame reject exit 1 then warn exit 0, disabled-without-table still reports, pin mismatch loud + --order bypass, undeclared order surfaces unordered)

Round 432 — frame-at-T read projection:.decision_summary: Phase 1A build Round C, completing the ratified A/B/C decomposition: the frame-at-T read projection lands. frame_view answers the question the whole slice was built for — what does frame F hold at canon point T — by routing the SAME holds_at semantics the continuity gate uses, so gate and view share one predicate and cannot drift (the R390 single-source discipline, locked by a consistency test: any overlap violation the gate reports at point p must show both facts holding in the view at p). The projection is three-state honest under the declared partial order (guardrail B-1): a fact is holding, definitively not holding, or UNKNOWN when a coordinate its verdict depends on is not comparable to the query point — a quest-DAG arm queried from a sibling arm reports unknown rather than silently absent. Read-only verb report-frame-view (order/store resolution identical to validate-continuity), no schema change, no new authoritative state. Acceptance on the real corpus closes the North-Star slice: seward at ch-12 believes the illness while ground-truth at ch-10 simultaneously holds the vampire facts; at ch-16 seward's belief has swapped to F07 by derived closure with nothing written back — coexisting perspectival truths, frame-scoped queries, never cross-validated. The Phase 1A v1 slice (R430 entity + R431 gate + R432 projection) is complete; remaining Phase 1 work is consumer-pull.

three-state honesty under the declared partial order (B-1): holding / definitively not_holding (count) / unknown (a coordinate the verdict depends on is not comparable to the query point — a quest-DAG arm queried from a sibling arm is unknown, never silently absent)
