Round 471 — A/B authoring experiment exe.publishable_decision_summary: Executed the R469/R470-pinned blind A/B authoring experiment end to end (S1-S11, one day, claude-fable-5 throughout): plain vs Mnemosyne-loop agents each authored the Saltglass branching novella in 3 fresh sessions, one blind session re-extracted both proses under one recipe, three blind judges read matched world-line copies, and the reveal round graded the sealed predictions. Headline: the only pinned outcome (PRED-1 floor) is REFUTED on both conjuncts — the plain story re-extracted to 0 primary-endpoint defects (pin required >=3) and the loop story to 1 (boots-custody fork-boundary fault the loop author never recorded), so loop < plain failed too. The control self-organized: the plain agent invented an unprompted in-file continuity ledger, turning the comparison into freeform-notes vs typed-store+gates — at 29-30 scenes on this model tier, notes suffice for every deterministically-detectable class. What the experiment bought instead: PRED-4 confirmed with margin (judges cited >=4 real continuity defects on the plain story that no D-metric caught, and ~8 across both arms vs 1 gated total — the untyped residue dominates, and fork-seam leaks are its dominant class in BOTH arms), PRED-3 landed at 1.48x tokens/scene (below the predicted 1.5-4x band; overhead is cache-read, friction decays to 0 by session 3), preference PLAIN 2-1/6-3 with the recorded weighting inverted (judges chose whole-world bookkeeping over the loop's admitted prose+knowledge-discipline superiority), and the loop's protection was measured as min(store-coverage, gate-power) with coverage binding. Full tables and analysis: claudedocs/phase1-ab-experiment/ab-report.md (run-log, verdicts, sealed label map alongside).
publishable_changes_bullets: S1-S3 plain arm: 29 scenes / 3 worlds / 3 endings authored bare in /home/coin/saltglass-novella across 3 fresh sessions; the agent self-invented an in-file continuity ledger (ground truth, custody tables, payoff-debt list) — an unprompted paper Mnemosyne, making the control freeform-notes rather than nothing

S4-S6 loop arm: 30 scenes / 3 worlds / 3 endings authored store-first in /home/coin/saltglass-studio (71 facts, 4 predicates+rules, declared canon order, gates green every session); 3 repairs all substrate-friction (coordinate prefix, rules shape, canon-order shape), decaying to 0 by session 3 — no story-class gate catch during authoring

Round 472 — narrative-rules loader stric.decision_summary: The narrative-rules/v1 loader rejects unknown and class-incoherent JSON keys instead of silently dropping them — the R471 A/B run surfaced a transition rule carrying a dead per scope (the author had to encode recipe scoping in comments). The lenient flatten-based parse is replaced by flat wire DTOs under deny_unknown_fields, with leg/class coherence and a validated schema tag checked explicitly. This closes the R450/R468 silent-no-op class for the second declared gate-authority artifact.
changes_bullets: Replaced the `flatten`-based `NarrativeRule`/`NarrativeRuleSpec` deserialize with flat `NarrativeRulesWire`/`NarrativeRuleWire` DTOs under serde `deny_unknown_fields` (continuity.rs) — `deny_unknown_fields` is incompatible with `flatten`, which is exactly how the lenient parse swallowed unknown keys.

`narrative_rule_from_wire` checks leg/class coherence: a transition carrying `per` (the R471 S7 miss, live in saltglass-studio) or an exclusive carrying `allowed` now rejects by name; a missing leg is named, not defaulted.

Modeled the file-level `schema` tag (validated equal to narrative-rules/v1 — the wrong-version silent-no-op closed) and the `comment` annotation slot; a third unknown file-level key fails loud. The R449 in-module test had drifted to a non-canonical `version` key; the real dogfood corpus (Detroit/Intercept/saltglass) uses `schema`, now ratified canonical.

Dropped the now-unused `Deserialize` from internal `NarrativeRulesFile`/`NarrativeRule`/`NarrativeRuleSpec`; deserialization lives only in the wire DTOs (single parse path, no half-enforced invariant).
Same silent-no-op class already closed for the padded predicate (R450) and the unknown `--field` (R468); the registry-typo lesson is R436, the authority-input pin contract R428.
verification_bullets: New `rules_loader_rejects_unknown_and_incoherent_fields` test pins the S7 reproduction (transition + `per`), the symmetric exclusive + `allowed`, an unknown rule-level key, an unknown file-level key, a wrong `schema` value, and both missing legs.
Existing `rules_loader_validates_shape_and_pin` happy path moved to the canonical `schema`/`comment` shape; whitespace-normalization and rule-gate tests unchanged and green.
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets clean; full suite 859 tests 0 fail (was 858, +1).
carry_forward_bullets: Rule subjects/objects scoping stays deferred — the comment workaround held D1 at one-violation-per-defect in the A/B run; build on the second consumer pull.
Fork-seam audit and selectional-promise payoff generalization remain sec 10 backlog candidates, now with quantified pull from both A/B arms; gate on the next corpus, do not build speculatively.
canon-order/v1's loader shares the identical lenient shape (no deny_unknown_fields, `schema` ignored); not field-proven and not the named debt, recorded here as a sibling tightening trigger.

Round 473 — scale-floor experiment desig.decision_summary: The A/B trial (R469 design, R471 execution) refuted the floor hypothesis at 29-30 scenes because the plain control self-organized a freeform paper-Mnemosyne that sufficed at that scale; the R471 verdict named the next move first — push the single variable corpus-length x session-count past the context horizon the A/B run never reached. This design round spends itself entirely on the successor protocol, because the validity floor is again session separation (a session that designs the grading and then writes the story has contaminated both). The scale experiment HOLDS every A/B parameter fixed — 2 arms, blind re-extraction referee, D1-D4 endpoint, preference leg, single directional pin, model tier, branching-mystery genre, world-line count, the 2-fork/3-world shape — and moves ONLY scale: scenes_total_min 24->60, sessions 3->6 per arm, required setups 6->12, each world-line roughly 2.5x longer (the corpus grows by lengthening limbs, not by branching wider, so branch complexity stays the separate sec 10 backlog variable). The horizon is operationalized as RETRIEVAL cost, not information blackout: both arms get a byte-identical session-handoff regime (auto-inject premise + brief + own handoff artifact + preceding-session prose only; all earlier prose on disk, re-readable at token cost) and both MAY re-read everything — the asymmetry under test is purely the tool that recovers older state (plain free-form notes + linear re-read vs loop store + O(1) query + global validate-continuity), the gap the ledger claims pays off past the context horizon. The loop prompt carries the R471 finding-2 recipe upgrade (author under the grader's recipe: track every object two parties touch and every state the prose asserts, scenery included). PRED-1 holds the A/B threshold verbatim (plain >=3 primary-endpoint defects AND loop < plain) so the only changed thing is scale; it is the only pinned outcome (R464 V9 discipline). Honesty bound recorded plainly: this venue is confirmatory by design (built where the ledger should pay off), so a null result is the strong finding (improvised notes still suffice at 60 scenes) and a confirmation is expected-direction. Everything an execution session follows — premise (Belvoir, original, 1909 alpine-sanatorium locked-room mystery), prompts, recipes, rubric, runbook S0-S17 — lives in one manifest whose sha256 is pinned in this entry before any execution session exists.

session-handoff regime is the scale operationalization: identical auto-injection across arms (premise + brief + own handoff artifact + preceding-session prose only), all earlier prose on disk and re-readable at token cost; the tested asymmetry is retrieval tool only — plain free-form notes + linear re-read vs loop store + O(1) query + global validate-continuity (handoff_parity validity condition guards byte-identical injection)

Round 475 — scale-floor experiment execu.decision_summary: The scale-floor experiment designed in R473 (sec 7.19, manifest sha256 18ea118a) executed across 17 session-separated blind rounds; the label-map sha256 verified MATCH at reveal (50092c40, sealed S13 by coin flip with the orchestrator blind — the flip result was never displayed, so extraction and judging ran with nobody knowing the mapping). story-A = plain (60 scenes), story-B = loop (65 scenes). RESULT: the floor hypothesis is REFUTED AGAIN. Floor (blind re-extraction, one fixed recipe both stories): plain primary-endpoint = 3, loop = 5; D1/D2/D4 = 0 for both, all signal in D3 dangling required setups. PRED-1 (plain >= 3 AND loop < plain) fails on the second conjunct — scale broke plain's floor (A/B plain = 0 -> scale plain = 3) but loop did not produce fewer defects (5 > 3), the same direction as A/B. Preference (3 blind judges, one matched world-line each): plain SWEPT 3-0 (confront, confess, escalate). Economics: loop $0.553/scene vs plain $0.324 = 1.71x, ABOVE the A/B 1.48x — PRED-3 (loop cheaper at scale) refuted; the store sidecar is rewritten whole on each mutate, so its cost rises with corpus and never converges below plain. The decisive finding: loop's store passed EVERY deterministic gate (D1/D2/D4 = 0) yet LOST on plot causation the store does not model — judges cited a load-bearing self-contradiction ('Surel' called both an alias and the avenger's true name), a named murderer (a bed-patient) with no established access to the locked-room draught (the access fits the nurse Brandt), an unmotivated second killing, and a six-week investigation packed inside a three-week snow-in. STORE-CONSISTENCY IS NOT CAUSAL COHERENCE: the guarantee the store delivers (declared-state consistency) held perfectly but is orthogonal to what makes a mystery hang together, and that gap is precisely where the loop lost. The D-metric counts 'dangling' identically for both stories, but plain's 3 were intentional dramatic irony (the confront judge PREFERRED them) and loop's 5 were partly broken causation — the human judge separates craft from defect where the deterministic gate cannot (PRED-4 confirmed, subject INVERTED: loop carried the untyped residue, not plain). Honesty: this was a confirmatory venue (built where the store should pay off — long multi-session branching authoring), so the null result = improvised notes suffice at 60 scenes / Fable tier, NOT that the store is useless; it is silent on larger scale, weaker tiers, multi-author work, or the store's spec/audit home where declared-fact consistency IS the property the store delivered perfectly here. SSOT = scale-floor-report.md.

Round 475 — scale-floor experiment execu.publishable_decision_summary: The scale-floor experiment designed in R473 (sec 7.19, manifest sha256 18ea118a) executed across 17 session-separated blind rounds; the label-map sha256 verified MATCH at reveal (50092c40, sealed S13 by coin flip with the orchestrator blind — the flip result was never displayed, so extraction and judging ran with nobody knowing the mapping). story-A = plain (60 scenes), story-B = loop (65 scenes). RESULT: the floor hypothesis is REFUTED AGAIN. Floor (blind re-extraction, one fixed recipe both stories): plain primary-endpoint = 3, loop = 5; D1/D2/D4 = 0 for both, all signal in D3 dangling required setups. PRED-1 (plain >= 3 AND loop < plain) fails on the second conjunct — scale broke plain's floor (A/B plain = 0 -> scale plain = 3) but loop did not produce fewer defects (5 > 3), the same direction as A/B. Preference (3 blind judges, one matched world-line each): plain SWEPT 3-0 (confront, confess, escalate). Economics: loop $0.553/scene vs plain $0.324 = 1.71x, ABOVE the A/B 1.48x — PRED-3 (loop cheaper at scale) refuted; the store sidecar is rewritten whole on each mutate, so its cost rises with corpus and never converges below plain. The decisive finding: loop's store passed EVERY deterministic gate (D1/D2/D4 = 0) yet LOST on plot causation the store does not model — judges cited a load-bearing self-contradiction ('Surel' called both an alias and the avenger's true name), a named murderer (a bed-patient) with no established access to the locked-room draught (the access fits the nurse Brandt), an unmotivated second killing, and a six-week investigation packed inside a three-week snow-in. STORE-CONSISTENCY IS NOT CAUSAL COHERENCE: the guarantee the store delivers (declared-state consistency) held perfectly but is orthogonal to what makes a mystery hang together, and that gap is precisely where the loop lost. The D-metric counts 'dangling' identically for both stories, but plain's 3 were intentional dramatic irony (the confront judge PREFERRED them) and loop's 5 were partly broken causation — the human judge separates craft from defect where the deterministic gate cannot (PRED-4 confirmed, subject INVERTED: loop carried the untyped residue, not plain). Honesty: this was a confirmatory venue (built where the store should pay off — long multi-session branching authoring), so the null result = improvised notes suffice at 60 scenes / Fable tier, NOT that the store is useless; it is silent on larger scale, weaker tiers, multi-author work, or the store's spec/audit home where declared-fact consistency IS the property the store delivered perfectly here. SSOT = scale-floor-report.md.

Round 476 — scale-floor result refined:.decision_summary: R475 framed the loop's losses as "store-consistency != causal coherence", but on reflection (owner challenge: timeline should be store-guaranteed) that binary is too coarse. The line between what the store can and cannot guarantee is a MODELING-DEPTH CONTINUUM, not a fixed boundary, with three bands. Band 1 — what the store already gates: custody, life-status, scene order (loop scored D1/D2/D4 = 0). Band 2 — what the store COULD gate but currently does not: timeline/scalar arithmetic (the "six-weeks-in-three-weeks" fault; A/B's "1913 / twelve years / 1923" is the same band) and same-entity multi-fact contradiction (the "Surel" alias-vs-true-name fault, catchable by forcing typed predicates so two claims about one entity collide). The store RECEIVES these as facts; its current gates (exclusive/transition) just do no arithmetic and do not force typing. Band 3 — what looks structurally out of reach: the murderer's missing locked-room access. The refinement: even Band 3 is reachable by PRECONDITION/AFFORDANCE modeling — if an action fact ("Surel poisons via the consulting-room draught") is required to carry its precondition facts (access = same-location + authority), the author must fill them, and filling them surfaces the contradiction (Surel is a lower-floor bed-patient; the draught sits in the locked consulting room only the doctor's key opens). So Band 3 is not "forever invisible"; it is "deeper to model". The TRUE ceiling — what the store cannot reach at any depth — is prose aesthetics (voice, surprise, reader pleasure) and the unbounded commonsense of which world-facts are even relevant; those stay with the author/model (the manifest's recorded ceiling bound). The trade-off R475 understated: deeper modeling catches more but raises authoring burden, and at 60 scenes / Fable tier plain's loose improvised notes were cheaper than loop's strict-but-Band-1-only gating. The product implication SHARPENS: the carries are not just drift detection but a DEPTH LADDER — scalar/arithmetic gates, typed-predicate enforcement, precondition/affordance modeling, prose-vs-store drift — each catching a band the current store misses, each at a cost. This does NOT reverse R475 (loop still lost 3 vs 5, plain still swept preference 3-0); it relocates the loss from "the store's limit" to "the store's unbuilt depth", which is a product direction, not a dead end.
changes_bullets: refines R475's "store-consistency != causal coherence" binary into a MODELING-DEPTH CONTINUUM (owner challenge: timeline is store-guaranteeable, not out of reach)

band 3 seemingly-out-of-reach (murderer's locked-room access) is reachable by PRECONDITION/AFFORDANCE modeling: action facts required to carry access/location/authority preconditions surface the contradiction (bed-patient vs locked consulting room)
TRUE ceiling (store cannot reach at any depth) = prose aesthetics + the unbounded relevance-of-commonsense; stays with author/model (manifest ceiling bound)

does NOT reverse R475 (loop 3 vs 5, plain swept) — relocates the loss from the store's LIMIT to the store's UNBUILT DEPTH
verification_bullets: consistency with R475: the result is unchanged (loop endpoint 5 > plain 3; preference 3-0) — this entry refines the INTERPRETATION, not the measured data

band-2 evidence is cross-experiment: scalar/arithmetic residue appears in BOTH scale-floor (the timeline fault) and A/B (the date-arithmetic fault) — a repeated signal across two independent runs, not a one-off

the precondition/affordance claim is a DESIGN proposal, not a built primitive; recorded as a depth-ladder carry gated on consumer pull, NOT speculatively built (the no-speculative-substrate rule holds)

the continuum reframing tightens the honesty bound, not loosens it: it makes explicit that the store's loss was partly unbuilt-depth (recoverable) and partly true-ceiling (prose/commonsense, not recoverable)
carry_forward_bullets: DEPTH LADDER = the band-2/3 catch mechanisms, each a product candidate gated on consumer pull: (1) scalar/arithmetic gates (timeline, dates, counts); (2) typed-predicate enforcement (same-entity multi-fact collision); (3) precondition/affordance modeling (action facts carry access/location/authority preconditions); (4) prose-vs-store drift detection (R475 carry)

each ladder rung trades authoring burden for a caught band; the regime question (when is the burden worth it) is empirical and venue-dependent — larger scale / weaker tiers / spec-audit home are where the burden likely pays, untested here
true ceiling stays out of scope for the store at any depth: prose aesthetics + which commonsense is relevant — author/model territory (manifest ceiling bound), do not attempt to gate

Round 477 — drift detection designed: cl.decision_summary: The scale-floor carry's first depth-ladder rung — drift detection — is designed (design-only, build owner-gated). PROBLEM: a store fact's claim ("setup X pays off") is never checked against whether the prose supports it; in scale-floor (R475/R476) the loop self-marked "12/12 paid", passed every gate (D1/D2/D4 = 0), yet the prose left threads unresolved — a claim-vs-evidence DRIFT invisible to the deterministic gate. It is distinct from R404 byte-integrity (did the text change) and from R428 artifact-hash staleness (did a confirmed artifact change); this is whether the claim ever matched the evidence AT ALL — a semantic check that a deterministic gate cannot do, so it needs an LLM. REUSE, NOT NEW SUBSTRATE: the R428 confirmation-event infrastructure carries almost all of it — ConfirmerKind::Model, method = semantic_review, verdict = Confirm | Refute, required rationale, and crucially SELF-CONFIRM REJECT (max-rigor sec 4.7), which is the exact fix for the loop's false assurance: an authoring run can no longer confirm its own "paid" claim — an independent confirmer must review prose-vs-claim before the fact counts as confirmed. R457-459 discovery carries the rest (report-candidates -> import-verdicts, all-or-nothing, --dry-run, claim-sha staleness, the B-1 isolation that keeps LLM reasoning outside the store). THE ONE NEW PIECE: ConfirmationClaim (today section-only: VerifiesBinding / SectionCompleteness) gains a FactEvidence { fact_id } variant — "fact.claim is supported by fact.evidence" — which lets the entire confirmation machinery apply to a narrative fact; new surface = 1 enum variant + 2 verbs (report-drift-candidates / import-drift-verdicts), no new substrate. v1 scope = payoff facts (precisely the loop's 12/12 case); venue-neutral, extending to every claim-evidence fact and to spec normative_excerpt sections (the claim-evidence shape is identical). ACCEPTANCE corpus already exists: the Belvoir loop store carries the "12/12 paid" drift, so blind acceptance = run drift detection and check the confess/escalate fake-payoff facts come back Refuted, matching what the S13 blind prose extraction found independently (pre-pin the refute set, R453 mechanism). HONESTY: the confirmer is an LLM so the verdict is not deterministic, but it is recorded as an audit-trail confirmation-event with sha256 pins (R428 spirit: the determinism is in the ledger of who-confirmed-what, not in the judgment); self-confirm reject is the load-bearing rule; band boundary (R476) — drift detection catches "the claim is not in the evidence" (absence/mismatch), NOT "the claim is good" (aesthetics = the true ceiling). SSOT drift-detection-design.md.

REUSE: R428 confirmation-event infra (ConfirmerKind::Model, semantic_review, Confirm/Refute, self-confirm reject, sha256 staleness) + R457-459 discovery (report-candidates -> import-verdicts, all-or-nothing, dry-run, claim-sha staleness, B-1 isolation)

design-only (code 0, R441/R457 frozen-design pattern); build owner-gated though the pull is empirically established (scale-floor measured the drift, A/B residue is the same shape); SSOT drift-detection-design.md

boundary honest: drift detection is the band-1 rung of the R476 ladder (claim-vs-evidence), catching absence/mismatch NOT aesthetics (the true ceiling); it is venue-neutral (narrative facts + spec sections share the claim-evidence shape), not a narrative-special primitive

no speculative build: design-only, and the acceptance corpus (Belvoir loop store) plus the expected outcome (confess/escalate refuted, matching S13) are specified so a build round has a falsifiable target BEFORE any code is written

remaining depth-ladder rungs after drift (R476): scalar/arithmetic gate next (venue-neutral, two-experiment pull: scale-floor timeline + A/B date arithmetic), then typed-predicate enforcement (typed predicates already exist, R446); precondition/affordance modeling stays deferred (narrative-special, the lost venue, costliest)
the build trigger is the owner's: drift's pull is the strongest and venue-neutral, but the depth-ladder rungs are a sequence to be pulled one at a time, not built as a speculative block

design-only (code 0, R441/R457 frozen-design pattern); build owner-gated though the pull is empirically established (scale-floor measured the drift, A/B residue is the same shape); SSOT drift-detection-design.md
publishable_verification_bullets: reuse verified against the code: ConfirmationClaim is section-only today (VerifiesBinding / SectionCompleteness in mnemosyne-atomic lib.rs), and the confirmation-event already carries ConfirmerKind / method / verdict / self-confirm-reject / staleness — so FactEvidence is a minimal extension of one enum, not a parallel mechanism

boundary honest: drift detection is the band-1 rung of the R476 ladder (claim-vs-evidence), catching absence/mismatch NOT aesthetics (the true ceiling); it is venue-neutral (narrative facts + spec sections share the claim-evidence shape), not a narrative-special primitive

no speculative build: design-only, and the acceptance corpus (Belvoir loop store) plus the expected outcome (confess/escalate refuted, matching S13) are specified so a build round has a falsifiable target BEFORE any code is written

self-confirm reject is load-bearing and already built (R428 sec 4.7): the design adds no new trust mechanism, it points the existing independent-confirmation rule at narrative payoff claims — the precise structural answer to the loop's self-audit false assurance
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: BUILD drift detection when owner-triggered: the FactEvidence ConfirmationClaim variant + report-drift-candidates / import-drift-verdicts + report-drift, reusing R428 confirmation + R457-459 discovery; acceptance = the Belvoir loop store refute-set vs the S13 blind extraction; this design doc is the frozen shape (R441/R457 pattern)

remaining depth-ladder rungs after drift (R476): scalar/arithmetic gate next (venue-neutral, two-experiment pull: scale-floor timeline + A/B date arithmetic), then typed-predicate enforcement (typed predicates already exist, R446); precondition/affordance modeling stays deferred (narrative-special, the lost venue, costliest)
the build trigger is the owner's: drift's pull is the strongest and venue-neutral, but the depth-ladder rungs are a sequence to be pulled one at a time, not built as a speculative block

Round 478 — session review of R473-R477:.decision_summary: Session review of R473-R477 (the scale-floor design -> execution -> refinement -> drift-design arc). Findings repaid. PRIMARY: the R477 drift design was over-optimistic about reuse. Verified against the code — confirmation is SECTION-COUPLED today: ConfirmationClaim::section_id() assumes every variant carries a section_id, and append_confirmation_event fail-louds if that section does not exist (R287). So the FactEvidence variant is NOT a clean "1 enum variant + no new substrate"; it forces generalizing the confirmation target from "always a section" to "section | fact" (section_id() branches into a target-existence check, the append-time validation looks up narrative_facts for the fact case). It is still the SAME confirmation mechanism (self-confirm reject, staleness, projections all carry — no new trust model), but the honest build surface is "generalize the claim-target abstraction + the two verbs", not a one-line drop-in. drift-detection-design.md corrected with an R478 note. SECOND: the acceptance premise, previously unchecked, is now VERIFIED — the Belvoir loop store carries 162 facts, 21 of them pays_off WITH evidence, a real falsifiable drift target (the S13 blind extraction found confess 1 + escalate 4 fake payoffs from prose alone; drift detection should Refute exactly those). The acceptance corpus is not vapor. THIRD: the belvoir-{plain,loop,extraction,judging} workspaces are clean (agents cleaned their import-manifest temp files; no .sN-*.json residue to sweep). FOURTH: methodology lesson generalized — post-hoc normalization (the reading-copy metadata strip) never fully closes; I failed it twice because my strip and my verification shared the same pattern blind-spot, so the structural fixes are (a) input-side hygiene (keep store-work out of the graded deliverable) and (b) verification INDEPENDENT of implementation (the blind judge, prompted to actively flag non-story text, was the ground truth my self-check structurally could not be). FIFTH: memory bloat noted (project_ab_experiment_run.md now spans A/B + scale-floor + drift design) — a future split candidate, not repaid now (no functional harm). No code findings (the arc was design + experiment, code 0). Doctrine intact: store-direct, frozen-ledger respected (R476 refined R475 via a NEW entry, not a body edit), citation hygiene held.

Round 479 — evidence preserved as git SS.decision_summary: Session review R478 surfaced an SSOT smell on a deeper, owner-prompted pass: the scale-floor ledger entries (R475/R476) assert results in the git SSOT, but the EVIDENCE was not preserved there — the belvoir-* workspaces are local-only (no remote), run-log/report were gitignored, and the judge verdicts lived only in transcript jsonl. For a project that IS an audit-ledger, the conclusion was preserved while its grounds were not — a 5-year-later re-verification would have had nothing to check. R479 repays the evidence half: claudedocs/phase1-scale-floor-experiment/ is now git-tracked (a scoped .gitignore exception; the rest of claudedocs stays ignored), and an evidence/ subdir preserves the raw graded deliverables (plain-story.md, loop-story.md), the D-metric (defect-tables.md), and the final judge verdicts (confront 1st-round + confess/escalate re-judged — the metadata-clean valid set), alongside the already-present run-log / report / manifest / label-map. The conclusion (plain 3 / loop 5, preference 3-0) is now independently re-checkable against its own evidence in the same SSOT. DEFERRED (owner decision, not this round): the experiment-harness ad-hoc-scripting smell — the reading-copy assembly, the shuffle, and the metadata strip were one-off Python/bash scripts, deleted and non-reproducible, violating the no-silent-fail-scripting doctrine (and the strip failed twice, fragile). That repays as a tools/ Rust crate (medium-forge precedent) BEFORE the next experiment (R480+). Doctrine note: the gitignore exception is principled — claudedocs-gitignored holds for transient analysis scratch; the exception is scoped to permanent falsifiable evidence, which an audit-ledger project must preserve. The core (ledger entries, design docs, production code) was already textbook this arc; the smell was confined to experiment infrastructure.

Round 480 — experiment-harness built as.decision_summary: R479 deferred the experiment-harness ad-hoc-scripting smell: the A/B (R471) and scale-floor (R475) experiments did reading-copy assembly, the arm shuffle, and metadata stripping with one-off Python/bash that was then deleted, leaving the experiments non-reproducible and running silent-fail-capable scripting inside a project whose whole thesis is fail-loud verification. R480 repays it as tools/experiment-harness, a separate in-repo workspace (the bench/studio pattern; its own [workspace] so the root build, CI, and pre-commit clippy never compile it) depending only on serde, serde_json, and sha2, with randomness read directly from /dev/urandom so a missing entropy source is a loud error, never a silent fallback. Three subcommands, each repaying one named step. assemble (reading-copy assembly plus the metadata strip) joins story prose to the report-playthrough-manuscript --json (R466) per-world scene order by scene id, drops the scaffolding HTML comments and CHOICE: directive lines, and normalizes the heading sc-NN to title only; a world-order scene with no prose, a duplicate scene id, or a body that strips to nothing is a hard error — the exact silent-fail the deleted Python committed when an empty-default lookup emitted a blank scene unnoticed. shuffle does blind A/B labeling by a /dev/urandom Fisher-Yates and prints a sha256 seal to be recorded in the ledger before reveal. verify-seal is the reveal and audit half, exiting non-zero on a map edited after sealing. Proven by dogfood on the archived Belvoir store-A: assemble reproduces all three judged reading copies (confront, quiet-confess, quiet-escalate) with a prose-only diff of zero — the only delta is a handful of separator lines where the harness places a rule at every scene boundary more regularly than the deleted script. The deleted, non-reproducible transform is now faithfully reproduced by a versioned fail-loud tool, so an auditor can re-derive the exact blind copies the judges read. Token accounting stayed out of scope (manual gen-versus-reread judgment, not an ad-hoc-scripting artifact); deeper CHOICE and ENDING stripping is an explicit future flag, not a silent default. The harness is now the substrate the next experiment must use in place of throwaway scripts.
changes_bullets: new tools/experiment-harness crate: a separate in-repo workspace (the bench/studio pattern, own [workspace] plus committed Cargo.lock), deps serde/serde_json/sha2, randomness from /dev/urandom with a loud error on a missing source — no silent fallback

assemble (reading-copy assembly plus metadata strip): story prose joined to the report-playthrough-manuscript --json per-world order by scene id; strips HTML scaffolding comments and CHOICE: lines, normalizes the sc-NN heading to title only; missing, duplicate, or empty scenes are hard errors — the silent-fail the deleted empty-default lookup committed
shuffle (blind A/B labeling by a /dev/urandom Fisher-Yates plus a sha256 seal) and verify-seal (the reveal and audit half, exit 1 on a tampered map)
20 tests (16 unit plus 4 CLI smoke covering exit codes), clippy -D and fmt clean, README documenting the transform as the reproducibility SSOT
verification_bullets: dogfood on the archived Belvoir store-A: assemble reproduces all three judged reading copies (confront, quiet-confess, quiet-escalate) with a prose-only diff of zero; the only raw delta is 6/10/12 separator-and-blank lines, where the harness places a rule at every scene boundary more regularly than the deleted ad-hoc script

the deleted, non-reproducible ad-hoc transform is now faithfully reproduced by a versioned fail-loud tool — the R479 reproducibility debt is repaid and an auditor can re-derive the exact blind copies the judges read

world-name adapter surfaced and recorded: store worlds are confront/quiet-confess/quiet-escalate while the judged copies were named confront/confess/escalate, an orchestrator rename that is an adapter decision, not a defect
separate workspace verified: the root build, CI, and pre-commit clippy never compile the crate (the bench/studio invariant)
carry_forward_bullets: token accounting (per-session generation vs re-read tokens) stayed out of scope — manual judgment, not the ad-hoc-scripting smell; the run-log table stays hand-kept

deeper stripping (CHOICE option text, ENDING headers, inline scene-ids) is an explicit future flag, not a silent default; v1 matches the proven minimal transform the scale-floor experiment actually used

medium-forge (tools/medium-forge) is still Python — the same no-silent-fail debt in a different tool; flag for a future round, and the project memory that calls medium-forge a Rust tool needs correcting
the harness is now the substrate the next experiment (drift-detection acceptance, a larger floor) must use in place of throwaway scripts

Round 481 — claim-vs-evidence drift dete.decision_summary: Scale-floor (R475/R476) exposed that store-consistency is not causal coherence: the loop arm self-marked its setups paid, passed every deterministic gate, yet the prose left threads unresolved — a claim-vs-evidence drift the gates cannot see, because they validate the store's internal consistency and never whether a claim is borne out by the evidence it cites. R477 designed the fix and R478 corrected it (confirmation is section-coupled, so this generalizes the claim target, not a one-variant drop-in). R481 builds it by reusing the R428 max-rigor confirmation machinery and adding one claim target — ConfirmationClaim::FactEvidence { fact_id }, "fact.claim is supported by fact.evidence" — reviewed by a model on the semantic_review method with the load-bearing self-confirm reject: an authoring run can no longer rubber-stamp its own claim, which is exactly the false assurance the loop fell into. The R287 target-existence check generalized section to section|fact through a new ClaimTarget, and — because confirmation_events now has a second write path (import_drift_verdicts beside append_confirmation_event) — the invariant set was factored into one shared validate_confirmation_event that both call, with a field-invariant parity test (the R305 half-enforced-invariant rule). Two verbs mirror the R458/R459 discovery pattern: report-drift-candidates (read — every payoff fact with its claim, evidence quote, and claim_sha256 plus its current review status, serving as both the drift-verdicts/v1 authoring contract and the drift surface) and import-drift-verdicts (write — all-or-nothing, dry-run twin, claim-sha staleness re-check, self-confirm reject). A FactEvidence claim never reaches Confirmed via the verifies required-evidence-set (no deterministic tool can judge prose), so the surface reads the semantic-review leg directly; a verdict drifts to Stale when the claim text changes after review (R420 reused, store-only — no files). Schema v19 to v20 (a v20 store carrying a fact_evidence event must not silently load on a v19 binary). v1 scope is payoff facts; venue-neutral by construction — the claim-vs-evidence shape is identical for spec normative_excerpt sections, a later extension.

Round 481 — claim-vs-evidence drift dete.publishable_decision_summary: Scale-floor (R475/R476) exposed that store-consistency is not causal coherence: the loop arm self-marked its setups paid, passed every deterministic gate, yet the prose left threads unresolved — a claim-vs-evidence drift the gates cannot see, because they validate the store's internal consistency and never whether a claim is borne out by the evidence it cites. R481 builds it by reusing the R428 max-rigor confirmation machinery and adding one claim target — ConfirmationClaim::FactEvidence { fact_id }, "fact.claim is supported by fact.evidence" — reviewed by a model on the semantic_review method with the load-bearing self-confirm reject: an authoring run can no longer rubber-stamp its own claim, which is exactly the false assurance the loop fell into. The R287 target-existence check generalized section to section|fact through a new ClaimTarget, and — because confirmation_events now has a second write path (import_drift_verdicts beside append_confirmation_event) — the invariant set was factored into one shared validate_confirmation_event that both call, with a field-invariant parity test (the R305 half-enforced-invariant rule). Round 482 — drift acceptance pre-registe.decision_summary: R481 built drift detection and proved the surface on the Belvoir loop store (21 self-claimed payoffs, 0 reviewed). R482 pre-registers the BLIND acceptance (R453 mechanism) BEFORE the independent reviewer runs, so the bar cannot move after seeing the verdicts. An isolated Pinner session, given only the prior scale-floor finding (scale-floor-report.md + defect-tables.md) and the store — not the R481 internals — derived the expected result set and sealed it as claudedocs/phase1-drift-acceptance/expected-refutes.json, sha256 ff3e2b13a2ba2beb80060dba44dc95a223a4afe28d6a162a144767bec0fd9d79, pinned here before execution. The honest scope: drift v1 checks claim-vs-CITED-evidence per fact, not global causal coherence, so the Pinner kept only the IN-band threads. Expected refute set = 2 facts whose cited payoff scene's own prose negates the claim: f-confess-chart-explained (sc-59 leaves the burning never reaching) and f-escalate-method-toll (sc-63 leaves the chart never guessed and the kinship the reader's not hers; its bundled ledger leg does pay off). Degenerate guard = 4 clean payoffs a refute-everything reviewer must fail: f-diary-opened-confront, f-diary-opened-confess, f-roeder-suicide-confront, f-roeder-suicide-confess. Out of band (excluded, the R476 ceiling) = 5: two whose claims honestly narrate non-resolution and only break in global payoff-coverage bookkeeping (f-escalate-diary-sealed-final, f-escalate-forged-unproven), one impossible-physical-access (f-roeder-murder-escalate, Band 3), one cross-scene same-entity Surel-name contradiction (f-helene-identified-confront, Band 2), one delivered-payoff-with-missing-motive (f-escalate-lindqvist-payoff). Two scale-floor faults (the six-weeks-in-three-weeks timeline, the telegram-pays-off-nothing critique) have no asserting payoff fact among the 21 and are not entered. PASS pin: the blind reviewer refutes BOTH in-band facts AND confirms ALL 4 guards; an out-of-band miss is the expected drift ceiling, recorded not failed. The reviewer (S-REVIEW) runs next in an isolated workspace; the R481 builder must not run it (R453/R469 keystone).

Round 483 — drift acceptance graded: the.decision_summary: The blind reviewer (isolated, never shown the expected set / scale-floor / R481) reviewed all 21 payoff facts of the Belvoir loop store claim-vs-cited-evidence and returned 20 confirm / 1 refute, all validated. Graded against the R482 sealed set (sha256 ff3e2b13...0fd9d79, re-verified MATCH, no goalpost-moving): the PASS bar is NOT met. The two pinned in-band refute targets (f-confess-chart-explained, f-escalate-method-toll) both came back confirm (0/2 landed); all 4 confirm guards held; the reviewer instead refuted one fact the pin had excluded as out-of-band (f-helene-identified-confront). The disagreement cuts both ways and is the real yield. f-helene: the reviewer's refute is verifiably CORRECT — the fact cites sc-06 in its evidence, and sc-06 says "a name that was not her name ... Surel was a borrowed coat", directly contradicting the claim's "under her own true family name"; drift caught a real claim-vs-cited-evidence drift the pin wrongly discounted (the Pinner assumed the contradicting scene was not cited; it is). The other two are genuine subjective splits the builder must not adjudicate: f-confess-chart-explained's claim itself hedges "the burning is never traced to Brandt's hand", which matches sc-59; f-escalate-method-toll's sc-63 contains the buff-folder-to-the-stove line the reviewer read as carrying the payoff. So two independent model reviews (the Pinner reasoning from the prior scale-floor evidence, the blind Reviewer reading the prose) diverged on all three contested facts (3/21 ~ 14%), and the pre-registered expected set was itself a fallible model judgment with a verified error. This reproduces the max-rigor lesson (R416-R428: a single model verdict carries a 12-25% error rate) for the narrative venue: the self-confirm reject (>= 1 independent reviewer) is necessary but not sufficient, and v1's single-verdict drift surface is not trustworthy alone. The R481 mechanism itself worked end-to-end (report, blind review, all-or-nothing import of 21, surface flip to 20 reviewed / 1 drifting, self-confirm reject and claim-sha staleness enforced) and the build is sound. Evidence preserved as git SSOT (verdicts.json + GRADING.md, R479).

Round 484 — drift redesigned all-determi.decision_summary: R483's blind acceptance falsified the v1 thesis: drift v1 put an LLM verdict in the gate (a FactEvidence confirmation, model / semantic_review), and two independent model reviews diverged on 3 of 21 contested facts (~14%) while the pinned ground truth itself carried a verified error. A single model judgment is not authoritative, and a quorum of models only multiplies the same untrusted judgment. The owner's decision: the gate must be DETERMINISTIC and AI must leave the trust loop entirely. This is not new architecture but the original one — narrative.rs already states the typed leg exists so the deterministic rule gate covers the typed subset; v1 was the deviation, and the deterministic typed layer is already 0-error. v2 principle: the gate checks DECLARED typed structure against declared typed structure (the existing conflict / exclusivity / transition / coverage gates); AI is advisory only, proposing typed legs from prose via the R457-459 typing-discovery loop (report-candidates to import-proposals, all-or-nothing, deterministically validated, owner-confirmed) and never rendering a gating verdict; the irreducible NLU judgment ("does this prose mean what this typed leg says") is the AUTHOR's act of declaring a leg, not a gate. The two drift classes go deterministic. Class A (the scale-floor "claimed paid, prose never delivers"): payoff_coverage today credits a setup on the mere existence of a pays_off edge — it counts the edge, not the substance; strengthen it so an Expected setup carrying a typed state (subject, predicate, V0) is substantiated only by a payoff fact carrying a typed leg on the same (subject, predicate) with a different value V1 (a state change, optionally transition-rule-legal), yielding deterministic paid / unsubstantiated (a bare edge with no typed backing) / unverifiable (untyped on the load-bearing dimension, surfaced never silently passed). Class B (f-helene, claim contradicts its own cited evidence): needs no new mechanism — once the cited scene's load-bearing content is a typed fact, the existing typed exclusivity gate catches it (helene|name|true-family vs helene|name|borrowed = one subject, two incompatible values of an exclusive predicate). The v1 path (ConfirmationClaim::FactEvidence, drift-verdicts/v1, import-drift-verdicts, report-drift-candidates verdict surface, fact_claim_drifted, the drift.rs DriftStatus projection) is the falsified approach, retired in the SAME change that lands the deterministic substantiation gate (not before — removing it now would leave no drift surface); the R481 entry stays the frozen record of the dead end and R483 is its falsification. Build decomposition follows in later rounds; this round is design-only (code 0), grounded by reading R442 payoff_coverage, R446 TypedClaim, and R449 transition/exclusive rules.
changes_bullets: design-only (code 0): drift v2 = all-deterministic — the gate checks declared typed structure, AI is demoted to advisory typing-discovery (R457-459) and never renders a gating verdict; SSOT = drift-detection-design.md "v2 all-deterministic" section

Class A (claimed-paid-not-delivered, the scale-floor 12/12): a payoff is substantiated only by a typed state-change on the setup's own (subject, predicate); a bare pays_off edge = unsubstantiated, an untyped load-bearing dimension = unverifiable (surfaced, no silent pass)
Class B (f-helene contradicts-own-cited-evidence): the EXISTING typed exclusivity gate catches it once the load-bearing facts are typed — no new mechanism
R481's FactEvidence / drift-verdicts / fact_claim_drifted path is the falsified approach (R483), to be RETIRED in the same change that lands the deterministic gate (no-legacy-carry)
verification_bullets: grounded in the existing 0-error machinery (read R442 payoff_coverage, R446 TypedClaim/Predicate, R449 transition/exclusive rules); narrative.rs already declares the typed leg's purpose is the deterministic rule gate, so v1's AI verdict was the deviation
the f-helene contradiction maps to a deterministic typed exclusivity violation (one subject holding two incompatible values of an exclusive predicate), no AI
v2's acceptance is itself deterministic and re-runnable (fixtures + the gate) — unlike v1 it needs no blind reviewer and no pre-registered model-judgment set
carry_forward_bullets: build decomposition (later rounds): (1) typed payoff substantiation scan classifying paid/unsubstantiated/unverifiable per world + opt-in severity, (2) a Class-B fixture proving the exclusivity gate catches the f-helene shape once typed (close any gap deterministically), (3) retire the v1 FactEvidence/drift-verdicts surface in the SAME change as (1), (4) typing-discovery stays the only prose-to-type bridge (advisory)

honesty boundary: determinism covers declaration-vs-declaration consistency and payoff backing, NOT prose-vs-declaration faithfulness — that last mile is irreducible authoring judgment, author-owned with AI-advisory help, never a gate
AI is permanently OUT of the drift trust loop (the R483 lesson: neither a single nor a quorum model verdict is authoritative); the typed/structural gate is the trust anchor

A single model judgment is not authoritative, and a quorum of models only multiplies the same untrusted judgment. The owner's decision: the gate must be DETERMINISTIC and AI must leave the trust loop entirely. This is not new architecture but the original one — narrative.rs already states the typed leg exists so the deterministic rule gate covers the typed subset; v1 was the deviation, and the deterministic typed layer is already 0-error. v2 principle: the gate checks DECLARED typed structure against declared typed structure (the existing conflict / exclusivity / transition / coverage gates); AI is advisory only, proposing typed legs from prose via the R457-459 typing-discovery loop (report-candidates to import-proposals, all-or-nothing, deterministically validated, owner-confirmed) and never rendering a gating verdict; the irreducible NLU judgment ("does this prose mean what this typed leg says") is the AUTHOR's act of declaring a leg, not a gate. The two drift classes go deterministic. Class A (the scale-floor "claimed paid, prose never delivers"): payoff_coverage today credits a setup on the mere existence of a pays_off edge — it counts the edge, not the substance; strengthen it so an Expected setup carrying a typed state (subject, predicate, V0) is substantiated only by a payoff fact carrying a typed leg on the same (subject, predicate) with a different value V1 (a state change, optionally transition-rule-legal), yielding deterministic paid / unsubstantiated (a bare edge with no typed backing) / unverifiable (untyped on the load-bearing dimension, surfaced never silently passed). Class B (f-helene, claim contradicts its own cited evidence): needs no new mechanism — once the cited scene's load-bearing content is a typed fact, the existing typed exclusivity gate catches it (helene|name|true-family vs helene|name|borrowed = one subject, two incompatible values of an exclusive predicate). The v1 path (ConfirmationClaim::FactEvidence, drift-verdicts/v1, import-drift-verdicts, report-drift-candidates verdict surface, fact_claim_drifted, the drift.rs DriftStatus projection) is the falsified approach, retired in the SAME change that lands the deterministic substantiation gate (not before — removing it now would leave no drift surface); the R481 entry stays the frozen record of the dead end and R483 is its falsification. Build decomposition follows in later rounds; this round is design-only (code 0), grounded by reading R442 payoff_coverage, R446 TypedClaim, and R449 transition/exclusive rules.

Round 485 — drift v2 built all-determini.decision_summary: R484 designed the all-deterministic drift redesign; R485 builds it and retires the R481 LLM-verdict path in the same change (no-legacy-carry). The new deterministic surface is report-payoff-substantiation (read-only): per query world it refines payoff_coverage's credited (paid) set, classifying each credited setup as substantiated (the setup carries a typed state (subject, predicate, V0) AND a crediting payoff fact carries a typed leg on the SAME subject+predicate with a different value — a state change that discharges it), unsubstantiated (the setup is typed and at least one crediting payoff is typed, but none carries the discharging state-change — a hollow payoff, the deterministic analogue of the drift R481 chased with an LLM), or unverifiable (the typed data needed is absent — the setup is untyped, or every crediting payoff is prose-only — surfaced never silently passed; the author types it via the typing-discovery loop and the gate then decides). No model judgment anywhere: the verdict is a pure comparison of declared typed legs, deterministic and re-runnable. Class B (the f-helene "claim contradicts its own cited evidence" drift R483 found) needs no new mechanism — a fixture proves the EXISTING typed exclusivity gate catches it once both load-bearing legs are typed (one subject cannot hold two incompatible values of an exclusive predicate). The whole R481 path is retired: the FactEvidence ConfirmationClaim variant, the ClaimTarget generalization (target() reverted to section_id(), validate_confirmation_event re-inlined into append_confirmation_event — restoring the pre-R481 single write path), the drift-verdicts/v1 import primitive and structs, the validate drift.rs module, the confirmation.rs artifact_drifted fact-branch, the ops/cli/mcp drift wiring, the drift CLI smoke, and the 12 drift unit tests. Schema v20 to v21 records the FactEvidence removal (no canonical store carried such an event; the dogfood store's only ones lived in a throwaway grading copy). AI is now fully OUT of the drift trust loop (the R483 lesson banked) — the gate is the typed/structural deterministic layer, and the typing-discovery loop (R457-459, AI-advisory, deterministically validated) is the only prose-to-type bridge.

Round 486 — drift v2 deterministic accep.decision_summary: Closing the loop the R485 dogfood opened (92% of Belvoir payoffs unverifiable because untyped). v1's acceptance needed a blind reviewer because the verdict was an LLM judgment; v2 is deterministic, so its acceptance needs only the real corpus, faithful typing, and the gates — no AI in any verdict. On a working copy of the Belvoir loop store (the archive stays pristine), the load-bearing name facts were typed faithfully: a name-status scalar predicate, a trunk fact f-surel-name-cover typed helene|name-status|forged (faithful to sc-06's "a name that was not her name, a borrowed coat"), f-helene-identified-confront amended to type its claim helene|name-status|true-family (faithful to "under her own true family name"), and an r-name-status exclusive rule. Results, all deterministic and no AI: (1) Class B PROVEN — with both name-status facts in the same (frame, branch) scope, validate-continuity fires rule_exclusive_overlap at sc-53 (the f-helene contradiction v1 needed an unreliable LLM for, R483, now a pure typed-value comparison); (2) Class A works — report-payoff-substantiation shows the diary chain f-diary-sealed <- f-diary-opened-confront substantiated (a real integrity sealed->opened state-change discharge), f-helene-identified-confront -> f-forged-papers unsubstantiated (the payoff's name-status leg does not discharge the setup's held-by state — hollow or under-typed), and 10 still unverifiable (untyped); (3) a GAP found deterministically — the REAL f-helene defect is cross-branch (trunk forged inherited by confront vs confront true-family), and with the forged fact on trunk the gate reports 0 violations, because the (frame, branch) scoping (R433: sibling world-lines never conflict) excludes it even though confront INHERITS the trunk fact across the fork. Visibility inherits across a fork; conflict-scoping does not — an asymmetry. A branch contradicting its own inherited ancestor (not a sibling) is a within-world contradiction that should be catchable; the fix (a follow-on round) is ancestor-aware conflict scoping (compare against ancestor-inherited facts, still not siblings), distinct from the R433 sibling-divergence-is-data invariant. Code 0; the re-runnable recipe and findings are preserved in claudedocs/phase1-drift-acceptance/DETERMINISTIC-ACCEPTANCE.md (git SSOT, R479).

Round 487 — R486 cross-branch gap retrac.decision_summary: R486 reported a deterministic-gap finding: that the exclusivity gate missed the real f-helene contradiction because it is cross-branch (a trunk assertion the confront branch inherits vs the confront claim), and proposed an ancestor-aware conflict-scoping fix as the follow-on. On building that follow-on, the premise was checked first and found false: it was a TEST ERROR, not a gap. The Belvoir trunk facts live on the spine branch (58 of them; confront and quiet fork from spine at sc-20, confess/escalate from quiet at sc-40). R486 declared the trunk forged-name fact on the main branch instead — an unrelated world-line that confront never inherits — so the gate correctly treated it as data and reported zero violations. Placing the same fact on the real trunk branch (spine), the gate FIRES: validate-continuity reports rule_exclusive_overlap on r-name-status at sc-53 in the confront world, comparing the confront true-family claim against the inherited spine forged fact. The join_world scope resolver (mnemosyne-validate continuity) already makes an ancestor branch and its descendant the same conflict scope (a pair is in-scope when one branch is the other's ancestor-or-equal); only SIBLING world-lines are data (the R433 divergence-is-data invariant). So ancestor-inherited contradictions were always caught — there is no scoping gap and no fix is needed; the user-greenlit ancestor-aware-scoping change is unnecessary. Code 0: this round retracts the false R486 gap finding (frozen ledger — corrected by a new entry, not by rewriting R486) and corrects the evidence doc. Methodology lesson banked: R486's gap was an unverified post-hoc inference (reasoned about scoping without checking the branch topology), the same scale-floor R476/R478 trap — a conclusion that survives only because nothing independent checked it; the independent check here (what branch is the trunk?) is the ground truth. Net: drift v2 is complete and field-correct — exclusivity catches contradictions same-branch AND ancestor-inherited, substantiation classifies payoffs substantiated/unsubstantiated/unverifiable, and AI is out of the verdict.

Round 488 — wrong-branch footgun made lo.decision_summary: R486's "gap" was a wrong-branch authoring error (a trunk fact defaulted to `main` while the trunk was the named branch `spine` the forks inherit), and the system accepted it silently — add-fact validated, validate-continuity passed, and the conflict gate simply never compared the misplaced fact, so a real contradiction would have gone uncaught. R488 makes that footgun fail loud. New deterministic continuity check: a fact's canon coordinate (canon_from, and canon_to when set), IF positioned in the store's canon by some branch's order, must be a node in the fact's OWN branch composed order; otherwise the fact sits on the wrong world-line and emits FactCanonOffBranch. A coordinate named by NO branch order is the orderless / forward-declared mode and is tolerated unchanged (the R440 boundary doctrine). The check reuses CanonOrder (a new names(branch, node) helper over the existing reach_for composition) and runs in scan_continuity, the deterministic gate — the right layer, because the composed canon order is a separate declared artifact the lower atomic write primitives cannot see (so write-time reject is not available; the gate is the trust anchor, the R485 principle). Honest boundary: this catches "the coordinate is not in this branch at all" (the default-to-main case, and any sibling whose world-line lacks the node); it does NOT catch a coordinate that is validly in multiple world-lines declared on the wrong-but-valid sibling — that residual is the same prose-vs-declaration intent-faithfulness class the gate cannot close by construction. Round 488 — wrong-branch footgun made lo.publishable_decision_summary: R486's "gap" was a wrong-branch authoring error (a trunk fact defaulted to `main` while the trunk was the named branch `spine` the forks inherit), and the system accepted it silently — add-fact validated, validate-continuity passed, and the conflict gate simply never compared the misplaced fact, so a real contradiction would have gone uncaught. R488 makes that footgun fail loud. New deterministic continuity check: a fact's canon coordinate (canon_from, and canon_to when set), IF positioned in the store's canon by some branch's order, must be a node in the fact's OWN branch composed order; otherwise the fact sits on the wrong world-line and emits FactCanonOffBranch. A coordinate named by NO branch order is the orderless / forward-declared mode and is tolerated unchanged (the R440 boundary doctrine). Round 489 — timeline-scalar interval gat.decision_summary: Depth-ladder rung 1 (R476: scalar/arithmetic gates) is designed as a surface-not-gate after the timeline-scalar PoC (2026-06-13, code 0, phase1-timeline-poc/, gitignored) field-proved the pull the linear scale-floor experiment could not see. The owner challenge re-opened the R475/R476 deferral: the North-Star venue is AAA-game narrative, where the player traverses a branching order the author cannot control, so timeline faults amplify. The PoC: a 2-world-line inheritance slice where the SAME inherited rule (ratification needs 42 days) is clean in the lawful world-line (84-42=42) and a contradiction in the hasty one (31-10=21), while validate-continuity maximally armed (canon order + four exclusive rules + severity reject) returned violations 0 — the fault is a cross-predicate magnitude relation no same-predicate equality gate can express. FIT is small: the scalar value already rides TypedObject::Value, canon order + holds_at already give the per-world read, so the missing piece is read-side arithmetic only, no store-schema change. Design: an interval rule class in narrative-rules/v1 (third beside exclusive/transition, additive under deny_unknown_fields), shape value(left)-value(right) op bound evaluated per frame-world-subject; one class hosts both the gap fault (rung 1) and the ordering/precondition fault (rung 3) via a const-0 bound. Determinism preserved (AI never in the gate, R484/R485): a pure numeric comparison of declared typed legs; the scalar parse fail-louds (interval_operand_unverifiable, the R450/R468/R485 no-silent-skip), the author types it via advisory typing-discovery R457-459. Surface-not-gate: the primary consumer is a read verb report-timeline-gaps (read-first like report-payoff-substantiation R485, cross-frame = data), an opt-in severity wiring into validate-continuity is a follow-on (R413/R419/R423 knob). Reader reuse: report-timeline-gaps is the next holds_at reader (irony R455 4th, playthrough R466 5th), owns no interval algebra (R441 binding rule); one evaluator function both consumers call (R305 parity). v1 boundary: bare-number arithmetic in a single implicit unit; cross-unit normalization is v2 on pull. Design-only, code 0; the build is a separate owner-triggered round restating this shape (R452). SSOT = fictional-adapter-design.md sec 7.20; pull evidence = phase1-timeline-poc/findings.md.

Round 490 — timeline-scalar interval gat.decision_summary: R489 designed the interval rule (depth-ladder rung 1, design sec 7.20); R490 builds it. A new narrative-rules/v1 class `interval` checks, per (frame x world x subject), value(left) - value(right) op bound — op in {ge,le,eq,gt,lt}, bound a literal const or a same-subject scalar predicate. Same-subject by construction (the measured pull: codicil ratified-on-day - codicil signed-on-day >= codicil min-ratify-gap-days), the cross-predicate magnitude relation no exclusivity gate can express. Determinism is preserved (AI never in the gate, R484/R485): a pure numeric comparison of declared typed legs. The scalar parse fail-louds — an operand that is non-numeric, absent on the right/bound leg, or resolves to more than one distinct holding value is surfaced as interval_unverifiable (a count, never gated, the R450/R468/R485 no-silent-skip), never silently passed. ONE evaluator (scan_interval_rule) keyed on the left operand fact and evaluated at THAT fact canon coordinate, resolving the earlier right/bound operands via WorldCtx::holds_at — the gate single point-quantified reader, no parallel time algebra (R441); report-timeline-gaps (the read surface, sec 7.20 step 2) will call the same function (R305/R390 parity). The predicate existence check now covers every referenced predicate (left + right + predicate-bound) in one site (R436 typo guard). Leg/class coherence is extended (R443): an interval rule rejects per/allowed, exclusive/transition reject right/op/bound, and the bound rejects unless exactly one of predicate/const. Wired into scan_continuity under the existing opt-in --severity (default off = surface-not-gate; the read verb and a per-class severity are follow-ons). Live: the timeline PoC hasty world now fires rule_interval_violation (31-10=21 < 42), lawful stays clean (84-42=42 >= 42), interval_unverifiable 0 — the fault that passed the maximally-armed gate (violations 0, R488 PoC) is caught. AS-BUILT DEVIATION from sec 7.20: the design wrote operands as left/right; the build reuses the rule existing primary predicate field for the LEFT operand. RUNG-3 CORRECTION: the PoC ordering fault (signed-on-day vs coming-of-age-day) is CROSS-subject (codicil vs edmund), so it is NOT a const-0 bound on this same-subject class — cross-subject relations are a wider shape, deferred. 5 unit tests, clippy clean, cargo test --workspace green; no store-schema change (v21).

Round 491 — report-timeline-gaps built:.decision_summary: R489 designed the timeline-scalar interval gate and R490 built it (the gate, under validate-continuity opt-in severity); R491 builds design sec 7.20 step 2, the report-timeline-gaps READ surface. Surface-not-gate: per query world the same interval evaluator is presented as a read report — each declared interval rule outcome classified violated / unverifiable / satisfied, never gating (no severity, exit 0). Single-evaluator no-drift (R305/R390): scan_interval_rule was refactored to return one IntervalOutcome per (world x holding left fact) with a Satisfied/Violated/Unverifiable verdict; the continuity gate ADAPTS those outcomes (Violated to a RuleIntervalViolation, distinct (frame,world,subject) Unverifiable to the count) and the read verb groups them per world, so the gate and the report consume ONE evaluator and cannot diverge — a parity test pins it. The predicate-existence check was extracted to one shared check_rule_predicates used by both readers (no half-enforced check, R436). report-timeline-gaps is the next holds_at reader after irony (R455) and playthrough (R466), owning no time algebra. Wired: ops timeline_gaps_report (resolves the same narrative-rules artifact as the gate), CLI report-timeline-gaps (--order/--rules/--world/--sidecar/--json), MCP report_timeline_gaps. Live on the timeline PoC store: hasty world violated=1 (ratified 31 minus signed 10 >= 42 fails), lawful satisfied=1 (84-42=42), main empty — the per-world dashboard the gate bare count could not give. 2 new unit tests + clippy clean + 242 validate tests; no store-schema change (v21). Remaining sec 7.20 follow-ons: per-class opt-in severity (step 3), cross-unit normalization (v2), rung-3 cross-subject.

Single-evaluator no-drift (R305/R390): scan_interval_rule was refactored to return one IntervalOutcome per (world x holding left fact) with a Satisfied/Violated/Unverifiable verdict; the continuity gate ADAPTS those outcomes (Violated to a RuleIntervalViolation, distinct (frame,world,subject) Unverifiable to the count) and the read verb groups them per world, so the gate and the report consume ONE evaluator and cannot diverge — a parity test pins it. The predicate-existence check was extracted to one shared check_rule_predicates used by both readers (no half-enforced check, R436). report-timeline-gaps is the next holds_at reader after irony (R455) and playthrough (R466), owning no time algebra. Wired: ops timeline_gaps_report (resolves the same narrative-rules artifact as the gate), CLI report-timeline-gaps (--order/--rules/--world/--sidecar/--json), MCP report_timeline_gaps. Live on the timeline PoC store: hasty world violated=1 (ratified 31 minus signed 10 >= 42 fails), lawful satisfied=1 (84-42=42), main empty — the per-world dashboard the gate bare count could not give. 2 new unit tests + clippy clean + 242 validate tests; no store-schema change (v21). Remaining sec 7.20 follow-ons: per-class opt-in severity (step 3), cross-unit normalization (v2), rung-3 cross-subject.

Round 492 — per-class interval severity:.decision_summary: R489 designed the timeline-scalar interval gate, R490 built it (wired into scan_continuity under the shared --severity), R491 built the report-timeline-gaps read surface; R492 builds design sec 7.20 step 3 — per-class opt-in severity, decoupling the interval (timeline) gate from the structural continuity gate. New [continuity].interval_severity (Option Severity, default OFF) plus a validate-continuity --interval-severity override. The gate exit now partitions: structural violations (conflict / off-branch / succession / exclusive / transition) gate under severity; interval (RuleIntervalViolation) violations gate ONLY under interval_severity, OFF by default. So interval is surface-not-gate by default — the violation is always produced as data (in the gate report and in report-timeline-gaps) but never causes a nonzero exit unless the author opts in. R490 had interval riding the shared --severity (rejecting structural also rejected interval); R492 separates them. Doctrine refinement: R449 recorded that rule violations ride severity with no separate knob because a same-frame rule violation is wrong data never a legitimate intermediate state — that holds for EXCLUSIVE and TRANSITION, but INTERVAL is the exception: a scalar timeline gap can be an intentional authored time-bend (games bend time deliberately), so it gets its own knob, the SpecDriftSection legitimate-intermediate-state rationale. The multi-severity precedent is the code-refs gate (severity_missing/binding/coverage/...). Wired: config ContinuitySection.interval_severity, ops ContinuityScanReport (interval_severity plus interval_violation_count), CLI partition plus --interval-severity plus the violations line printing structural=N interval=N, MCP description. Live on the timeline PoC store (interval-only violation): --severity reject alone exits 0 (was exit 1 under R490), --interval-severity reject exits 1, --interval-severity warn exits 0. 3 new tests plus clippy clean; no store-schema change (v21). Remaining sec 7.20 follow-ons: cross-unit normalization (v2), rung-3 cross-subject, the R453 trap-corpus blind grade.

Round 493 — causal coherence is authorin.decision_summary: Two causal-coherence candidates were PoC-tested this session (code 0) and BOTH REFUTED; the meta-conclusion is preserved as git SSOT. The owner asked whether before/after causal relationships are deterministically verifiable, and from the depth-ladder two candidate mechanisms were tested. (1) A causes edge class (a recorded fact-to-fact causal dependency): REFUTE. Live on a flashback slice, the effect-before-cause invariant is ALREADY surfaced as payoff_before_setup (R442, legal mystery/flashback structure, surfaced never gated) — a causes gate over DISCOURSE order would false-positive on every flashback; over STORY time it is the interval gate (rung-1, R490). Acyclicity reuses the SuccessionCycle scan (R463); dangling-cause is rung-3. The only net-new piece (a causal cycle among non-succession facts) is unmeasured, YAGNI. (2) rung-3 cross-subject preconditions (the locked-room: Surel poisons via the consulting-room draught but is a keyless ground-floor patient — the scale-floor measured causal defect): REFUTE new substrate. Two live tests: Test 1 (action declared, no preconditions) = violations 0, the fault invisible (the gate cannot derive that administer-draught requires co-location + authority, commonsense); Test 2 (preconditions declared as concrete typed facts) = EXISTING exclusivity fires both (per-subject location, per-object custody). So the contradiction-check is existing exclusivity (zero new gate); the gap is FORCING (nothing makes the author declare the preconditions); the cross-subject must-equal relation is NOT needed (a concrete required leg suffices for the static case); the only value-add is an AI-advisory precondition-discovery (reuse R457-459), a future pull not justified by one instance. META-CONCLUSION: both decompose into existing mechanisms (exclusivity / SuccessionCycle / interval / payoff) + more authoring (declaring the load-bearing facts). The store causal-coherence reach is bounded by AUTHORING BURDEN, not by missing gates — the empirical confirmation of the R476 depth-ladder thesis. The causal map: of the bands the scale-floor exposed, rung-1 (scalar/timeline arithmetic) was the ONLY one needing net-new substrate (built R490-492, inexpressible by existing mechanisms); causal precedence/paradox and preconditions are already covered by existing gates + authoring. Strategic implication: more causal-gate PoCs will keep decomposing; the productive frontier is the authoring side (AI-advisory discovery to lower the burden) or a different axis (sec 10 backlog). Both PoC dirs preserved as git SSOT (R479 pattern; scratch stores excluded per R404, manifests reproduce them). SSOT: phase1-causes-poc/findings.md, phase1-rung3-poc/findings.md.

Round 494 — AI-first coverage is bound b.decision_summary: R493 concluded causal coherence is authoring-bound (causes + rung-3 both REFUTED — the gates are sufficient, the gap is authoring); R494 LOCATES that gap precisely via a self-extraction PoC and unifies the session. The agent (an AI) performed the self-extraction LIVE — authoring a 3-scene slice and extracting its own load-bearing facts — with the deterministic continuity gate as the OBJECTIVE judge (not self-graded). One gate (at-location exclusivity per subject), two contradictions of the same shape differing only in EXPLICIT (stated) vs IMPLICIT (entailed): Helene confined to the sickroom + seen at the conservatory (both stated) = explicit; the letter burned in the study with only Helene motive but her location unstated = implicit. Three live results: (1) Run 1, faithful extraction of stated facts only = 1 violation, the explicit contradiction (sickroom vs conservatory) fires, the implicit one is SILENT (helene-at-study was never stated, so a confined Helene burning the letter has no facts to fire on — the coverage gap, deterministic); (2) Probe — report-typing-candidates surfaces 0 untyped facts: the discovery tool surfaces UNTYPED EXISTING facts, but the load-bearing fact is MISSING entirely, so no deterministic tool can surface a fact never recorded; (3) Run 2 — inferring the unstated precondition (helene-at-study) and adding it makes the implicit contradiction fire, the loop closes but only via INFERENCE. Secondary: un-extracted MOVEMENT transitions left three locations co-holding (the same implicit-fact gap in a second guise — prose carries states, not the moves between them). CONVERGENCE: all three session PoCs point to one conclusion — the gates are sufficient; AI-first end-to-end coverage (the scale-floor R475 binding constraint) is bound by the AI INFERENCE of IMPLICIT load-bearing facts (preconditions, transitions), which no deterministic tool can supply (a missing fact is invisible). LEVERAGE: an AI-advisory implicit-fact inference step in the authoring loop — propose the entailed preconditions/transitions from each scene prose (the R457-459 discovery pattern but AI-driven, not human-approved, per the AI-first North Star), record them, let the existing gates check. Testable only under the scale-floor blind session-separated protocol (R469): does AI inference of implicit facts measurably raise coverage without costing prose quality (the open regime question). HONEST BOUNDARY: a single self-authoring agent cannot blind-measure its own coverage RATE (the contradictions are mine) — this is a MECHANISM demonstration (faithful extraction structurally omits unstated entailments), not a statistic; the AAA-scale rate is the deferred blind protocol. The true ceiling (R476) stands: the gate checks declared facts; whether inferred preconditions are the right/complete set is commonsense, author/model. PoC preserved as git SSOT (R404 scratch store excluded; manifests + prose reproduce it). SSOT phase1-selfextract-poc/findings.md.

no deterministic tool closes it: report-typing-candidates surfaces 0 untyped facts (a MISSING fact is invisible — the discovery tool sees untyped EXISTING facts, not absent ones); only inference closes the gap

convergence: causes + rung-3 + self-extraction all conclude the gates are sufficient and AI-first coverage is bound by the AI inferring implicit load-bearing facts (preconditions, transitions) — the scale-floor R475 binding constraint located to one cause

leverage: an AI-advisory implicit-fact inference step (R457-459 pattern, AI-driven not human-approved per the AI-first North Star), testable only under the R469 blind session-separated protocol; preserved as git SSOT (R404 scratch store excluded)

Round 495 — facts-first dissolves the ex.decision_summary: R494 located the AI-first coverage bottleneck in prose-to-facts extraction (the AI under-infers implicit facts); R495 records the owner facts-first reframing, PoC-validated at the mechanism level: flip the order to facts-to-prose and the extraction gap DISSOLVES. The agent authored a Belvoir-mystery fact-base TRUTH-FIRST (the load-bearing helene-at-study in the truth frame up front, because authoring the murder truth cannot omit placing Helene at the scene), gate-checked it, and hand-rendered prose as a PROJECTION. Three live results: (1) the fact-base gate-checks clean with the truth-vs-belief divergence as DATA (validate-continuity violations 0, cross_scope 1) — the perspectival frame model (north-star: agent-belief is not ground-truth, both true) carries the misdirection without a false alarm; (2) report-irony-intervals (R455) surfaces the divergence as a deterministic window (household sickroom vs truth study, opens at sc-murder) — the mystery engine made queryable, not lost; (3) contrast — a same-frame error (Helene in two locations IN the truth frame) IS caught (rule_exclusive_overlap, exit 1), so facts-first does not weaken the gate: cross-frame = mystery (data), same-frame = real error (gated). Truth/revelation separation was demonstrated by two hand-rendered prose projections of the SAME base: the household projection withholds the truth (never states helene-at-study) = mystery; the truth projection reveals it. Mystery and consistency coexist because the truth lives in the checked base and the prose reveals selectively. WHY THE GAP DISSOLVES: in prose-to-facts the load-bearing fact was never stated so extraction missed it (R494); in facts-to-prose it is in the base by construction, there is no lossy extraction step, the gate validates the base BEFORE prose, and the prose is a projection that cannot assert what the facts lack. This re-derives the north-star architecture thesis (every artifact is a projection of the one substrate) and is the CYOA-renderer backlog direction. WHAT IT ADVANCES: the AI-first authoring loop is not extract-better-from-prose but author-the-fact-base-first (gates checking it live), then project prose. HONEST BOUNDARIES: hand-rendered (no automated facts-to-prose renderer exists, CYOA unbuilt); prose CRAFT untested (does facts-first yield good narrative or wooden list-prose — the scale-floor craft tension where plain won; needs the blind session-separated judge R469); fact RELEVANCE still authoring-bound (facts-first forces declaring the load-bearing facts but does not invent which are relevant — the R476 ceiling). PoC preserved as git SSOT (R404 scratch store excluded; manifests reproduce it). SSOT phase1-factsfirst-poc/findings.md.

Round 496 — facts-first render loop desi.decision_summary: The sec 10 Tier-3 CYOA renderer and the R495 facts-first authoring render step are the SAME facts->prose->playable pipeline seen from two ends; designed as one loop (sec 7.21). Layer B = the LLM prose-generation render step (a protocol, not a verb; craft deferred to the R469 blind judge); Layer A = the deterministic assembler (an L3 presentation consumer + the one new read verb report-fork-tree, the choice graph the backlog assumes but no surface emits). The contract between them: the render-brief (a frame-filtered playthrough_manuscript slice = the sec 7.17 deferred per-frame split, no new substrate) and report-fork-tree (pure projection over store.branches, schema unchanged, fork_ancestry reuse). Prose round-trips to the content-SSOT, not the fact base, so the extraction gap stays dissolved (R495). The fork-tree verb has a present pull; the playable assembler stays behind sec 10's play-it gate. Design-only, code 0.
