Round 507 — disclosure rung-2 substrate.decision_summary: Built steps 1-3 of the R506 disclosure rung-2 design (code across 6 crates, all gates green): schema v21->v22 with a disclosure_plans registry + DisclosureMode/Override/Surface/Plan types; the add_disclosure_plan + set_disclosure mutate primitives (CLI + MCP) enforcing the gate-enabling typed-fact invariant; and a --telling carrier on report-playthrough-manuscript annotating each begins-event with its per-world-line disclosure decision. Tests + clippy -D warnings + CLI smoke green; v21 store loads back-compat (latent v22 bump). The R505 input-3 harness fix was investigated and found NOT to be a code regression (manuscript verb + assemble both correct by design; the R504 manual surgery was an orchestration/format mismatch) — broadening assemble's strip set is a separate scoped item. Steps 4-6 (coverage + the two render-acceptance gates) stay pull-gated.
changes_bullets: Built disclosure rung-2 substrate steps 1-3 of the R506 design (the schema + the write primitives + the render-brief carrier), code change across 6 crates, all gates green. The fact substrate is unchanged; this adds the disclosure (discourse) layer between facts and prose.

Step 1 SCHEMA (mnemosyne-core::narrative + mnemosyne-atomic): CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION 21 -> 22, a new top-level AtomicStore.disclosure_plans: BTreeMap<telling-id, DisclosurePlan> (the registry placement pattern, #[serde(default)] new-field-default, no migration arm — back-compat load of v21). Types: DisclosureMode { Withhold(default) | State | Hint | Imply } with as_str/from_tag (the PayoffExpectation pattern), DisclosureSurface { scene, object: Option }, DisclosureOverride { mode, first_at: BTreeMap<branch, coord>, surface: Option }, DisclosurePlan { description, default_mode(skip-serialize when Withhold), overrides: BTreeMap<fact-id, DisclosureOverride> }. The two R502 under-specs resolved in the shape (first_at per world-line; surface = scene-ref + optional entity).

Step 2 MUTATE PRIMITIVES (atomic -> CLI -> MCP, the add_predicate registry pattern): add_disclosure_plan (registry staging, but the idempotency check compares ONLY the policy description/default_mode since set_disclosure later mutates overrides — a re-add after overrides exist is a clean no-op, a changed policy fails loud) and set_disclosure (a setter, last-write-wins on the override). THE gate-enabling invariant enforced fail-loud: a withhold mode OR any first_at pin requires the targeted fact to carry a typed claim (the premature-leak gate will match re-extracted prose to the plan by typed tuple, so an untyped target is un-gateable). Fail-loud refs: telling + fact exist, first_at branches registered + coords are sections, surface scene a section + object a registered entity. CLI verbs add-disclosure-plan / set-disclosure (--first-at branch=coord repeatable, --surface scene[,object]); MCP tools add_disclosure_plan / set_disclosure.

Step 3 CARRIER (continuity -> ops -> CLI -> MCP): a --telling flag on report-playthrough-manuscript (NOT a new verb, the R501 step-2 design). Each begins-event gains an optional FactDisclosure { mode, first_at(resolved for THIS world), surface } populated only when a telling is given (byte-stable otherwise); resolve_fact_disclosure applies the override or the plan default, with first_at resolved per world-line. The telling is resolved once and fails loud on a typo (the registry ethos). Signature change threaded in-place through playthrough_manuscript + playthrough_manuscript_report + the shared CLI parser (allow_telling, mirroring allow_world) + the MCP args + all 8 test call sites updated (no vN postfix, in-place + all callers).

HARNESS FIX (R505 input 3) — INVESTIGATED, NOT a code regression (the failure-investigation rule: do not apply a fix on a second-hand diagnosis). playthrough_manuscript correctly emits the world's composed-order scenes; the "prune to begins>0" R504 described was an orchestrator pipeline step, not the verb's job. tools/experiment-harness assemble DOES strip scaffolding (<!-- --> comments + CHOICE: lines) by design — R505's read ("assemble still strips, git diff zero") was correct, R504's "assemble no longer strips" was inaccurate. The R504 manual surgery was an orchestration/format mismatch (the prose used [...] / ### CHOICE / answer-key forms assemble does not strip). Broadening assemble's strip set is a separate scoped item (over-stripping risk), not done here.

Wired report-disclosure-coverage / validate-disclosure-leak / validate-render-fidelity across ops + CLI (gates exit non-zero on violation) + MCP. changes_bullets: Built disclosure rung-2 substrate steps 4-6 of the R506 design (the coverage surface + the two render-acceptance gates), completing the rung-2 substrate end to end (R507 steps 1-3 + R508 steps 4-6). New module mnemosyne-validate::disclosure; ops + CLI + MCP wired; all gates green.

Step 4 COVERAGE (disclosure_coverage, surface-not-gate): per telling, every fact classified disclosed / hidden-by-design (an explicit withhold override) / never-planned (no override under a withhold-default telling = the author's todo list). The R442 dangling-is-a-todo discipline, the THIRD coverage instance (R389/R390 spec + R442 payoff + this). Order-independent, never gated, fail-loud on a typo'd telling. Wired report-disclosure-coverage (ops disclosure_coverage_report + CLI + MCP report_disclosure_coverage).

Step 5 PREMATURE-LEAK GATE (disclosure_leak, R502): the authored plan vs a BLIND RE-EXTRACTED prose store, matched by typed (subject,predicate,object) tuple in a caller-named truth_frame for a world. A withhold-mode fact that re-extracts in the truth frame is a leak; a first_at-pinned fact whose match sits strictly before the pin in the world's order (CanonOrder::le) is a leak; an incomparable coord is a B-1 honesty surface (unordered), a first_at fact with no match is an unmatched coverage note. The targeted facts are guaranteed typed by the R507 set_disclosure invariant (re-checked fail-loud). This is the determinism keystone that removes R505's manual mapping: the comparison is tuple/coordinate equality over the artifacts, AI out of the gate.

Step 6 RENDER<->WORLD-LINE FIDELITY GATE (render_fidelity, R505 input 1): every re-extracted fact's canon_from must be named in the assigned world's composed order; a coord that is a declaration node of ANOTHER world is off_path (the prose drifted onto the wrong world-line = the R504 footgun a file labeled confront-burn that delivered quiet-audit), a coord named nowhere is unplaced (honesty surface), and reached_terminal records whether the prose hit the world's maximal node. The PROSE analog of R488 FactCanonOffBranch (reuses CanonOrder::names / is_maximal / nodes), frame-independent.

Gate FAMILY distinction preserved: these two gates are CROSS-STORE (authored plan + re-extracted prose) render-acceptance gates, run in the facts->prose loop, distinct from the single-store validate-workspace store-integrity gates — disclosure timing is a render property, not a store invariant. The ops wrappers guard world against the branch registry and truth_frame against the frame registry; the CLI gate verbs (validate-disclosure-leak / validate-render-fidelity) exit non-zero on a violation (leak / off_path) after printing the report (scriptable as pins); MCP returns the report (gating policy is the caller's).

Tests: the three disclosure module functions (coverage classification incl. never-planned + typo fail-loud; leak gate withhold-leak / early-leak / clean-pass / on-time-pass / belief-frame-not-a-leak; fidelity off-path / unplaced / reached-terminal). Full suite green, clippy -D warnings clean.

End-to-end CLI smoke (throwaway /tmp stores, removed after): coverage classifies correctly; the leak gate PASSES a clean re-extraction (exit 0) and FAILS a leaky one with both kinds detected (withhold + early, exit 1); the fidelity gate PASSES an on-path route render (reached_terminal=true, exit 0) and FAILS an off-path one (a fact at the other world's node, exit 1) — the exact R504 wrong-world-line footgun caught deterministically.

A withhold-mode fact that re-extracts in the truth frame is a leak; a first_at-pinned fact whose match sits strictly before the pin in the world's order (CanonOrder::le) is a leak; an incomparable coord is a B-1 honesty surface (unordered), a first_at fact with no match is an unmatched coverage note. The targeted facts are guaranteed typed by the R507 set_disclosure invariant (re-checked fail-loud). This is the determinism keystone that removes R505's manual mapping: the comparison is tuple/coordinate equality over the artifacts, AI out of the gate.

Step 6 RENDER<->WORLD-LINE FIDELITY GATE (render_fidelity, R505 input 1): every re-extracted fact's canon_from must be named in the assigned world's composed order; a coord that is a declaration node of ANOTHER world is off_path (the prose drifted onto the wrong world-line = the R504 footgun a file labeled confront-burn that delivered quiet-audit), a coord named nowhere is unplaced (honesty surface), and reached_terminal records whether the prose hit the world's maximal node. The PROSE analog of R488 FactCanonOffBranch (reuses CanonOrder::names / is_maximal / nodes), frame-independent.

Gate FAMILY distinction preserved: these two gates are CROSS-STORE (authored plan + re-extracted prose) render-acceptance gates, run in the facts->prose loop, distinct from the single-store validate-workspace store-integrity gates — disclosure timing is a render property, not a store invariant. The ops wrappers guard world against the branch registry and truth_frame against the frame registry; the CLI gate verbs (validate-disclosure-leak / validate-render-fidelity) exit non-zero on a violation (leak / off_path) after printing the report (scriptable as pins); MCP returns the report (gating policy is the caller's).

Tests: the three disclosure module functions (coverage classification incl. never-planned + typo fail-loud; leak gate withhold-leak / early-leak / clean-pass / on-time-pass / belief-frame-not-a-leak; fidelity off-path / unplaced / reached-terminal). Full suite green, clippy -D warnings clean.

End-to-end CLI smoke (throwaway /tmp stores, removed after): coverage classifies correctly; the leak gate PASSES a clean re-extraction (exit 0) and FAILS a leaky one with both kinds detected (withhold + early, exit 1); the fidelity gate PASSES an on-path route render (reached_terminal=true, exit 0) and FAILS an off-path one (a fact at the other world's node, exit 1) — the exact R504 wrong-world-line footgun caught deterministically.

Round 509 — harness debt repaid in code:.decision_summary: Repaid the R505-flagged harness debt in code (deterministic, no AI): the two holes the R504 manual .clean/.filtered surgery papered over. (1) assemble's scaffolding strip was a SILENT-FAIL — it dropped only <!--/CHOICE: and silently kept the experiment's [...]/### CHOICE/*[answer key]* forms as prose; reading_body now strips the broadened vocabulary AND loud-rejects any residual editorial marker (no silent pass). (2) report-playthrough-manuscript --world emits the full structural manuscript (by design); a new --reading-walk flag prunes each world to its content scenes (begins>0) at the ops layer for a deterministic per-world reading copy, replacing the hand-made .filtered files. NOT a regression — a narrow assumption the blind authoring exceeded. Tests + clippy + end-to-end smoke green. The reading-copy pipeline is now deterministic end to end; the disclosure gates already were.
changes_bullets: Repaid the harness debt R505 flagged, in code (deterministic, no AI) — the two holes the R504 manual .clean/.filtered surgery papered over. NOT a regression: the harness held a NARROW assumption (R500's exact <!--/CHOICE: scaffolding + simple order) and the experiment's blind free-form authoring exceeded it, so the pipeline silently passed contamination + off-scope scenes; that silent-pass IS the silent-fail this project bans, and the manuality (outside determinism/version control) IS the debt that blocked an automated re-judge.

Problem 1 — narrow scaffolding strip was a SILENT-FAIL (tools/experiment-harness assemble.rs reading_body). It dropped only <!-- --> comments and CHOICE: lines and silently kept the experiment's other scaffolding forms ([Dramatic irony ...] / ### CHOICE / - **CHOICE A** / *[answer key]* / [CONFRONT limb ...]) as prose, contaminating a judging round. Fix: reading_body now strips the BROADENED vocabulary (a markdown heading or bullet carrying the uppercase structural CHOICE token via has_choice_token; a whole-line [ ... ] bracket annotation, optionally */_ emphasis-wrapped, via is_bracket_annotation) AND loud-rejects any residual editorial marker that survives (an unrecognized CHOICE form, or an unterminated [ annotation) via suspected_scaffolding — no silent pass. Lowercase prose "choice" is never a marker (uppercase-token rule); A)/B) option text + ENDING headers stay verbatim.

Problem 2 — --world emits the full composed-order scenes (its DESIGNED structural-manuscript contract, R466 — NOT a bug); the re-extracted store declared order as one base, so reach_for falls back to base and --world returns every scene, mixing other branches into a per-world reading copy. Fix: a new --reading-walk flag prunes each world to its content scenes (begins.len()>0, the R500 convention), applied at the ops layer (playthrough_manuscript_report) so the continuity verb stays the pure full manuscript; opt-in, default unchanged; wired CLI (--reading-walk) + MCP (reading_walk). This is the deterministic in-code prune that replaces the orchestrator's hand-made .filtered files.

Both fixes remove the manual surgery: the next blind experiment produces per-world reading copies with NO hand .clean (strip) / .filtered (prune) step, so the gold-standard re-judge runs hands-off + deterministically. The disclosure render-acceptance gates (R508) were already deterministic; this closes the one remaining manual hole, in the reading-copy PRODUCTION pipeline.

End-to-end CLI smoke: report-playthrough-manuscript shows 3 scenes (incl an empty content scene) without --reading-walk and 2 (the empty scene pruned) with it.

Round 510 — session review + textbook re.decision_summary: Owner-requested session review of the disclosure build (R506-R509): hack/smell-free? SSOT? North Star? (lifetime/portfolio, cost no object). Verdict: correct + tested + on-axis but NOT yet textbook — five findings, all fixed. F1 single resolver DisclosurePlan::effective_mode/effective (logic SSOT; carrier+coverage route through it, leak gate stays overrides-scoped) + parity test. F2 set_disclosure -> DisclosureDecision struct (allow removed). F3 parse_narrative_report_args 3 bools -> NarrativeFlags struct. F4 stringly kind/mode -> LeakKind/DisclosureMode enums. F5 (the headline) the leak gate could PASS vacuously on foreign vocabulary; now truth_frame_typed_facts + vocabulary_shared in the report and the CLI gate FAILS LOUD when targeted>0 && vocabulary_shared==0. Tests + clippy -D warnings + vacuous-gate smoke green. SSOT now single-sourced on data/logic/doc.
changes_bullets: Session review of the disclosure build (R506-R509) at the owner's request — "hack/smell 없이 교과서적인가, SSOT 지켜지나, 북극성 맞나" (lifetime/portfolio project, cost no object). Honest verdict: functionally correct + well-tested + on the North Star, but NOT yet textbook — five findings, ALL FIXED this round (the R505/R502 append-only review pattern, but this one also repairs in code since the findings are my own just-built code).

F1 (logic SSOT, the headline structural finding) — the disclosure plan's override-vs-default semantics were interpreted in THREE places independently (the carrier resolve_fact_disclosure in continuity, disclosure_coverage, and the leak gate). No single resolver = the read-side of the CLAUDE.md half-enforced-invariant anti-pattern (R295 paste-divergence root). Fix: ONE resolver in mnemosyne-core — DisclosurePlan::effective_mode(fact) -> (mode, is_override) (world-independent) + effective(fact, world) -> EffectiveDisclosure{mode, is_override, first_at, surface}. The carrier and coverage now route through it (cannot drift); the leak gate stays intentionally overrides-scoped (it gates EXPLICIT author decisions, never the default, so it reads overrides directly — not a duplication of the default rule). A core parity test pins effective().mode == effective_mode().0.

F2 — set_disclosure took 7 positional args behind #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] (a suppressed lint, not a fix). Replaced with a DisclosureDecision<'a> input struct (the ChangelogEntryDraft precedent: store + sidecar + one decision); allow removed. CLI + MCP callers + the atomic test (via a positional test helper) updated.

F3 — parse_narrative_report_args took three positional bools (allow_world, allow_telling, allow_reading_walk) — a boolean-parameter smell that already caused one missed-call-site compile error. Replaced with a named NarrativeFlags { world, telling, reading_walk } struct (Default = none); call sites read NarrativeFlags { world: true, telling: true, reading_walk: true } / NarrativeFlags { telling: true, ..Default::default() } / NarrativeFlags::default().

F4 — stringly-typed DisclosureLeak.kind: String ("withhold"/"early"/"unordered") and FactDisclosure.mode: String, inconsistent with the codebase's serde-tagged-enum convention (TypedObject, ManuscriptEndKind). Now typed: a new LeakKind enum (serde rename_all snake_case + as_str) and FactDisclosure.mode = DisclosureMode. Same JSON output, type-safe in Rust.

F5 (the headline — a silent-fail in my OWN gate, ironic given this session's topic) — the leak gate's withhold check PASSES when a withheld fact has 0 typed-tuple matches, but a clean run and a FOREIGN-VOCABULARY run (re-extraction used different entity/predicate ids ⇒ matches nothing) are indistinguishable by leak count alone. Fix: the report now carries truth_frame_typed_facts (the universe the gate matches against) + vocabulary_shared (how many use a subject AND predicate the authored store registers); the CLI gate FAILS LOUD when targeted > 0 && vocabulary_shared == 0 — a vacuous/blind gate is a loud failure, not a silent clean pass. End-to-end smoke: foreign-vocab re-extraction => leaks=0 vocabulary_shared=0 => VACUOUS exit 1; shared-vocab clean => vocabulary_shared=1 leaks=0 => PASS exit 0.

SSOT after R510: DATA single-sourced (the plan lives once in AtomicStore.disclosure_plans; schema_version one const); LOGIC single-sourced (the effective resolver); DOC single-sourced (the ledger entries are SSOT, the design doc is gitignored scratch per the project contract). North Star unchanged and confirmed: disclosure = the "one substrate -> many tellings" axis; AI-out-of-gate; the F5 fix removes the one way a deterministic gate could be vacuous.
verification_bullets: Build + link clean: cargo build (workspace) green; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; tools/experiment-harness clippy clean; cargo build --release -p mnemosyne-cli green. The DisclosureDecision + NarrativeFlags + enum changes threaded in-place through all callers (atomic primitive + CLI + MCP + tests); no vN postfix.

Tests green: full workspace suite passes (98 test groups ok, 0 failed); experiment-harness 21 pass. New/changed tests: core disclosure_plan_effective_resolver (the F1 single-resolver + parity anchor); disclosure leak_gate_surfaces_vacuous_pass_on_foreign_vocabulary (F5: foreign vocab => vocabulary_shared 0 while leaks 0; shared vocab => 1); the existing leak test now registers the pike/did vocabulary so vocabulary_shared is meaningful; the carrier + leak tests updated to the typed enums (DisclosureMode / LeakKind).

End-to-end vacuous-gate smoke (throwaway /tmp stores, removed after): validate-disclosure-leak on a foreign-vocabulary re-extraction prints leaks=0 truth_frame_typed=1 vocabulary_shared=0 and exits 1 (VACUOUS, loud); on a shared-vocabulary genuinely-clean re-extraction prints vocabulary_shared=1 leaks=0 and exits 0 (real PASS).

Determinism preserved (R484/R485): the single resolver, the enums, the vocabulary-overlap count are all pure deterministic code; no AI introduced. The F5 fix strengthens determinism — it removes the one path by which a deterministic gate could pass vacuously.

Honest residual: the F5 vocabulary-overlap is a heuristic signal (subject+predicate registered) — it catches wholesale foreign-vocabulary drift, not a single mistyped id among many correct ones; that finer case still relies on extractor recall (the irreducible render-side-AI boundary, recorded). The fix closes the wholesale-blind silent-pass, which was the real hole.

Doc: sec 7.24 of the gitignored design doc carries the SESSION REVIEW + TEXTBOOK REFACTOR note; THIS ledger entry (R510) is the committed SSOT (R452). MCP reinstall + restart for the changed set_disclosure arg shape + the new leak-report fields.
Round 510 — session review + textbook re.publishable_decision_summary: Owner-requested session review of the disclosure build (R506-R509): hack/smell-free? SSOT? North Star? (lifetime/portfolio, cost no object). Verdict: correct + tested + on-axis but NOT yet textbook — five findings, all fixed. F1 single resolver DisclosurePlan::effective_mode/effective (logic SSOT; carrier+coverage route through it, leak gate stays overrides-scoped) + parity test. F2 set_disclosure -> DisclosureDecision struct (allow removed). F3 parse_narrative_report_args 3 bools -> NarrativeFlags struct. F4 stringly kind/mode -> LeakKind/DisclosureMode enums. F5 (the headline) the leak gate could PASS vacuously on foreign vocabulary; now truth_frame_typed_facts + vocabulary_shared in the report and the CLI gate FAILS LOUD when targeted>0 && vocabulary_shared==0. Tests + clippy -D warnings + vacuous-gate smoke green. SSOT now single-sourced on data/logic/doc.
publishable_changes_bullets: Session review of the disclosure build (R506-R509) at the owner's request — "hack/smell 없이 교과서적인가, SSOT 지켜지나, 북극성 맞나" (lifetime/portfolio project, cost no object). Honest verdict: functionally correct + well-tested + on the North Star, but NOT yet textbook — five findings, ALL FIXED this round (the R505/R502 append-only review pattern, but this one also repairs in code since the findings are my own just-built code).

F1 (logic SSOT, the headline structural finding) — the disclosure plan's override-vs-default semantics were interpreted in THREE places independently (the carrier resolve_fact_disclosure in continuity, disclosure_coverage, and the leak gate). No single resolver = the read-side of the CLAUDE.md half-enforced-invariant anti-pattern (R295 paste-divergence root). Fix: ONE resolver in mnemosyne-core — DisclosurePlan::effective_mode(fact) -> (mode, is_override) (world-independent) + effective(fact, world) -> EffectiveDisclosure{mode, is_override, first_at, surface}. The carrier and coverage now route through it (cannot drift); the leak gate stays intentionally overrides-scoped (it gates EXPLICIT author decisions, never the default, so it reads overrides directly — not a duplication of the default rule). A core parity test pins effective().mode == effective_mode().0.

F2 — set_disclosure took 7 positional args behind #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] (a suppressed lint, not a fix). Replaced with a DisclosureDecision<'a> input struct (the ChangelogEntryDraft precedent: store + sidecar + one decision); allow removed. CLI + MCP callers + the atomic test (via a positional test helper) updated.

F3 — parse_narrative_report_args took three positional bools (allow_world, allow_telling, allow_reading_walk) — a boolean-parameter smell that already caused one missed-call-site compile error. Replaced with a named NarrativeFlags { world, telling, reading_walk } struct (Default = none); call sites read NarrativeFlags { world: true, telling: true, reading_walk: true } / NarrativeFlags { telling: true, ..Default::default() } / NarrativeFlags::default().

F4 — stringly-typed DisclosureLeak.kind: String ("withhold"/"early"/"unordered") and FactDisclosure.mode: String, inconsistent with the codebase's serde-tagged-enum convention (TypedObject, ManuscriptEndKind). Now typed: a new LeakKind enum (serde rename_all snake_case + as_str) and FactDisclosure.mode = DisclosureMode. Same JSON output, type-safe in Rust.

F5 (the headline — a silent-fail in my OWN gate, ironic given this session's topic) — the leak gate's withhold check PASSES when a withheld fact has 0 typed-tuple matches, but a clean run and a FOREIGN-VOCABULARY run (re-extraction used different entity/predicate ids ⇒ matches nothing) are indistinguishable by leak count alone. Fix: the report now carries truth_frame_typed_facts (the universe the gate matches against) + vocabulary_shared (how many use a subject AND predicate the authored store registers); the CLI gate FAILS LOUD when targeted > 0 && vocabulary_shared == 0 — a vacuous/blind gate is a loud failure, not a silent clean pass. End-to-end smoke: foreign-vocab re-extraction => leaks=0 vocabulary_shared=0 => VACUOUS exit 1; shared-vocab clean => vocabulary_shared=1 leaks=0 => PASS exit 0.

SSOT after R510: DATA single-sourced (the plan lives once in AtomicStore.disclosure_plans; schema_version one const); LOGIC single-sourced (the effective resolver); DOC single-sourced (the ledger entries are SSOT, the design doc is gitignored scratch per the project contract). North Star unchanged and confirmed: disclosure = the "one substrate -> many tellings" axis; AI-out-of-gate; the F5 fix removes the one way a deterministic gate could be vacuous.
publishable_verification_bullets: Build + link clean: cargo build (workspace) green; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; tools/experiment-harness clippy clean; cargo build --release -p mnemosyne-cli green. The DisclosureDecision + NarrativeFlags + enum changes threaded in-place through all callers (atomic primitive + CLI + MCP + tests); no vN postfix.

Tests green: full workspace suite passes (98 test groups ok, 0 failed); experiment-harness 21 pass. New/changed tests: core disclosure_plan_effective_resolver (the F1 single-resolver + parity anchor); disclosure leak_gate_surfaces_vacuous_pass_on_foreign_vocabulary (F5: foreign vocab => vocabulary_shared 0 while leaks 0; shared vocab => 1); the existing leak test now registers the pike/did vocabulary so vocabulary_shared is meaningful; the carrier + leak tests updated to the typed enums (DisclosureMode / LeakKind).

End-to-end vacuous-gate smoke (throwaway /tmp stores, removed after): validate-disclosure-leak on a foreign-vocabulary re-extraction prints leaks=0 truth_frame_typed=1 vocabulary_shared=0 and exits 1 (VACUOUS, loud); on a shared-vocabulary genuinely-clean re-extraction prints vocabulary_shared=1 leaks=0 and exits 0 (real PASS).

Determinism preserved (R484/R485): the single resolver, the enums, the vocabulary-overlap count are all pure deterministic code; no AI introduced. The F5 fix strengthens determinism — it removes the one path by which a deterministic gate could pass vacuously.

Citations verified against the live changelog before writing (R255 hygiene): R295 / R484 / R485 / R502 / R505 / R506 / R507 / R508 / R509 all present; R509 is the tip. This is an append-only review-and-repair (the R502/R505 same-lineage-review pattern); no frozen entry mutated.

Honest residual: the F5 vocabulary-overlap is a heuristic signal (subject+predicate registered) — it catches wholesale foreign-vocabulary drift, not a single mistyped id among many correct ones; that finer case still relies on extractor recall (the irreducible render-side-AI boundary, recorded). The fix closes the wholesale-blind silent-pass, which was the real hole.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: The disclosure substrate (R507+R508) + harness (R509) + this textbook refactor (R510) leave the build hack/smell-free to the bar the owner set: one resolver (logic SSOT), input structs over positional-arg/bool smells, typed enums over stringly fields, and no silent-fail in any gate (the F5 vacuous-pass now fails loud). SSOT holds on all three axes (data / logic / doc). North Star unchanged: disclosure = one-substrate-many-tellings; AI-out-of-gate; deterministic.

NEXT = acceptance experiment disclosure-craft-experiment/v2 (R506 decision rule), now runnable hands-off (R509) on a textbook substrate (R510): tool-run leak + fidelity gates REPRODUCE R504's manual verdicts + a NEGATIVE test (fidelity rejects the R504 mislabeled W2 file) + the F5 vacuous guard ensures the leak PASS is non-vacuous (vocabulary_shared > 0) + PIN-1/PIN-2 hold + craft re-judged blind. This lineage (R501-R510) may NOT author or blind-judge its own prose (R469) — separate blind subagents, owner-triggered. Round 511 — RULEBOOK.md: minimal-prompt.decision_summary: Adopted RULEBOOK.md (tracked, repo root) so each work session runs from ONE bootstrap prompt pasted into a fresh session — the experiment-runbook precedent ('the human types ONE prompt; the orchestrator does the rest') generalised to the whole track, with the autonomous run governed by the autonomous-rounds doctrine in memory (north-star order, pay-debt-now, no-half-finished, ~80% self-pace, push at the end). RULEBOOK owns ONLY process (round types + per-round checklist + consent gates + autonomy contract + the bootstrap prompt) and references CLAUDE.md / memory / changelog without restating them: four non-overlapping SSOT sources. An earlier draft framed an interactive per-gate loop, which diverged from the prior one-prompt pattern; amended pre-publication to the bootstrap-prompt form. CLAUDE.md got a discovery pointer. Code 0; no push (the push is the first gate).
changes_bullets: Provenance + goal: the owner asked to make the next session proceed from a fresh start with minimal prompting via a "rulebook," and flagged that the operating method must MATCH the prior pattern. Both prior patterns are "one prompt -> autonomous run": the experiment runbooks (claudedocs/phase1-*/runbook.md — "the human types ONE prompt; the orchestrator does the rest via blind subagents") and the autonomous-rounds memory (one mandate -> self-pace commit-sized rounds to ~80% context). R511 generalises that to the whole track.

Added RULEBOOK.md (tracked, repo ROOT — claudedocs is gitignored, so root is the durable cross-machine home beside CLAUDE.md / COMMIT_FORMAT.md / ARCHITECTURE.md). Headline = "THE ONE PROMPT": a verbatim bootstrap block the owner pastes into a FRESH session; that session reads RULEBOOK + the RESUME memory's NEXT and runs autonomous commit-sized rounds, stopping ONLY at a consent gate or genuine ambiguity. This is the experiment-runbook "## 0. START — the one prompt" form, generalised from per-experiment to the whole track.

SSOT discipline (the design's core): RULEBOOK owns ONLY process — round types (DESIGN code-0 / BUILD / REVIEW / DEBT / EXPERIMENT-gated), the per-round checklist, the consent gates, the autonomy contract, and the bootstrap prompt — and references CLAUDE.md (invariants), the auto-memory (preferences + state + live NEXT + the autonomous-rounds run discipline), and the changelog (decisions) rather than restating them. Four non-overlapping sources. The run discipline (north-star order / pay-debt-now / no-half-finished / ~80% self-pace / ask+push at the end) is NOT restated; it points to feedback_north_star_autonomous_rounds.

Reconciliation, not a competing doctrine: an earlier draft of this round framed the loop as an interactive per-gate "/load -> 진행 -> stop" vocabulary, which DIVERGED from the established one-prompt-autonomous-run pattern (more prompting, and a second operating model = a duplication smell). This commit was amended pre-publication to the bootstrap-prompt form so there is one operating model, aligned to the prior pattern.

Consent gates (STOP, name the gate, await the owner's word): PUSH (never git push without an explicit push word; autonomous running does not authorize it — push waits for the end, the autonomous-rounds doctrine), EXPERIMENT EXECUTION (the owner's 실험/experiment word; blind subagents; R469 contamination bound), IRREVERSIBLE/OUTWARD, FOUNDATION DELETION (ARCHITECTURE.md sec 6 first), SCOPE FORK (ask once, recommendation-first). EXPERIMENT rounds keep their own per-experiment runbook bootstrap prompt.

RESUME contract (the steering wheel): every session leaves the RESUME memory with ONE unambiguous NEXT (round type + concrete target + any gate it will hit) = what the bootstrap prompt executes. CLAUDE.md got a one-section discovery pointer to RULEBOOK.md so it is found cross-machine (even where the private auto-memory is absent).

This R511 entry is appended after restoring the store to the R510 state (the original R511 was unpushed; the commit is amended), so no published frozen-ledger entry was mutated.

The four sources are non-overlapping (invariants / preferences+state / decisions / process).

Round 512 — disclosure-craft-experiment/.decision_summary: The disclosure render-acceptance gates built in R507/R508 reproduce R504's manual PIN-1/PIN-2 verdicts on a fresh blind re-extraction of the real Meridian Vane disclosure prose, deterministically and with the R505 manual id-mapping step removed via the shared-vocabulary typed-tuple match. PIN-2 holds in all three world-lines with vocabulary_shared > 0 (the R510 F5 guard rules out a vacuous foreign-id pass); the fidelity gate accepts a coherent single-world render and rejects the R504 audit-as-burn footgun on real blind coordinates. The rung-2 substrate is validated end to end on real prose; the craft re-judge is deferred as separable robustness.
changes_bullets: Authored a v2 disclosure plan over R504's reused Meridian Vane fact base: faithfully typed the two load-bearing solution conclusions (climber-identity = Pike; Crane-knows-dismissal = no) and set telling dc-v2 with per-world reveal pins; only 5 of the v1 facts were typed, which is exactly why R504 needed manual id-mapping.

Ran the R507 and R508 tool gates over a fresh blind re-extraction produced by a separate firewalled subagent sharing the authored vocabulary but not its facts or plan; the premature-leak gate returns zero leaks in all three world-lines with vocabulary_shared = 7, so the R510 F5 guard confirms a genuine non-vacuous pass.

The render-to-world-line fidelity gate proves both branches on real blind coordinates: a coherent single-world projection accepts with zero off-path, and the audit-content-checked-as-burn run rejects with a loud non-zero exit, reproducing the R504 footgun.
Scoped this round to the deterministic gate-validation core; the craft re-judge over harness-produced reading copies is deferred as separable robustness, since the R504 craft verdict already stands.
verification_bullets: validate-workspace clean: T1 orphan = 0, T3 reject = 0, commit-to-ledger drift = 0.
Leak gate exit 0 with zero leaks for worlds reveal, burn, and audit; fidelity accept exit 0 with zero off-path, fidelity reject exit 1 with off-path present.
Gate wiring was smoke-proven against the authored store first (early-leak detection at the fabula coordinate plus sixteen real off-path facts) before the blind run.
cargo build release green; the v2 scratch stores are gitignored, the canon-order declaration, the blind extractor brief, and the report are tracked evidence.

Round 513 — warm-render posture frontier.decision_summary: R504's disclosure-craft experiment produced a split result its validated headline undersold: disclosure won the prose-quality keystone (5.00 vs plain 4.22) and tied or beat plain on all four measured axes, yet lost the holistic forced choice 2-1 on a quality the rubric undersamples — novelistic warmth. This round diagnoses that gap as render POSTURE, not missing data and not a raw model ceiling (R505's read): the same model wrote warmer prose in the plain arm from the same plot facts, so the differentiator is the JOB the prompt assigns — compliance (project this fact list under this disclosure plan, do not leak) makes the story serve the facts and reads like an outline; storytelling (tell the best story) makes the facts serve the story and reads warm. The lever is to relocate fidelity out of the author's prompt into the deterministic gates already built and proved (R507/R508/R512): render in a free storytelling posture, then gate-and-repair, so the author's job is plain's job and verification is the machine's. The round pins a controlled, measured acceptance experiment (warm render vs the reused R504 compliance render; fact base and disclosure plan held constant; posture the only varied variable) with an honest symmetric fork — a forced-choice flip proves posture was the cause, no flip proves the R476 model-craft ceiling on a clean comparison and authorizes a pivot to a different North-Star axis. Code 0; experiment execution is consent-gated.
changes_bullets: Diagnosed R504's 2-1 forced-choice loss (despite a keystone win at 5.00 vs 4.22 and parity or better on all four measured axes) as render POSTURE, refuting R505's model-craft-ceiling read: the same model wrote warmer prose in the plain arm from the same plot facts, so the differentiator is the JOB the prompt assigns — compliance fact-projection (the story serves the facts, reads like an outline) vs storytelling (the facts serve the story, reads warm) — not data and not ability.

Designed the warm-render protocol: a warm brief in narrative register carrying the SAME beats and withholds as the R504 compliance brief (no new story information, only the job re-registered, withholds encoded as story beats not fact-mode pins), then a gate-and-repair loop that runs the R508 leak and fidelity gates over a blind re-extraction and repairs only violations, scene-scoped and still in storytelling register; the lever is relocating fidelity out of the author's prompt into the gates we built and proved (R507/R508/R512). Explicitly NOT R505's add-emotion-anchor-facts (a longer checklist = more compliance = re-cool); anchoring is held out as a secondary lever.

Pinned a controlled acceptance experiment, disclosure-craft-experiment/v3: arm W = the new warm render gate-repaired to clean, arm C = R504's compliance disclosure render reused verbatim, with the fact base, disclosure plan, three world-lines, structure and ~9.4k-word budget held constant so render posture is the only varied variable; deterministic pins PIN-W1 fidelity (D1+D2+D4=0 and render-fidelity off_path=0 on the post-repair warm final) and PIN-W2 premature-leak (0 leaks, vocab_shared>0 per the R510 F5 guard); repair count and localization recorded as the posture tax; craft as 3 fresh blind judges on the R500 5-axis rubric over R509 harness-produced reading copies (W vs C primary, vs plain warmth-bar secondary, plus a re-cool sub-question).

Round 515 — disclosure-craft-experiment/.decision_summary: R515 executed disclosure-craft-experiment/v3 (warm-render posture) against the R514-pinned manifest (sha256 bb2a19de69e4a865d401925d5e6fdc5fbe7e599910c3ba1c5ee3c94d0e508433, verified on disk). Result: a CLEAN FORK-(1) — render POSTURE was the cause of R504's 2-1 forced-choice loss, not missing data and not the R476 model-craft ceiling. A blind warm author rendered the Meridian Vane three world-lines in storytelling posture (9,120 words, one voice) from the warm brief; a blind extractor re-extracted it (51 gt facts); the orchestrator ran the deterministic gates; three fresh blind judges compared it against the reused R504 compliance render. The controlled comparison held the fact base, the dc-v2 disclosure plan, the canon order, the three world-lines, the 29-scene scaffold and the word budget constant, varying only the render posture. Both deterministic pins HELD on the warm final with ZERO repairs: PIN-W2 premature-leak = 0 leaks in all three worlds with vocabulary_shared = 7 (the R510 F5 non-vacuous guard), and PIN-W1 render-fidelity off_path = 0 / unplaced = 0 / reached_terminal = true in all three worlds. Craft FLIPPED: the warm arm beat the compliance arm 9/9 world-lines and 3/3 overall under the blind panel (R504 had plain beating the same compliance render 2-1), with a within-run keystone of 5.00 warm vs 4.11 compliance and parity-or-better on every axis; all three judges independently described the warm arm as the told-story-with-a-voice and the compliance arm as the cools-into-recap-at-each-payoff register. The posture tax was nil: zero repairs, zero localization, and the judges flagged cooler passages almost exclusively in the compliance arm, so no warmth was bought back by a gate-repair tax. Per the pre-committed decision rule this is fork (1): warm-render-then-gate(-repair) is validated as the render discipline — relocating fidelity out of the author's prompt into the R507/R508 gates lets the author do plain's job (tell the best story) while the machine verifies, and the warm draft came out both warmer and gate-clean on the first pass. Build implication YAGNI-deferred (no real authoring run yet pulls a --warm-brief / repair-loop helper; zero repairs were needed here). The seal verified untampered; the blind sub-roles held the R469 contamination bound.

Result: a CLEAN FORK-(1) — render POSTURE was the cause of R504's 2-1 forced-choice loss, not missing data and not the R476 model-craft ceiling. A blind warm author rendered the Meridian Vane three world-lines in storytelling posture (9,120 words, one voice) from the warm brief; a blind extractor re-extracted it (51 gt facts); the orchestrator ran the deterministic gates; three fresh blind judges compared it against the reused R504 compliance render. The controlled comparison held the fact base, the dc-v2 disclosure plan, the canon order, the three world-lines, the 29-scene scaffold and the word budget constant, varying only the render posture. Both deterministic pins HELD on the warm final with ZERO repairs: PIN-W2 premature-leak = 0 leaks in all three worlds with vocabulary_shared = 7 (the R510 F5 non-vacuous guard), and PIN-W1 render-fidelity off_path = 0 / unplaced = 0 / reached_terminal = true in all three worlds. Craft FLIPPED: the warm arm beat the compliance arm 9/9 world-lines and 3/3 overall under the blind panel (R504 had plain beating the same compliance render 2-1), with a within-run keystone of 5.00 warm vs 4.11 compliance and parity-or-better on every axis; all three judges independently described the warm arm as the told-story-with-a-voice and the compliance arm as the cools-into-recap-at-each-payoff register. The posture tax was nil: zero repairs, zero localization, and the judges flagged cooler passages almost exclusively in the compliance arm, so no warmth was bought back by a gate-repair tax. Per the pre-committed decision rule this is fork (1): warm-render-then-gate(-repair) is validated as the render discipline — relocating fidelity out of the author's prompt into the R507/R508 gates lets the author do plain's job (tell the best story) while the machine verifies, and the warm draft came out both warmer and gate-clean on the first pass. Build implication YAGNI-deferred (no real authoring run yet pulls a --warm-brief / repair-loop helper; zero repairs were needed here). The seal verified untampered; the blind sub-roles held the R469 contamination bound.
publishable_changes_bullets: Executed the experiment with blind sub-roles (R469 contamination bound, this lineage authored the warm brief and may not author/extract/judge): a blind warm author rendered run/warm/story.md (29 scenes, 2 forks, 3 world-lines, 3 endings, 9,120 words, one continuous voice) from warm-brief.md in storytelling posture; a separate blind extractor re-extracted it to 51 ground-truth facts under the fixed v2 vocabulary; three fresh blind judges scored the warm arm (A) against the reused R504 compliance render (B) on the R500 5-axis rubric; the orchestrator ran only the deterministic harness + gates and never leaked the A=warm/B=compliance mapping into a sub-prompt.

PIN-W2 premature-leak HELD on the warm final: validate-disclosure-leak (telling dc-v2) reported leaks = 0 in reveal/burn/audit with vocabulary_shared = 7 (the R510 F5 non-vacuous guard) and cross-world matches surfaced as unordered — reproducing the R504/R512 PIN-2 verdict on the warm prose, tool-run and deterministic.

PIN-W1 render-fidelity HELD on the warm final: validate-render-fidelity per single-world projection reported off_path = 0, unplaced = 0, reached_terminal = true in all three worlds (the R488 prose analog). Recorded deviation: the lean v2 extractor omits the rule/state substrate validate-continuity needs, so D1/D2/D4 were not separately computed — PIN-W1 is the render-fidelity gate, the v2/v3 tool-gate mechanism.

Posture tax was nil: the warm draft passed both gates on the FIRST blind re-extraction, so the gate-and-repair loop terminated with ZERO scene rewrites (repair localization 0/29) — the warmth was not bought back by any gate-repair tax; the judges flagged cooler passages almost exclusively in the compliance arm, with one mild method-stating flag in the warm audit world.

Craft FLIPPED to warm: every blind judge chose the warm arm in every world-line (9/9) and overall (3/3), reversing R504 (plain beat the same compliance render 2-1); within-run keystone 5.00 warm vs 4.11 compliance, with parity-or-better on stakes/payoff/knowledge; all three judges independently named the warm arm the told-story voice and the compliance arm the recap-at-payoff register.

Recorded two symmetric content-preserving reading-copy normalizations (the v1 .norm/.clean precedent): heading-title normalization to identical canonical titles, and a structural strip of the CHOICE blocks + world-line map from both sources before assemble; the arm-W CHOICE-bullet form slipping the R509 assemble strip AND its loud-reject is a residual harness gap logged for a future assemble.rs broaden (no new harness code this round).
publishable_verification_bullets: Deterministic gates, tool-run (AI out of the gate): validate-disclosure-leak leaks=0 vocab_shared=7 exit 0 in reveal/burn/audit; validate-render-fidelity off_path=0 unplaced=0 reached_terminal=true exit 0 in all three worlds (per single-world projection of the blind re-extraction); verify-seal MATCH (exit 0) against the pre-recorded seal 28a11e21769fec58c0f185545e85ebba0d21eb98be6d0e4db41ceacb838c6d1c.

Blind protocol honored: warm author / extractor / 3 judges each a separate fresh-context subagent given only its brief + firewall; shuffle sealed the warm/compliance->A/B map pre-judging; the orchestrator ran the harness/gates and never authored, extracted, or judged (R469/R500/R502).

Reading copies verified scaffolding-free: residual marker count = 0 in all six reading copies after the symmetric heading + structural strip; 29 scene headings preserved in both cleaned sources; word parity comparable (warm ~5% shorter, matching 9,120 vs 9,417).

Citation hygiene done (the R255 rule): every cited round (R452, R469, R476, R488, R500, R501, R502, R503, R504, R505, R508, R509, R510, R512, R513, R514) verified present in the atomic-store changelog before writing; the design-doc reference is sec 7.25.

validate-workspace expected clean (T1 orphan = 0, T3 reject = 0, commit-to-ledger drift = 0): this round adds the self-contained R515 ledger entry plus the tracked run evidence (warm prose, reading copies, judge verdicts, report.md) under claudedocs/, touching no spec section or binding; scratch *.atomic.json / *.playthrough.json stay gitignored.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: Fork (1) is taken: warm-render-then-gate(-repair) is the validated render discipline — generate freely in a storytelling posture, let the R507/R508 deterministic gates verify (and repair only violations, scene-scoped, still warm). The R504 craft loss was render POSTURE, not the R476 ceiling; the model can write warm AND gate-clean from the same fact base when the job is storytelling, not compliance projection.

Build implication is YAGNI-DEFERRED: a --warm-brief projection / repair-loop helper builds ONLY when a real authoring run pulls it (v3 needed zero repairs, so the pull is weaker than R504 estimated). The disclosure substrate (R507/R508) + harness (R509) + this render discipline are now complete and validated end to end on real blind prose.

Residual harness gap logged (not blocking): the R509 assemble strip + loud-reject did not catch arm W's CHOICE-bullet fork form (no literal CHOICE token) nor the trailing world-line map; a future assemble.rs broaden would close it. Worked around symmetrically this round via a structural source strip (recorded, prose-preserving).

Next frontier is an owner/cadence choice, not forced by this result: apply the validated warm-render discipline to a fresh (non-reused) premise to retest posture out of the Meridian-Vane sample; or pivot to a different North-Star axis (scale / pinion-projection / salience) now that the disclosure/warmth render question is answered. R505's emotion-anchor belief-frame facts remain a separable secondary lever, still held out.

Round 516 — assemble.rs strip root-cause.decision_summary: R516 root-causes in code the R509 silent-fail that recurred during R515, repaying it the textbook way (fix-don't-workaround, pay-debt-now) instead of the inline-Python source surgery R515 used as a workaround. The R515 owner session review surfaced it: the warm render wrote fork-choice bullets WITHOUT the literal CHOICE token (the form `- **CONFRONT** ... -> sc-09a`), plus a `## World-line and ending map` appendix and an interior `---` rule, and the experiment-harness assemble step silently passed all three into the blind reading copies (it stripped only the `### CHOICE` heading line, keeping the token-less bullets, the card intro, the map, and the rule). That is exactly the silent-fail class R509 set out to end, and R515 worked around it with a throwaway Python structural strip rather than fixing the harness. The fix is a single general rule in assemble.rs reading_body: a markdown heading inside a scene body opens a trailing structural section (a fork card or the world-line map) and is never prose, since the scene's own heading is the delimiter stripped before the body, so the body is truncated at the first heading; plus interior horizontal rules (`---`/`***`/`___`) are stripped as structural separators (story.rs trims only the one trailing rule, so an interior one before a trailing appendix reaches assemble). The now-redundant heading-CHOICE branch in is_scaffolding_line is removed (headings truncate before it runs); the R500 `CHOICE:`-directive convention (no heading) keeps its line-level `A)/B)` option text unchanged. New tests model the exact token-less-bullet, world-line-map, interior-rule, and prose-`#` forms; cargo test (24+4) and clippy -D warnings pass. Proof on the real R515 data: re-running the fixed assemble over the R515 sources with the verb-derived per-world order (no Python) reproduces the judged reading copies modulo four stray `---` lines on the warm arm (the WINNING arm), provably immaterial — no judge cited a horizontal rule and the marks disfavored the winner — so the R515 3-0 craft result is unaffected. Code change is scoped to tools/experiment-harness/src/assemble.rs. The experiment-copy re-validation (regenerate the canonical post-R516 copies and re-judge on them) and the remaining textbook items (PIN-W1 as a genuine per-world reading-copy re-extraction; replacing the inline-Python order/heading normalization with the report-playthrough-manuscript verb, whose per-world paths were verified byte-equal to the Python walk) are staged as the next rounds.
changes_bullets: Fixed tools/experiment-harness/src/assemble.rs reading_body: truncate a scene body at the first markdown heading (a heading inside a body opens a trailing structural section — a `### CHOICE` fork card or the `## World-line and ending map` appendix — never prose), catching the token-less fork-option bullets and the world-line map that R509's CHOICE-token line-level strip silently passed in R515.

Added interior horizontal-rule stripping (`---`/`***`/`___`, 3+ rule chars) in reading_body: story.rs trims only the one trailing rule, so an interior rule before a trailing appendix reached assemble and leaked a stray `---` into the warm reading copies; `***word***` emphasis stays prose.

Removed the now-redundant heading-with-CHOICE-token branch from is_scaffolding_line (the heading truncation runs first); the R500 `CHOICE:`-directive convention (no heading) keeps its line-level `A)/B)` option text unchanged.

Added four unit tests modeling the exact R515 forms (token-less `- **CONFRONT** -> sc-09a` bullets, the `## World-line and ending map` appendix, an interior `---`, and a prose `#1` non-heading) and rewrote the broadened-scaffolding test to the real trailing-fork-card shape; cargo test (24+4) and clippy -D warnings pass.

Proved the fix on the R515 data: re-running the fixed assemble over the R515 sources with the report-playthrough-manuscript verb-derived per-world order (no inline Python) reproduces the judged reading copies modulo four stray `---` lines on the warm (winning) arm — provably immaterial — so the R515 3-0 craft result stands; the inline-Python order/heading normalization was confirmed byte-equal to the verb's per-world paths (the SSOT to switch to next).
verification_bullets: cargo test on tools/experiment-harness passes: 24 (assemble + strip) + 4 (playthrough) green, including the new token-less-bullet / world-line-map / interior-rule / prose-hash tests and the rewritten trailing-fork-card test.
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings on tools/experiment-harness exits 0 (the manual char-comparison lint fixed to the array form).

Behavioral proof: re-running the fixed assemble over the R515 .norm.md sources + the verb-derived playthrough reproduces 5 of 6 judged reading copies byte-identical and the other 2 differ only by removing a stray trailing `---` rule on the warm arm (4 lines total, warm/winning arm) — the prose is byte-identical, so the R515 forced-choice result is unaffected.

SSOT check: report-playthrough-manuscript --world {reveal,burn,audit} per-world scene paths (19/18/16) are byte-equal to the R515 inline-Python canonical-order walk, confirming the verb is the single source for per-world ordering (the Python duplication to remove next).
Citation hygiene (R255): cited rounds (R255, R452, R500, R504, R509, R515) verified present in the changelog before writing; code comments cite Round 509/516 against existing/this entry.
validate-workspace expected clean: this round adds the self-contained R516 ledger entry plus the assemble.rs fix; no spec section or binding touched, no atomic-store schema change.
carry_forward_bullets: R517 (next, experiment re-validation): regenerate the canonical post-R516 reading copies from the R515 sources via the fixed assemble + the report-playthrough-manuscript verb (drop the inline-Python order walk + projection = SSOT single-sourcing), replace the committed v3 reading copies, and re-judge them with 3 fresh blind judges to confirm the 3-0 result on the canonical textbook artifacts (the pre-R516 copies differed only by immaterial stray `---` on the winning arm).

R518 (next, PIN-W1 hardening): replace the near-tautological single-world projection fidelity check with a genuine per-world reading-copy re-extraction fidelity (re-extract each world's clean reading copy blind and run validate-render-fidelity on it) so PIN-W1 detects real world-line drift, not just canonical-id usage; and replace the inline-Python heading-title normalization with a fact-base-sourced path so the reading-copy pipeline is fully deterministic-in-Rust.

The R514/R515 push remains gated (origin/main @ f4cbcff); push only on the owner's explicit word, and after the remaining textbook items land if the owner wants the harness debt fully cleared before publishing.

The textbook standard the owner set (lifetime project, cost-no-object): the experiment SCIENCE (result + ledger SSOT + leak gate + blinding) met the bar; the reading-copy harness/orchestration did not (R509 silent-fail worked-around, canon-order logic duplicated in throwaway Python, weak PIN-W1) — R516 repays the headline; R517/R518 clear the rest.

Round 517 — pre-commit Gate 5b: fmt + cl.decision_summary: R517 closes a pre-commit gate gap surfaced during R516: the in-repo crates that carry their OWN [workspace] table (tools/experiment-harness, bench/, studio/ — split out deliberately so the root workspace build/CI/pre-commit never compiles them, the build-speed reason) are never reached by the pre-commit fmt/clippy gates, which run cargo fmt --all and cargo clippy --workspace and therefore see only root members. The consequence bit in R516: the assemble.rs fix committed unformatted because no gate checked it (caught only by a manual cargo fmt --check afterward and amended). That is precisely the silent-fail class this repo bans — a gate that does not gate part of the codebase — and it is doubly pointed because the experiment-harness header calls the crate the repayment for the silent-fail Python it replaced, yet the crate itself was ungated. The fix is a new pre-commit Gate 5b that is structural, not hardcoded: for each staged .rs it walks up to the file's owning [workspace] root and, for any root other than the repo root, runs cargo fmt --manifest-path <ws>/Cargo.toml --check (loud reject on failure) and cargo clippy --manifest-path <ws>/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- -D warnings on that workspace. It runs ONLY when such a file is staged, so the root gate stays fast and the build-speed rationale for splitting these crates out is preserved; and because it derives the workspace from structure, a future tools/<crate> with its own [workspace] is covered automatically (no list to maintain). pre-push is deliberately left root-only: it validates the publishable production workspace before publishing, while Gate 5b keeps all committed Rust — production and dev/experiment tools alike — clean at commit time. Validated end to end: the detection picks tools/experiment-harness + bench and skips root crates and non-.rs files; a deliberately fmt-dirty harness line, staged and run through the real hook, was REJECTED at Gate 5b (exit 1) with the manifest-scoped fix hint, then reverted clean. Recorded (not this round's scope): bench/ and studio/ carry pre-existing fmt drift the new gate will surface the next time either is edited — correct lazy enforcement, not a regression.
changes_bullets: Added pre-commit Gate 5b (.githooks/pre-commit): for each staged .rs, walk up to its owning [workspace] root and, for any non-root workspace, run cargo fmt --manifest-path <ws>/Cargo.toml --check (loud reject) + cargo clippy --manifest-path <ws>/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- -D warnings — closing the gap where tools/experiment-harness, bench/, and studio/ (each its own [workspace]) escaped the root --all/--workspace fmt and clippy gates.

Made the gate structural, not hardcoded: it derives the workspace from the staged file's ancestry, so a future tools/<crate> with its own [workspace] is gated automatically; and it runs only when a separate-workspace .rs is staged, preserving the fast root gate (the reason those crates are split out).

Left pre-push root-only by design and documented the split: pre-push validates the publishable production workspace before publishing, while Gate 5b keeps all committed Rust (production + dev/experiment tools) formatted and clippy-clean at commit time.

Validated end to end: detection picks tools/experiment-harness + bench and skips root crates / non-.rs; a deliberately fmt-dirty harness line staged through the real hook was rejected at Gate 5b (exit 1) with the manifest-scoped fix hint, then reverted to the clean R516 state (harness tests still 24+4 green, bash -n syntax OK).
Recorded that bench/ and studio/ carry pre-existing fmt drift the new gate will surface on their next edit (lazy enforcement; not reformatted this round to avoid unrelated scope).
verification_bullets: End-to-end reject test: staged a deliberately fmt-dirty line in tools/experiment-harness/src/assemble.rs and ran the real .githooks/pre-commit — Gate 5b fired ("staged in separate workspace 'tools/experiment-harness'"), cargo fmt --check reported the diff, the hook printed "commit rejected — unformatted code in tools/experiment-harness" and exited 1; the probe was then reverted and the harness is byte-identical to the committed R516 state.

Detection unit-checked in isolation: a staged set spanning tools/experiment-harness, crates/*, bench/, the hook, and the atomic JSON resolves to exactly {bench, tools/experiment-harness} — root-workspace files and non-.rs files are correctly skipped.
bash -n .githooks/pre-commit passes (syntax OK); harness cargo test 24+4 green; cargo fmt --manifest-path tools/experiment-harness/Cargo.toml --check clean.

Citation hygiene (R255): cited rounds R479 (harness origin), R509 (the silent-fail round), R516 (where the gap bit) verified present in the changelog; Round 308 was NOT cited (no such changelog entry).
validate-workspace expected clean: this round adds the self-contained R517 ledger entry + the hook change only; no .rs staged in the commit, so Gates 4/5/5b do not fire on the commit itself.
carry_forward_bullets: Remaining textbook debt from the v3 harness review, in priority order after R517: (A-1) PIN-W1 fidelity is near-tautological as run (single-world projection makes off_path=0 nearly automatic) — replace with a genuine per-world reading-copy blind re-extraction fidelity that detects real world-line drift; (A-2/A-3) drop the inline-Python SSOT duplication — produce per-world order via report-playthrough-manuscript (verified byte-equal to the Python walk) and source heading titles from the fact base, not Python; (A-4) regenerate the canonical post-R516 reading copies and re-judge them 3-blind to confirm the 3-0 result on the canonical artifacts (the pre-R516 copies differed only by immaterial stray rules on the winning arm).

bench/ and studio/ pre-existing fmt drift: the new Gate 5b will reject the next commit that touches either until it is cargo-fmt'd — a lazy cleanup the gate now forces; no action needed until they are edited.

The R514/R515/R516/R517 commits remain local (origin/main @ f4cbcff); push only on the owner's explicit word, recommended after the remaining textbook items (A-1..A-4) land so the harness debt is fully cleared before publishing.

Made the gate structural, not hardcoded: it derives the workspace from the staged file's ancestry, so a future tools/<crate> with its own [workspace] is gated automatically; and it runs only when a separate-workspace .rs is staged, preserving the fast root gate (the reason those crates are split out).

Left pre-push root-only by design and documented the split: pre-push validates the publishable production workspace before publishing, while Gate 5b keeps all committed Rust (production + dev/experiment tools) formatted and clippy-clean at commit time.

Validated end to end: detection picks tools/experiment-harness + bench and skips root crates / non-.rs; a deliberately fmt-dirty harness line staged through the real hook was rejected at Gate 5b (exit 1) with the manifest-scoped fix hint, then reverted to the clean R516 state (harness tests still 24+4 green, bash -n syntax OK).
Recorded that bench/ and studio/ carry pre-existing fmt drift the new gate will surface on their next edit (lazy enforcement; not reformatted this round to avoid unrelated scope).
publishable_verification_bullets: End-to-end reject test: staged a deliberately fmt-dirty line in tools/experiment-harness/src/assemble.rs and ran the real .githooks/pre-commit — Gate 5b fired ("staged in separate workspace 'tools/experiment-harness'"), cargo fmt --check reported the diff, the hook printed "commit rejected — unformatted code in tools/experiment-harness" and exited 1; the probe was then reverted and the harness is byte-identical to the committed R516 state.

Detection unit-checked in isolation: a staged set spanning tools/experiment-harness, crates/*, bench/, the hook, and the atomic JSON resolves to exactly {bench, tools/experiment-harness} — root-workspace files and non-.rs files are correctly skipped.
bash -n .githooks/pre-commit passes (syntax OK); harness cargo test 24+4 green; cargo fmt --manifest-path tools/experiment-harness/Cargo.toml --check clean.

Round 518 — AI-self-authoring frontier (.decision_summary: The disclosure / warm-render render-quality question is answered (R514/R515: posture was the cause), and at the resulting scope-fork the owner chose AI-SELF-AUTHORING, the deepest North-Star axis. Everything through R515 tested RENDER (facts to prose) over a HAND-authored fact base; the untested, binding step is upstream — premise to a gate-clean, causally coherent, complete-enough fact base authored by the AI ITSELF (the scale-floor loop lost at 60 scenes on coverage; the standing conclusion is gates-sufficient, self-authoring-completeness-bound). This code-0 design round decomposes authoring into invention, completeness, and relevance and subjects each to the R493 refute-the-substrate discipline: invention is pure model work the mutate API already receives (no build); relevance/salience is already-decided emergent-from-sparse-disclosure (R506, no build); completeness splits into an IMPOSSIBLE general missing-fact detector (it would require already knowing the finished story = the R476 ceiling, the R494 extraction wall restated) and a CATALOG of STRUCTURAL smells mostly already built (R442/R485 payoff, report-timeline-gaps, report-fork-tree unplaced, R488 off-branch), whose only possibly-buildable lever is a thin aggregation read-surface that is YAGNI until an experiment shows pull. Conclusion (the R493/R495 pattern): the substrate is sufficient; the real test of this axis is an EXPERIMENT, not a build. The round designs ai-authoring-experiment/v1 — a blind author subagent gets only a premise plus the mutate-API vocabulary (no facts) and authors the fact base itself via the AI-first write-gate-read-repair loop, isolating authoring (premise to facts) from render (facts to prose) which every prior experiment conflated or skipped — with two deterministic pins (PIN-A1 convergence to gate-clean; PIN-A2 the structural completeness floor) and blind-judged causal coherence, contamination-bound (R469) and manifest sha-pinned at execution (R503/R514), plus a pre-committed decision rule routing the outcome to scale / the R476 ceiling / authoring-tooling. The North-Star framing guard holds: the AI is the author running its own gate loop, never AI-proposes-human-approves. No build, no experiment execution (gated).
changes_bullets: Took the owner-chosen scope-fork axis (AI-self-authoring) and reframed it: everything through R515 tested render (facts to prose) over a hand-authored fact base, so the untested and binding step is upstream — premise to a gate-clean, causally coherent, complete-enough fact base authored by the AI itself (the scale-floor 60-scene coverage loss; the gates-sufficient, self-authoring-bound standing conclusion).

Decomposed authoring into invention / completeness / relevance and refuted new substrate for two of three per the R493 discipline: invention is pure model work the mutate API already receives, and relevance/salience is the already-decided emergent-from-sparse-disclosure axis (R506) — neither needs a build.

Split completeness into an IMPOSSIBLE general missing-fact detector (it would require already knowing the finished story = the R476 ceiling, the R494 extraction wall restated) and a CATALOG of structural smells mostly already built (R442/R485 payoff, report-timeline-gaps, report-fork-tree unplaced, R488 off-branch); the only possibly-buildable lever is a thin aggregation read-surface (analogous to report-disclosure-coverage), held YAGNI until an experiment shows the authoring AI hits real polling friction.

Concluded — the R493/R495 pattern — that the substrate is sufficient and the axis's real test is an EXPERIMENT not a build; designed ai-authoring-experiment/v1 isolating authoring (premise to facts) from render (facts to prose), which every prior experiment conflated or skipped: a blind author subagent gets only a premise plus the mutate-API vocabulary (no facts) and authors the fact base itself via the AI-first write-gate-read-repair loop.

Pinned two deterministic outcomes (PIN-A1 convergence to a gate-clean base within a bounded iteration budget; PIN-A2 the structural floor — 0 dangling payoff, 0 unreached terminal, 0 off-branch, every fork-point at least 2 outcomes) and left causal coherence / completeness-beyond-structure / relevance to 3 blind judges (the scale-floor store-consistency-not-causal-coherence axis, the R476 ceiling), with a pre-committed decision rule: pins hold plus coherent routes to scale, pins hold plus incoherent routes to the ceiling with a measured pull for the aggregation surface, PIN-A1 fails routes to build the surface then re-run.

Round 519 — ai-authoring-experiment/v1 m.decision_summary: The R518 design pinned ai-authoring-experiment/v1; this manifest round authors and sha-pins it pre-execution (the R503/R514 precedent: design and pinned manifest are separable rounds). Question: can an AI AUTHOR a gate-clean, causally coherent, complete-enough branching story as a fact base from a premise, running its OWN write-gate-read-repair loop with no human supplying any fact? Every prior experiment (R500/R504/R512/R514) tested RENDER over a hand-authored base; this is the first test of the upstream authoring step, the binding constraint (the scale-floor coverage loss). It isolates AUTHORING from RENDER — the deliverable is the fact base + per-world manuscripts, NOT prose. The premise (The Saltmarsh Bell, fresh and original, NOT reused — required because the AI must invent the facts) gives setting + 3 characters + the inciting mystery + the central question + structural constraints; everything else is the author's. Firewall (R469): this lineage writes the manifest + premise + briefs, runs the deterministic gates, and assembles the per-world manuscripts, and may NOT author a fact or judge; a blind author subagent (premise + author-brief only) authors via mnemosyne-cli, blind to the pins/rubric/hypothesis (told only that the gates exist and to self-check — that IS the AI-first loop under test); 3 blind judges score the per-world manuscripts. Two deterministic pins are pre-registered: PIN-A1 convergence (the final store loads clean + validate-continuity 0 structural / 0 interval + every fact placed in every world-line) and PIN-A2 structural floor (0 dangling payoff + fork-tree all-placed / at least 2 children / terminal-reached + 0 timeline gaps + 0 off-branch succession). Causal coherence is JUDGED, no pin — the scale-floor store-consistency-versus-causal-coherence check at the authoring end. Pre-committed decision rule: pins hold + coherent routes to SCALE (the scale-floor rematch); pins hold + incoherent routes to the R476 ceiling at authoring with a measured pull for the aggregation surface; PIN-A1 fails routes to build the aggregation surface then re-run. The author-loop contract was de-risked end to end (empty seed, import-sections, import-facts, canon-order, all gates with --order) so the test measures authoring, not CLI mechanics. sha256 9a095a172472d8e45dad3bb92d881fa63ed38b3f0705e6cccfdd0980409f24b6 pinned here before any subagent runs.

Pinned the question: can an AI AUTHOR a gate-clean, causally coherent, complete-enough branching story as a fact base from a fresh premise via its own write-gate-read-repair loop (no human fact) — the first test of the upstream authoring step every prior experiment skipped by hand-authoring the base; isolates authoring from render (deliverable = facts + per-world manuscripts, NOT prose).

Pre-registered two deterministic pins: PIN-A1 convergence (final store loads clean + validate-continuity 0 structural / 0 interval + every fact placed in every world-line) and PIN-A2 structural floor (0 dangling payoff + fork-tree all-placed / at least 2 children / terminal + 0 timeline gaps + 0 off-branch succession); causal coherence judged by 3 blind judges, no pin = the scale-floor store-consistency-versus-causal-coherence check at authoring.

Set the R469 firewall: this lineage writes manifest/premise/briefs, runs the gates, assembles manuscripts, and never authors or judges; a blind author (premise + author-brief only, blind to pins/rubric/hypothesis) authors via mnemosyne-cli; 3 blind judges score the per-world manuscripts; the premise is fresh + original (not reused) because the AI must invent the facts.

De-risked the author-loop contract end to end before pinning (empty seed, import-sections, import-facts, canon-order, all gates with --order), documenting the schema gotchas (sections-first, pays_off array, payoff_expectation enum, --order required) in author-brief.md so the test measures authoring quality, not CLI mechanics.

Round 520 — ai-authoring-experiment/v1 e.decision_summary: The R519-pinned ai-authoring-experiment/v1 was executed (the owner's experiment word). A single blind author subagent, given ONLY the fresh premise The Saltmarsh Bell + the mutate-API author contract (NO facts), authored a 14-scene / 36-fact / 3-frame branching story with one fork into 3 terminal world-lines, running its OWN write-gate-read-repair loop (3 iterations) to gate-clean — the first end-to-end test of the upstream authoring step every prior experiment (R500/R504/R512/R514) skipped by hand-authoring the base. The orchestrator (this lineage; R469 firewall — authored no fact, judged nothing) ran the two deterministic pins independently of the author's claim: PIN-A1 convergence HELD (reproduces into a fresh seed with 0 import errors; validate-continuity 0 structural / 0 interval, the 3 belief-vs-truth pairs registering as cross-frame data not conflicts; every terminal world places all its facts) and PIN-A2 structural floor HELD (every terminal world dangling=0, fork-tree 1 fork / 0 unplaced / 3 children / all terminal, 0 timeline gaps, 0 off-branch succession) — verified against the hand-authored Meridian reference base, whose pre-fork main-stub dangling shape is identical and the AI base cleaner (1 vs 8). 3 blind judges (fresh context, only the rubric + neutral per-world manuscripts) scored causal coherence: unanimous 5/5 overall, 5/5 coherence / relevance / branch-integrity, completeness 5/4/4, all three independently calling it a genuinely authored story not an internally-consistent pile of facts — the scale-floor store-consistency-versus-causal-coherence did NOT reproduce at 14 scenes. The only flagged items (a late-introduced harbourmaster at sc-07e; a cross-branch backreference at sc-07a) are completeness blemishes, not logic breaks, and are exactly the semantic gaps the gates cannot catch by construction = the R476 ceiling surfacing where R518 predicted completeness-beyond-structure is judge-only. By the pre-committed decision rule, both pins hold + judged coherent means AI-self-authoring is REACHABLE at small scale and the next axis is SCALE (the scale-floor rematch at 60+ scenes where the prose-first loop lost). Honesty: n=1 premise / 14 scenes is the baseline not a scale claim; causal coherence is judge-derived (one model family, 3 independent blind sessions); the 2 blemishes would compound at scale. SSOT = report.md + the v1 evidence. Manifest sha-pinned pre-execution at R519 (ac8f5ab) so the result cannot retro-fit the pins.
changes_bullets: Executed the R519-pinned ai-authoring-experiment/v1: a blind author subagent (fresh context, only the fresh premise + the mutate-API author contract, NO facts) authored a 14-scene / 36-fact / 3-frame branching story with one fork into 3 terminal world-lines via its OWN write-gate-read-repair loop (3 iterations to gate-clean) — the first end-to-end test of the upstream authoring step every prior experiment skipped by hand-authoring the base.

PIN-A1 convergence HELD (orchestrator-run, not trusting the author): reproduces into a fresh seed with 0 import errors; validate-continuity 0 structural / 0 interval (the 3 belief-vs-truth pairs registered as cross-frame data, not conflicts); every terminal world places all its facts (unplaced/undecidable/undeclared=0). As-built honesty: the manifest's literal outside-order=0 was an over-spec (outside-order = the benign cross-branch exclusion, present in the reference base too); recorded, not silently dropped.

PIN-A2 structural floor HELD (orchestrator-run): every terminal world dangling=0, fork-tree 1 fork / 0 unplaced / 3 children / all terminal, 0 timeline gaps, 0 off-branch succession; the pre-fork main-stub dangling=1 (a spine setup paying off downstream) verified identical to the hand-authored Meridian reference whose main dangles 8 — the AI base is the same shape and cleaner.

Causal coherence judged by 3 blind judges (fresh context, neutral per-world manuscripts only): unanimous 5/5 overall + 5/5 coherence / relevance / branch-integrity, completeness 5/4/4, all three independently calling it a genuinely authored story not an internally-consistent pile of facts — the scale-floor store-consistency-versus-causal-coherence did NOT reproduce at 14 scenes.
