Round 533 — convergence-JOIN build step.decision_summary: Build step 2 of the R528/sec-7.30 convergence-JOIN arc, pull-justified by R531's OUTCOME-1 (the blind author was forced to duplicate the shared ending 6 beats x2 because a fact carries one branch and the forest has no native join node). The MODEL, decided in R528 Q3 and field-confirmed by R531: the order algebra (closure_of/le/linearize, Kahn) is ALREADY a general DAG machine, so 'forest by construction' lived only in the ANCESTRY layer (single-parent fork_chain + single-lineage visibility) — a JOIN is a bounded generalization of that layer, NOT an order-algebra rewrite. A query world W's composition = its backward fork ancestors (fork_chain) union {W} union its FORWARD confluence-suffixes (every confluence C where W is transitively in C.converges_from). A fact authored ONCE on C is visible (holds_at) in EVERY converging parent world past the merge and not before (visibility is structural membership; the composed order bounds the timing) — so R531's 2x duplication is removed by construction (12 shared-tail facts become 6). The confluence itself is not a query world (exclusive-OR: one playthrough = one parent path + the shared suffix; querying the merge as a standalone world would union nothing useful and surface false dangling-payoff findings). Cross-merge conflict reconciliation (a suffix fact vs a parent-middle fact, and cross-confluence succession) is the explicit R534 scope, not this round — confluence is new substrate, so deferring it is a staged build, not a regression or a half-enforced invariant.
changes_bullets: core::forward_confluences — the forward dual of fork_chain: a BFS over the converges_from reverse edges returning every confluence a query world flows INTO transitively (the shared continuations downstream of a merge); BTreeSet-dedup makes termination structural, so no hop cap (unlike fork_chain's non-deduped linear walk).

continuity Lineage gains a forward set beside the backward cut; lineage_of populates both; visibility() now returns In for a fact on a forward confluence-suffix UNCONDITIONALLY (the confluence is shared downstream of every parent — WHEN it holds is the order's job, not visibility's), the dual asymmetry of the fork-cut bound.

CanonOrder::from_declaration takes a forward map beside ancestry; a world's composed order unions backward-ancestor AND forward-suffix edge sets (closure_of topo-closes the resulting DAG unchanged — the order algebra was already DAG-general); new fork_confluences map mirrors fork_ancestry; compose_canon_order (the single read-side order path) builds both, so every CLI/MCP report composes the suffix.

query_worlds excludes confluences from the per-world sweep (timeline-gaps / rule gate / payoff coverage / irony / edge-candidates / manuscript default) — a confluence is a structural merge, not a playthrough, and sweeping it as its own world would render a prefix-less fragment and surface false per-world findings (a suffix setup paying off in a parent middle would read as dangling there); query_world_lineages still keys EVERY branch (join_world indexes it by any fact's branch); explicit --world still renders a confluence fragment for inspection.

DEBT paid (R532): the mnemosyne-cli import-facts smoke asserted schema_version 22, but R532 bumped CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION 22->23 and ran only the atomic+validate suites (not the cli smoke) — the stale assertion is corrected to 23.
verification_bullets: BUILD green: cargo build --workspace clean (build-error-first; none).

TESTS green: full workspace suite — mnemosyne-validate 256 (incl. 3 new confluence tests), mnemosyne-atomic 184, every crate 0 failed; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --all --check clean.

new tests: confluence_suffix_visible_in_both_parents_after_merge (forward holds_at in both parents at the merge, NOT before, exclusive middles do not cross, main excluded); confluence_suffix_authored_once_renders_in_both_parent_manuscripts (the shared suffix renders in BOTH parent manuscripts authored once, no middle leak, dawn excluded from the default dump); confluence_conflict_scoping (the clean diamond scans without contradictions + a suffix-suffix conflict scopes to the confluence).

end-to-end CLI smoke (Harlow Mill diamond via import-facts + report-playthrough-manuscript --order, fresh binary): --world sluice = [tr-0,tr,sl,rk,rv], --world ride = [tr-0,tr,rd,rk,rv] — the shared f-reckon/f-river begin in BOTH parents (authored once on dawn); default dump worlds = [main,ride,sluice] (dawn excluded). Temp workspace removed.
carry_forward_bullets: NEXT (build step 3, R534): the per-parent "no unreconciled dependency across a confluence" gate — the R488/R522 le() reachability checked from EVERY incoming parent at its merge coordinate; cross-merge succession reconciliation (a suffix fact superseding a parent belief) becomes a bounded extension of supersedes_in_frame (today SuccessionCrossBranch rejects all cross-branch succession, and join_world returns None for a suffix-vs-parent-middle pair — the deliberate R533 deferral, not a regression: confluence is new substrate).

then step 4 (R535): re-run convergence-probe/v2 = the fork->join validation (the shared ending authored ONCE not 6 beats x2, the merge visible in report-fork-tree, the merge gate exercised, the 12->6 duplication tax confirmed removed).

the installed mnemosyne-cli is pre-R532 (schema 22 stamp; backward-compat harmless for the meta-store, which holds no narrative data); cargo install --path crates/mnemosyne-cli --force to refresh for CLI confluence authoring.

Round 534 — session review (owner-reques.decision_summary: Owner asked for a textbook session review of R533 (hack/smell-free? long-term-correct structure? North Star? SSOT?) — the R510 review pattern. Honest verdict: R533 is correct, tested, squarely on the North Star (convergence/JOIN = the L1 structural-join substrate the open-world AAA-narrative goal needs), and it IMPROVED SSOT — query_worlds extracted six duplicated per-world sweep constructions into one source, the holds_at/visibility predicate changed exactly once (R390), and the forward walk is single-sourced in core::forward_confluences mirroring fork_chain. ONE genuine structural smell: CanonOrder::from_declaration is a medium-agnostic order-closure algebra, but R533 passed it TWO maps (backward ancestry, forward confluence-suffixes) that it merged identically — leaking the narrative backward/forward distinction into the algebra, plus an include-all-vs-filter asymmetry between fork_ancestry and fork_confluences. Fixed: one generic composition map (world_order_composition), the direction distinction kept ONLY where it is load-bearing (Lineage, for visibility). The conflict-gate cross-merge gap is NOT a silent-fail (it is counted as cross_scope_pairs) but IS a momentary inconsistency with the new visibility — recorded as the explicit R535 reconciliation scope, not half-built here (the general case needs an In-set intersection). Verdict after the fix: textbook to the owner's bar; SSOT clean on data/logic; North Star confirmed.
changes_bullets: SMELL FIXED (layering / SSOT) — CanonOrder::from_declaration took TWO maps (backward `ancestry` + forward `forward`) that it merged IDENTICALLY, leaking the narrative backward/forward distinction into what should be a medium-agnostic order-closure algebra. Collapsed to a single generic `composition` param; the algebra never sees why a branch contributes edges, only that it does.

fork_ancestry + fork_confluences (both only ever fed from_declaration, one filtering empties and one not — an asymmetry) collapsed into ONE `world_order_composition(branches)` = per world, its backward fork ancestors ∪ forward confluence-suffixes. The backward/forward distinction now lives in exactly ONE place — `Lineage` (cut vs forward) — where it is load-bearing for visibility; nowhere else.

Behavior-preserving (omitting empty-contributor worlds is identical to the prior include-all: an empty composition closes to `base`, which `reach_for` already falls back to). Full suite + clippy + fmt + e2e CLI smoke re-run green; the R533 manuscript/holds_at results are byte-identical.
verification_bullets: REVIEW VERDICT: R533 is correct, tested, on-axis, and IMPROVED SSOT (query_worlds extracted the 6 duplicated per-world sweep constructions into one source; the holds_at/visibility predicate changed once per R390; forward primitive single-sourced in core::forward_confluences mirroring fork_chain). One real structural smell (the two-map from_declaration), now fixed.

TESTS: full workspace suite green (mnemosyne-validate 256, mnemosyne-atomic 184, every crate 0 failed); clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --all --check clean; e2e CLI smoke (Harlow Mill diamond) unchanged — sluice/ride both render the shared rk/rv once, default dump excludes the confluence.

VERIFIED no per-world sweep was missed: all six gate/surface sweeps use query_worlds; query_world_lineages still keys every branch for join_world. North Star: convergence/JOIN = the L1 structural-join substrate (R529 taxonomy) the open-world AAA-narrative goal needs — squarely on-axis.
carry_forward_bullets: FINDING recorded, NOT a silent-fail, fixed by R535 — a cross-merge CONFLICT pair (a confluence-suffix fact vs a parent-MIDDLE fact) is bucketed into `cross_scope_pairs` (surfaced + counted, never silently zero) by the recorded-conflict gate, because join_world still scopes by backward ancestry only; yet R533 visibility now places BOTH facts In the parent world, and the RULE gate (per-world visibility sweep) already gates such a pair. The two gates momentarily disagree on cross-merge pairs. This is exactly the R528-Q3 reconciliation scope.

NEXT (R535) = the per-parent confluence RECONCILIATION gate: generalize join_world so a suffix and a parent-middle share the parent world (the common case is clean; the sibling-confluence-common-parent case needs an In-set intersection); allow confluence-direction cross-branch SUCCESSION (SuccessionCrossBranch); add the per-parent le() dependency reachability gate at converges_from[i].at. Then R536 = re-run convergence-probe/v2 (12->6 tax confirmed removed). The merge-gate round shifted from R534->R535 because this review took R534.

NOTED acceptable (no action): forward_confluences rebuilds its converges_from reverse index per call -> O(branches^2) in order construction, but branch counts are small + STRUCTURAL (not per-scene), order construction is build-time, and a build-once-query-many API would complicate the clean single-world primitive (YAGNI); revisit only if branch counts ever explode.

Round 535 — convergence-JOIN build step.decision_summary: Round 535 — convergence-JOIN build step 3: the per-parent CONFLUENCE RECONCILIATION gate ("no unreconciled dependency across a confluence"), closing the R534-recorded cross-merge scope (the R528-Q3 reconciliation). THREE parts over the existing order algebra (no schema change — R532's converges_from suffices): (a) CONFLICT scoping — join_world generalized with a FORWARD case: when one fact is on a confluence the other's branch flows INTO (Lineage.forward), the JOIN world is the PARENT (the suffix is visible there), so a suffix-vs-parent-middle conflict co-holds and is caught — the pair R533/R534 left bucketed as cross_scope_pairs; the sibling-confluence-common-parent case stays unscoped (the deferred series-parallel generalization, surfaced not silent). (b) SUCCESSION reconciliation — cross-branch supersedes_in_frame is now legitimate in TWO inheritance directions via a SINGLE new core predicate mnemosyne_core::succession_branch_inherits (predecessor a backward fork-ancestor OR successor a forward confluence-suffix), called by BOTH enforcement points (the write-path check_succession_edge in mnemosyne-atomic AND the scan re-check in continuity.rs) so the multi-write-path invariant cannot drift (the two-write-path-one-invariant discipline; the prior backward-only write-path stayed stricter than the relaxed scan, which a failing test caught — the relaxation would have been dead code); bounded — it authorizes the edge, computes no merged state (no auto-merge engine). (c) the per-parent dependency GATE — the R488/R522 le() evidence-reachability, for a fact on a confluence, checked from EVERY incoming parent against the fact's own canon_from (which routes through that parent's merge coordinate in its composed order — NOT converges_from.at, so suffix-internal evidence reaches correctly), emitting a NEW ConfluenceEvidenceUnreconciled when evidence is reachable from only one parent; this ALSO fixes a latent false-positive (a confluence's own order is prefix-less, so the pre-R535 le(confluence, ...) wrongly flagged legitimate trunk/shared evidence). Byte-stable for pre-confluence stores (the non-confluence path is byte-identical to R522; forward sets are empty). VERIFIED: 4 new tests (the core predicate 4-direction parity unit test = the invariant SSOT; conflict-scopes-to-parent; succession reconcile-accepted/sibling-rejected; evidence reconciled-per-parent) + full suite (atomic 184 / validate 259 / workspace green) + clippy -D + fmt + live-store validate clean (281, byte-stable) + an end-to-end CLI smoke (a Harlow Mill diamond built via add-branch --converges; validate-continuity --json surfaced exactly the one confluence_evidence_unreconciled with parent=ride, cross_scope_pairs=0). NEXT (R536) = re-run convergence-probe/v2 (the fork->join validation: the shared ending authored ONCE, the merge gate exercised, the R531 12->6 duplication tax confirmed removed).

The v1 forest forced 12 facts of 2x duplication for the 6-beat shared ending (R531, OUTCOME-1); v2 authored the shared dawn ending ONCE — 5 facts on a single 'dawn' confluence (converges_from sluice@sc-11a + ride@sc-11b), 0 shared-ending facts duplicated. ALL THREE pre-committed pins HOLD: PIN-1 tax removed (5 facts once, not 12); PIN-2 fidelity+visible (validate-continuity 0/0 with cross_scope_pairs=0, report-fork-tree shows the DIAMOND, both parents' manuscripts carry the SAME shared-suffix fact-ids once with 0 unplaced, terminal payoff dangling=0 / substantiation 0 / timeline 0); PIN-3 gate correct (clean on the well-formed store AND firing confluence_evidence_unreconciled {fact f-301, parent ride, evidence sc-09a} on a one-parent-only negative control — non-vacuous, not a false-positive machine). M5: the blind author found the confluence cleanly natural, no duplication forced (the exact inverse of v1's 'duplication is unavoidable'), using the confluence corollary as designed (path-specific reckoning content on the FORK branches at the shared scene; confluence evidence on the shared spine/ending only) so the R535 gate never falsely fired. DECISION = OUTCOME-A: the convergence-JOIN build (R532-R535) is VALIDATED, the fork->join loop CLOSED end to end, the convergence-JOIN arc DONE; the next frontier is breadth (R529's one binding bottleneck) — the NPC-breadth probe / the SCXML-seam + L3 concurrency scope rounds / v3 A-1/A-4. SSOT = v2/report.md + this entry.

Round 538 — explicit CLI path overrides.decision_summary: Round 538 — explicit CLI path overrides resolve CWD-relative (the textbook fix surfaced by R537's de-risk; foundation primitive, owner-requested cost-no-object review). The convergence-probe/v2 de-risk surfaced that a relative --sidecar / --order CLI override resolved against the WORKSPACE ROOT (cascade::resolve_sidecar joined the anchor = the discovered root the CLI feeds via workspace_anchor), NOT the CWD — so an explicit relative --sidecar from a subdirectory silently planted a store at the repo root, AND the code VIOLATED its own doc comment ('a relative override joins the dir the command was invoked against' = CWD). The textbook root-cause fix (the R510/R534 review bar): an EXPLICIT CLI path argument is CWD-relative (the universal CLI convention, least surprise — like --manifest and every standard tool), while a config-DECLARED path ([atomic] sidecar_path / [continuity] canon_order_path) stays workspace-root-relative (a project-level declaration). SSOT restored: ONE new pure rule resolve_explicit_cli_path(cwd, raw) shared by BOTH --sidecar (resolve_sidecar) and --order (resolve_canon_order_file) so the two cannot drift to different anchors; a functional-core / imperative-shell split (the pure resolve_sidecar_in + resolve_explicit_cli_path are unit-tested, the thin resolve_sidecar / resolve_canon_order_file shells inject the process CWD). Blast radius = 0 regression (no hook/script passes an explicit relative --sidecar/--order; repo-root invocations have CWD == workspace_root, identical), verified by full suite + clippy -D + fmt + an e2e smoke (a relative --sidecar from a subdir now lands in the CWD, repo-root pollution gone). On-axis: a mutate-API-safety / tool-ergonomics fix that also removes the absolute-path crutch the R537 author-brief had to carry.
changes_bullets: cascade.rs: new pure resolve_explicit_cli_path(cwd, raw) = the single explicit-CLI-path rule (relative -> CWD, absolute -> verbatim); resolve_sidecar split into a CWD-injecting shell + the pure testable resolve_sidecar_in; the explicit override is now CWD-relative, the config/default branches still workspace-root

ops/lib.rs: resolve_canon_order_file's --order override now CWD-relative via the same shared rule (the config-declared canon_order_path stays workspace-root-relative; the sha pin is still bypassed on an explicit override, unchanged)
atomic_cli.rs: 2 CLI resolve_sidecar tests realigned to the CWD-relative contract (the override still wins over config; the malformed-config short-circuit still holds)
verification_bullets: 3 new/updated unit tests: resolve_explicit_cli_path (relative->cwd / absolute->verbatim), resolve_sidecar_in explicit override = cwd-relative + not-under-anchor + absolute-passthrough, and the 2 CLI tests realigned
full workspace test suite green, clippy --release --all-targets -D clean, cargo fmt clean

blast radius audited = 0 regression: no hook/script passes an explicit relative --sidecar/--order (all use the config default or run from the repo root where CWD == workspace_root, identical behavior); only the subdir-explicit-relative case changes = exactly the footgun, now correct
carry_forward_bullets: the R537 convergence-probe/v2 author-brief 'use absolute paths' guidance is no longer needed for future authoring runs (a relative --sidecar/--order now resolves CWD-relative); the R537 brief is left UNEDITED as a frozen executed-experiment record

Round 538 — explicit CLI path overrides.publishable_decision_summary: Round 538 — explicit CLI path overrides resolve CWD-relative (the textbook fix surfaced by R537's de-risk; foundation primitive, owner-requested cost-no-object review). The convergence-probe/v2 de-risk surfaced that a relative --sidecar / --order CLI override resolved against the WORKSPACE ROOT (cascade::resolve_sidecar joined the anchor = the discovered root the CLI feeds via workspace_anchor), NOT the CWD — so an explicit relative --sidecar from a subdirectory silently planted a store at the repo root, AND the code VIOLATED its own doc comment ('a relative override joins the dir the command was invoked against' = CWD). The textbook root-cause fix (the R510/R534 review bar): an EXPLICIT CLI path argument is CWD-relative (the universal CLI convention, least surprise — like --manifest and every standard tool), while a config-DECLARED path ([atomic] sidecar_path / [continuity] canon_order_path) stays workspace-root-relative (a project-level declaration). SSOT restored: ONE new pure rule resolve_explicit_cli_path(cwd, raw) shared by BOTH --sidecar (resolve_sidecar) and --order (resolve_canon_order_file) so the two cannot drift to different anchors; a functional-core / imperative-shell split (the pure resolve_sidecar_in + resolve_explicit_cli_path are unit-tested, the thin resolve_sidecar / resolve_canon_order_file shells inject the process CWD). Blast radius = 0 regression (no hook/script passes an explicit relative --sidecar/--order; repo-root invocations have CWD == workspace_root, identical), verified by full suite + clippy -D + fmt + an e2e smoke (a relative --sidecar from a subdir now lands in the CWD, repo-root pollution gone). On-axis: a mutate-API-safety / tool-ergonomics fix that also removes the absolute-path crutch the R537 author-brief had to carry.
publishable_changes_bullets: cascade.rs: new pure resolve_explicit_cli_path(cwd, raw) = the single explicit-CLI-path rule (relative -> CWD, absolute -> verbatim); resolve_sidecar split into a CWD-injecting shell + the pure testable resolve_sidecar_in; the explicit override is now CWD-relative, the config/default branches still workspace-root

ops/lib.rs: resolve_canon_order_file's --order override now CWD-relative via the same shared rule (the config-declared canon_order_path stays workspace-root-relative; the sha pin is still bypassed on an explicit override, unchanged)
atomic_cli.rs: 2 CLI resolve_sidecar tests realigned to the CWD-relative contract (the override still wins over config; the malformed-config short-circuit still holds)
publishable_verification_bullets: 3 new/updated unit tests: resolve_explicit_cli_path (relative->cwd / absolute->verbatim), resolve_sidecar_in explicit override = cwd-relative + not-under-anchor + absolute-passthrough, and the 2 CLI tests realigned
full workspace test suite green, clippy --release --all-targets -D clean, cargo fmt clean
e2e smoke: import-sections with a relative --sidecar from a subdirectory resolved the store INTO that subdir (CWD), not the repo root — the R537 footgun fixed, no repo-root pollution

blast radius audited = 0 regression: no hook/script passes an explicit relative --sidecar/--order (all use the config default or run from the repo root where CWD == workspace_root, identical behavior); only the subdir-explicit-relative case changes = exactly the footgun, now correct

NEXT (unchanged) = the BREADTH frontier (the R529 one binding bottleneck): NPC-breadth probe / SCXML-seam + L3 concurrency scope / v3 A-1/A-4 — owner cadence; authoring probes are EXPERIMENT-gated on the owner word

Round 539 — NPC-breadth frontier DESIGNE.decision_summary: A code-0 design round scoping the R529 finding-#9 NPC-breadth probe — the adjacent untested axis of the one binding bottleneck (AI self-authoring at scale + BREADTH + coherence; R524 proved 70-scene DEPTH, breadth is untested). Refute-first (the R527/R528 discipline) confirms the substrate is SUFFICIENT, not a structural gap: per-NPC personality = Frame.description + Entity{kind:character} + a render-brief voice property (the R513/R514 warm-render posture, blind-judged, NOT substrate); per-NPC knowledge = the frames registry (a frame per NPC) + report-frame-view --frame F --branch B --entity E --at S (frame_view continuity.rs:2268 — the code already names this the NPC-context query frame x branch x entity at T, three-state honest, R390 single-predicate-with-the-gate); breadth scaling = the frames BTreeMap expresses N frames by construction (like N branches); within-frame consistency = the continuity gates, cross-frame belief/truth divergence = DATA not conflict (R519/R520). The ONE genuine gap (grep-confirmed empty in continuity.rs): NPC knowledge ACQUISITION is unchecked — a frame may assert a fact with any canon_from regardless of a scene-path to learn it (the per-NPC analog of the R502 disclosure leak + the R488 off-branch footgun); at a broad cast = the omniscience-leak risk. Per R518/R529 YAGNI it is DESIGNED-not-built (a structural-minimum acquisition anchor, partly the R476 ceiling), the experiment decides the pull. Designed ai-npc-breadth-experiment/v1 isolating BREADTH from depth: a blind author given premise-only authors a frame-per-NPC base via its own write-gate-read-repair loop; PIN-B1 = convergence to gate-clean (the R519/R524 PIN-A1), PIN-B2 = the breadth floor (>=N distinct NPC frames each holding >=1 fact at its key scene, every NPC frame-view non-empty + three-state honest, 0 within-frame contradiction, + the PIN-A2 structural floor); judged (no pin, 3 blind judges) = per-NPC knowledge coherence (the acquisition question, judge-only = the R476 ceiling) + cast distinctness (frames vs palette-swaps) + breadth-vs-depth spine integrity; pre-committed decision rule (pins-hold+coherent -> breadth REACHES -> combine breadth x depth or build the acquisition gate on a measured pull; pins-hold+incoherent -> breadth ceiling real -> build the designed acquisition gate then re-run; PIN-B1 fails -> diagnose). Contamination bound R469; manifest sha-pinned at execution (R503/R514); EXECUTION is the experiment gate. No build, no decision committed beyond the design.
changes_bullets: (sec 7.32) Scoped the R529 finding-#9 NPC-breadth probe = the adjacent untested axis of the one binding bottleneck (AI self-authoring at scale + BREADTH + coherence); R524 proved 70-scene DEPTH at ~3 frames, breadth = a broad cast of NPC knowledge-frames, untested.

Refute-first substrate sufficiency (the R527/R528 discipline): per-NPC PERSONALITY = Frame.description (whose epistemic frame) + Entity{kind:character} + a render-brief VOICE property (the R513/R514 warm-render posture, blind-judged, NO substrate); per-NPC KNOWLEDGE = the frames registry (a frame per NPC) + report-frame-view --frame F --branch B --entity E --at S (frame_view, continuity.rs:2268 — the code already NAMES this "the NPC-context query is frame x branch x entity at T", three-state honest, R390 single-predicate-with-the-gate); BREADTH scaling = the frames BTreeMap expresses N frames by construction (like N branches); WITHIN-frame consistency = the continuity gates, cross-frame belief/truth divergence = DATA not conflict (the R519/R520 handling). SUBSTRATE SUFFICIENT — the test is an EXPERIMENT not a build (the R518/R524 shape, NOT the R527 structural gap).

The ONE genuine gap (grep-confirmed empty in continuity.rs — no acquir/learns/witness check): NPC knowledge ACQUISITION is unchecked — a frame may ASSERT a fact with any canon_from regardless of a scene-path to LEARN it (the per-NPC analog of the R502 disclosure leak + the R488 off-branch footgun); at 3-frame depth it rarely bites, at a broad cast it is the omniscience-leak risk. Per R518/R529 YAGNI: DESIGNED-not-built (a structural-minimum acquisition anchor; the general "could-NPC-know-X" is partly the R476 commonsense ceiling); the experiment decides the pull.

Designed ai-npc-breadth-experiment/v1, isolating BREADTH from depth (the R518/R519 shape): a blind author, given premise-only (a premise that DEMANDS a broad cast — many stakeholders each with private knowledge + agenda) + the mutate vocabulary (frames/entities/facts/branches, no facts), authors a frame-per-NPC base via its OWN write->gate->read->repair loop.

PINS (deterministic, orchestrator-run, independent of the author's claim): PIN-B1 = convergence to gate-clean within budget (the R519/R524 PIN-A1, incl. the R522/R535 gates if used); PIN-B2 = the BREADTH FLOOR — >= N distinct NPC frames each HOLDING >= 1 fact at its key scene (a real cast, not 2 heads + a crowd), every NPC frame-view at its scene non-empty + three-state honest (no mis-scoped-branch unknown leakage), 0 within-frame contradiction, + the PIN-A2 structural floor (0 dangling payoff / 0 off-branch / fork-tree clean).

JUDGED (no pin, 3 blind judges, R500-family 5-axis rubric adapted to breadth): per-NPC knowledge COHERENCE (does each NPC know what they plausibly should AND not what they shouldn't — the acquisition question, judge-only = the R476 ceiling); cast DISTINCTNESS (differentiated frames vs palette-swaps); breadth-vs-depth SPINE integrity (did breadth degrade spine coherence — the scale-floor risk at a new axis).

DECISION RULE (pre-committed): pins-hold + coherent broad cast -> AI-self-authoring REACHES breadth -> next = breadth x depth combined OR build the acquisition gate on a MEASURED pull; pins-hold + INCOHERENT (judges flag omniscience leaks / palette-swap cast) -> the breadth ceiling is real -> build the designed acquisition gate then re-run; PIN-B1 fails -> convergence doesn't scale to breadth -> diagnose. Contamination bound R469 (this lineage writes manifest/premise/briefs + runs gates, authors no fact, judges nothing); manifest sha-pinned at execution (R503/R514); EXECUTION is the experiment gate.
verification_bullets: Code 0 (a DESIGN round, the R496/R499/R518 pattern); no build, no test, no schema change — the substrate was confirmed sufficient by reading the existing surfaces, not by writing new ones.

Grounding verified in code: Frame (narrative.rs:53) + Entity (narrative.rs:234) registries; frame_view (continuity.rs:2268) = the frame x branch x entity-at-T NPC-context query, three-state honest (holding / not_holding / unknown), routed through the SAME holds_at the continuity gate uses (R390); report-frame-view CLI (main.rs:2016) + the --entity dossier; frames is a BTreeMap (N-frame by construction).

The gap confirmed by ABSENCE: grep for acquir/learns/knew/overhear/witness in continuity.rs = empty -> no NPC-knowledge-acquisition gate exists today, so the omniscience-leak risk is genuinely untested.

Depth baseline measured: the tracked dogfood bases carry 3 frames (v3/authored.atomic.json = 3 frames / 12 entities / 52 facts) -> "breadth" = scaling the FRAME count, a distinct untested axis from R524's scene/branch DEPTH.

Round 540 — ai-npc-breadth-experiment/v1.decision_summary: The manifest round for the R539-designed NPC-breadth probe (the R530/R536 manifest-round pattern; code 0, no build). A blind author will SELF-AUTHOR, from a premise only, a story with a BROAD CAST — >=8 distinct people each a knowledge-frame holding what THAT person knows/believes, several wrong about each other — on the current forest substrate, and the orchestrator measures whether the AI can keep a broad individuated cast coherent without an enforcing knowledge-acquisition gate. Scene count held moderate (~22-30, far below R524's 70) so the variable is cast BREADTH not depth; forest-only so breadth is isolated from the just-validated convergence-JOIN axis. Fresh original premise The Lantern House at Corvath Ford (a flood strands a dozen strangers in a river waystation around an inciting event known differently by nearly everyone). The author-brief = the R521/R524 top-down contract + the R522 backreference rule + the R526 sec-7.28 precondition clause + the breadth move (a frame per person). TWO deterministic pins: PIN-B1 convergence to gate-clean, PIN-B2 the breadth floor (>=8 populated person-frames + the structural floor). JUDGED (no pin, 3 blind judges): coherence / completeness / knowledge-realism (per-NPC acquisition) / cast-distinctness / branch-integrity / overall. Pre-committed decision rule: pins-hold+coherent -> breadth REACHES; pins-hold+omniscience-leaks/palette-swap -> breadth ceiling real -> build the R539-designed acquisition gate (the R488/R522 le()/evidence family) then re-run; PIN-B1 fails -> tooling friction; PIN-B2 fails -> cast collapses toward depth-with-bystanders. manifest sha256 f1f07bfd…6bde pinned BEFORE execution (the R503/R514 pre-commit integrity); tool de-risked on a throwaway 5-frame diamond. Contamination bound R469 (10th use): this lineage authored the design/manifest/briefs so it may NOT author a fact or judge — execution = separate firewalled subagents (R541).

The author-brief = the R521/R524 TOP-DOWN contract + the R522 structural-backreference rule + the R526 sec-7.28 precondition clause + the BREADTH move (a frame per significant person, each holding only their own road's knowledge — storytelling guidance, NOT the pin thresholds / tested variable = no teaching-to-test).

TWO deterministic pins (orchestrator-run, R469 firewall): PIN-B1 convergence to gate-clean (import + validate-continuity incl R522/R488 + manuscript placement + timeline); PIN-B2 breadth floor (>=8 populated person-frames via report-frame-view + the structural floor: payoff-coverage / fork-tree 3-children / off-branch all 0).

JUDGED (no pin, 3 blind judges, the breadth rubric): causal coherence / completeness / KNOWLEDGE REALISM (per-NPC acquisition coherence) / CAST DISTINCTNESS / branch integrity / overall — the per-world manuscripts are frame-labelled so who-knows-what is readable.

Pre-committed decision rule: pins-hold + coherent broad cast -> breadth REACHES; pins-hold + omniscience-leaks/palette-swap cast -> breadth ceiling real -> build the R539-designed NPC-knowledge-acquisition gate (the R488/R522 le()/evidence family) then re-run; PIN-B1 fails -> tooling friction; PIN-B2 fails -> cast collapses toward depth-with-bystanders.

Tool de-risked (the R536 precedent): a throwaway 5-frame schema-23 diamond proved import + the gates + the per-NPC report-frame-view dossier; the R538 relative-from-run/author incantation + schema 23 folded into the brief; mnemosyne-cli reinstalled to R539-head.

The author-brief = the R521/R524 TOP-DOWN contract + the R522 structural-backreference rule + the R526 sec-7.28 precondition clause + the BREADTH move (a frame per significant person, each holding only their own road's knowledge — storytelling guidance, NOT the pin thresholds / tested variable = no teaching-to-test).

TWO deterministic pins (orchestrator-run, R469 firewall): PIN-B1 convergence to gate-clean (import + validate-continuity incl R522/R488 + manuscript placement + timeline); PIN-B2 breadth floor (>=8 populated person-frames via report-frame-view + the structural floor: payoff-coverage / fork-tree 3-children / off-branch all 0).

JUDGED (no pin, 3 blind judges, the breadth rubric): causal coherence / completeness / KNOWLEDGE REALISM (per-NPC acquisition coherence) / CAST DISTINCTNESS / branch integrity / overall — the per-world manuscripts are frame-labelled so who-knows-what is readable.

Pre-committed decision rule: pins-hold + coherent broad cast -> breadth REACHES; pins-hold + omniscience-leaks/palette-swap cast -> breadth ceiling real -> build the R539-designed NPC-knowledge-acquisition gate (the R488/R522 le()/evidence family) then re-run; PIN-B1 fails -> tooling friction; PIN-B2 fails -> cast collapses toward depth-with-bystanders.

Round 541 — ai-npc-breadth-experiment/v1.decision_summary: The R540 manifest's pre-committed rule fires (branch 1). A blind author (R469 bound, given only the premise + the breadth author-brief, no hypothesis) SELF-AUTHORED a 29-scene / 80-fact / 3-world-line crowded-house mystery with 12 distinct person-frames (+ the gt frame) from the premise only, in 2 write-gate-repair iterations. The orchestrator rebuilt the store FRESH from the author's sections+facts+order JSON (independent of the author's store) and BOTH PINS HOLD: PIN-B1 convergence (validate-continuity reject 0/0 incl R522 evidence-reachability + R488 off-branch; fork-tree 3 worlds 0 unplaced all-terminal; manuscripts unplaced=0/undecidable=0; timeline 0) and PIN-B2 breadth floor (12/12 person-frames populated, far above 8, each report-frame-view non-empty three-state-honest) + structural floor (payoff dangling=0 in every world, 0 unsubstantiated, 0 off-branch). 3 BLIND JUDGES scored UNANIMOUS 5/5 overall + 5/5 knowledge-realism + 5/5 cast-distinctness; the omniscience-leak risk (the unenforced breadth axis under test) did NOT materialize — all three independently named the individuated cast the strength (no one holds a fact from a world-line they are not on). DECISION: AI-self-authoring REACHES breadth; the R524 depth result extends to a broad individuated cast. The lone blemish (all 3 judges, the same one) = HOLD sc-32 bond-leaf provenance, a missing-precondition COMPLETENESS slip (the R476 ceiling / R526 case-1 type, NOT a knowledge break) — the R526 sec-7.28 clause reduced but did not eliminate the recurring missing-setup, as at R524. The designed NPC-knowledge-acquisition gate (R539) stays YAGNI-deferred (no pull; knowledge 5/5/5; it would not catch a missing-setup case anyway). SSOT = v1/report.md + this entry.

DECISION (pre-committed rule, branch 1): both pins hold + coherent broad cast => AI-self-authoring REACHES breadth; the R524 DEPTH result extends to a broad, individuated cast at moderate length.

Round 542 — the prose-ghost precondition.decision_summary: A code-0 design/refutation round (the R518/R496 refute-first pattern) formalizing the owner-dialogue conclusion to the R541 sc-32 hole. The hole-class (R541 sc-32 + R524 act-dig): a load-bearing object/state existing ONLY in a fact's free-text claim, never a registered node — the deterministic gates pass because every DECLARED link is valid and the ghost is not a node, so nothing dangles. Diagnosis: soft rules are ignorable (R526 sec-7.28 was ignored at R541), only hard gates count, and a hard gate can read only structure not prose (AI-out-of-the-gate). Refute-first on the REAL R541 base REFUTES the cheap gate (force a typed claim at payoff facts + ground the typed-object): 11 of 14 payoff facts are legitimately prose-only (79% false-positive flood, the R526 noise reconfirmed on data), AND f-hold-04 is structurally identical to a legitimate reveal (entities [wend, deed_bond] both real), so a forced-typed author evades by naming the real grounded entity and the ghost relocates to a POSSESSION error structure cannot see. The hole-class's true nature is a possession/state error (the bond in the wrong hands on this branch), whose deterministic catch needs per-branch possession/location state + predicate exclusivity = WORLD-STATE SIMULATION = the R527 'world-state recorded-not-simulated' axis = a large axis, not a small gate. DECISION: the cheap prose-ghost gate REFUTED (do not build, noisy + evadable); the real lever deferred to its own R527 world-state scope round behind real pull (n=2 is not it); for now the blind judge is the floor's backstop (caught sc-32 3/3) and the R526 soft clause stays as incidence-reducing guidance. The deliverable = naming, on data, the IRREDUCIBLE residue of 'store-consistency != causal coherence' that no AI-out-of-gate check removes without world-state simulation — the true boundary of the deterministic floor. SSOT = design-doc sec 7.33 + this entry.
changes_bullets: Formalized the owner-dialogue conclusion to the R541 sc-32 hole as a design round (code 0, the R518/R496 refute-first pattern). Hole-class = a load-bearing object/state living ONLY in a fact's free-text claim, never a registered node (R541 sc-32 + R524 act-dig); the deterministic gates pass because every DECLARED link is valid (f-hold-04 entities [wend, deed_bond] both real, evidence reachable, pays_off exists) and the ghost ("the copy") is not a node, so nothing dangles.

Diagnosis formalized: SOFT rules are ignorable (the R526 sec-7.28 precondition clause was a brief instruction and the R541 author ignored it); only HARD gate-enforced rules count; a hard gate can read only STRUCTURE, never prose (reading the claim to find the ghost = AI-in-the-gate = the banned invariant) — so the question is the MAX deterministic forcing + the honest residue.

REFUTE-FIRST on the REAL R541 80-fact base: the candidate gate (scope to payoff facts, force a TYPED claim, ground the typed-object via the R488/R522 le() family) is REFUTED on data — 14 facts have pays_off but only 3 are typed, 11 are LEGITIMATELY prose-only (e.g. f-wend-13 "Wend learns the bond is on Cray", referencing the real grounded deed_bond) = a 79% false-positive flood (the R526 entity-first-appearance noise, reconfirmed on data).

Worse, EVADABLE: f-hold-04 is structurally IDENTICAL to a legitimate reveal (entities [wend, deed_bond], both registered), so even forced to type, a non-adversarial author types the action with the real grounded entity (deed_bond exists earlier) -> grounding PASSES; the ghost merely relocates from "missing node" to "wrong possession" (the bond is in Cray's hands on HOLD, not Wend's) — which structure-only cannot see. A narrower variant (state-changing predicates at terminal scenes) cuts noise but keeps the evasion; marginal, not worth a build.

The hole-class's TRUE nature = a POSSESSION/STATE error (a load-bearing object in the wrong hands on this branch), NOT a missing-node error -> the deterministic catch needs per-branch object possession/location state + predicate EXCLUSIVITY = WORLD-STATE SIMULATION = exactly the R527 "world-state recorded-not-simulated" open-world gap = a LARGE axis (new state model + cardinality-constrained predicates), not a small gate.

DECISION (pre-committed): the cheap prose-ghost gate is REFUTED — do NOT build (noisy + evadable; the refutation is the value, the R518 discipline). The real lever (possession/location state + predicate exclusivity) is DEFERRED to its own scope round under the R527 world-state axis, behind real pull (recurring possession errors; n=2 is not it). For now the blind judge is the floor's backstop (it caught sc-32 3/3 — the full author->gate->judge loop already detects this class, just not by the gate alone); the R526 sec-7.28 SOFT clause stays as authoring guidance (reduces incidence even though it cannot guarantee).

North-Star honest read (the deliverable): "store-consistency != causal coherence" has an IRREDUCIBLE residue that no AI-out-of-the-gate check removes without full world-state simulation = the true boundary of the deterministic floor; naming it precisely, on data, is worth more than a noisy gate that pretends the boundary is elsewhere.
verification_bullets: Code 0 (a design/refutation round, the R518/R496 pattern); no build, no test, no schema change — the conclusion is the refutation + the named boundary.

Refutation grounded in the COMMITTED R541 base (claudedocs/phase1-ai-npc-breadth-experiment/v1/run/author/facts.json): 14 facts carry pays_off, 11 are prose-only (untyped) and legitimate -> the "force typed at payoffs" gate would false-positive on 79%; f-hold-04 carries entities [wend, deed_bond] both registered = structurally indistinguishable from a legitimate reveal, so a structure-only gate cannot pick the ghost out, and a forced-typed author can evade by naming the real grounded entity.

For the next AUTHORING run: keep the R526 sec-7.28 SOFT clause (incidence-reducing) AND rely on the blind judge as the backstop for prose-ghost / possession errors (the gate cannot catch them deterministically — the named floor boundary).

Round 543 — the breadth × depth combined.decision_summary: A code-0 design round (the R518/R496 design-round pattern) scoping the combined-axis acceptance test the owner chose at the R543 scope-fork (5 candidates). Both halves of the ONE binding bottleneck R529's through-line names — AI self-authoring at scale + BREADTH + coherence — are now proven REACHABLE but SEPARATELY: R524 a 70-scene / 7-branch DEPTH base (judged coherent; prose-first LOST at ~60), R541 a 29-scene / 12-frame BREADTH cast (both pins held; 3 blind judges 5/5 knowledge-realism + cast-distinctness). The untested question is whether holding BOTH in one base degrades either — the closest approach yet to AAA scale. REFUTE-FIRST (the R527/R528 discipline): combining is the additive CROSS-PRODUCT of two already-supported axes, NOT a new structural primitive — frame_view is ALREADY frame × branch × entity at T (continuity.rs:2268), the continuity gates run per-frame AND per-branch, the frames BTreeMap is N-frame by construction, and the convergence build (R532-R537, converges_from / forward_confluences) already expresses the world-line JOIN a broad + deep plot tends to want. SUBSTRATE SUFFICIENT — the test is an EXPERIMENT not a build (the R518/R524 shape, unlike R527's structural gap). Designed ai-breadth-depth-experiment/v1: a blind author given premise-only (a premise DEMANDING BOTH a consequential branching spine AND a broad private-knowledge cast) + the FULL proven mutate vocabulary (fork AND confluence as ordinary tools, the R536 posture) authors via its OWN write-gate-read-repair loop. THREE deterministic PINs (orchestrator rebuilt fresh from the author's JSON): PIN-D1 gate-clean convergence (the R519/R524 PIN-A1 + R522/R488/R535 + the R541 PIN-B1); PIN-D2 the DEPTH floor (at least 60 scenes, at least 3 branches + 3 terminal world-lines, the R524 PIN-A2 structural floor — 60 beats the prose-first scale-floor); PIN-D3 the BREADTH floor (at least 10 distinct NPC frames each holding a fact, every report-frame-view non-empty + three-state honest, 0 within-frame contradiction, the R541 PIN-B2). JUDGED (no pin, 3 blind judges): coherence at COMBINED scale, cast distinctness when the plot is ALSO deep, branch-spine integrity when the cast is ALSO broad — the cross-tension is the real question (the R476 ceiling). PRE-COMMITTED DECISION RULE + a pull-map: each combined-scale failure mode points to the deferred lever it would pull — omniscience leak to the R539-designed acquisition gate; convergence-duplication-at-scale to the R528 series-parallel lattice; possession / prose-ghost error (the R542 sc-32 class) to the R527 world-state axis; if none appears (R541-style clean) all three stay YAGNI-deferred. Contamination bound R469 (this lineage writes manifest/briefs + runs gates + assembles; authors no fact, judges nothing); manifest sha-pinned at execution (R503/R514). Build NOT taken; no decision committed beyond the design.

Fresh original premise "The Ember Winter at Grayloam" (NOT reused) — engineered to DEMAND both axes AT ONCE: a long winter seals an upland iron-and-charcoal settlement, the ironmaster (charter + accounts + cellar key + everyone's secrets) is dead/vanished as the pass closes, and the undermaster Coll must get the snowed-in settlement through the winter AND work out what happened, facing SEVERAL consequential decisions across the dark months. DEPTH (a season-long arc, 60+ scenes, 3+ sequential/nested forks, 3+ distinct terminal endings) AND BREADTH (10+ individuated person-frames sustained across the whole arc). The author-brief composes the proven contracts: R521/R524 top-down skeleton-first, R522 structural backreference, R526 sec-7.28 precondition-per-reveal, R539 frame-per-person, R536 confluence-as-ordinary-tool (available, NOT required — a deep+broad plot may want a JOIN; whether the author reaches for it is a RECORDED metric).

THREE deterministic PINs (orchestrator rebuilds fresh from the author's JSON, independent of the author's store): PIN-D1 gate-clean convergence (continuity 0/0 incl R522/R488/R535, manuscripts 0-unplaced, timeline 0); PIN-D2 the DEPTH floor (60+ scenes / 3+ branches / 3+ terminals / 2+ forks placed / 3+ payoffs / 0 dangling / 0 off-branch); PIN-D3 the BREADTH floor (10+ person-frames each holding >=1 fact, every report-frame-view non-empty three-state honest, 0 within-frame contradiction). JUDGED (no pin, 3 blind judges): coherence / completeness / knowledge-realism / cast-distinctness / branch-integrity at COMBINED scale — the depth-vs-breadth cross-tension (depth diluting the cast / breadth flattening the branches) is the real question.

Pre-committed decision rule + a pull-map: all-pins-hold + coherent (overall + knowledge + cast + branch all >=4 majority) -> AI self-authoring SCALES on the combined axis (the R529 binding bottleneck met near AAA scale); pins-hold-but-incoherent -> build the lever the dominant failure mode names (omniscience leak -> the R539 acquisition gate; duplication-at-scale -> the R528 series-parallel lattice; possession/prose-ghost error of the R542 class -> the R527 world-state axis); PIN-D1 fails -> tooling friction at combined scale; PIN-D2/D3 fails -> one axis collapsed the other (the headline combined finding). Round 545 — ai-breadth-depth-experiment/.decision_summary: The R544 manifest's pre-committed decision rule fires (all_pins_hold_and_coherent). A blind author (R469 bound, given premise-only + the mutate vocabulary, blind to pins/rubric/cross-tension) authored "The Ember Winter at Grayloam" — a 95-scene / 4-terminal / 12-person-frame deep-AND-broad winter mystery (a secretly-dying guilty ironmaster doses himself + forges the ledger to frame the innocent collier; 3 sequential forks at sc-20/35/48) from a premise only in 3 write-gate-repair iterations; convergence available but unused (a natural forest). The orchestrator rebuilt the store FRESH from the author's JSON (independent of the author's store) and ALL THREE deterministic pins HOLD: PIN-D1 gate-clean (continuity 0/0 incl R522/R488, every world-line unplaced=0, timeline 0); PIN-D2 DEPTH floor (95 scenes >=60, 6 branches, 3 forks placed, all 4 terminals dangling=0, 5+ payoffs, 0 off-branch); PIN-D3 BREADTH floor (12 person-frames >=10 each holding 2-15, frame-views non-empty three-state honest [the acquisition boundary visible: coll's death-knowledge grows sc-86->sc-90], 0 within-frame contradiction). Both floors held SIMULTANEOUSLY. 3 fresh blind judges scored UNANIMOUS 5/5 overall + 5/5 coherence + 5/5 KNOWLEDGE-REALISM (0 knowledge breaks, all three named the frame-scoped knowledge architecture the biggest STRENGTH — the feared omniscience-leak did NOT appear at 95x12x4) + 5/5 BRANCH-INTEGRITY (breadth did not flatten the four distinct motivated endings) + 4/4/5 cast-distinctness (majority 4) + 5/4/4 completeness (majority 4). DECISION = AI SELF-AUTHORING SCALES ON THE COMBINED AXIS — the R529 binding bottleneck (scale + breadth + coherence) is met near AAA scale on depth (R524), breadth (R541), AND both together. THE HONEST BLEMISH (all 3 judges, same finding, NOT downplayed): the broad cast is front-loaded — vivid in the shared trunk, it THINS to principals in the deep branch tails after the midwinter forks, and several eyewitness setups pay off on only some roads (soldier/prospector close only in village, child only in ration, the prospectors' ore thread + the bonded servant dangle in law/settle); breadth proved HARDER TO SUSTAIN across a long deep arc than to establish = the measured texture of the combined ceiling (the R476/R526-clause completeness family, not a coherence/knowledge break; the lone fully-sustained road village drew cast 5/5/5). NO deferred lever pulled (R541-style clean): no omniscience leak (R539 acquisition gate stays YAGNI), no convergence-duplication (R528 lattice deferred), no possession error (R527 world-state deferred); the cast-sustainment slip is a craft/authoring-brief observation (the next run's brief asks the cast to stay active into the tails, the R526-clause register), not a gate lever. SSOT = v1/report.md + the R544/R545 ledgers.

Breadth proved HARDER TO SUSTAIN across a long deep arc than to ESTABLISH = the measured texture of the combined ceiling (the R476/R526-clause completeness family, NOT a coherence/knowledge break; the lone fully-sustained road `village` drew cast 5/5/5, proving the author CAN sustain it but did not on all four).

Round 546 — SCXML seam + L3 concurrency.decision_summary: SCXML seam + L3 concurrency SCOPED (the owner-chosen R546 axis-3, code 0, the R518/R527 scope-a-frontier pattern = the future scope round R528 Q5 explicitly deferred). HALF A (the facts→SCXML seam, R529 #2) REFUTED as a substrate gap: fork_tree (continuity.rs:3267) already emits the full state-machine topology (world-lines=states, fork edges=transitions+choice labels, converges=JOIN, is_maximal=final, linearize=beat sequence), ARCHITECTURE.md frames SCXML as a projection of the one fact substrate + §6 invariant 4 forbids SCXML in Layer 0 (adapter-only, the report-* / R451 precedent), and the SCE-UC-1 ledger-vs-verified dual is the R529 #2 option-A/B boundary already in production; the real open seam = the declarative-truth / executable-behavior line, mapped onto SCXML's own element split (declarative topology = codegen option B; cond-guards = L2 / world-state; onentry/assign/script/send = executable behavior hand-authored + verified option A). HALF B (L3 concurrency, R529 #7) split: ordering free-order is ALREADY expressible (canon order is a partial order — comparable()=false for unordered nodes = any interleaving, per R529 #6 / R528 Q1a), so the genuine gap is interfering AND-threads with completion semantics (AND-join vs the exclusive-OR diamond JOIN R533-R537; state COMBINATION = real conflicts needing an independence / event-structure relation; gating entangled with world-state), and it is DOWNSTREAM of the world-state axis (R527 / R546 axis 4) — the sequencing finding ORDERS axis 4 before any L3 build. NO pull (R545 reached for neither), both halves YAGNI-deferred per R529/R493/R528; 3 questions handed to the world-state design round. SSOT = sec 7.35 + this entry.

HALF B (L3 concurrency, R529 #7) split: ordering free-order is ALREADY expressible (canon order is a partial order — comparable()=false for unordered nodes = any interleaving, per R529 #6 / R528 Q1a), so the genuine gap is interfering AND-threads with completion semantics (AND-join vs the exclusive-OR diamond JOIN R533-R537; state COMBINATION = real conflicts needing an independence / event-structure relation; gating entangled with world-state), and it is DOWNSTREAM of the world-state axis (R527 / R546 axis 4) — the sequencing finding ORDERS axis 4 before any L3 build. NO pull (R545 reached for neither), both halves YAGNI-deferred per R529/R493/R528; 3 questions handed to the world-state design round. SSOT = sec 7.35 + this entry.
publishable_changes_bullets: HALF A REFUTED: facts→SCXML is a projection adapter, not a substrate gap — fork_tree (continuity.rs:3267) + CanonOrder already emit the full state-machine topology (world-lines=states, fork edges=transitions+choice labels, converges=JOIN, is_maximal=final, linearize=beat sequence); ARCHITECTURE §6 invariant 4 keeps SCXML in adapters, never Layer 0

the R529 #2 real seam = the declarative-truth / executable-behavior boundary, mapped onto SCXML's own element split: topology = codegen (option B, drift-free); cond-guards = L2/world-state; onentry/assign/script/send = executable behavior hand-authored + verified (option A) = the SCE-UC-1 ledger-vs-verified dual already in production

HALF B split: ordering free-order is ALREADY expressible (canon order is a partial order — comparable()=false for unordered nodes = any interleaving, continuity.rs:224, per R529 #6 / R528 Q1a); the genuine gap is interfering AND-threads (AND-join completion vs the exclusive-OR diamond JOIN R533-R537; state COMBINATION = real conflicts needing an independence / event-structure relation)

sequencing finding: L3 interfering-threads is DOWNSTREAM of the world-state axis (R527 / R546 axis 4) — world-state (L2) must land before L3 can be designed; this ORDERS the remaining R546 axes rather than leaving them siblings
captured as design-doc sec 7.35 (append-only over sec 7.31/R529 + sec 7.30/R528, the R450/R498/R526 discipline); no build, no experiment, no substrate change

Round 547 — world-state / possession axi.decision_summary: World-state / possession axis (the owner-chosen R547 axis-4, R527 gap #4 / R542's deferred lever) REFUTED as a new-substrate gap on code (code 0, the R518/R493/R528 refute-first pattern). HEADLINE: the declarative world-state / possession model is ALREADY BUILT — R542's asked-for cardinality-constrained predicate IS the R449 exclusive narrative rule (NarrativeRuleSpec::Exclusive{per}, continuity.rs:414 + the eval at :2051-2096 = one holder per object / one value per subject, keyed subject|object), succession-aware via supersedes_in_frame + holds_at (continuity.rs:1185), Transition{allowed} for valid state steps, per-world-line (R438) + per-frame scoped, with a proven location-chain test (:4928). R542 under-credited the substrate. The sc-32 catch is REFRAMED as an authoring contract over the EXISTING gate (type load-bearing possession + declare the exclusive rule = the gate fires on un-transferred co-holding), HARD-gate-backed unlike R526's soft clause — but only over the TYPED slice; force-typed-possession was refuted on data (noisy + evadable), so prose-only possession stays the irreducible residue (store-consistency != causal coherence, blind-judge backstop). The conditional-simulation half (L2 gating if-state-then-opens) is DELEGATED to pinion/SCE by R527 gap #4's own framing + R546 HALF A (executable behavior = option-A hand-authored + verified, never codegen from facts). The R546 L3 ordering is REFINED: the declarative prerequisite is already met so the world-state-before-L3 blocking ordering COLLAPSES; L3-(ii) interfering AND-threads still needs the independence relation + AND-completion (R528 Q5) = the L3 build proper, pull-gated and now unblocked-but-unpulled. DECISION: NO Mnemosyne build (substrate-sufficient + correctly-delegated); the deliverable is the authoring-typing clause; the refute corrects R542 on code. SSOT = sec 7.36 + this entry.
changes_bullets: HEADLINE refute (corrects R542 on code): the declarative world-state / possession model is ALREADY BUILT — R542's asked-for "cardinality-constrained predicate" IS the R449 `exclusive` narrative rule (NarrativeRuleSpec::Exclusive{per}, continuity.rs:414/2051-2096; "one holder per object / one value per subject", keyed subject|object), with succession-aware effective extent via supersedes_in_frame + holds_at (continuity.rs:1185), Transition{allowed} for valid state steps, per-world-line (query_worlds/for_each_world_pair, R438) + per-frame scoping, and a proven test rule_exclusive_catches_forgotten_location_chain (:4928) = exactly the location/possession case

The L3-(ii) interfering-AND-thread build TRIGGER probe (R528 Q5 / R546 HALF B / R547): a blind author authors 'The Backfire at Mara Ridge' — TWO crews on one ridge through one night (simultaneity, not exclusive branches), contending on one water cart (shared exclusive resource), with a timing interference (the backfire must not outrun the cut) + a hard AND-join dawn deadline (survival = both threads complete in a combined state that fits). Honest lean = 2b/2a. manifest sha256 3b99b5978475ec58760bb282defc54c47b7787e2a2df5108f91737685638c8b3 pinned BEFORE the author runs; contamination bound R469. NEXT = R549 execution. Round 549 — concurrency-probe/v1 execute.decision_summary: concurrency-probe/v1 EXECUTED blind (R549; the owner's 실험 covered manifest+execution). OUTCOME-2b: a TOLD story does NOT need interfering-concurrency substrate. The blind author (fresh context, premise+brief only, blind to the L3 hypothesis) rendered the two-concurrent-crews premise (The Backfire at Mara Ridge) as a COUPLED SHARED SPINE + ONE EXCLUSIVE FORK at sc-09 (where the single water cart goes → town-lives/town-lost), 18 scenes. M2 headline: it reached for the R547 idiom UNPROMPTED — typed cart_with/line_state + two R449 exclusive rules + supersedes_in_frame succession — to make the cart contention + line state gate-enforced invariants (the R547 possession idiom is natural + discoverable). M1=zero fork-explosion (one genuine story fork); M3=the AND-join combined-state condition lives in a prose claim not a structural multi-predecessor node (the irreducible R547 boundary); M4=no concurrency-loss friction logged. Judged by 3 blind A/B judges over shuffled scene-pairs (arm->version randomized per judge, label-map orchestrator-only), forced choice per scene + overall on dialogue-naturalness and the shelter dramatized-sustainment seam. Decision rule: arm R preferred + pins hold => targeted craft-repair raises completeness without re-cooling or breaking fidelity => the critique->re-render->re-gate stage is a validated render-improve loop (a prompt/stage, not substrate; build YAGNI-deferred); arm R ties/loses => single-pass warm craft is the R476 ceiling here (record re-cool vs no-better); a pin fail => the gate caught a repair-introduced break, proving the gate is the safety net for an automated repair loop. Round 553 — npc-dialogue-experiment/v2 e.decision_summary: npc-dialogue-experiment/v2 EXECUTED blind (R553; the owner directed the test — re-render only the deficient parts and measure the lift). RESULT = CONFIRMED. A blind repair-renderer re-rendered the 4 judge-flagged weak scenes (report sc-16, confront sc-29+sc-30, shelter sc-27); the orchestrator spliced them into otherwise-byte-identical v1 manuscripts (arm R); a blind re-extractor and 3 blind A/B judges completed the R469 firewall. All three deterministic pins HOLD: PIN-R3 localization (only the 4 scene blocks differ; 66 spine facts byte-identical on the blind re-read), PIN-R1 no new leak (0 every world; the shelter sc-27 dramatization did not voice the withheld murder), PIN-R2 no new drift (off_path=0 every world). 3 blind A/B judges, arm->version randomized per judge, UNANIMOUSLY preferred the REPAIR on all 4 scenes and overall (12/12 + 3/3); the seam-detection check cut FOR the repair (2 judges named arm C's original sc-27 summary as the out-of-step scene). By the pre-committed rule, targeted craft-repair raises completeness without re-cooling or breaking fidelity => the critique -> re-render -> re-gate stage is a validated render-improve loop (the render analog of the author write-gate-read-repair loop); a prompt/stage not substrate, build YAGNI-deferred. The one residue (not downplayed): the blind extractor flagged an in-frame acquisition question at the repaired confront sc-30 (Ysolt's deduction premise) that the craft judges did not flag and PIN-R2 cannot catch (the R539 in-frame boundary) — the same store-consistency != causal-coherence residue, judged not gated. n=1; the base already scored 5/5 (small headroom, the repair won anyway; larger value on a lower-scoring base, untested).

ALL THREE PINS HOLD: PIN-R3 localization (scene-id sequence identical, ONLY the 4 blocks changed, and the blind re-extraction independently found 66 spine facts byte-identical); PIN-R1 leak=0 every world (the shelter sc-27 dramatization did NOT voice the withheld murder, vocab_shared=13); PIN-R2 fidelity off_path=0/unplaced=0/terminal every world.

3 blind A/B judges UNANIMOUS for the REPAIR — 12/12 scene choices + 3/3 overall, with the arm->version mapping RANDOMIZED per judge (label-map orchestrator-only). They named the same wins: dramatized pressure not announced stakes (sc-16); a confession breaking across a hearth not declaimed to the square (sc-29); the murder wrung out through resistance (sc-30); a lived curing-shed close with a line for the boy not a narrated roll-call (sc-27).
The seam-detection check cut FOR the repair: 2 judges named arm C's ORIGINAL sc-27 summary as the out-of-step scene; no judge flagged a repaired scene as foreign.

DECISION = CONFIRMED: targeted craft-repair RAISES completeness without re-cooling or breaking fidelity. The critique -> re-render -> re-gate stage is a VALIDATED render-improve loop (the render analog of the author write-gate-read-repair loop); it is a prompt/stage not substrate, build YAGNI-deferred, recipe in v2/report.md.

ALL THREE PINS HOLD: PIN-R3 localization (scene-id sequence identical, ONLY the 4 blocks changed, and the blind re-extraction independently found 66 spine facts byte-identical); PIN-R1 leak=0 every world (the shelter sc-27 dramatization did NOT voice the withheld murder, vocab_shared=13); PIN-R2 fidelity off_path=0/unplaced=0/terminal every world.

3 blind A/B judges UNANIMOUS for the REPAIR — 12/12 scene choices + 3/3 overall, with the arm->version mapping RANDOMIZED per judge (label-map orchestrator-only). They named the same wins: dramatized pressure not announced stakes (sc-16); a confession breaking across a hearth not declaimed to the square (sc-29); the murder wrung out through resistance (sc-30); a lived curing-shed close with a line for the boy not a narrated roll-call (sc-27).

DECISION = CONFIRMED: targeted craft-repair RAISES completeness without re-cooling or breaking fidelity. arms spliced by the orchestrator (PIN-R3 guaranteed by construction + confirmed by diff: only the 4 scene blocks differ); the gates ran over the blind re-extraction (159 facts) against the v1 authored store + order (verify.atomic.json carrying the disclosure plan holm); PIN-R2 used the single-world projection (the R512 method).

Round 555 — ad-hoc-scripting debt repaid.decision_summary: Paid the R550-R553 ad-hoc-scripting debt the R554 session review found. The PIN-2 sustainment scan, the per-world fidelity projection, and the arm-R splice had been run as throwaway Python (deleted after use), re-incurring exactly the debt tools/experiment-harness was built (R480) to repay — non-reproducible, silent-fail-language orchestration in a project whose thesis is fail-loud verification, and the deterministic PIN-2 verdict rested on a script that no longer existed. R555 elevates the three recurring ops into the harness as fail-loud Rust subcommands matching the existing idioms: cast-sustainment (the R545/R551 deep-tail cast-presence metric, thresholds as explicit flags, exits 1 on FAIL), project-world (the single-world projection validate-render-fidelity expects per R512, leaving the production gate's coord-based single responsibility intact rather than mutating it), and splice (replace named scene blocks preserving every other byte, an unmatched id a hard error, making the R553 PIN-R3 localization a mechanical guarantee). Proved paid by re-deriving every pin through the new commands: cast-sustainment reproduces v1 PIN-2 (12 frames / all worlds PASS / HOLD); project-world + validate-render-fidelity reproduces off_path=0 (kept 110/102/101); splice reproduces arm R byte-for-byte (report+confront identical; shelter regenerated, dropping one spurious EOF blank line, prose/gates/verdict unchanged). 35 unit + 4 smoke tests, clippy -D clean, fmt applied. Also fixed the review's F3 (the MEMORY.md live-pointer had duplicated the changelog+report.md against its own rule — trimmed to a true pointer) and recorded F2 (an A/B that judges craft only is blind to a repair degrading in-frame knowledge — the sc-30 residue) as a standing clause for future repair experiments. No production crate or gate touched.
changes_bullets: Paid the R550-R553 ad-hoc-scripting debt found in the R554 session review: elevated the throwaway Python orchestration into the Rust tools/experiment-harness (the R480 precedent + the crate's own charter — fail-loud, version-controlled, reproducible — which this session had re-violated).

Added three fail-loud subcommands (strict Flags parser, HResult, per-module #[cfg(test)], matching the existing assemble/shuffle/verify-seal idioms): `cast-sustainment` (the R545/R551 PIN-2 deep-tail cast-presence metric over facts.json x order.json, every threshold an explicit flag — also retiring the R551 hand-picked-floor caveat; exits 1 on FAIL); `project-world` (the single-world projection validate-render-fidelity expects per R512 — keep world+spine facts, drop sibling-branch; the textbook fix for the multi-world off-path workaround, leaving the production gate's coord-based single-responsibility intact); `splice` (replace named ## sc-NN blocks, preserving every other byte, an unmatched id a hard error — makes the R553 PIN-R3 localization a mechanical guarantee).

Proved the debt paid by re-deriving the pins through the new commands: cast-sustainment reproduces v1 PIN-2 (12 person-frames, all worlds PASS, HOLD); project-world + validate-render-fidelity reproduces off_path=0 (kept 110/102/101); splice reproduces arm R byte-for-byte (report+confront identical; shelter regenerated, dropping one spurious EOF blank line my Python had added — prose/structure/gates/3-0 verdict unchanged).

Fixed F3 (SSOT live-pointer bloat) in memory: the MEMORY.md pointer had duplicated the changelog + report.md against its own stated rule; trimmed to a true pointer (detail delegated to report.md + changelog). Recorded F2 (the v2 A/B judged craft only, blind to repair degrading in-frame knowledge — the sc-30 residue) as a standing clause for future repair/A-B experiments.
Updated both runbooks + both report.md with an honest R555 reproducibility note (the orchestration is the named harness commands now, not a deleted script).
verification_bullets: tools/experiment-harness: cargo build clean; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean (fixed the private_interfaces warning by making compute() private + the trim_split_whitespace lint); cargo fmt --all applied; 35 unit + 4 cli-smoke tests pass (incl. new tests: sustainment counts + thin-tail-fails + no-edges-errors; projection keeps-world-drops-siblings + missing-narrative_facts-errors; splice replaces-only-named + preserves-separators + unmatched-errors + heading-parse).

Reproduction proofs run THIS round (the debt-paid evidence): cast-sustainment on v1 facts.json+order.json = 12 frames / report+shelter+confront all PASS / HOLD (== R551 PIN-2); project-world + validate-render-fidelity per world = off_path=0 unplaced=0 terminal, kept 110/102/101 (== R551/R553); splice regenerated arm R, report+confront BYTE-IDENTICAL to the tracked R553 files, shelter differs by exactly one removed EOF blank line (git diff = 1 deletion).
Citation hygiene (R255): R480/R512/R516/R517/R525/R539/R545/R551/R553 all verified in the changelog before writing.

Gate 5b (the R517 pre-commit hook) fmt+clippy-gates the harness's own workspace on the staged .rs; build artifacts under tools/experiment-harness/target are gitignored (confirmed via git check-ignore). The root workspace + pre-push gates do not compile the harness (separate workspace, by design).
This change adds the tool the harness charter says experiments must use; it does NOT touch any production crate or gate (the fidelity gate's single-world contract is preserved, not mutated).

R555 pays it; the pins now reproduce from tracked inputs via fail-loud Rust.

STANDING CLAUSE (F2, for the next repair / A-B craft experiment, not a build): an A/B that judges craft only is blind to a repair degrading in-frame knowledge-realism (the R553 sc-30 residue lived outside both the pins and the judges). Future repair experiments add a knowledge-realism check to the repaired-scene A/B (or a pin), so craft preference and grounding are measured together.

Round 556 — pinion projection seam DESIG.decision_summary: R556 = the DESIGN round opening the pinion projection track (the owner's R556 scope-fork choice A: start the pinion live/runtime PROJECTION layer; code 0, the R496/R499 design-round pattern). Scope = the Mnemosyne-side SEAM ONLY: a Layer-3 GUI-free read projection emitting the playable-world surface a pinion narrative runtime consumes; the pinion engine itself is the sibling repo at ../pinion (never depended on by core, ARCHITECTURE sec 6) and the existing in-tree studio/ viewer is the SPEC-side Layer-3 app, a separate concern. Refute-first audit (the R493/R547 discipline): the projection DATA is ~90% already built — report-playthrough-manuscript (R466) emits, per world-line, the composed-canon-order scene walk with each begin event ALREADY carrying its resolved disclosure (mode, first_at, surface = scene + optional diegetic object) under the active telling; report-fork-tree (R497) emits the cross-world choice graph (fork + R532 converge edges); EffectiveDisclosure (R510) is the single resolver of (fact x telling x world-line). The GENUINE gap is two small pieces: (1) a typed MapLocator — the seam contract pinion consumes — = the authored DisclosureSurface RESOLVED against the world's walk into a stable POINTER { world_line, scene, scene_ordinal, object, mode, first_at } carrying NO baked geometry (pinion dereferences scene to place, object to prop, ordinal to order, mode to how-surfaced); it is the CQRS read-side pointer projection (R337), the owner's frozen-substrate-pointer model with no frame accumulation; (2) a single composing projection that JOINs the fork topology + per-world walk + per-scene MapLocators into ONE playable-world read, so a runtime stitches the world in one call instead of re-deriving across three verbs. MapLocator is a PROJECTION not new stored state (sec 6 inv 2): every field derives from the manuscript walk + the EffectiveDisclosure resolver, so the locator cannot drift from the coverage surface or the manuscript carrier (the R510 single-resolver / half-enforced-invariant guard). Placement obeys sec 6: the projection lives in the GUI-free read crates beside report-playthrough-manuscript / report-fork-tree (inv 4 core domain-agnostic), is pure read (inv 2), and the 3D/GUI runtime stays in pinion gated on a concrete consumer (inv 3, YAGNI). DECISION: build a Layer-3 read verb report-playable-world emitting, per telling, the fork topology + per-world scene walk + per-scene MapLocators; new substrate = exactly the MapLocator type + the composing JOIN, everything else reused. Honest scope (not inflated, the dont-downplay discipline): this is a SMALL build, and the smallness IS the finding — the substrate was already sufficient (the recurring R493/R547 refrain), the only missing thing was the typed seam + the single-read composition. Build STAGED, code 0 this round; NEXT = R557 BUILD.

Round 557 — pinion projection seam BUILT.decision_summary: R557 = the BUILD round implementing the R556-designed pinion projection seam (the map_locator playable-world Layer-3 read projection). Built: (1) MapLocator + PlayableWorld + PlayableWorldReport + the playable_world(store, order, world, telling) JOIN in mnemosyne-validate::continuity beside playthrough_manuscript (R466) and fork_tree (R497); (2) the thin playable_world_report ops wrapper (the established *_report idiom: resolve policy/order/store, call continuity); (3) the report-playable-world CLI verb (--telling required, --world optional, --json the contract face) following the parse_narrative_report_args / NarrativeFlags pattern; (4) the report_playable_world MCP tool + ReportPlayableWorldArgs. The verb emits, per telling, the cross-world fork topology + each world-line's scene walk + the per-scene disclosure MapLocators (the authored DisclosureSurface resolved to a stable POINTER {world_line, fact_id, scene, scene_ordinal, object, mode, first_at}, where scene_ordinal indexes the world's walk = the CQRS read-side pointer, no baked geometry). As-built refinements faithfully recorded (the R533/R535 precedent): (a) PLACEMENT — the JOIN + types live in continuity.rs beside the two projections it composes (one store/order resolution), with ops the thin wrapper, rather than the R556-phrased ops-level JOIN over the two ops fns (which would re-resolve store+order twice); this matches the established types-and-logic-in-continuity / ops-wraps idiom and is more SSOT-faithful. (b) MapLocator carries fact_id (the R556 field sketch omitted it) — a locator without its fact id cannot be dereferenced to content, so it is load-bearing not decorative. The locators derive entirely from playthrough_manuscript's already-resolved per-begin disclosure (the R510 EffectiveDisclosure single resolver) + the fork tree, so they add no traversal and cannot drift from the coverage surface or the manuscript carrier. Pure read projection, never gated (a playable surface is a reading surface). Proved: 2 new unit tests (per-world locator resolution with correct ordinals + per-world first_at + a surfaceless fact yielding no locator; an off-walk surface resolving scene_ordinal=None, the R466 unplaced idiom); full validate+ops+cli+mcp suites green; clippy -D clean; fmt applied; an end-to-end CLI smoke on the tracked disclosure v3 store (5 worlds, 4 branches, scene walks, 0 locators since v3 authored no surface) plus a crafted smoke (a scene-only surface added via set-disclosure resolves to a locator scene=sc-08 scene_ordinal=7 in both human and JSON, and set-disclosure fail-loud-rejected an unregistered surface object = the entity-registry invariant held). NEXT = R558 owner cadence.
changes_bullets: Built the R556-designed pinion projection seam (the `map_locator` playable-world Layer-3 read projection): `MapLocator` + `PlayableWorld` + `PlayableWorldReport` + `playable_world(store, order, world, telling)` in `mnemosyne-validate::continuity` beside `playthrough_manuscript` (R466) and `fork_tree` (R497), composing both at one store/order resolution.

Wired the read path end to end: the thin `playable_world_report` wrapper in mnemosyne-ops (the established `*_report` idiom), the `report-playable-world` CLI verb (`--telling` required, `--world` optional, `--json` the contract face) via `parse_narrative_report_args` / `NarrativeFlags`, and the `report_playable_world` MCP tool + `ReportPlayableWorldArgs`.

The verb emits per telling: the cross-world fork topology + each world-line's scene walk (section ids in order) + the per-scene disclosure `MapLocator`s — the authored `DisclosureSurface` resolved to a stable POINTER `{world_line, fact_id, scene, scene_ordinal, object, mode, first_at}`, `scene_ordinal` indexing the world's walk; NO baked geometry (the CQRS read-side pointer, R337). Derived entirely from `playthrough_manuscript`'s already-resolved per-begin disclosure (the R510 single resolver) + the fork tree = no new traversal, no drift.

As-built refinement 1 (PLACEMENT, the R533/R535 record-deviations discipline): the JOIN + types live in continuity.rs beside the two projections it composes (one store/order resolution), with ops the thin wrapper — NOT the R556-phrased ops-level JOIN over the two ops fns (which would re-resolve store+order twice). Matches the types-and-logic-in-continuity / ops-wraps idiom; more SSOT-faithful.

As-built refinement 2: `MapLocator` carries `fact_id` (the R556 field sketch omitted it) — a locator without its fact id cannot be dereferenced to content, so it is load-bearing. Pure read projection, never gated (a playable surface is a reading surface, not a defect detector).
verification_bullets: cargo build clean (validate/ops/cli/mcp); 2 new unit tests pass — `playable_world_resolves_surface_locators_per_world` (per-world locators with correct scene_ordinal + per-world first_at; a surfaceless default-withhold fact yields no locator) and `playable_world_surfaces_unplaced_ordinal` (an off-walk surface resolves scene_ordinal=None, the R466 idiom).

Full suites green (mnemosyne-validate lib 261 tests incl. the 2 new; ops/cli/mcp all pass, 0 failed); `cargo clippy -p validate/ops/cli/mcp --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --all` applied (no reformat of the new code).

End-to-end CLI smoke on the tracked disclosure v3 store (`authored.atomic.json` + `meridian-order.json`): `report-playable-world --telling dc-v2` = 5 worlds, 4 registered branches, per-world scene walks, 0 locators (v3 authored no surface — the empty-but-correct path).

Crafted locator smoke (a /tmp copy, deleted after): a scene-only surface added via `set-disclosure --surface sc-08` resolves to `{world_line:main, fact_id:gt-account-discrepancy-surfaced, scene:sc-08, scene_ordinal:7, mode:state}` in both human and `--json`; `set-disclosure` fail-loud-rejected an unregistered surface object (`ledger`) = the entity-registry invariant held.
