Round 576 — ratify the structured-fact S.decision_summary: Ratify the structured-fact SSOT structure as the textbook fix for the pinion R1013.1 class (prose acting as a write-side source of store-homed facts: false supersede/decided/bundled claims that passed every gate). A structured fact (relation/status/binding) lives once in the store; current-state prose may only point/project, never restate; a syntactic lint forbids fact-assertion verbs beside a section ref in current-state surfaces, changelog audit-narrative exempt. Verification was the wrong frame (it presupposes two copies; SSOT forbids the second), so the prior claim-substantiation-gate draft is superseded. Reuse existing homes and verification (decision_status/superseded_by, BindingKind + max-rigor, R342/R574); typed relations are SSOT completion scoped to used relation verbs, not a speculative ontology. Design-only; build deferred pending open items (notably an open-question state has no DecisionStatus home).
changes_bullets: Authored claudedocs/structured-fact-ssot-design.md and deleted the superseded claim-substantiation-gate-design.md draft (verification-as-frame was the smell).

Ratify the structure: a structured fact (relation/status/binding) has one store home; current-state prose (code comments, section caveats/intent/rationale/body) may only point or project, never restate; the changelog audit-narrative is exempt.

The only new mechanism is a syntactic lint (a fact-assertion verb beside a section ref in current-state surfaces); everything else reuses existing homes and verification (decision_status/superseded_by, BindingKind + max-rigor R413-R428, supersede invariant R342/R574).
Reframe typed relations as SSOT completion: every used relation needs one home so prose can project it; build per used verb only, never a speculative ontology.
verification_bullets: Motivating case verified against the pinion SSOT: §5.37 = self-hosted text engine (no font-bundle policy), §5.36 Active with superseded_by None, Nanum/Noto only under pinion-text-font/tests/fonts/ as parser fixtures — the false claims had no store backing yet passed every gate.
Confirmed Mnemosyne DecisionStatus = Active/Superseded/Removed (no Open variant) — recorded as the gating open item before the deferred/open verbs can be linted.
Design composes with existing mechanisms (R342/R574 supersede invariant, max-rigor R413-R428 bindings, R400 SSOT/projection precedent); no new verification subsystem is introduced.
Design-only, nothing built: v1 = the lint plus a read-side projection helper; acceptance test = authoring the R1013.1 comment is rejected at the lint, leaving the fabrication no honest home.
carry_forward_bullets: Build deferred pending open items: open-question representation (no DecisionStatus Open), current-state surface boundary, pointer-window bound, multilingual verb set, code-comment read-side projection surface.
typed relation edges (depends_on / refines / conflicts_with) built only when a relation verb is first used (SSOT completion, demand-pulled), scoped to that verb.
Detailed design lives in claudedocs/structured-fact-ssot-design.md (gitignored working artifact); memory anchor project_sce_adoption_uc1.md.

Round 577 — implement v1 of the structur.decision_summary: Build v1 of the structured-fact SSOT design (ratified R576): a new opt-in prose-fact-assertion axis in validate-code-refs that flags a current-state code comment which RESTATES a structured fact (a relation/status verb adjacent to a §<id>) instead of pointing to it. The fact has a single store home (decision_status/superseded_by/bindings, authored via the mutate API); prose only projects. Deterministic and AI-free; opt-in via severity_prose_fact_assertion (None=off). Scoped to used relation/status verbs; implements excluded (binding idiom). The design acceptance test — the real R1013.1 comment shape — is rejected by the lint. Section-body surface, open/deferred-state verbs, and a CLI override are deferred per design sec 12.
changes_bullets: Add the prose-fact-assertion axis to validate-code-refs (mnemosyne-validate): a current-state code comment that RESTATES a structured fact — a relation/status verb adjacent to a §<id> citation — is flagged ProseFactAssertion, because the fact has a single store home and prose must only point to it.

New ViolationKind::ProseFactAssertion + DefectClass::ProseFactAssertion + extract_prose_fact_assertions helper (closed verb set, backtick-skipped, one hit per line, independent of whether the cited id resolves).

New opt-in config axis severity_prose_fact_assertion (Option<Severity>, None = off), wired through the cli tally / summary line / JSON / reject gate exactly like severity_verification and severity_blanket.

Scope: relation/status verbs in use (supersede, decided in, ratified in, deferred to, depends on, refines, conflicts with, plus Korean 폐기/대체); implements is excluded (binding idiom, already governed by the binding axis + max-rigor).
verification_bullets: New unit tests pass: prose_fact_assertion_flags_verb_beside_section_ref (positive + 'see §X' pointer / bare pointer / backtick-example negatives) and prose_fact_assertion_rejects_the_r1013_1_comment_shape (the design acceptance test on the real R1013.1 comment text — both 'decided in §5.37' and 'supersedes §5.36' lines flag).
cargo fmt --all --check clean; cargo clippy -D warnings clean on mnemosyne-config + mnemosyne-validate + mnemosyne-cli; cargo test across those three crates = 0 failed.

Axis is opt-in (None default), so no behavior change for existing consumers (Mnemosyne dogfood, SCE) until a workspace sets the knob; the 4 SetEqualityValidatorConfig struct literals updated to carry the new field.
carry_forward_bullets: Deferred to later rounds (design sec 12 open items): open-question representation (DecisionStatus has no Open variant, gating the deferred/open verbs), the section-body/caveat scan surface, a CLI severity override, multilingual verb expansion, and an explicit read-side projection helper (the existing query read-path already renders a section's status/title).

Live acceptance against pinion R1013.1 (enable severity_prose_fact_assertion in pinion's mnemosyne.toml and confirm the already-committed paint_adapter.rs comment flags) is pending — a pinion-side config step.
Design SSOT: claudedocs/structured-fact-ssot-design.md, ratified R576.

Round 578 — add DecisionStatus::Open to.decision_summary: Implement design sec 12a (the open-question SSOT home): add DecisionStatus::Open, the not-yet-decided lifecycle state the enum was missing (it had only Active/Superseded/Removed). Open joins Removed as axiom-exempt (no in-force decision to back) via a new is_axiom_exempt() helper that is the single source for the coverage/verification/confirmation/spec-drift exemption set, replacing 6 inline == Removed checks (closing the half-enforced-invariant risk). Wired through the fact codec (discriminator 4), as_str, the CLI set-section-decision-status parser, and superseded_by handling (Open carries none). This lets prose POINT at an open question (a section in Open state) instead of restating it. Does NOT yet enable the lint open/deferred verbs (downstream: needs the deferred-to typed relation per sec 6).
changes_bullets: Add DecisionStatus::Open (4th state, appended to preserve the existing Ord) — the not-yet-decided lifecycle state the enum was missing; Open resolves to Active or is withdrawn to Removed, never Superseded.

Add DecisionStatus::is_axiom_exempt() (Removed | Open) as the single source for the decision-backing exemption, replacing the 6 inline == Removed checks across coverage / verification / misclassification / blanket / verifies-linkage / confirmation (closes the half-enforced-invariant risk).
Exempt Open from spec rev-drift (an Open question is not a ratified live mirror) alongside Superseded/Removed.

Wire Open through the fact codec (discriminator 4), as_str ("open"), the CLI set-section-decision-status parser + help text, and set_section_decision_status (an Open section carries no superseded_by).
verification_bullets: New tests pass: core decision_status_open_label_and_axiom_exemption (as_str "open" + exemption set Removed/Open true, Active/Superseded false), facts section_fact_round_trip_open_status (discriminator 4 round-trips), code_refs classify_coverage_partitions extended (an Open Normative section with no coverage is lifecycle-excluded, NOT a normative gap).
cargo fmt --all --check clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test --workspace = 0 failed.

Compiler-confirmed exhaustiveness: the one non-exhaustive match the new variant broke (set_section_decision_status superseded_by) was handled; every other DecisionStatus match across the 20 touched files compiles.
carry_forward_bullets: Downstream (design sec 12 + sec 6): enable the lint's open/deferred verbs, which needs the deferred-to typed relation (open question -> resolving section) and resolving the "open" verb noise (a common English word).
No CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION bump (additive enum value, pre-release no-compat); revisit only if a forward-compat guard is later wanted.
MCP does not expose set-section-decision-status (CLI-only, unchanged); add only if a named consumer needs to set Open via MCP (YAGNI).

Round 579 — resolved_by forward-pointer.decision_summary: Build SSOT design sec 12a/sec 6 step 2 in one round. (A) Add resolved_by — the open-question SSOT home for 'deferred to §Y', symmetric with superseded_by (dedicated cardinality-1 forward-pointer, set only when Open, own invariant; projected as a resolved_by cross-ref so it is orphan-checked; deliberately NOT a premature general relation store, which would risk a shared-field half-enforced invariant). set_section_decision_status gains a resolving param; the CLI gains --resolving guarded to --status open. (B) Curate the prose-fact-assertion lint to 'lint a verb iff its fact has a store home': add deferred to / open question / still open; remove depends on / refines / conflicts with (no home yet — R577 over-included them = alternative-less ban); keep bare 'open' out (noise).
changes_bullets: Add AtomicSection.resolved_by (Option<String>, serde-default) — the open-question forward-pointer to its expected resolver, symmetric with superseded_by: a dedicated cardinality-1 field set only when decision_status==Open (optional there), each lifecycle pointer keeping its own invariant (no general relation bag = no half-enforced-invariant risk).
set_section_decision_status gains a `resolving` param (sets resolved_by for Open, clears it on every other transition); the CLI gains `--resolving §<M>` guarded to `--status open`, plus help text.

Project resolved_by as a `resolved_by`-kind cross-ref so it is orphan-checked through the existing machinery — no new validator (parity with superseded_by, which also has no reverse-direction out-of-band check).

Curate the prose-fact-assertion lint verb set to the rule "lint a verb iff its fact has a store home": ADD deferred to (resolved_by) / open question / still open (decision_status=Open); REMOVE depends on / refines / conflicts with (no typed-relation home yet — R577 over-included them = alternative-less ban); bare "open" stays excluded (noise).
verification_bullets: New tests pass: atomic set_section_decision_status_open_sets_and_clears_resolved_by (Open sets the pointer, → Active clears it), project resolved_by_projects_a_resolved_by_cross_ref (projection symmetric with supersession), code_refs prose_fact_assertion_verb_set_is_homed_only (deferred-to/open-question flag; depends-on/refines/bare-open do NOT).
cargo fmt --all --check clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo test --workspace = 0 failed.

Compiler-guided caller migration of the now-6-arg set_section_decision_status: all 11 call sites updated (10 atomic tests + 1 code_refs test) plus the CLI; no production caller missed (MCP does not expose this primitive).
carry_forward_bullets: depends on / refines / conflicts with re-enter the lint only when their typed-relation homes are built (sec 6, demand-pulled — none exists today).

No out-of-band validator for "resolved_by set implies Open": parity with superseded_by (which likewise has no reverse-direction check); the single write path + the projection orphan-check are the enforced invariants.
No CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION bump (additive serde-default field, pre-release no-compat).

Round 580 — extend the prose-fact-assert.decision_summary: Close the last enforcement gap (design sec 12b): the prose-fact-assertion lint now also covers the store-side surface — a section's own prose (intent/rationale/caveat/inputs/outputs) must POINT to a section, not RESTATE a structured fact about it, exactly as a code comment must. New scan_section_prose_fact_assertions reuses extract_prose_fact_assertions (one rule, two surfaces), surfaced + gated in validate-workspace under the same severity_prose_fact_assertion axis (one knob governs both code comments and section prose). The code-comment surface runs in validate-code-refs (source scan); the section-prose surface runs in validate-workspace (raw store) — the natural split, since SectionView snapshots carry no prose. Opt-in; gates only at reject (mirrors the commit-ledger drift surface).
changes_bullets: Add scan_section_prose_fact_assertions (mnemosyne-validate) — iterates store sections' intent/rationale/caveat/inputs/outputs prose, reusing extract_prose_fact_assertions, returning SectionProseFinding {section_id, field, target, verb}. One rule, two surfaces.

Wire it into validate-workspace as a CLI surface (print_section_prose_fact_assertion_surface): off unless severity_prose_fact_assertion is set; prints per-finding; gates the exit only at reject (mirrors the commit-ledger drift surface).

One axis (severity_prose_fact_assertion) now governs BOTH surfaces — code comments (validate-code-refs, source scan) and section prose (validate-workspace, raw store) — the natural split since SectionView snapshots carry no prose.
verification_bullets: New test section_prose_fact_assertion_flags_caveat_not_pointer: a caveat that restates "supersedes §X" is flagged (field=caveat, target=X); a bare "see §Y" pointer in intent is not.

Round 581 — session cleanup: sync the SS.decision_summary: Mop up two cheap loose ends from the R576-R580 structured-fact SSOT arc surfaced by the session review: (1) sync the gitignored design doc to reflect what shipped (R577-R580) and resolve its sec 12 open items — it was stale at the R576 'design only' state; (2) remove the redundant AtomicStore::load in the section-prose surface by reusing the store cmd_validate_workspace already loaded. No behavior change. The deeper review items (CLI-side gating split from the ops pipeline; the resolved_by/superseded_by out-of-band defense-in-depth gap; the opt-in-unconsumed strategic question) are NOT cheap cleanup and are recorded as carry, not done here.
changes_bullets: Sync claudedocs/structured-fact-ssot-design.md (gitignored working artifact): Status -> IMPLEMENTED (R577-R580); add a sec 0 "Implementation status" mapping each round and resolving the sec 12 open items; note the latent-payoff / demand-gated remainder. It was stale at the R576 "design only" state; the changelog stays the SSOT.
Dedupe the store load: print_section_prose_fact_assertion_surface now takes the &AtomicStore already loaded by cmd_validate_workspace instead of reloading it.
verification_bullets: cargo fmt --all --check clean; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; mnemosyne-cli builds.
No behavior change: the section-prose surface reads the same store + config; only the redundant load is removed (the store is now shared from the caller).
carry_forward_bullets: Out of "cheap cleanup" scope (flagged in the session review, kept as separate decisions): the section-prose gate lives in the CLI (cmd_validate_workspace), not the reusable ops::validate_workspace pipeline, so a non-CLI consumer gets no section-prose gate (consistent with the commit-ledger surface precedent); and neither resolved_by nor superseded_by has an out-of-band "set only on the right status" check (the direct-JSON-edit escape hatch).

Strategic open question (session review): the SSOT lint is opt-in and currently unconsumed — enable it in a real consumer (pinion warn->reject) to realize the payoff, or return to the narrative north-star bottleneck, before any further spec-side lint rounds.

Round 582 — harden the prose-fact-assert.decision_summary: Respond to pinion's warn-adoption field report (it found real matcher false positives my unverified 'zero FP / zero section-prose' advisory claims missed). FP-1: skip a section ref inside a double-quoted span — a quoted verb+ref is a meta-mention (e.g. a corrective quoting the old overclaim to refute it), mirroring the backtick-skip, syntactic. FP-2: the section-prose finding no longer pins a single target ref — free-prose source->target binding is unreliable (passive voice bound the agent and produced source==target self-loops), so a target was false precision; the advisory finding now names only (section, field, verb). Both make the heuristic honestly coarse, NOT more precise. Declined the report's prose<->decision_status cross-check: it re-introduces the verification frame the SSOT design rejected (sec 9 forbids reject-grade precision on semantic prose); the value is already delivered by flag -> force-to-store -> R342/R574 rejecting false edges. The lint stays advisory (warn/info, never reject); the structural store invariants are the primary SSOT enforcement.
changes_bullets: FP-1: extract_prose_fact_assertions now skips a section ref inside a double-quoted span (in_quote state mirroring in_backtick) — a quoted verb+ref is a meta-mention (e.g. a corrective that quotes the old overclaim to refute it), not an assertion.

FP-2: SectionProseFinding drops the `target` field; the section-prose finding now names only (section_id, field, verb). Free-prose source->target binding is unreliable (passive voice "X superseded by Y" bound the agent and produced source==target self-loops), so a pinned target was false precision. CLI surface + test updated.

Both fixes are syntactic and make the heuristic honestly coarse, NOT more precise. Declined the report's prose<->decision_status cross-check (#3) and left the "deferred to" home doc (#4) out, per the "two cheap fixes only" scope.
verification_bullets: New test prose_fact_assertion_skips_quoted_meta_mention: a quoted "decided in §X" does not flag; an unquoted supersede on the next line still flags. Existing section-prose test updated to assert (section, field, verb), not a target.

Honest correction: the adoption advisory's "0 false positives / 0 section-prose findings" were UNVERIFIED (the R577 demo was a stale snapshot; the R580 section-prose scan was never run on pinion). The pinion field report's measurements (4 code-surface, 6 section-surface) are correct; this round fixes the two real FP classes it found.

No further matcher precision-chasing (treadmill). The pinion field report's chief value was proving that advisory is the ceiling for this surface.

Round 583 — correct the over-declared MS.decision_summary: A 1.88 consumer (pinion) field-flagged that workspace.package.rust-version = 1.94 was an inaccurate over-declaration — the code builds and runs on rustc 1.88 (HEAD) and no language/dep feature requires 1.94 (it was the then-current toolchain in the Phase 0 scaffold, never validated). Correct the MSRV to the field-proven floor 1.88. To keep the claim continuously verified (not another unverified declaration — the same accuracy principle the prose-fact lint enforces) add a msrv CI job that reads rust-version from Cargo.toml (single source, no drift) and runs cargo check --workspace on that toolchain via RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN, overriding the rust-toolchain.toml dev pin of 1.94.1 (dev-on-latest, support-down-to-MSRV). Discovered while doing so: mnemosyne-validate.yml was malformed (unparseable indentation) so the GitHub CI had been silently non-running; rewrote it into valid YAML in the same change. The cargo install --git adoption commands in README/SCHEMA_GUIDE are correct (they include mnemosyne-cli); the missing-package error was only in this session's chat advisory.
changes_bullets: Correct workspace.package.rust-version 1.94 -> 1.88 (the field-proven floor; the 1.94 was a stale, unverified Phase 0 default with no actual >1.88 language/dep requirement, flagged by a 1.88 consumer that built + ran HEAD).

Rewrite .github/workflows/mnemosyne-validate.yml from a malformed (unparseable) indentation state into valid YAML — the GitHub CI had been silently non-running, so the local pre-commit/pre-push hooks were the only actual gate.

Add a `msrv` CI job: reads rust-version from Cargo.toml (single source, no drift), installs that toolchain, runs `cargo check --workspace` under RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN (overriding the 1.94.1 rust-toolchain.toml dev pin) so the MSRV claim is verified on every push — not another unverified declaration.
verification_bullets: python yaml.safe_load parses the rewritten workflow (jobs: validate, msrv); the MSRV-derivation grep extracts "1.88" from Cargo.toml; cargo clippy on the dev toolchain (1.94.1) still builds (rust-version is metadata only).

The MSRV build on rustc 1.88 itself is verified by the new CI job on first run — I cannot test 1.88 locally (only the 1.94.1 dev toolchain), which is exactly why the CI job exists; the consumer field-proved 1.88 builds HEAD.
Honest note: the discovery that mnemosyne-validate.yml was unparseable means prior "GitHub CI green" assumptions were unfounded; the local hooks were the real gate. Both now run.
carry_forward_bullets: Install-command (consumer report item B): README.md / docs/SCHEMA_GUIDE.md already pin the package name (`... mnemosyne-cli`); the missing-package "multiple packages" error was only in this session's chat advisory, not the repo. Corrected form: `cargo install --git <url> --rev <sha> mnemosyne-cli --locked`. No repo change needed.
rust-toolchain.toml stays at 1.94.1 (dev toolchain); MSRV (the consumer floor) is 1.88 and now CI-verified. Future MSRV bumps update only Cargo.toml rust-version; the CI job follows automatically.

Round 584 — exempt the append-only cavea.decision_summary: pinion's follow-up to R582 surfaced that the R580 section-prose lint produces permanent, unremediable noise on the caveats field: caveats are append-only (only add_section_caveat; no set/remove primitive — an audit ledger accumulating per-round decisions), and direct JSON edits are contract-forbidden, so a homed-verb inside a caveat has NO sanctioned 'demote the prose' path (my R582 prescription was unexecutable for that surface). Confirmed in code: caveats has only an add primitive while intent/rationale/inputs/outputs each have a setter (replaceable → remediable). Resolution = pinion option (a): exempt caveats from the section-prose lint — it is an append-only audit surface, so the same audit-vs-current-state line that exempts the changelog applies. Rejected option (b) (a caveat remove/replace primitive) as a frozen-ledger violation. The lint now scans only the settable current-state fields, where a flagged assertion can actually be remediated via its setter. This confirms the sec 9 irreducible-residual classification with the concrete append-only mechanism.
changes_bullets: scan_section_prose_fact_assertions no longer scans caveats_bullets — caveats are append-only (only add_section_caveat; no set/remove primitive), an audit ledger accumulating per-round decisions, and direct JSON edits are contract-forbidden, so a homed-verb inside a caveat has NO sanctioned remediation path; linting it = permanent noise. Exempt like the changelog (the same audit-vs-current-state line).

The lint now scans only the SETTABLE current-state prose fields (intent / rationale / inputs / outputs), each replaceable via its setter so a flagged assertion is actually remediable. SectionProseFinding.field doc updated.
Rejected the alternative of adding a caveat remove/replace primitive (a frozen-ledger violation).
verification_bullets: New test section_prose_lints_settable_fields_but_exempts_append_only_caveat: a supersede in rationale (settable) flags; the same in a caveat (append-only) is exempt; a bare pointer in intent does not flag.
Confirmed in code: caveats has only add_section_caveat (no set/remove); intent/rationale/inputs/outputs each have a setter (replaceable → remediable).

The MSRV 1.94->1.88 fix (R583) is committed but UNPUSHED — pinion still sees 1.94 on origin until R583 (+ this R584) are pushed. Push delivers both the MSRV correction and this caveat exemption.

Round 585.decision_summary: Register the AI-first-authoring support-surface debt: the deterministic, medium-neutral surfaces Mnemosyne must add so an external agent's generate-gate-repair loop authors narrative unattended at scale. The LLM loop stays an outside agent/MCP client; only these deterministic affordances land in the core. No build in this entry, debt registration only.
changes_bullets: describe-schema: emit the authoring contract as machine-readable data (entity kinds, predicate vocabulary, the quest encoding pursues/requires/completed_by with completion paying off an Expected setup, disclosure modes, typed-claim rules, all invariants) so an agent self-serves instead of reading source

propose-verdict transaction: one deterministic op = apply a candidate batch to a scratch sidecar, run every gate, return commit-or-rollback plus structured violations (today the loop hand-orchestrates import-facts + --sidecar + gates)
actionable violations: every gate violation carries rule + locus (fact/field) + expected + repair hint in --json, so an agent repairs programmatically rather than parsing prose

authoring-frontier report: one consolidated coverage-gap surface (scenes/nodes with zero facts, quests with no giver, dangling setups, never-planned disclosures, per-node coverage) an unattended loop pulls work from; today the pieces are scattered across payoff-coverage and disclosure-coverage
all-primitive batch manifest: one manifest importing facts + entities + quests + branches + disclosure for throughput (today only import-facts / import-sections)
AI-failure guardrail gates as Validator plugins: hallucinated-ref / wrong-branch / orphan / citation-hygiene deterministic checks via the existing PluginRegistry Validator slot
verification_bullets: grounded in this session's dogfood: authoring a 26-chapter game store in ../the-tide-that-counts surfaced each gap first-hand
read crates/mnemosyne-validate/src/continuity.rs to learn the quest convention (the describe-schema gap)
a batch failed mid-apply on one fact with a prose-only error (the transaction + actionable-violations gap)
hand-derived that two chapters had zero anchored facts (the authoring-frontier gap)
boundary confirmed against PluginRegistry: only SymbolResolver + Validator slots exist, both deterministic and compiled-in, so the LLM loop cannot be a plugin
carry_forward_bullets: pay in priority order: (1) describe-schema (largest correctness win), (2) propose-verdict transaction + actionable violations (the loop's atomic unit), (3) authoring-frontier report (unattended pull)
then: all-primitive batch manifest (throughput), guardrail Validator gates (feedback completeness)
invariant: the generate-gate-repair LLM loop stays an external agent/MCP client; only deterministic and medium-neutral surfaces land in the core
serves the north star: gates = self-consistency feedback, discovery = self-extraction, now made machine-actionable for an unattended agent

Round 586.decision_summary: Extend the R585 AI-first-authoring support debt with the spatial support surface: relational (not metric) space needs a place-graph projection report plus deterministic spatial-integrity and spatial-fidelity gates, so text/2D/3D renderers project one topological SSOT and cannot drift from it. Storing and projecting works today with user predicates; only the deterministic guarantee is the debt.
changes_bullets: place-graph report: project the containment hierarchy + adjacency for a renderer (the spatial analogue of report-quest-graph)
spatial-integrity gate (Validator plugin): containment cycle-free, no orphan place, no mutually-exclusive double-containment, adjacency consistency
spatial render-fidelity gate: a rendered description must not contradict declared spatial relations (extend the R505 render-fidelity pattern to spatial predicates)
convention (no feature needed to store): relational space via user predicates located-in / part-of / adjacent-to / direction-of as typed entity relations; no metric coordinates
verification_bullets: extends the R585 support-surface debt with the spatial axis
grounded in this session's the-tide game direction: staged text to 2D to 3D, model-complete and render-progressive (one topological SSOT, a projection per fidelity)

relational space is authorable today as user predicates + typed facts, the same path quests took before report-quest-graph; no reserved spatial constant exists in mnemosyne-validate (confirmed by source grep)
render-fidelity for narrative facts (R505) is the pattern the spatial fidelity gate extends
carry_forward_bullets: start now with no feature: author relational space as user predicates + facts; a renderer reads them via query (report-entity / query --term) and projects
promote when load-bearing: place-graph report + spatial-integrity gate + spatial-fidelity gate (deterministic and medium-neutral, so in-core Validator + report, not the external harness)
invariant: relations are the stable SSOT; metric coordinates are renderer-local, volatile, untracked; each renderer solves coordinates to satisfy the declared relations

Round 587 — describe-schema: the authori.decision_summary: Build describe-schema (R585 debt item 1, the largest correctness win): the medium-neutral authoring contract emitted as machine-readable data so an external generate-gate-repair agent self-serves the schema (registries, fact shape, fixed vocabularies, rule classes, quest encoding, write-time invariants) instead of reading source. A pure STATIC projection: store-independent (the contract is fixed; store contents are query/list-*), medium-neutral by construction. The generate-gate-repair LLM loop stays an outside agent/MCP client; only this deterministic surface lands. (Renumbered from the earlier R586 to resolve a round-number collision with a parallel R586 spatial-debt entry.)
changes_bullets: crates/mnemosyne-validate/src/schema.rs: new module — SchemaContract + describe_schema() emitting the registries (sections/frames/branches/entities/predicates/disclosure_plans), the narrative-fact shape (required + optional fields), the fixed vocabularies (disclosure_mode / payoff_expectation / predicate_object_kind), the deterministic rule classes (exclusive / transition / interval), the quest encoding (Entity{kind:quest} + pursues/requires/completed_by, completion pays off an Expected setup), and 12 write-time fail-loud invariants

drift guard: the vocabularies are built from the real mnemosyne-core enums via as_str() + an exhaustive per-value match, the rule classes from the real continuity::RuleClass — an added variant or class breaks the exhaustive match (a compile error at the description site), so the contract cannot silently fall behind the code

single-sourcing: continuity's quest-convention consts (QUEST_ENTITY_KIND / pursues / requires / completed_by) and RuleClass promoted to pub(crate); mnemosyne-atomic CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION promoted to pub so the contract reports its schema generation
surfaces: mnemosyne-ops::describe_schema() thin wrapper; describe-schema [--json] CLI verb (human render + machine JSON, fail-loud on an unexpected arg); describe_schema no-arg MCP tool

the invariant set is grounded in the actual mutate-primitive rejects (build_candidate_fact / build_typed_claim / set_disclosure), not asserted from doc-comments — the R585 evidence-before-claiming lesson
static and store-independent by design: the contract is fixed, so it loads no store and cannot fail; a store's live registered predicates/entities stay query / list-*
verification_bullets: cargo build green; cargo test -p mnemosyne-validate schema = 2/2 (contract completeness + the enum-mirror assertions proving the drift guard is in force; JSON serialization)

e2e CLI smoke: describe-schema --json parses, schema_version=23, 6 registries, 3 vocabularies with the exact enum values, 3 rule classes, quest kind=quest + 3 predicates, 4 required fact fields, 12 invariants; human render clean; describe-schema --bogus fail-loud exit 1
clippy -D warnings clean across the workspace; validate-workspace unaffected by the store-independent surface: T1 orphan=0 / T3 reject=0 / entries pre-append 333
the MCP tool needs a reinstall to appear (cargo install --path crates/mnemosyne-mcp --force + restart) — the R557/R561 installed-binary-skew gotcha
carry_forward_bullets: R585 debt item 2 is next: the propose-verdict transaction + actionable violations (the loop's atomic unit) — apply a candidate batch to a scratch sidecar, run every gate, return commit-or-rollback plus structured rule + locus + expected + repair-hint violations so an agent repairs programmatically

then item 3 authoring-frontier report (one consolidated coverage-gap surface an unattended loop pulls work from), item 4 all-primitive batch manifest (throughput), item 5 guardrail Validator gates (hallucinated-ref / wrong-branch / orphan / citation-hygiene)
invariant held: the generate-gate-repair LLM loop stays an external agent/MCP client; only the deterministic, medium-neutral describe-schema surface landed
describe-schema stays a STATIC contract; a store's live registered predicates/entities are query / list-*, deliberately not folded in here

Round 588 — propose-verdict: the generat.decision_summary: Build propose-verdict (R585 debt item 2, the loop's atomic unit): one deterministic dry-run op that applies a candidate import-facts batch to a THROWAWAY in-memory clone of the store, runs the shape invariants + the continuity gate, and returns commit-or-rollback plus ACTIONABLE violations (rule + locus of facts/field/frame/branch/at + expected + repair_hint + message) so an external agent repairs programmatically instead of parsing prose. Collapses the loop's hand-orchestration of import-facts + --sidecar + gates into one call. Pure dry run — the real store is never written; the generate-gate-repair LLM loop stays outside, only the deterministic surface lands. (Follows the renumbered R587 describe-schema; the earlier R587 label was reassigned to resolve a round-number collision.)
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-atomic: factored apply_facts_manifest (in-memory apply, no I/O) out of import_facts — behavior-identical (import_facts = apply then a single persist, same no-op / byte-stable idempotency contract), proven by the existing 29 import tests including the add-vs-import write-path parity test

mnemosyne-validate/src/verdict.rs (new): ActionableViolation {source, rule, locus, expected, repair_hint, message} + ViolationLocus + continuity_actionable() mapping ALL 16 ContinuityViolation variants via an EXHAUSTIVE match (an added variant fails to compile — the describe-schema drift guard applied to violations)
shape violations are carried verbatim from the atomic primitive's fail-fast Validation message (which already names the fact + repair) rather than reverse-parsed into fields — the no-NLP discipline

mnemosyne-ops: propose_verdict() = load base store, clone, apply (shape), run the continuity gate over the mutated clone, verdict commit iff clean else rollback + actionable violations; ProposeVerdict enum + ProposeVerdictReport

surfaces: propose-verdict --manifest [--order] [--rules] [--sidecar] [--json] CLI verb (exit 1 on rollback for loop branching) + propose_verdict MCP tool (reads a candidate manifest path); both pure dry runs
scope: runs the store-integrity gates a fact batch faces (shape + continuity); render-acceptance gates (leak / fidelity) need re-extracted prose and stay a separate phase
verification_bullets: cargo build + clippy -D warnings clean; cargo fmt clean; full suites green (atomic 186, validate 274) including 2 new verdict tests (exhaustive-mapping population + shape passthrough); the import parity tests are unbroken by the apply refactor

e2e against the R585 dogfood ../the-tide-that-counts (built via build-store.sh: 7 frames / 3 branches / 26 chapters / 58 facts): a clean candidate -> verdict commit, 0 violations, exit 0; a bad-frame fact -> rollback, 1 shape violation (verbatim registry message + repair), exit 1; two conflicting co-holding facts -> rollback, 1 continuity frame_conflict_overlap with full locus {facts, frame, branch, at} + expected + repair, exit 1
DRY-RUN proven: the tide store held 58 facts before and after every propose-verdict call — the real store is never written
the MCP tool needs a reinstall to appear (cargo install --path crates/mnemosyne-mcp --force + restart)
carry_forward_bullets: R585 debt item 3 is next: the authoring-frontier report (one consolidated coverage-gap surface an unattended loop pulls work from — scenes/nodes with zero facts, quests with no giver, dangling setups, never-planned disclosures, per-node coverage)
then item 4 all-primitive batch manifest (facts + entities + quests + branches + disclosure in one manifest for throughput), item 5 guardrail Validator gates
propose-verdict is rollback-on-ANY-violation (the loop authors clean); a severity-aware verdict (warn vs reject) is deferred until a consumer needs it
shape violations are fail-fast (one at a time); collecting every shape error in one pass would need a continue-on-error apply, deferred (a core-semantics change)

Round 589 — report-authoring-frontier: t.decision_summary: Build report-authoring-frontier (R585 debt item 3): one consolidated read that JOINs the scattered coverage-gap projections into the single surface an unattended generate-gate-repair loop pulls its next work from — zero-fact scenes + per-scene coverage + per-world dangling setups (R442) + (with a telling) unresolved quests (R568) + never-planned disclosures (R507). total_gaps is the distinct union across categories (a setup dangling in N worlds is counted once) — the loop's work-remaining gauge

a pure read JOIN over existing tested projections (R442/R507/R568), no new authoritative state, never gated (the dangling-is-a-todo discipline)

Round 590 — the all-primitive batch mani.decision_summary: Build the all-primitive batch manifest (R585 debt item 4): fold disclosure (plans + per-fact overrides) into the FactsManifest so frames + branches + entities + predicates + facts + disclosure land in ONE atomic import-facts transaction (registries -> facts -> disclosure, so an override references a same-manifest fact). Quests need no dedicated kind — a quest is an Entity{kind:quest} + pursues/requires/completed_by typed facts, already authored through the existing kinds. The two disclosure setters are refactored to share their in-memory cores with the manifest path (write-path parity), so no invariant is bypassed. propose-verdict now validates disclosure batches for free. The generate-gate-repair LLM loop stays outside; only the deterministic manifest surface grows.
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-atomic: FactsManifest gains disclosure_plans[] (DisclosurePlanImport{telling_id, default_mode?, description, overrides:[{fact_id, mode, first_at?, surface?}]}); apply_facts_manifest applies them AFTER facts so an override references a same-manifest fact and satisfies the typed-fact invariant

write-path parity (CLAUDE.md multi-write-path rule): factored apply_disclosure_plan + apply_disclosure_override in-memory cores out of add_disclosure_plan / set_disclosure — the manifest path and the standalone setters share the SAME invariant enforcement, so a manifest override can never bypass a check

byte-stable idempotency preserved: apply_disclosure_override returns whether the stored decision actually changed, so a no-op manifest re-import writes nothing; set_disclosure keeps its unconditional-persist contract

import-facts + propose-verdict pick up disclosure_plans automatically (both parse FactsManifest); CLI help + parse-hint updated; quests need no dedicated kind (Entity{kind:quest} + typed facts already authors them)

Round 591 — close R585 debt item 5 (AI-f.decision_summary: Close R585 debt item 5 (AI-failure guardrail Validator gates) as ALREADY-SATISFIED after a read-verified investigation — do NOT rebuild. The four named guardrails (hallucinated-ref, wrong-branch, orphan, citation-hygiene) already exist as the architecturally-correct kind of check, and re-implementing them as new Validator plugins would REGRESS correctness: a core store-integrity invariant belongs as a built-in fail-loud check re-checked by the continuity gate, never as an optional plugin (a plugin can be unregistered), and a second implementation is the half-enforced-invariant anti-pattern. Deliverable = this evidence-based closure + one describe-schema invariant_enforcement note so an agent discovers the out-of-band guardrail coverage; zero redundant code. The unified guardrail-dispatch runner stays YAGNI (the PluginRegistry Validator extension point already exists, pull-gated).

propose-verdict (item 2, R588) already surfaces the narrative guardrails as ActionableViolations for the loop — the machine-actionable feedback item 5 was after already exists

NOT built, with rationale: converting built-in invariants to plugins downgrades a hard invariant to an optional check (a plugin can be unregistered) = safety loss; a second implementation duplicates enforcement (the CLAUDE.md half-enforced-invariant ban); a unified guardrail-dispatch runner is YAGNI (the PluginRegistry Validator extension point already exists, pull-gate it when a real second consumer arrives)

Round 592 — textbook review of R587-R591.decision_summary: Owner-requested cost-no-object textbook review of the R585 authoring-support arc (R587-R591), via an independent adversarial reviewer + self-audit. Verdict: sound craft but NOT textbook as-is — one real correctness+SSOT defect at the center (propose-verdict applied a stricter, divergent, non-single-sourced gate policy than validate-continuity). All 5 findings fixed this round: single-source the continuity gate policy, a fact-shape drift guard + honest drift-claim scoping, ops unit tests, a single-sourced manifest-shape hint, and stored-result parity in the disclosure test.
changes_bullets: FINDING 1 (HIGH, correctness+SSOT): propose-verdict ignored the configured [continuity] severity — it rolled back on store-VALID content (interval time-bends with interval_severity OFF by default, warn/info severity, gate-disabled workspaces), diverging from validate-continuity and re-deriving a second gating rule. FIX: extracted evaluate_continuity_gate (ContinuityGateOutcome) as THE single-sourced per-class reject policy; both cmd_validate_continuity and propose_verdict call it; propose_verdict now mirrors the store policy exactly, surfaces ALL violations, and reports gating_violation_count

FINDING 2 (SSOT): describe-schema's "cannot silently fall behind the code" was overstated (only the enum/rule-class parts are compile-guarded). FIX: narrowed the module doc to three honest tiers (compile-guarded / test-guarded / hand-authored prose) + added a fact-shape drift-guard test binding fact.fields to FactImport's serde field set

FINDING 3 (tests): propose_verdict + authoring_frontier_report had no unit tests. FIX: added ops tests (commit / shape-rollback / severity-mirror [which locks in finding 1] / frontier gaps+telling-gating) + the pure evaluate_continuity_gate policy test

FINDING 4 (drift): the manifest shape was described by hand in >=4 spots that had drifted (cmd_propose_verdict still said "frames + facts", MCP omitted disclosure_plans). FIX: single-sourced FACTS_MANIFEST_SHAPE const for the runtime parse hints + corrected the MCP static doc
FINDING 5 (nit): the disclosure parity test checked accept/reject parity only. FIX: added stored-result equality (store_a.disclosure_plans == store_b) on the accept cases
verification_bullets: method: an independent adversarial reviewer (fresh-context agent, the R469/R500 self-report-not-trusted discipline) + self-audit; the reviewer confirmed the disclosure write-path parity is sound, the dry run genuinely never persists, idempotency holds, R591's close-as-satisfied restraint was right, and layering is right

cargo build + clippy -D warnings + fmt clean; full suites green (atomic 187, validate 276, ops 13) including 5 new tests; e2e: a conflict under reject severity -> rollback (1 gating); the severity-mirror unit test proves warn severity -> commit-with-surfaced-violation (the finding-1 fix)

honest note (not downplayed): I had rated finding 1 "minor" — the reviewer correctly escalated it to the central defect (the exact downplaying my own guidance forbids); recording it so the lesson sticks
carry_forward_bullets: the per-class continuity gate policy is now single-sourced (evaluate_continuity_gate); the other is_reject gate sites (disclosure-leak / render-fidelity / spec-drift) are DIFFERENT gates with their own severity, out of scope
describe-schema's registry/invariant descriptions remain hand-authored semantic prose (the one un-auto-guarded tier, now documented as such) — projecting the enforcement, not a second source of it
the R585 authoring-support arc is now review-hardened: R587-R591 built, R592 reviewed and fixed; next frontier is the narrative-authoring track / pinion consumer per the RESUME

Round 593 — unattended generate-gate-rep.decision_summary: design/manifest round for unattended-loop-experiment/v1: prove the R585-R592 generate-gate-repair loop authors a small game unattended (sha-pinned, code 0)
changes_bullets: Authored claudedocs/phase1-unattended-loop-experiment/v1/ (design/manifest round, code 0): premise.md (fresh IP-free "The Ban Tower" small game — 14-20 scenes, 4-6 frames, one two-branch fork, a withheld secret, one quest, setup->payoff chains), loop-agent-brief.md, render-brief.md, judge-brief.md, runbook.md, manifest.json.

The experiment runs the generate->propose-verdict->repair->report-authoring-frontier loop UNATTENDED: a blind agent authors from describe-schema + premise + the CLI only (no source reading), self-repairs from the actionable violations, and pulls its next work from the frontier until it reports 0 zero-fact scenes / 0 dangling setups / 0 unresolved quests. It closes R592's honest residue — the R585-R592 surfaces are built + reviewed but the loop that CONSUMES them was never run end to end (infra is not proof, R500).

Three sha-pinned pins: PIN-1 convergence (orchestrator re-derives on a FRESH rebuild from the agent's JSON, R500 self-report-not-trusted), PIN-2 surfaces-drove-it (every repair traces to a propose-verdict violation or a frontier gap, from loop-log.md — falsifiable), PIN-3 coherence (warm render, leak 0 + fidelity off_path 0, 3 blind judges Overall+Coherence >= 4/5 — the store-consistency != coherence backstop).

R469 firewall: this lineage orchestrates + verifies only; blind subagents author/render/judge. Decision rule pre-committed four ways: loop_proven / surfaces_insufficient / coherence_gap / contaminated. Three predictions preregistered (converges but >= 2 repair rounds; early violations are reference-shape; the honest next gap is a missing guidance surface not a missing gate).

carry_forward_bullets: NEXT = R594 EXECUTE unattended-loop-experiment/v1 behind the experiment-execution consent gate (the owner chose (A)): run runbook.md's THE ONE PROMPT — spawn the blind loop agent, verify PIN-1 on a fresh rebuild, audit PIN-2 from loop-log.md, assemble the render road outline + quest briefing, spawn the blind render + extractor + 3 judges for PIN-3, apply the decision rule, write report.md + the R594 changelog entry. Round 595 — describe-schema emits the ma.decision_summary: close unattended-loop Finding 1: describe-schema now documents the JSON wire format (registry keys, typed-object scalar->value tagging, first_at tuples) + a drift-guarded worked example that passes propose-verdict; also paid a stale import-facts-smoke test from R590
changes_bullets: Closed unattended-loop-experiment/v1 Finding 1 (the headline): describe-schema now emits the manifest JSON WIRE FORMAT, not just the semantic contract. Added ManifestWireSpec to SchemaContract (mnemosyne-validate::schema) — the six-array apply order, per-kind serialized JSON key names, the typed-object enum wire tagging, the first_at [branch, section] tuple shape, and a complete worked example. The CLI renders it; --json carries it via serde.

Documented the load-bearing naming quirk head-on: a predicate object_kind `scalar` serializes its typed object with tag `value` (not `scalar`), an `entity` object_kind with `{kind:entity, id}`. Chose to DOCUMENT rather than rename the wire enum — a rename would break deserialization of every existing store's {kind:value} typed legs (a data migration), so the doc fix removes the trap at zero risk.

The worked example (MANIFEST_EXAMPLE_JSON) is drift-guarded: a unit test parses it through the real mnemosyne_atomic::FactsManifest and pins its contents (the scalar Value variant, the entity Entity{id} variant, the fork forks_from string, the first_at pair) — a wire-format change breaks the build here. Not just serde-parseable: the example extracted from describe-schema --json passes propose-verdict (commit, 0 violations) against a store carrying its sections, so an agent can copy it and it validates end to end.

DEBT paid (surfaced by this round's test run, RULEBOOK same-session rule): import_facts_smoke.rs asserted the pre-R590 summary string ("... N facts created") — stale since R590 added the disclosure-plans/overrides suffix; the pre-commit/pre-push hooks run validate+clippy but not the full suite, so it slipped R590-R594. Updated both assertions to the R590 format (the atomic-lib unit tests already had it; only this CLI smoke was missed).

Round 600 — session review of R593-R599:.decision_summary: cost-no-object textbook review (independent adversarial reviewer + self-audit) of the R593-R599 arc: 4 findings all fixed — the wire-format prose is now drift-guarded (was advertised-but-not), the dangling transform de-duplicated to one home, canon-order prose guarded, a doc imprecision corrected; cost-compromises + a north-star pivot surfaced
changes_bullets: REVIEW round (owner-requested cost-no-object textbook review of the R593-R599 session): one independent adversarial reviewer + a self-audit. 4 findings, ALL FIXED this round; the deterministic parts verified clean, not rubber-stamped.

Finding 1 (top, SSOT/drift): describe-schema's ManifestWireSpec doc advertised "drift-guarded" but only example_json was — the per-kind json_keys + typed_object_wire prose was an un-tested SECOND encoding, and keys named only in prose (converges_from / canon_to / supersedes_in_frame / conflicts_with / quote / entities.description / surface) were renameable in serde without breaking a test, so describe-schema could hand an agent a stale wire contract. FIXED: manifest_wire_prose_names_every_serde_key serializes a fully-populated FactsManifest and asserts every serde key (incl. nested confluence/typed/surface shapes) is named in the wire prose — a rename now breaks the build; the doc-comment + the module drift-guard tiers made accurate.

Finding 2 (SSOT/DRY): the payoff->dangling-by-world transform was copy-pasted in propose_verdict (R599) and authoring_frontier_report (R589). FIXED: PayoffCoverageReport::dangling_by_world() — one home beside the data, both call sites use it.

Finding 3 (drift): the canon_order prose was un-guarded static over CanonOrderFile. FIXED: added Serialize to CanonOrderFile (loader only deserializes it) + the drift test pins its structural keys (edges/branches) to the prose.

Finding 4 (doc imprecision): the dangling_setups doc said "the verdict stays commit"; a populated dangling map can ride a rollback caused by OTHER findings. FIXED: reworded to "dangling never flips the verdict". Verified CLEAN (sound, not touched): unordered_scenes across edge cases (terminal/branch-only scenes, empty order, zero-fact/unordered disjointness), the value-tag prose.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace green (exit 0, 96 test binaries) — the new drift guard CAUGHT a real bug during development (a stale test data-key vs a struct field) before passing, and pins the wire+canon-order prose to serde; cargo clippy --workspace -D warnings clean; cargo fmt clean; validate-workspace clean (T1 orphan 0 / T3 reject 0).

The SSOT fix is behaviour-preserving (dangling_by_world is the extracted copy-paste, byte-identical); the drift guard is additive test coverage; CanonOrderFile.Serialize is additive (no code writes it). No production gate/schema/data semantics changed.
carry_forward_bullets: Honest cost-compromises the review SURFACED (named for the record, not defects): (1) the wire format is hand-authored prose + a drift-guard test, NOT schemars-derived schema generation — chosen to keep the empirically-validated concise agent-readable format (v2: 0 wire probes) over verbose derived schema, with the drift hole now closed by the test; (2) the unattended loop never exercised the continuity gate (no premise stressed exclusive/interval/transition = a proof gap); (3) n=1 throughout; (4) v2 had no render/judge (confirmation, not re-proof); (5) the "no source read" firewall claim rests on the loop-log narrative, not the agent transcript.

STRATEGIC (north-star): R595-R600 are north-star-load-bearing (the surfaces ARE consumed by the loop; fixes validated by re-running it), NOT the R576-R584 spec-tooling treadmill. BUT the loop is now proven + hardened; the remaining findings (gap B leak-visibility, Finding 2 fork-lineage doc) are diminishing-returns polish. The next high-value move is likely the pinion CONSUMER or a harder authoring frontier (scale / the unexercised continuity muscle), not more friction-closing.

NEXT = R601 owner cadence: (A) pinion CONSUMER [the north-star consumer — recommended pivot off the friction-closing treadmill] / (B) close gap B + Finding 2 (cheap polish) / (C) stress the continuity gate with a possession/timeline premise (fills the proof gap) / (D) another experiment. Round 601 — describe-schema documents th.decision_summary: Close unattended-loop v2 gap B + v1 Finding 2 by DOCUMENTING both in describe-schema (the root-cause fix, not a structural check): a new disclosure_encoding contract field spells out the withhold+first_at per-road reveal idiom and that a clean report-authoring-frontier is necessary-but-not-sufficient for a leak-free telling (a leak is a render property, not structurally checkable pre-render — a frontier leak-check would be a half-enforced invariant), and the branches registry documents the fork-lineage dead-prefix dangling trap two independent loop agents hit.
changes_bullets: describe-schema: new SchemaContract.disclosure_encoding field — the withhold+first_at per-world reveal idiom (a per-road secret stays mode=withhold with a first_at pin on the reveal-road only; mode=state/hint/imply is world-independent and discloses on EVERY world-line, so it leaks), plus the caveat that a clean report-authoring-frontier does NOT certify a correct telling (a premature leak/early-reveal is a render property, caught only by the disclosure leak gate + report-playthrough-manuscript over re-extracted prose).

describe-schema: the branches registry description documents the FORK-LINEAGE TRAP two independent loop agents hit — a fork inherits the parent prefix, but bare main stays its own dead-prefix world-line still carrying the trunk setups; forking both roads off main and never continuing bare main leaves those expected setups dangling on main. Fix recorded: continue main AS one road, or pay the trunk setups off before the fork.

CLI describe-schema human render prints the new disclosure-encoding section; JSON + MCP auto-include via serde. New unit test disclosure_encoding_and_fork_lineage_trap_are_documented pins the load-bearing concepts (withhold / first_at / leak / report-authoring-frontier + dead-prefix / dangle) — the tier-3 hand-authored-prose guard; contract_serializes_to_json now also asserts disclosure_encoding ships.
verification_bullets: cargo build + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --all applied (one long assert line reflowed).
cargo test -p mnemosyne-validate -p mnemosyne-ops -p mnemosyne-cli green, incl. the new disclosure_encoding_and_fork_lineage_trap_are_documented test and the extended contract_serializes_to_json.

e2e smoke: mnemosyne-cli describe-schema renders both new sections (disclosure encoding + FORK-LINEAGE TRAP); validate-workspace clean (T1 orphan=0, T3 reject=0, atomic orphan_refs +0 new, commit-ledger drift missing=0).
carry_forward_bullets: The unattended authoring loop is now PROVEN (R594) + HARDENED across every R594/R598 next-layer finding: gap A (R599 dangling in the dry run), gap B + v1 Finding 2 (R601, documented in describe-schema).

CONSIDERED AND REJECTED (recorded, not a defect): folding a leak check into report-authoring-frontier. A leak is a RENDER property (the two-store render-acceptance gate family, needs re-extracted prose), and pre-render a mode=state secret is structurally indistinguishable from a legitimately open fact; a partial frontier leak-check would be a half-enforced invariant (CLAUDE.md) giving false frontier-clean=leak-free confidence. The root cause was the missing encoding idiom — now in describe-schema.disclosure_encoding.

NEXT (R600 strategic note, owner cadence — ask): the loop is proven + hardened and remaining findings are diminishing-returns polish; the high-value move is off the friction treadmill — (A) pinion CONSUMER [recommended] render the verified projection in 3D+GUI, or (B) stress the continuity gate's rule classes (exclusive/interval/transition), the R600 proof gap. Gate on the owner's choice.

Round 602 — continuity-stress-experiment.decision_summary: Design continuity-stress-experiment/v1 (sha-pinned): does the unattended loop exercise the continuity gate's rule classes (exclusive/transition/interval) and does the gate BITE on real authored content? = the R600 proof gap. Grounded design finding: describe-schema documents the rule CLASSES but not the rules-FILE wire nor the [continuity].rules_path wiring, so the loop was never told how to turn the rules on — pre-registered PRED-1 (surface_gap the likely outcome, teeth still proven). Premise 'The Night Lock at Harrow Sluice' forces all three classes (lantern custody / gate state-machine / fill-time). Pins: PIN-1 declared+typed, PIN-2 teeth-bite via deterministic negative control, PIN-3 loop-log friction record; judged coherence backstop. R469 firewall; DESIGN round code 0; EXECUTE = R603 (consent given via 실험).
changes_bullets: Authored claudedocs/phase1-continuity-stress-experiment/v1/: manifest.json (sha256 184358e1…, pre-execution pin), premise.md ('The Night Lock at Harrow Sluice' — one lantern custody = exclusive per:object, gate barred->cracked->open = transition, pound fill >= N min = interval), loop-agent-brief.md (adds the rule-declaration task; deliberately withholds the rules-file wire = the surface under test), runbook.md (orchestrator steps incl. the PIN-2 deterministic negative-control probes).

Grounded design finding (verified in continuity.rs + the describe-schema output): the rules-FILE authoring surface is undocumented — describe-schema emits the rule CLASSES + parameters but not the rules-file JSON wire ({schema, rules:[{id,predicate,class,per|allowed|right/op/bound}]}) nor the [continuity].rules_path wiring (canon-order has canon_order_path documented; rules has no equivalent). Pre-registered as PRED-1: likely WHY v1/v2 never exercised the classes — the loop was never told how to turn the rules on.

R469 firewall: this lineage orchestrates only (seed + gates + the PIN-2 negative-control = the R570 remove-and-rescan VERIFICATION, not narrative authoring); blind subagents author (loop) / render / judge. Pins: PIN-1 declared+typed (>= 1 of each class on real typed legs), PIN-2 teeth-bite (each rule's violation rejects + removal passes), PIN-3 loop-log friction record; judged coherence backstop.

Round 604 — describe-schema documents th.decision_summary: Close the continuity-stress-experiment/v1 surface_gap (R603 finding): describe-schema now documents the rules-file authoring surface AND a misspelled [continuity] key fails loud. New SchemaContract.narrative_rules_wire field = the rules-file JSON wire (incl. the bound tagged shape), the [continuity].rules_path/rules_sha256 wiring, and the interval_severity opt-in; ContinuitySection gains deny_unknown_fields so a typo like narrative_rules_path rejects instead of silently leaving the rules unwired. 2 of the 3 R603 frictions fixed structurally; the interval surface-only default is R491-deliberate so it is DOCUMENTED, not changed. R595/R596 pattern for the strongest gates + a genuine no-silent-fail safety fix (CLAUDE.md).
changes_bullets: describe-schema: new SchemaContract.narrative_rules_wire field — the rules-file JSON wire ({schema?, rules:[{id,predicate,class,per|allowed|right/op/bound}]}, incl. bound as a tagged {const}|{predicate}, never a bare number), the [continuity].rules_path (+ rules_sha256) wiring, and that interval rules are surface-only unless [continuity].interval_severity=reject. CLI render line + JSON/MCP auto-include; a unit test pins the load-bearing concepts (rules_path / rules_sha256 / interval_severity / the bound tags / all 3 classes).

config fail-loud (the real safety fix): ContinuitySection gains #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] so a misspelled [continuity] key (e.g. narrative_rules_path for rules_path) REJECTS at config load instead of being silently ignored — the R603 footgun where the loop had to sweep candidate key names. A unit test proves the reject names the bad key AND that valid keys still parse (no over-reject); other config sections are a noted follow-up (each needs its own audit).

interval-default (the 3rd friction) is DOCUMENTED, not changed: interval's surface-only default is DELIBERATE (R491 — a scalar timeline gap can be a legitimate authored time-bend), so the fix is to spell out interval_severity=reject in describe-schema, not to flip the default.
verification_bullets: cargo build + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --all applied; cargo test (mnemosyne-config / validate / ops / cli) green incl. 2 new tests: narrative_rules_wire_documents_the_authoring_surface, continuity_rejects_an_unknown_key_fail_loud.

e2e smoke (built binary): describe-schema renders the narrative-rules-wire section; validate-workspace on a real misspelled [continuity] key fails loud naming the offending key (TOML parse error at narrative_rules_path), and the correct rules_path parses + validate runs.
repo validate-workspace clean (entries 350 -> 351 on this append); citation hygiene: R603 / R595 / R596 / R491 verified present before citing.
carry_forward_bullets: surface_gap CLOSED: a blind loop can now declare + wire a narrative rule from describe-schema alone (no reverse-engineering the wire shape / the rules_path key / the interval opt-in), and a misspelled [continuity] key fails loud instead of silently leaving the gate off. The continuity gate's strongest classes (exclusive/transition/interval, R600/R603-proven to bite) are now REACHABLE without the R603 frictions.

Follow-up (noted, YAGNI until pulled): deny_unknown_fields on the OTHER config sections (each needs its own audit before flipping); an optional notice when an interval rule is declared with interval_severity off (an R491-opt-in nudge). Neither pulled yet.

Round 605 — cost-no-object review of R60.decision_summary: Owner-requested cost-no-object session review of R601-R604 (independent adversarial reviewer + self-audit) raised 5 findings; ALL FIXED this round. F1: the deterministic leak+fidelity gates R601's own doc preaches but R603 skipped are now RUN (blind re-extraction -> leaks=0 vocabulary_shared=13 non-vacuous + fidelity off_path=0), so 'disclosure clean' rests on the gate not a judge read. F2: narrative_rules_wire moved into the TEST-guarded tier (3 wire structs gain Serialize + a reflection test pins the prose to every serde key, mirroring manifest_wire; the latent comment-key omission it exposed is fixed). F3: deny_unknown_fields extended from one section to WorkspaceConfig + all 15 sections, exposing + cleaning R400-era dead config keys (docs/default_doc/output_path) across 13 files. F4: PIN-1/PIN-2/PIN-3 gate outputs preserved verbatim under pins/. F5 folds into F2. Independent adversarial reviewer + self-audit; not sycophantic.
changes_bullets: F2 (SSOT/drift): narrative_rules_wire is a SERIALIZATION contract, so the 3 rules-file wire structs (NarrativeRulesWire/NarrativeRuleWire/IntervalBoundWire) + RuleClass/ExclusiveKey/IntervalOp gain Serialize and a new reflection test (narrative_rules_wire_prose_names_every_serde_key) pins the describe-schema prose to every emitted serde key, mirroring manifest_wire's guard and replacing the vacuous substring test; the latent `comment`-key omission the reflection guard exposed is added; the module header moves the rules wire into the TEST-guarded tier (every serialization contract is now reflection-pinned, none left hand-authored).

F3 (no-silent-fail, CLAUDE.md): #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] extended from ContinuitySection alone to WorkspaceConfig + all 15 config sections (no flatten anywhere = mechanically safe), so a misspelled section HEADER or leaf key fails loud, not just one leaf. This exposed R400-era DEAD config keys silently ignored since the markdown model was removed — docs/default_doc under [workspace], output_path under [atomic] — across 13 test files + 2 stale doc-comments; all removed (dead-key removal, never legacy revival).

F1+F4 (rigor): the deterministic render gates R601's own describe-schema doc preaches ("run them, not an afterthought") but R603 skipped now RUN on the R603 render — a blind re-extraction (render-reextracted-facts.json) -> validate-disclosure-leak leaks=0/vocabulary_shared=13 (non-vacuous, the withheld secret re-extracted only at its sc-11 pin) + validate-render-fidelity off_path=0/reached_terminal=true; PIN-1/PIN-2/PIN-3 gate outputs are preserved verbatim under pins/, so every fresh-rebuild claim is independently re-checkable. report.md's disclosure finding now rests on the gate, not the judge (F5 folds into F2).
verification_bullets: cargo build + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --all applied; cargo test --workspace green (96 test binaries) incl. the new reflection test + continuity_rejects_an_unknown_key_fail_loud; the full sweep confirms zero remaining dead config keys (no `unknown field` errors).

F1 e2e (pins/PIN-3-render-gates.txt): validate-disclosure-leak --telling default --world main --truth-frame ground-truth = leaks=0 unordered=0 unmatched=0 truth_frame_typed=13 vocabulary_shared=13 (1 targeted); validate-render-fidelity --world main = off_path=0 unplaced=0 reached_terminal=true. F4: pins/PIN-1-fresh-rebuild.txt + pins/PIN-2-negative-control.txt hold the verbatim rebuild + negative-control verdicts.
repo validate-workspace clean (entries 351 -> 352 on this append); citation hygiene: R601 / R603 / R604 / R510 / R570 / R491 / R400 verified present before citing.
carry_forward_bullets: R601-R604 now meet the cost-no-object bar: the rules-file wire is reflection-guarded (cannot silently drift, and the guard already caught a real omission), config typos fail loud repo-wide (with the R400 dead-key debt cleaned), and the experiment's disclosure claim + fresh-rebuild verdicts are deterministically backed + tracked. The review's sharpest finding (R601 preaches the leak gate; R603 skipped it) is resolved by actually running it.

Follow-ups (noted, YAGNI until pulled): an interval-off notice (the R491 opt-in nudge, the one silent-surprise left as doc-only); the transition rule under-dramatizes in the prose (a craft note from the judge, not a defect). Neither pulled.

Round 606 — validate-continuity names a.decision_summary: The R604/R605 YAGNI-deferred follow-up is paid: the one remaining silent surprise in the continuity opt-in — a DECLARED interval rule that silently never gates because interval_severity is OFF by default (Round 491: a timeline gap can be a deliberate authored time-bend, so gating is the author opt-in) — is now LOUD at runtime, not doc-only. validate-continuity prints a NOTICE naming the count of declared interval-class rules whenever at least one is declared and interval_severity is not reject. The count rides a new interval_rules field on ContinuityReport (and its ContinuityScanReport DTO mirror), the interval-class subset of the existing rules count; the NOTICE combines that datum with the resolved interval_severity policy in the CLI presentation layer (SSOT: the count is data in the report, the nudge is presentation in the CLI). describe-schema gains a matching clause so the doc (Round 604) and the runtime agree. This NUDGES, it does not gate — the OFF default is deliberate (verify-before-claiming), so the notice stays advisory, never a policy change.

Round 607 — check_store_boundary exempts.decision_summary: A consumer (the-tide-that-counts, adapting a novel into a branching game) hit a false reject: add-branch <C> --converges main=<sec> --converges <fork>=<sec> succeeds at mutate, but every canon-order consumer (validate-continuity / report-fork-tree / report-payoff-coverage / manuscript) then fails with 'canon-order declares an edge set for branch main, which is not in the branch registry'. Root cause: check_store_boundary's declared-branch loop was the only one of four sibling registry checks missing the MAIN_BRANCH exemption. world_order_composition keys MAIN_BRANCH when main is a confluence PARENT (its own docstring: 'a confluence parent may be main'), that key flows through from_declaration into branch_reach, and declared_branches() then yields main — which the unguarded loop rejects because main is never registered. The fix adds the b != MAIN_BRANCH guard, identical to the fact / branch / world siblings in the same function. This unblocks the documented main-as-confluence-parent topology, letting a completed main trunk be re-woven with a forked braid without shattering the canon trunk into a dead prefix (the R601 fork-lineage trap).
changes_bullets: check_store_boundary (continuity.rs): the declared-branch registry loop gains the `b != MAIN_BRANCH` guard, matching its fact / branch / world sibling checks in the same function — main is legitimately yielded by declared_branches() only as a confluence parent

verification_bullets: the new test fails without the guard with the exact reported error (branch `main` ... not in the branch registry) and passes with it — an empirically proven regression guard, not just a green assertion

negative path intact: an order declaring a genuinely unregistered branch (ghost) is still rejected with the original registry message
carry_forward_bullets: the four store-boundary registry checks now share the MAIN_BRANCH exemption uniformly — no half-enforced-invariant asymmetry remains in check_store_boundary (declared_branches has exactly one caller, so the fix is total)

Round 608 — the continuity gate scopes m.decision_summary: An adversarial self-review of R607 found the boundary fix was necessary but NOT total; R607's own carry-forward claimed 'every canon consumer unblocked / the fix is total', which is false. query_world_lineages — the lineage map consumed by scan_continuity, timeline_gaps, and edge_candidates — hardcoded main to Lineage::default() (empty forward) and computed lineage_of only for registered branches, and main is never a registered branch. The READ surfaces (payoff_coverage / frame_view / irony_intervals / playthrough_manuscript) instead call lineage_of per world, which computes forward = forward_confluences(world). So for a main-as-confluence-parent store the gate saw main.forward = {} while the view saw main.forward = {the confluence}. Consequence: a conflict between a main-exclusive-middle fact and a confluence-suffix fact returns None from join_world (the R535 forward arm lineages[main].forward.contains(suffix) is false), drops into cross_scope_pairs, and is NEVER checked — a silent false-negative in the flagship validate-continuity gate, for exactly the topology R607 unblocked. The gate and the view drifted, violating the 'holds_at is THE single holds-semantics shared by gate and view' invariant. Fix: query_world_lineages now flows main through the same lineage_of walk as every branch (main is no longer special-cased), making it genuinely 'THE single construction' its docstring claims. Byte-stable for pre-confluence stores, where forward_confluences(main) is empty so lineage_of(main) equals Lineage::default().
changes_bullets: query_world_lineages (continuity.rs): main flows through the same lineage_of walk as every registered branch instead of a hardcoded Lineage::default() — its forward confluence-suffixes are now populated when main is a confluence parent, so the construction is genuinely single-sourced

regression test confluence_cross_merge_conflict_scopes_to_main_parent, built through import_facts (the real write path): a suffix fact conflicting with main's exclusive middle is scoped to main, not bucketed into cross_scope_pairs
verification_bullets: the new test fails on R607 code (cross_scope_pairs=1, violations=[]) and passes after the fix — an empirically proven false-negative, the exact main analog of the passing confluence_cross_merge_conflict_scopes_to_parent (registered-parent) test

swept for other hardcoded-main-lineage sites: the removed Lineage::default() at line 1393 was the only one, no sibling gate path missed
carry_forward_bullets: corrects R607's frozen carry-forward: 'the fix is total' was an over-claim — the boundary fix unblocked the topology but this second gate path (query_world_lineages feeding scan_continuity / timeline_gaps / edge_candidates) still under-scoped it; the ledger self-corrects forward, never mutating the frozen R607 entry

found by an independent adversarial reviewer (R605 cost-no-object precedent); the read surfaces (payoff_coverage / frame_view / playthrough_manuscript) were already correct because they call lineage_of per world

Round 609 — is_known_world single-source.decision_summary: The R607 review's MEDIUM finding: the guard 'a valid world-line reference is MAIN_BRANCH or a registered branch' was hand-copied as world != MAIN_BRANCH && !branches.contains_key(world) at 12 sites across mnemosyne-core's consumers (mnemosyne-validate continuity x6, mnemosyne-atomic write path x4, mnemosyne-ops x2), with no single source. That un-single-sourced invariant IS the R607 bug's root: one site (the declared-branch loop) was added without the guard because nothing named or forced the concept. Fix: a single primitive mnemosyne_core::is_known_world(branches, world) beside its world-line siblings (fork_chain / forward_confluences / MAIN_BRANCH), with all 12 sites routed through !is_known_world(...). Pure refactor: !(world == MAIN || contains(world)) is (world != MAIN && !contains(world)) by De Morgan, and each call site keeps its own bespoke fail-loud message. Now a 13th world-ref guard cannot silently drop the main exemption — the concept has one name and one definition.
changes_bullets: new primitive mnemosyne_core::is_known_world(branches, world) = world == MAIN_BRANCH || branches.contains_key(world), placed beside fork_chain / forward_confluences and re-exported at the crate root

12 hand-copied `!= MAIN_BRANCH && !contains_key` guards routed through !is_known_world across mnemosyne-validate (6), mnemosyne-atomic (4), mnemosyne-ops (2); each site keeps its own fail-loud message
unit test is_known_world_covers_main_and_registered_only pins the predicate: main known on an empty registry, a registered branch known, an unknown id not

Round 611 — a fork off a confluence-chai.decision_summary: MNEMO-GAP-003, filed by the-tide-that-counts (the game-adaptation consumer) as the next step after GAP-002/R607: once the canon trunk becomes a CHAIN of confluences — which any upstream re-convergence braid creates — a DIVERGENT line forking off that trunk stops seeing the mid-trunk facts the merge displaced onto the confluence, even facts at-or-before its own fork point. Reproduced on the installed R609 CLI: with a braid re-converging into `weave` (which then OWNS the s3-s4 suffix), an `ending` forking off main@s3 lost `f-mid`@s3 — while `main` and the braid walked the full trunk correctly, so the re-convergence itself was never broken; only the fork off it was. ROOT CAUSE: fork inheritance was solved against the ancestor's OWNED facts rather than the ancestor's COMPOSED walk — `visibility`'s backward arm read only `fork_chain` (`cut`) and never the `forward_confluences` those ancestors flow into, so a re-convergence that moved a trunk segment onto a confluence made that segment invisible to anything forking off the trunk. The engine had already named this the deferred series-parallel generalization (the `join_world` docstring). DECISION: extend fork inheritance from "the ancestor's own facts" to "the ancestor's composed walk", BOUNDED at the departure — a third lineage relation `cut_forward` (a confluence an ancestor flows into -> that ancestor's departure point), resolved through the SAME bounded ladder `cut` uses. Three legs, because any one alone is a half-invariant: visibility (one fix point for every read surface), order composition (so the divergent world can compare a displaced coordinate to its fork), and join_world (so recorded-conflict scoping tracks the visibility instead of silently bucketing a co-holding pair as cross-scope). The BOUND is what stops a divergent ending from stealing the SHARED tail: it inherits the trunk prefix only up to its fork; the re-convergence suffix past it stays Out. Transitivity is free — `forward_confluences` is already a transitive closure, so a fork off a two-link chain inherits BOTH links (the consumer's real F1+F2+F4 topology). Byte-invariant for fork-only and single-confluence stores.
changes_bullets: Lineage gains cut_forward (a confluence a fork ANCESTOR flows into -> that ancestor's departure bound), built in lineage_of from fork_chain x forward_confluences; bounded_visibility extracted so the cut and cut_forward arms share ONE ladder and cannot drift

visibility gains the bounded backward-then-forward arm — one fix point for every read surface (payoff/dangling, playthrough manuscript, frame-at-T, irony intervals, the typed-rule world sweep all route through visibility)

world_order_composition adds each fork ancestor's confluences as order contributors, so a divergent world can COMPARE a displaced coordinate to its fork point (else a past-the-fork suffix fact is Unknown rather than definitively Out, and the fork's own fact on a displaced coordinate false-trips the R488 FactCanonOffBranch guard)

join_world gains the matching arm so recorded-conflict scoping tracks the extended visibility — without it the walk would show two facts co-holding while the conflict gate silently bucketed them as cross_scope_pairs (the half-enforced-invariant trap)
