Round 611 — a fork off a confluence-chai.publishable_decision_summary: MNEMO-GAP-003, filed by the-tide-that-counts (the game-adaptation consumer) as the next step after GAP-002/R607: once the canon trunk becomes a CHAIN of confluences — which any upstream re-convergence braid creates — a DIVERGENT line forking off that trunk stops seeing the mid-trunk facts the merge displaced onto the confluence, even facts at-or-before its own fork point. Reproduced on the installed R609 CLI: with a braid re-converging into `weave` (which then OWNS the s3-s4 suffix), an `ending` forking off main@s3 lost `f-mid`@s3 — while `main` and the braid walked the full trunk correctly, so the re-convergence itself was never broken; only the fork off it was. ROOT CAUSE: fork inheritance was solved against the ancestor's OWNED facts rather than the ancestor's COMPOSED walk — `visibility`'s backward arm read only `fork_chain` (`cut`) and never the `forward_confluences` those ancestors flow into, so a re-convergence that moved a trunk segment onto a confluence made that segment invisible to anything forking off the trunk. The engine had already named this the deferred series-parallel generalization (the `join_world` docstring). DECISION: extend fork inheritance from "the ancestor's own facts" to "the ancestor's composed walk", BOUNDED at the departure — a third lineage relation `cut_forward` (a confluence an ancestor flows into -> that ancestor's departure point), resolved through the SAME bounded ladder `cut` uses. Three legs, because any one alone is a half-invariant: visibility (one fix point for every read surface), order composition (so the divergent world can compare a displaced coordinate to its fork), and join_world (so recorded-conflict scoping tracks the visibility instead of silently bucketing a co-holding pair as cross-scope). The BOUND is what stops a divergent ending from stealing the SHARED tail: it inherits the trunk prefix only up to its fork; the re-convergence suffix past it stays Out. Transitivity is free — `forward_confluences` is already a transitive closure, so a fork off a two-link chain inherits BOTH links (the consumer's real F1+F2+F4 topology). Byte-invariant for fork-only and single-confluence stores.

Round 612 — world-line membership is ONE.decision_summary: A cost-no-object review of R611 (three INDEPENDENT adversarial reviewers, the discipline the memory prescribes and that R611 skipped) returned a verdict I accept: cut_forward was a PATCH, not the model. R438 added one lineage relation, R533 a second, R611 a third — and each round the enumeration missed a case the next one would have to bolt on. The reviewers proved the miss is not hypothetical: a fork off a CONFLUENCE (the second link of every subway-braid chain, i.e. the consumer's actual F1+F2+F4 topology) lost the ENTIRE pre-merge trunk, silently; a non-monotone fork chain leaked; a confluence world returned a prefix-less fragment; and R611 left succession at the OLD width, so the gate would report a contradiction in a divergent world and then reject the only sanctioned way to fix it. DECISION: replace the enumerated relations with the series-parallel LATTICE the gap report itself pointed at. World-line membership is a map from branch to the departure coordinates a fact must be at-or-before — ALL of them, conjoined. FORK = conjoin a bound. MERGE = INTERSECT the parents. That is closed: a fork off a confluence, a fork off a fork off a confluence, nested confluences, a confluence whose parent is a confluence all fall out of those two rules, so there is no fourth relation to add, ever. Two consequences make it more than a refactor. (a) Carrying the bound as a SET rather than folding it into a `min` keeps the lattice ORDER-FREE, which is what finally lets the write path (succession) and the read path (visibility) share ONE definition instead of three that drift — the canon order compares against each bound later, at visibility time. (b) The intersection at a merge is a genuinely new and provably right semantics: a confluence continues only what EVERY incoming road carried, so the path-independent trunk survives while each parent's exclusive middle is dropped — which is exactly why a fork off it now inherits the trunk. join_world stops being a hand-enumerated relation and becomes the bounded visibility itself ("a world where BOTH facts are In"), which as a side effect CLOSES the sibling-confluence-common-parent case deferred since R533 — it literally is the "In-set intersection over the query worlds" that docstring said would be needed. HONEST BOUNDARY, stated so this entry cannot be misread: R612 fixes the FACT axis only. The COORDINATE axis (CanonOrder::names / linearize / is_maximal read R611's unbounded order widening) is STILL BROKEN — render-fidelity is blind for divergent worlds, the R488 wrong-branch guard is silent past the fork, and the pinion seam emits phantom scenes. Verified still reproducing after this round. That is the next round, and it must first answer a substrate question this arc exposed: a world-line has no declarable END.
changes_bullets: mnemosyne-core gains WorldMembership + world_membership(): branch -> the departure coordinates a fact must be at-or-before (ALL of them; the constraints CONJOIN). Fork = conjoin a bound; merge = INTERSECT the parents. Memoised over the branch DAG, fails loud on a cyclic out-of-band edit

the lattice is ORDER-FREE (bounds are carried as a SET, not folded into a min), which is what lets the write path and the read path share ONE definition — the whole point, and why the bound is compared against the canon order later, at visibility time

continuity: Lineage/cut/forward/cut_forward DELETED (no legacy carry); visibility, world_order_composition contributors, and join_world all now DERIVE from the one membership. join_world is the bounded visibility itself (a world where BOTH facts are In), not a fourth hand-enumerated relation

succession_branch_inherits routed through the same membership, with the exact direction discriminator: a NON-EMPTY bound set means UPSTREAM, an EMPTY one means own-or-DOWNSTREAM (every backward step conjoins a coordinate). Both arms require the successor to lie downstream — no leak-back, no sideways
7 new tests (5 core lattice + 2 continuity read-surface), fork_chain deleted, dangling intra-doc links to the deleted items cleaned
verification_bullets: FIXED and each reproduced first on the shipped R611 binary, then re-run: (1) a fork off a CONFLUENCE lost the ENTIRE pre-merge trunk — fork_chain terminated at a confluence, and this is the SECOND link of every subway-braid chain (the consumer's real F1+F2+F4 shape) and was silent (payoff coverage showed dangling 0 for a world that had lost its prefix); (2) a non-monotone fork chain leaked the whole span between the two departures; (3) a confluence world returned a prefix-less fragment; (4) the R611 succession half-invariant — the gate reported a conflict in the divergent world and then REJECTED the only sanctioned fix (supersede), its error message factually false per R611's own visibility

CLOSED FOR FREE, not special-cased: the sibling-confluence-common-parent case deferred since R533 ("would need an In-set intersection over the query worlds") — join_world searching the query worlds for a world where both are In IS that intersection

proof the merge really INTERSECTS (the load-bearing new semantics): a confluence and a fork off it inherit the path-independent trunk while BOTH parents' exclusive middles stay out — asserted on core membership AND on the read surfaces (frame_view + payoff_coverage)
GAP-003 (R611's own case) re-verified unchanged end-to-end on the CLI: the divergent ending still regains the displaced f-mid and still does NOT steal the shared tail
green: mnemosyne-core 24, mnemosyne-validate 288, cargo test --workspace, clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings, fmt --all --check, validate-workspace
carry_forward_bullets: STILL OPEN, NOT fixed by this round (the COORDINATE axis, verified still reproducing after R612 — do not read this entry as closing it): R611 widened world_order_composition without a bound, and CanonOrder::names / linearize / is_maximal use that widened node set as a coordinate-membership predicate. Consequences, all reproduced: validate-render-fidelity for a divergent world no longer detects prose that delivered the trunk's ending (off_path blind, and reached_terminal reports the TRUNK's terminal, so the gate rewards the drifted render and flags the faithful one — the R504 footgun, R514/R515-pinned); the R488 FactCanonOffBranch guard goes silent past the fork; and playable_world (the pinion seam) emits phantom scenes. The generosity of le/comparable must be KEPT (it repaired the disclosure-leak, typed-exclusivity, interval and edge-candidate gates), so the fix is a BOUNDED node set for names/linearize/is_maximal, not a narrower order

STILL OPEN: a world-line has no declarable END. In a base-declared topology the trunk tail lives in the base, so is_maximal answers the TRUNK's terminal for a divergent line and the store cannot say "this ending ends here". R611's motivation (many divergent endings off a confluence trunk) makes this load-bearing; it is the design question the coordinate-axis round must answer first

STILL OPEN: describe-schema's `branches` authoring contract still says only "a fork inherits the parent's prefix". It never mentions that authoring a merge RELOCATES trunk ownership onto the confluence, nor the confluence half of the model. A blind author cannot self-serve the semantics from it, and no prose guard pins those concepts

CORRECTIONS to the R611 entry (frozen ledger, so recorded here): it claimed visibility is "one fix point for every read surface ... playthrough manuscript" — FALSE, the manuscript takes its scene SEQUENCE from order.linearize, not visibility, which is why the phantom scenes above exist; it said "both new tests" when three shipped (all three are load-bearing, re-verified); and its "byte-invariant for ... single-confluence stores" was ambiguous (it holds for stores with no fork off the chain, which is what was measured)

PROCESS: R611 was pushed after SOLO self-review. The three defects above and the four in this entry were all found by INDEPENDENT adversarial reviewers afterwards, exactly as the cost-no-object rule in memory predicts. Independent review belongs BEFORE the push, not after

Round 613 — the COORDINATE axis designed.decision_summary: The COORDINATE axis, designed — and the design ROUND is the finding, because my first design was REFUTED before it could be built. Setting: R611 fixed the FACT axis by silently widening the COORDINATE axis, which BLINDED two deterministic gates (validate-render-fidelity for a divergent world now REWARDS a render that delivered the trunk's ending and FLAGS the faithful one; R488's wrong-world-line guard goes silent past the fork) and put phantom scenes on the pinion seam. R612 fixed the fact axis properly with the membership lattice and deliberately did not touch this. So: `CanonOrder` is asked TWO different questions and answers both from one derived node set — PRECEDENCE ("can this world's order COMPARE a and b?", which must stay GENEROUS: R611's widening is MEASURED to have repaired four gates, and both reviewers confirm no case needs it narrowed) and ROAD ("which coordinates does this world TRAVEL, and where does it END?", which must be BOUNDED). Splitting them is the round's surviving thesis. THE DRAFT I WROTE derived the road from the R612 membership lattice. Two independent adversarial reviewers implemented it against the real crates and ran it over all 46 store-times-order pairs in the repo, and it is WRONG — not marginally: its own seed clause re-admitted the exact leak it was written to block; applying the FACT bound to EDGES collapsed a playable world's road to a single node, turning its own facts into false positives; on a REAL store it silently made a world's ENDING the story's OPENING scene, so the terminal gate would pass prose that renders only the briefing — a silent wrong answer from the gate that exists to catch silent wrong answers; and it would newly reject 381 facts across 13 pairs in four breakage classes where I had admitted one, INCLUDING the confluence's own facts, which are the very subject of MNEMO-GAP-003. THE LAW THAT EXPLAINS ALL OF IT, and the reason no patch to the draft can work: FACTS INTERSECT AT A MERGE, ROADS UNION AT A MERGE. They are DUALS. A fork CUTS facts (conjoin a bound) but OVERRIDES the road (its own out-edge at the departure coordinate replaces the inherited one). R612's intersect deletes the parents from a confluence's membership BY DESIGN — that is what makes the fact axis right — so for any world whose prefix runs through a merge, the member edge set no longer holds the edges that carry the road. One lattice cannot serve both axes. THE MODEL: road = an OVERRIDE WALK. A coordinate is on W's road iff it is a declared SEED, or an edge into it leaves a coordinate ALREADY on the road, declared by the CLOSEST member branch declaring an out-edge there (own beats nearest fork ancestor beats the base). No fact-bound is consulted on the road axis at all — which is precisely why it neither collapses nor forces the order to compare coordinates on a parallel chain (the R456 incomparable-is-first-class idiom the draft violated). Validated against every topology in the repo, including the two real stores the draft broke, which it leaves PASSING. THE SUBSTRATE FINDING (and the correction of my own false refute-first): the store CANNOT distinguish "W forks in FACTS ONLY and continues down the trunk" from "W forks and ENDS at its own beat" — both encode as forks_from with no edge set, and my draft silently reinterpreted the first as the second, which IS the 381. The resolution is not a new field but a DECLARATION-COMPLETENESS fail-loud that NAMES the ambiguity and hands the author the lever, so the default (undeclared = the trunk) is both correct and loudly named. That converts a LYING gate into an honest one at zero migration cost. Code 0. The build is R614, gated on ratification.
changes_bullets: design-doc sec 7.39 (code 0): the COORDINATE axis. Splits `names`/`linearize`/`is_maximal` (the ROAD: which coordinates a world TRAVELS, and where it ENDS) off `le`/`comparable` (PRECEDENCE: can the order COMPARE two coordinates), which must stay generous — R611's widening is MEASURED to have repaired four gates and both reviewers confirm no case needs it narrowed

THE LAW the round found: FACTS INTERSECT at a merge, ROADS UNION at a merge — they are DUALS, and the R612 fact-membership lattice therefore CANNOT derive the road. A fork CUTS facts by conjoining a bound; a fork OVERRIDES the road (its own out-edge at the departure coordinate replaces the inherited one). Recorded as the round's headline finding

the model: road = an OVERRIDE WALK — n is on W's road iff n is a declared SEED, or an edge (u -> n) exists with u ALREADY on the road, declared by the CLOSEST member branch declaring an out-edge at u (own > nearest fork ancestor > ... > base; a forward confluence applies where no closer branch declares one). NO fact-bound is consulted on the road axis at all — which is why it neither collapses nor asks the order to compare coordinates on a parallel chain (the R456 incomparable-is-first-class idiom)

the SUBSTRATE finding: the store cannot distinguish "W forks in FACTS ONLY and continues down the trunk" from "W forks and ENDS at its own beat" — both encode as forks_from + no edge set. Resolution is NOT a new field but a DECLARATION-COMPLETENESS fail-loud that names the ambiguity and hands the author the lever, so the default (undeclared = the trunk) is correct AND loudly named

carried into the build round, each surfaced by review: is_maximal must return a SET of ends (a trunk-prefix road has its FORK POINT as its maximum — today's false-negative must not be traded for the R510 F5 vacuous-pass); PIN the merge-edge convention (unpinned today, the two in-repo fixtures DISAGREE, and the confluence-side encoding is the leak vector); evidence reachability must move to the road axis too or say why not; two divergence coordinates are REAL in a shipped store and nothing cross-checks them
verification_bullets: THE FIRST DRAFT OF THIS DESIGN WAS REFUTED, and that refutation is the round's most valuable output — it is recorded in sec 7.39.1, not hidden. Two INDEPENDENT adversarial reviewers built it verbatim against the real crates (linking world_membership + CanonOrder::from_declaration) and ran it over ALL 46 store x order pairs in the repo

the draft was WRONG in three measured ways: (1) its SOURCE seed clause RE-ADMITTED the exact leak it was written to block (excluding a sibling's edge makes that sibling's coordinate in-degree-0, so the seed clause let it back in and R488 stayed silent) — the two clauses of the definition contradicted each other, and all four readings of the ambiguity were measured; (2) applying the FACT bound to EDGES collapsed the road (road(braid2) = {s1}: a playable world could not reach its own declared edge, and its own fact became a FALSE POSITIVE) — so the draft's headline table row was true only VACUOUSLY; (3) on the REAL phase1-detroit-poc store a world's road silently collapsed to 3 of 13 coordinates with NO violation raised, making its is_maximal the story's OPENING scene, so reached_terminal would return true for prose that renders only the briefing — a silent wrong answer produced by the gate that exists to catch silent wrong answers

BLAST RADIUS of the draft, measured: 381 facts newly rejected across 13 of 46 pairs, in FOUR breakage classes where the draft admitted ONE — including the CONFLUENCE's own facts, i.e. MNEMO-GAP-003's own f-mid/f-tail, the very subject of the gap report. The draft's carve-out ("a confluence is not a query world") was also false: R488 sweeps ALL facts keyed by fact.branch, not query_worlds

the draft's own "REFUTE-FIRST: no new substrate needed" was FALSE and is corrected in this entry (see the substrate finding) — I asserted it without proving it, the same failure mode as R611's unproven code comments

the REPLACEMENT (the override walk) was validated by the reviewer's probe against every topology in the repo — including the two real stores the draft broke, which it leaves PASSING (non-breaking). The build round must RE-PROVE each row as a regression lock rather than inherit the claim; this is a design round, code 0, nothing is built

PROCESS (the reason this round is worth what it cost): the review ran BEFORE ratification, not after the push. R611 shipped defects because I inverted that order. Here the design was refuted while it was still only words

STILL OPEN and unchanged by this round (R611 is on origin; R612 is committed but NOT pushed): validate-render-fidelity is BLIND for divergent worlds (it rewards the drifted render and flags the faithful one), the R488 wrong-branch guard is SILENT past the fork, and playable_world emits phantom scenes. This round designs the fix; it does not ship it
Round 613 — the COORDINATE axis designed.publishable_decision_summary: The COORDINATE axis, designed — and the design ROUND is the finding, because my first design was REFUTED before it could be built. Setting: R611 fixed the FACT axis by silently widening the COORDINATE axis, which BLINDED two deterministic gates (validate-render-fidelity for a divergent world now REWARDS a render that delivered the trunk's ending and FLAGS the faithful one; R488's wrong-world-line guard goes silent past the fork) and put phantom scenes on the pinion seam. R612 fixed the fact axis properly with the membership lattice and deliberately did not touch this. So: `CanonOrder` is asked TWO different questions and answers both from one derived node set — PRECEDENCE ("can this world's order COMPARE a and b?", which must stay GENEROUS: R611's widening is MEASURED to have repaired four gates, and both reviewers confirm no case needs it narrowed) and ROAD ("which coordinates does this world TRAVEL, and where does it END?", which must be BOUNDED). Splitting them is the round's surviving thesis. THE DRAFT I WROTE derived the road from the R612 membership lattice. Two independent adversarial reviewers implemented it against the real crates and ran it over all 46 store-times-order pairs in the repo, and it is WRONG — not marginally: its own seed clause re-admitted the exact leak it was written to block; applying the FACT bound to EDGES collapsed a playable world's road to a single node, turning its own facts into false positives; on a REAL store it silently made a world's ENDING the story's OPENING scene, so the terminal gate would pass prose that renders only the briefing — a silent wrong answer from the gate that exists to catch silent wrong answers; and it would newly reject 381 facts across 13 pairs in four breakage classes where I had admitted one, INCLUDING the confluence's own facts, which are the very subject of MNEMO-GAP-003. THE LAW THAT EXPLAINS ALL OF IT, and the reason no patch to the draft can work: FACTS INTERSECT AT A MERGE, ROADS UNION AT A MERGE. They are DUALS. A fork CUTS facts (conjoin a bound) but OVERRIDES the road (its own out-edge at the departure coordinate replaces the inherited one). R612's intersect deletes the parents from a confluence's membership BY DESIGN — that is what makes the fact axis right — so for any world whose prefix runs through a merge, the member edge set no longer holds the edges that carry the road. One lattice cannot serve both axes. THE MODEL: road = an OVERRIDE WALK. A coordinate is on W's road iff it is a declared SEED, or an edge into it leaves a coordinate ALREADY on the road, declared by the CLOSEST member branch declaring an out-edge there (own beats nearest fork ancestor beats the base). No fact-bound is consulted on the road axis at all — which is precisely why it neither collapses nor forces the order to compare coordinates on a parallel chain (the R456 incomparable-is-first-class idiom the draft violated). Validated against every topology in the repo, including the two real stores the draft broke, which it leaves PASSING. THE SUBSTRATE FINDING (and the correction of my own false refute-first): the store CANNOT distinguish "W forks in FACTS ONLY and continues down the trunk" from "W forks and ENDS at its own beat" — both encode as forks_from with no edge set, and my draft silently reinterpreted the first as the second, which IS the 381. The resolution is not a new field but a DECLARATION-COMPLETENESS fail-loud that NAMES the ambiguity and hands the author the lever, so the default (undeclared = the trunk) is both correct and loudly named. That converts a LYING gate into an honest one at zero migration cost. Code 0. The build is R614, gated on ratification.

THE LAW the round found: FACTS INTERSECT at a merge, ROADS UNION at a merge — they are DUALS, and the R612 fact-membership lattice therefore CANNOT derive the road. A fork CUTS facts by conjoining a bound; a fork OVERRIDES the road (its own out-edge at the departure coordinate replaces the inherited one). Recorded as the round's headline finding

the model: road = an OVERRIDE WALK — n is on W's road iff n is a declared SEED, or an edge (u -> n) exists with u ALREADY on the road, declared by the CLOSEST member branch declaring an out-edge at u (own > nearest fork ancestor > ... > base; a forward confluence applies where no closer branch declares one). NO fact-bound is consulted on the road axis at all — which is why it neither collapses nor asks the order to compare coordinates on a parallel chain (the R456 incomparable-is-first-class idiom)

publishable_verification_bullets: THE FIRST DRAFT OF THIS DESIGN WAS REFUTED, and that refutation is the round's most valuable output — it is recorded in sec 7.39.1, not hidden. Two INDEPENDENT adversarial reviewers built it verbatim against the real crates (linking world_membership + CanonOrder::from_declaration) and ran it over ALL 46 store x order pairs in the repo

the draft was WRONG in three measured ways: (1) its SOURCE seed clause RE-ADMITTED the exact leak it was written to block (excluding a sibling's edge makes that sibling's coordinate in-degree-0, so the seed clause let it back in and R488 stayed silent) — the two clauses of the definition contradicted each other, and all four readings of the ambiguity were measured; (2) applying the FACT bound to EDGES collapsed the road (road(braid2) = {s1}: a playable world could not reach its own declared edge, and its own fact became a FALSE POSITIVE) — so the draft's headline table row was true only VACUOUSLY; (3) on the REAL phase1-detroit-poc store a world's road silently collapsed to 3 of 13 coordinates with NO violation raised, making its is_maximal the story's OPENING scene, so reached_terminal would return true for prose that renders only the briefing — a silent wrong answer produced by the gate that exists to catch silent wrong answers

BLAST RADIUS of the draft, measured: 381 facts newly rejected across 13 of 46 pairs, in FOUR breakage classes where the draft admitted ONE — including the CONFLUENCE's own facts, i.e. MNEMO-GAP-003's own f-mid/f-tail, the very subject of the gap report. The draft's carve-out ("a confluence is not a query world") was also false: R488 sweeps ALL facts keyed by fact.branch, not query_worlds

the draft's own "REFUTE-FIRST: no new substrate needed" was FALSE and is corrected in this entry (see the substrate finding) — I asserted it without proving it, the same failure mode as R611's unproven code comments

the REPLACEMENT (the override walk) was validated by the reviewer's probe against every topology in the repo — including the two real stores the draft broke, which it leaves PASSING (non-breaking). The build round must RE-PROVE each row as a regression lock rather than inherit the claim; this is a design round, code 0, nothing is built

PROCESS (the reason this round is worth what it cost): the review ran BEFORE ratification, not after the push. R611 shipped defects because I inverted that order. Here the design was refuted while it was still only words

STILL OPEN and unchanged by this round (R611 is on origin; R612 is committed but NOT pushed): validate-render-fidelity is BLIND for divergent worlds (it rewards the drifted render and flags the faithful one), the R488 wrong-branch guard is SILENT past the fork, and playable_world emits phantom scenes. Round 614 — the ROAD axis built: names/l.decision_summary: The COORDINATE axis, BUILT to the R613 design — and the three deterministic gates R611 blinded are measurably un-blinded. `CanonOrder` was answering TWO different questions from one derived node set: PRECEDENCE ("can this world's order COMPARE a and b?") and ROAD ("which coordinates does it TRAVEL, and where does it END?"). R611 had to widen the first — that widening is MEASURED to have repaired four gates, including a disclosure leak that was passing at exit 0 — and in doing so it silently widened the second, which is what made `validate-render-fidelity` REWARD a render that delivered the trunk's ending and FLAG the faithful one, silenced R488's wrong-world-line guard past a fork, and put phantom scenes on the pinion seam. R614 splits them: `reach` keeps powering `le`/`comparable`, UNCHANGED and generous; a new `road` powers `names`/`linearize`/`is_maximal`, bounded. THE ROAD IS AN OVERRIDE WALK, not a bound: a coordinate is on W's road iff it is a declared SEED, or an edge reaches it FROM a coordinate ALREADY on the road, declared by the CLOSEST member branch declaring an out-edge there. A branch that declares its own successor at a shared coordinate REPLACES the inherited one; a branch that declares nothing CONTINUES its lineage's road. The `u is already on the road` requirement is what refuses the leak: a confluence legitimately declares a merge edge from EACH parent, so a SIBLING's exclusive coordinate is NAMED by a branch every downstream world belongs to — under a node-set union it leaks (the R611 defect, and the R613 draft's seed clause re-admitted it); under the override walk the edge simply cannot fire from a source the world never reaches. CRUCIALLY the road lineage is NOT `world_membership`: facts INTERSECT at a merge, roads UNION, and R612's intersect deletes the parents from a confluence's membership BY DESIGN — deriving the road from the fact lattice is what collapsed the refuted draft. No fact-bound is consulted on the road axis at all, which is also why it never asks the order to compare coordinates on a parallel chain, where they are legitimately incomparable rather than past. THE SUBSTRATE AMBIGUITY IS NAMED, NOT GUESSED: a branch that declares no road rides its lineage's road on, so its ENDING is the trunk's — correct for a world-line diverging only in FACTS, wrong for a divergent ending whose road was never declared, and the store cannot tell them apart. So `undeclared_roads` + a CLI NOTICE hands the author the lever instead of silently picking a reading. That is what keeps this NON-BREAKING: measured over 70 real store-times-order pairs, ZERO newly-rejecting — against 381 newly-rejected facts for the refuted draft on the same corpus. `is_maximal` now reads the road and `terminals()` returns the END SET, because a world-line legitimately has several ends.
changes_bullets: CanonOrder now carries TWO derived structures from ONE input (the declaration + THE branch registry, so a caller can no longer hand in an order composition that disagrees with the branch graph): PRECEDENCE (`reach`, powering le/comparable — UNCHANGED and deliberately generous) and ROAD (`road`, powering names/linearize/is_maximal — bounded)

`road_of` = the OVERRIDE WALK: a coordinate is on W's road iff it is a declared SEED, or an edge reaches it FROM a coordinate ALREADY on the road, declared by the CLOSEST member branch declaring an out-edge there (own > nearest fork ancestor > ... > base; a forward confluence applies where nothing closer does; ties take the UNION — roads union at a merge). `road_lineage` is deliberately NOT world_membership: facts INTERSECT at a merge, roads UNION

is_maximal now reads the road and `terminals()` returns the END SET (a world-line legitimately has several ends); linearize walks the road, so the divergent world's manuscript and the playable_world pinion seam stop emitting phantom scenes

ContinuityReport.undeclared_roads (+ ops DTO mirror) + a CLI NOTICE that TEACHES the fix: a branch declaring no road rides its lineage's road on, so its ENDING is the trunk's — correct for a facts-only divergence, wrong for a divergent ending whose road was never declared, and the substrate cannot tell them apart, so it is NAMED not guessed

4 test fixtures corrected: they handed from_declaration a branch graph that DISAGREED with their own store (an empty registry, or empty contributors for branches their comments call "forks"). The road axis makes fork-vs-standalone load-bearing, so the new signature exposed the latent inconsistency
verification_bullets: ALL THREE BLIND GATES FIXED, each driven END-TO-END through the CLI, not just unit-tested. D1 render-fidelity, the scariest (its verdict was INVERTED): a render that delivered the TRUNK's ending now FAILS (off_path=[s4,s5], reached_terminal=false) and the FAITHFUL render that ends at the world's own beat now PASSES (off_path=[], reached_terminal=true) — before, exactly the reverse. D2: `fact_canon_off_branch` fires again on a fact authored onto a SIBLING's exclusive road (silent on R611/R612). D3: the divergent world's scene walk is [s1,s2,s3,e1] — its own road, no trunk tail, no sibling road

NON-BREAKING, MEASURED: 70 real store x order pairs in the repo scanned with the pre-R614 binary and the new one — ZERO newly-rejecting pairs. The REFUTED R613 draft scored 381 newly-rejected facts across 13 pairs on the same corpus, so this is the specific failure the round existed to avoid, and it is measured rather than asserted

the CONSUMER's own MNEMO-GAP-003 store (base-declared, no roads declared) scans with 0 violations and gets the NOTICE naming its 3 undeclared-road branches — the draft rejected 4 of its 6 facts, including the confluence-owned f-mid/f-tail that ARE the gap's subject

NEGATIVE CONTROL: le/comparable untouched, so the four gates R611's order-widening repaired (disclosure leak, typed exclusivity, interval rules, edge candidates) stay repaired — asserted directly in the new road test and covered by the suite

carry_forward_bullets: CARRIED to R615, named by the R613 review and deliberately NOT silently dropped: (1) describe-schema's `branches` authoring contract still says only "a fork inherits the parent's prefix" — it must gain the ROAD rule (a branch's edge set IS its road segment; the base edges are main's road), the END rule, the merge-edge convention (unpinned today — the two in-repo fixtures disagree), and the fact that authoring a MERGE relocates trunk ownership onto the confluence. A blind author cannot self-serve any of it today; (2) evidence reachability (continuity.rs) still reads `le`, so a fact may cite a SIBLING's exclusive scene as evidence and pass — "could this world have SEEN that scene?" is a ROAD question, and its own docstring calls itself the R488 principle extended via the SAME le, so it must move to the road axis or say why not; (3) the two-divergence-coordinates well-formedness check (a branch's own edges must ATTACH to its road; a converging branch must REACH its own merge coordinate) — REAL in the shipped detroit store, where a branch's declared road starts EARLIER than its fork point, and nothing cross-checks the two
sections_outside_order now means "not on this world's road" rather than "not in the composed order" — correct, but it will report more for divergent worlds; re-word or re-scope in R615
NOT pushed. R611 (on origin) carried the blind gates; R612 + R613 + R614 are committed locally and together close them

Round 615 — evidence is a ROAD question:.decision_summary: Paying the debt R614 itself created, and closing the R613 review carry. R614 moved the R488 wrong-world-line guard onto the ROAD axis but left EVIDENCE reachability on `le` alone — and the two are ONE principle ("could this world have SEEN that scene?"), so a single rule was left carrying two different invariants: exactly the half-enforced-invariant trap this project forbids, and I introduced it one round earlier. PROVEN exploitable before fixing, not assumed: on the shipped R614 binary a fact on a divergent `ending` cited a scene that only its SIBLING travels, and the gate returned 0 violations — the generous precedence order still connects that scene, because a confluence declares a merge edge from EVERY parent. Both halves now ask the road. The naive tightening then DOUBLED the violation count on two real stores while naming ZERO additional facts, so the round also fixes the reporting: a fact whose OWN coordinate is off-road is already named by FactCanonOffBranch, and there is no world in which to ask "could it have seen that" when the beat itself is not on the road — the evidence axis stays quiet about it. Also closed: an ORPHANED ROAD EDGE (a declared edge whose source the branch never reaches) now fails LOUD instead of silently truncating a world-line, possibly at its own ending — 0 of 83 real pairs trip it, including the detroit store whose branch legitimately declares a road segment starting BEFORE its fork point. And the AUTHORING CONTRACT: describe-schema's `branches` registry now carries the world-line model a blind author cannot otherwise self-serve — the two axes and their DUAL merge behaviour (facts INTERSECT, roads UNION), that authoring a MERGE RELOCATES TRUNK OWNERSHIP onto the confluence, that the base `edges` ARE main's road while each `branches` entry is that world-line's own road segment, that a branch declaring no road inherits the TRUNK'S ENDING and what that costs at the render-fidelity gate, and that evidence is a road question — with a concept-pin test so the prose cannot drift off the code again, which is precisely how R611 and R614 went wrong. Finally, the R613 review asked for the merge-edge convention to be PINNED; having built the walk, the honest answer is that no pin is needed — the "you must GET there" requirement makes the choice unobservable — and the contract now says so rather than inventing a rule.
changes_bullets: evidence reachability moved to the ROAD axis: a cited scene must be one the world actually TRAVELS (`names`) AND at-or-before the fact (`le`). Both halves of ONE principle now ask the same axis; R614 had moved R488 (canon_from) to the road and left evidence on `le` alone, so a divergent world could cite a SIBLING's exclusive scene and pass. Applied to the confluence arm too (each parent's road)

no-double-report rule: a fact whose OWN coordinate is off-road is already named by FactCanonOffBranch, so the evidence axis stays quiet about it (there is no world in which to ask "could it have seen that" when the beat itself is not on the road) — the naive tightening produced 14 duplicate violations and 0 new facts on the real corpus

ORPHANED ROAD EDGE now fails LOUD at from_declaration: a declared edge whose SOURCE the branch's own road never reaches can never be travelled, so the branch would silently lose whatever it carried — possibly its own ENDING. A branch's edge set must ATTACH to the road it rides in on

describe-schema `branches` contract rewritten to carry the WORLD-LINE MODEL a blind author cannot otherwise self-serve: the two axes and their DUAL merge behaviour (facts INTERSECT, roads UNION), that authoring a MERGE RELOCATES TRUNK OWNERSHIP onto the confluence, that the order file's base `edges` ARE main's road while each `branches` entry is that world-line's own road segment, that a branch declaring no road inherits the TRUNK's ENDING (and what that costs at the render-fidelity gate), that a segment must attach to its road, and that evidence is a road question. A new concept-pin test (the R601 tier-3 pattern) guards the prose from drifting off the code

sections_outside_order renamed sections_off_road: post-R614 it means "scenes this world does not TRAVEL" (including a sibling's exclusive road), not "coordinates the declaration never names" — the old name and doc were now false
verification_bullets: the evidence hole was PROVEN exploitable before it was fixed, not assumed: a fact on a divergent `ending` (road = the trunk, s4b NOT on it) citing `s4b` — a scene ONLY the sibling `braid2` travels — scanned with 0 violations on the shipped R614 binary. After the fix it fires evidence_unreachable, while a fact citing a trunk scene the world DID travel still passes

NON-BREAKING, MEASURED TWICE: 70 real store x order pairs, ZERO newly-rejecting; and separately, ZERO stores that loaded on R614 but hard-fail on R615 (the naive skip-on-error sweep could have HIDDEN a load regression behind the new fail-loud, so it was re-run as an explicit load-failure diff)

the new ORPHANED-EDGE fail-loud fires on 0 of 83 real store x order pairs — including phase1-detroit-poc, whose branch declares a road segment starting EARLIER than its own fork point (the override walk keys on the declared edges, not on forks_at, so that legitimate encoding passes; this was the R613 reviewer's MED-3 case)

the describe-schema rewrite is guarded, not just written: a new concept-pin test asserts the contract names ROAD, INTERSECT+UNION, RELOCATES TRUNK OWNERSHIP, "`edges` ARE `main`'s road", undeclared_roads + validate-render-fidelity, and evidence — so the prose cannot silently drift off the model again (the R611/R614 failure mode)

green: mnemosyne-validate 292 lib (2 new: the evidence road test + the contract concept-pin), cargo test --workspace, clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings, fmt --all --check, validate-workspace
carry_forward_bullets: the R613/R614 carry is now CLOSED: evidence axis (done), describe-schema authoring contract + concept guard (done), the two-divergence-coordinates well-formedness check (done, as the orphaned-road-edge fail-loud), sections_outside_order wording (done, renamed to the truth)

the MERGE-EDGE CONVENTION is now DOCUMENTED as deliberately free (declare the merge edge on the parent OR on the confluence, one per parent) rather than pinned to one side — the override walk's "you must GET there" requirement makes the choice unobservable, since a confluence's merge edge from a sibling can never put that sibling's exclusive scene on your road. The R613 review asked for a PIN; the honest answer after building it is that no pin is needed, and the contract says so

NEXT: no known Mnemosyne-side debt from the R611-R615 arc. The consumer (the-tide-that-counts) can author the F1/F2 upstream re-convergence braids AND divergent endings, and now has a describe-schema contract that teaches the road/END rules. Natural next moves: let the consumer pull the next gap, or the pinion/experiment fork
Round 615 — evidence is a ROAD question:.publishable_decision_summary: Paying the debt R614 itself created, and closing the R613 review carry. R614 moved the R488 wrong-world-line guard onto the ROAD axis but left EVIDENCE reachability on `le` alone — and the two are ONE principle ("could this world have SEEN that scene?"), so a single rule was left carrying two different invariants: exactly the half-enforced-invariant trap this project forbids, and I introduced it one round earlier. PROVEN exploitable before fixing, not assumed: on the shipped R614 binary a fact on a divergent `ending` cited a scene that only its SIBLING travels, and the gate returned 0 violations — the generous precedence order still connects that scene, because a confluence declares a merge edge from EVERY parent. Both halves now ask the road. The naive tightening then DOUBLED the violation count on two real stores while naming ZERO additional facts, so the round also fixes the reporting: a fact whose OWN coordinate is off-road is already named by FactCanonOffBranch, and there is no world in which to ask "could it have seen that" when the beat itself is not on the road — the evidence axis stays quiet about it. Also closed: an ORPHANED ROAD EDGE (a declared edge whose source the branch never reaches) now fails LOUD instead of silently truncating a world-line, possibly at its own ending — 0 of 83 real pairs trip it, including the detroit store whose branch legitimately declares a road segment starting BEFORE its fork point. And the AUTHORING CONTRACT: describe-schema's `branches` registry now carries the world-line model a blind author cannot otherwise self-serve — the two axes and their DUAL merge behaviour (facts INTERSECT, roads UNION), that authoring a MERGE RELOCATES TRUNK OWNERSHIP onto the confluence, that the base `edges` ARE main's road while each `branches` entry is that world-line's own road segment, that a branch declaring no road inherits the TRUNK'S ENDING and what that costs at the render-fidelity gate, and that evidence is a road question — with a concept-pin test so the prose cannot drift off the code again, which is precisely how R611 and R614 went wrong. Finally, the R613 review asked for the merge-edge convention to be PINNED; having built the walk, the honest answer is that no pin is needed — the "you must GET there" requirement makes the choice unobservable — and the contract now says so rather than inventing a rule.
publishable_changes_bullets: evidence reachability moved to the ROAD axis: a cited scene must be one the world actually TRAVELS (`names`) AND at-or-before the fact (`le`). Both halves of ONE principle now ask the same axis; R614 had moved R488 (canon_from) to the road and left evidence on `le` alone, so a divergent world could cite a SIBLING's exclusive scene and pass. Applied to the confluence arm too (each parent's road)

no-double-report rule: a fact whose OWN coordinate is off-road is already named by FactCanonOffBranch, so the evidence axis stays quiet about it (there is no world in which to ask "could it have seen that" when the beat itself is not on the road) — the naive tightening produced 14 duplicate violations and 0 new facts on the real corpus

ORPHANED ROAD EDGE now fails LOUD at from_declaration: a declared edge whose SOURCE the branch's own road never reaches can never be travelled, so the branch would silently lose whatever it carried — possibly its own ENDING. A branch's edge set must ATTACH to the road it rides in on

describe-schema `branches` contract rewritten to carry the WORLD-LINE MODEL a blind author cannot otherwise self-serve: the two axes and their DUAL merge behaviour (facts INTERSECT, roads UNION), that authoring a MERGE RELOCATES TRUNK OWNERSHIP onto the confluence, that the order file's base `edges` ARE main's road while each `branches` entry is that world-line's own road segment, that a branch declaring no road inherits the TRUNK's ENDING (and what that costs at the render-fidelity gate), that a segment must attach to its road, and that evidence is a road question. A new concept-pin test (the R601 tier-3 pattern) guards the prose from drifting off the code

sections_outside_order renamed sections_off_road: post-R614 it means "scenes this world does not TRAVEL" (including a sibling's exclusive road), not "coordinates the declaration never names" — the old name and doc were now false
publishable_verification_bullets: the evidence hole was PROVEN exploitable before it was fixed, not assumed: a fact on a divergent `ending` (road = the trunk, s4b NOT on it) citing `s4b` — a scene ONLY the sibling `braid2` travels — scanned with 0 violations on the shipped R614 binary. After the fix it fires evidence_unreachable, while a fact citing a trunk scene the world DID travel still passes

NON-BREAKING, MEASURED TWICE: 70 real store x order pairs, ZERO newly-rejecting; and separately, ZERO stores that loaded on R614 but hard-fail on R615 (the naive skip-on-error sweep could have HIDDEN a load regression behind the new fail-loud, so it was re-run as an explicit load-failure diff)

the new ORPHANED-EDGE fail-loud fires on 0 of 83 real store x order pairs — including phase1-detroit-poc, whose branch declares a road segment starting EARLIER than its own fork point (the override walk keys on the declared edges, not on forks_at, so that legitimate encoding passes; this was the R613 reviewer's MED-3 case)

Round 616 — MNEMO-GAP-005 reviewed: both.decision_summary: MNEMO-GAP-005 (from the-tide-that-counts) asked for two things: a per-fact provenance facet (Canon/Invented/Structural) and a branch-owned-density projection. A cost-no-object review (three independent adversarial reviewers plus measurement against the real 827-fact consumer store) refuted BOTH designs before any build — the R613 pattern, where the refutation is the round's most valuable output. Density: the proposed denominator (own segment = road(B) minus the union of parent roads) collapses to EMPTY on every confluence, because a converging parent forward-inherits the merge suffix, so it is a divide-by-zero on a legal store — proven on the real R614 fixture. Provenance: measured against the real store, the trichotomy is the wrong shape because its two axes have opposite derivability and opposite granularity. STRUCTURAL (quest plumbing) is per-fact and fully derivable (typed quest predicates unioned with the completed_by->pays_off giving-setup binding the quest graph already computes) — verified to reproduce all 80 plumbing facts exactly with zero false-positives — so storing it would only duplicate the quest structure. CANON-vs-INVENTED is per-branch and genuinely NOT derivable (branch != main mislabels the 543 canonical weave confluence facts, 66 percent of the store), but it is adaptation-fidelity metadata — vacuous for an original work and the same class the gap itself defers as out-of-charter for its canon-manifest denominator. Owner decision C: derive structural natively (zero schema) and keep canon-vs-invented consumer-side, consistent with the north-star (original-work authoring; adaptation fidelity is a consumer concern like SCE). The per-fact trichotomy is dropped; density lands corrected (own-declared-segment denominator, road-consistent numerator, Option for the empty-own-segment signal). This is a code-0 design round; R617 builds density and R618 builds the derived structural coverage split.
changes_bullets: REJECTED the gap's per-fact provenance facet (`NarrativeFact.provenance {Canon,Invented,Structural}`): three independent adversarial reviewers plus measurement against the real 827-fact consumer store converged that it is the wrong shape — it stores the one thing that is fully derivable and mis-locates the one thing that is not, per-fact, with a `canon_from` naming collision. No `NarrativeFact` schema change lands.

STRUCTURAL (quest plumbing) is DERIVED, not stored: `structural = (typed.predicate in {pursues,requires,completed_by}) UNION (an Expected fact paid off by a completed_by-typed fact)` — the same completed_by->pays_off giving-setup binding `quest_graph` already computes (R559/R568). Storing it would duplicate the quest structure and could drift; deriving it is exact.

CANON-vs-INVENTED stays CONSUMER-SIDE (owner decision C): it is a real per-world-line property but genuinely NOT soundly derivable, and it is adaptation-fidelity metadata (vacuous for an original work) — the same class the gap itself defers as out-of-charter in its canon-manifest denominator. Charter = original-work authoring; adaptation fidelity is a consumer concern (the SCE precedent). Considered and set aside: `Branch.canonical: bool` (a per-branch, byte-stable native home) — declined as out-of-charter under decision C, recorded for revisit if the charter scope changes.

The §3b density projection is corrected before build: the gap's denominator (own segment = `road(B) \ union(parent roads)`) collapses to EMPTY on every confluence (a converging parent forward-inherits the merge suffix, so `road(C)` is a subset of the parents' union), which is a divide-by-zero on a legal store. Ratified replacement: denominator = the world's OWN DECLARED road segment (a new `CanonOrder` field + `own_road_segment` accessor over the private per-branch edge set), numerator road-consistent (facts with `branch==B` whose `canon_from` is in that own segment), `density: Option<f64>` = None for an empty own segment (a facts-only / undeclared-road divergence that rides the trunk — a signal, never 0 or infinity), main as the baseline.

AFFIRMED: no `set-fact-provenance` (or any per-field fact setter) — facts have no per-field setters; add/import/amend all route the single `build_candidate_fact` (R305 parity), and a setter would be a second write path bypassing it.
verification_bullets: The density divide-by-zero was PROVEN, not assumed: computed the gap's `road(B) \ parent-roads` against the real R614 fixture `road_axis_override_walk_bounds_each_world_line` — the confluences `weave1` and `weave2` both yield an EMPTY own segment (their entire road is inherited from parents), so `owned/0.0` = NaN/inf, a serialize-or-lie failure on a valid store (the R613 failure mode: the gate that exists to catch silent wrong answers producing one).

STRUCTURAL derivation verified against the real consumer store (the-tide-that-counts, 827 facts): the derived predicate reproduces all 80 id-regex plumbing facts EXACTLY — 0 missed, 0 false-positive; and of 43 `Expected` setups, the 22 give-setups classify structural while the 21 genuine Chekhov setups do not. So deriving structural is exact, no schema needed.

CANON-vs-INVENTED non-derivability verified on the same store: `branch != main` = 639 facts but the consumer's invented set = 96 (braid + ending); the 543 `weave-*` facts are `converges_from` confluences = CANONICAL re-convergent threads, so a branch-based derivation would mislabel 66% of the store. Fork-vs-confluence topology aligns today but is fragile (`weave-firstcall` converges FROM the invented `braid-firstcall`), so it is not a sound derivation either — the axis must be declared, not inferred.

Three independent adversarial reviewers converged separately (charter/provenance, density road-model, derivation-sufficiency), each citing real source and, for the store measurements, the real consumer store; the two store measurements above were re-verified independently before this entry.
carry_forward_bullets: NEXT R617 = BUILD the corrected §3b density: the `own_road_segment` accessor (new `CanonOrder` field computed in `from_declaration`), the `BranchDensity` report carrying owned_facts / own_scene_facts / own_road_scenes / `density: Option<f64>` beside the `undeclared_roads` flag, wired into `authoring_frontier_report`; regression-locked on the R614 road fixtures (every confluence gets a non-empty own segment or an honest None, never a divide-by-zero).

NEXT R618 = BUILD the derived §3a coverage split: `scene_coverage` gains a derived `structural` count (typed-quest UNION completed_by->pays_off) beside `fact_count`; canon-vs-invented is NOT added (decision C — consumer-side). Zero `NarrativeFact` schema change.

CONSUMER REPORT (the-tide-that-counts): the provenance facet is declined (structural is derivable exactly; canon-vs-invented is out-of-charter adaptation fidelity, kept consumer-side); the coverage-honesty need is served by the derived structural split; the density projection lands corrected (own-declared-segment + Option) and is NOT claimed to match the external coverage.py divisor (that tool was not shared).

Round 617 — branch-owned density built:.decision_summary: Builds the corrected §3b density from R616's ratified design. The frontier now reports, per world-line, how much it OWNS on the road it DECLARED itself (own_scene_facts / own_road_scenes) versus the prefix it inherits — so a divergent world that looks full by inheritance but owns little is visible. The denominator is the world's own declared road segment (a new CanonOrder field + own_road_segment accessor), NOT the gap's road(B) minus the union of parent roads, which collapses every confluence to empty (a converging parent forward-inherits the merge suffix) and divides by zero on a legal store. The numerator is scoped to that same own segment, so a fact at the shared fork coordinate is counted in owned_facts but never inflates the density (the R613 fact/road dual-axis trap). An empty own segment yields density None (a facts-only or undeclared-road divergence riding the trunk — a signal, never 0 or infinity), distinguished by own_road_declared. Proven end-to-end on the real merged consumer store: no divide-by-zero, main a 3.62 baseline over the divergent endings' 0.83-2.08, weave-ledger 15.42 versus a facts-only weave-firstcall reporting None. The numbers are honest per world but are deliberately NOT claimed to match the consumer's external coverage.py divisor (that tool was not shared).
changes_bullets: CanonOrder gains an `own_road` field + public `own_road_segment(branch)` accessor (Round 617): a world-line's OWN declared road segment — the targets of its own declared edges (a ROOT also gets the seed those edges start from), never the inherited attach coordinate. A subset of the road, computed in `from_declaration` beside it, EMPTY for a branch that declares no road (an undeclared_roads world riding the trunk).

AuthoringFrontierReport gains `branch_owned_density: BTreeMap<String, BranchDensity>` (main + every registered branch): owned_facts (all facts branch==B), own_scene_facts (branch==B AND canon_from on the OWN segment — road-consistent, so an inherited-prefix or shared-fork-coordinate fact never inflates it), own_road_scenes (own segment size), density = Option<f64> (own_scene_facts / own_road_scenes, None when the own segment is empty), own_road_declared. A pure read, not fed into total_gaps.

The corrected denominator replaces the gap's `road(B) \ union(parent roads)`, which collapses EVERY confluence to empty (a converging parent forward-inherits the merge suffix) — a divide-by-zero on a legal store. The own-declared segment gives each confluence its relocated piece instead (R612/R614: a merge relocates trunk ownership onto the confluence).

report-authoring-frontier renders the per-branch density in the plain-text output (a None reads "rides the trunk" vs "no own segment" via own_road_declared) and, unchanged, in --json + the MCP tool (serde).
verification_bullets: Regression lock on the real R614 fixture road_axis_override_walk_bounds_each_world_line: own_road_segment gives the confluences weave1={s3,s4} and weave2={s5} (NON-EMPTY — the gap's set-difference gave empty here, the divide-by-zero), main the root piece {s1,s2}, ending {e1}, braid2 {s4b}, and the undeclared braid1 empty (density None, never a divide-by-zero).

New ops test authoring_frontier_branch_owned_density locks the two traps: a fact at the shared fork coordinate inflates owned_facts (2) but NOT own_scene_facts (1) — road-consistent numerator, the R613 dual-axis trap closed; and an undeclared-road world reports density None + own_road_declared false, never 0 or infinity.

e2e on the REAL merged consumer store (the-tide-that-counts, 827 facts, weave confluences): NO divide-by-zero (the gap's design would have NaN'd on the weaves); main 188/52 own scenes = 3.62 baseline > the divergent endings 0.83-2.08 (the thinness signal the consumer wanted), weave-ledger 185/12 = 15.42 vs weave-firstcall None (rides the trunk, 242 facts on the shared road). NOT claimed to match the external coverage.py divisor (that tool was not shared).
green: mnemosyne-validate 293 lib (+1 own-segment regression test), cargo test --workspace, clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings, fmt --all --check.

Zero NarrativeFact schema change.

CONSUMER (the-tide-that-counts): the density projection lands corrected (own-declared-segment denominator + Option for the rides-trunk signal); the numbers are honest per-world but are NOT the external coverage.py divisor. The provenance facet stays declined per R616 (structural derivable, canon/invented out-of-charter under decision C).

New ops test authoring_frontier_branch_owned_density locks the two traps: a fact at the shared fork coordinate inflates owned_facts (2) but NOT own_scene_facts (1) — road-consistent numerator, the R613 dual-axis trap closed; and an undeclared-road world reports density None + own_road_declared false, never 0 or infinity.

e2e on the REAL merged consumer store (the-tide-that-counts, 827 facts, weave confluences): NO divide-by-zero (the gap's design would have NaN'd on the weaves); main 188/52 own scenes = 3.62 baseline > the divergent endings 0.83-2.08 (the thinness signal the consumer wanted), weave-ledger 185/12 = 15.42 vs weave-firstcall None (rides the trunk, 242 facts on the shared road). NOT claimed to match the external coverage.py divisor (that tool was not shared).

Zero NarrativeFact schema change.

Round 618 — derived structural coverage.decision_summary: Builds the derived structural coverage split from R616's ratified design (decision C: derive, do not store). A new continuity helper structural_fact_ids classifies quest plumbing — the typed quest legs (pursues/requires/completed_by) plus the giving setups (Expected facts a completed_by fact pays off, mirroring quest_graph's own definition) — and SceneCoverage gains a structural count so a coverage read can subtract bookkeeping instead of counting it as narrative content. It is a pure derivation over the existing typed + payoff axes: verified on the real consumer store to reproduce its external id-regex plumbing set EXACTLY (80 facts, 0 missed, 0 false-positive), which is why the store carries no structural marker. Canon-vs-invented is deliberately NOT split here — it is per-branch adaptation-fidelity kept consumer-side. This closes the MNEMO-GAP-005 arc: the per-fact provenance facet the gap asked for was declined (R616), density landed corrected (R617), and the coverage-honesty need is served by this derived split with zero NarrativeFact schema change.
changes_bullets: New continuity helper structural_fact_ids(store) (Round 618): the DERIVED quest-plumbing fact set — a fact carrying a quest typed predicate (pursues/requires/completed_by) OR an Expected setup a completed_by-typed fact pays off (a quest giving setup). The giving-setup half mirrors quest_graph's own definition (R559/R568), kept in the same module beside it so the two cannot drift unseen.

SceneCoverage gains a `structural` count (a subset of fact_count) filled from that set in authoring_frontier_report's coverage loop, so a coverage read can subtract quest bookkeeping instead of counting it as narrative content (Round 589 counted every fact as one).

ZERO NarrativeFact schema change: structural is derived, not stored (verified exact, so a stored marker would only duplicate the quest structure). Canon-vs-invented is NOT split here — it is per-branch adaptation-fidelity kept consumer-side (decision C); a consumer wanting it combines this with the facts' branch axis.
verification_bullets: New continuity test structural_fact_ids_classifies_quest_plumbing_not_genuine_setups: the typed quest legs and the give-setup (Expected paid off by a completed_by fact) classify structural, while a genuine Chekhov setup (Expected paid off by a PLAIN fact), its plain payoff, and a plain fact do NOT — the 0-false-positive separation that keeps a coverage read from undercounting real narrative setups.

e2e on the REAL consumer store (the-tide-that-counts, 827 facts): the derived structural count sums to 80 across 18 scenes — EXACTLY the consumer's external id-regex plumbing set (f-give/f-pursue/f-complete/f-req), reproduced natively over the typed + payoff axes with no schema.
green: mnemosyne-validate 294 lib (+1 structural test), clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings, fmt --all --check.

The density projection is the corrected own-declared-segment version (R617), honest per world but NOT the external coverage.py divisor.

Round 619 — cost-no-object review of R61.decision_summary: An owner-requested cost-no-object review (three independent adversarial reviewers — SSOT/structure, north-star/cost, correctness/silent-fail — plus self-audit) held R617-R618 to the lifetime-portfolio bar where a smell or silent-fail is a defect. It found real defects a solo self-review had passed clean. Sharpest: the R617 density metric measured the WRONG question — its own-declared-segment denominator hid the very 'looks full by inheritance' dilution the gap was filed to surface (on the real store the divergent endings read nearly as dense as the trunk, and a fact-rich facts-only world read a confusing None), and it carried a correctness bug (a branch declaring an edge into its inherited attach coordinate counted that coordinate as own, a silent 2x error on a legal store). Both are fixed by dividing owned facts by the FULL traversed road instead, which also let the entire own_road machinery be deleted (net -304 lines). Second: structural_fact_ids duplicated quest_graph's giving-setup rule and had ALREADY diverged (flat vs kind:quest-gated) — fixed by extracting one shared kernel quest_giving_setups both call. Also: the structural coverage is now exposed as a flat fact-id set (not only a per-scene count) so the consumer can join it per-fact and retire its heuristic; the R618 structural test was vacuous on the Expected guard and is rewritten to lock all three guards; the MCP tool description is updated. Verified end-to-end on the real store: divergent forks now read low (0.12-0.47) against main 2.94, structural_facts = 80 matching the consumer's plumbing set. R617's committed entry stays as the audit trail of what was first built; this records the correction.
changes_bullets: Density denominator corrected: `owned_facts / |full road traversed|` (`linearize(B).len()`) replaces R617's own-declared-segment. The own-segment version measured the WRONG question — it divided by the handful of scenes a world declared, hiding the long inherited span that IS the "looks full by inheritance" dilution the gap wanted flagged (a divergent world read nearly as dense as the trunk; a fact-rich facts-only world read a confusing "rides the trunk" None). The full traversed road surfaces dilution (a world riding a long road owning little reads LOW) and is bug-free.

REMOVED the own_road machinery entirely (field + own_road_of + own_road_segment accessor + its test): besides measuring the wrong signal it had a correctness bug — a branch declaring an edge INTO its inherited attach coordinate (the shape R615's own error message invites) counted that inherited coordinate as own, a silent 2x error on a legal store. The full-road denominator needs none of it. BranchDensity is now {owned_facts, road_scenes, density: Option<f64>} (None only for a store with no order).

SSOT: extracted `quest_giving_setups` as the SINGLE home of "a quest's giving setup is an Expected fact its own completed_by fact pays off (gated on kind:quest)". `quest_graph` (per-quest index) and `structural_fact_ids` (union) both read it, so they can no longer disagree — the R618 duplication had ALREADY diverged (structural was flat, quest_graph gated on kind:quest), a real "one semantic, two resolvers, divergent answers" defect.

`structural_facts` (the flat structural fact-id set) now rides the frontier report beside the per-scene `scene_coverage.structural` count, so a consumer can JOIN it to each fact's branch and actually RETIRE its external id-prefix heuristic (a per-scene aggregate alone could not be joined per-fact). MCP tool description updated for the new report fields.
verification_bullets: Density on the REAL consumer store (827 facts) now surfaces the gap's signal: the divergent forks read LOW (braid 0.12-0.17, ending 0.27-0.47) against main 2.94 — a 6-10x deficit where R617's own-segment version read a signal-hiding 2-4x; the fact-rich confluence weave-firstcall reads 3.78, not the confusing "n/a rides trunk". structural_facts = 80, matching the per-scene structural sum exactly (the consumer's id-regex plumbing set).

Rewritten ops density test locks the R619 shape end-to-end through authoring_frontier_report: main density > 1.0 (facts-per-scene, legitimate), a CONFLUENCE gets a real Some density (the own-segment version's fatal divide-by-zero case), a facts-only/undeclared divergence gets a real Some density (never the confusing None).

Rewritten structural test locks the THREE guards the R618 test missed (it was vacuous on the Expected guard): an Unmarked fact a completion pays off is NOT a giving (Expected guard), a completed_by fact whose subject is not a registered quest binds no giving (kind:quest gate = the shared kernel, so structural agrees with quest_graph), and a genuine Chekhov setup is not structural. The 3 quest_graph tests still pass (the SSOT refactor is behaviour-preserving).
The fix SIMPLIFIED the code: -304 / +183 lines. green: mnemosyne-validate 293 lib, cargo test --workspace, clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings, fmt --all --check.
carry_forward_bullets: The cost-no-object review was done by THREE independent adversarial reviewers (SSOT/structure, north-star/cost, correctness/silent-fail) plus self-audit — and they found real defects a solo self-review had passed clean (the wrong-question density, the own_road bug, the SSOT divergence, the vacuous test). This is the standing lesson: independent adversarial review is required at this bar, not optional.

Round 620 — discoverability defects: the.decision_summary: The first playable consumer (the-tide-that-counts: 852-fact store, running branching VN) filed a field report concluding Mnemosyne lacked world-state, player projection, carrier semantics, open predicates, and a map — and was wrong 7/7, rebuilding ~1,100 lines of Python outside the store. Reviewed adversarially: the count is 9, not 7 (the report also missed QUEST_PRED_REQUIRES = the declarative prerequisite, and its own claim that no round ever discussed fact-to-fact derivation is refuted by R493, which PoC-tested a causes edge and REFUTED it on data). The report's thesis is therefore CONFIRMED and its evidence is the proof: this is a DISCOVERABILITY failure, not a capability gap. Measured causes, all Mnemosyne-side and all mechanical: (1) the CLI bare-invocation usage string was a second hand-maintained command list that had drifted to omit every narrative verb (validate-continuity, describe-schema, report-playable-world, report-quest-graph, report-typing-candidates) while still naming 52, so it read exhaustive; (2) the auto-loaded MCP onboarding resources affirmatively taught that the store has 4 closed-form entity types, citing a Round 60 that is off-main, a claim already stale by R273; (3) CLAUDE.md's own citation-hygiene protocol told agents to prefix-match round-NNN-- against list_sections, which returns spec sections only, so it answers does-not-exist for all 366 rounds; (4) README framed the narrative half as deferred future work and speculated primitives that were never built. The consumer was not careless: the documented onboarding path told them the substrate did not exist. DECISION: fix the front door, and kill the drift class rather than refresh its instances — no restated counts, point at describe-schema. R585 built describe-schema because THIS consumer reported THIS gap; it still did not reach them, so the fix is routing, not another artifact.

Round 621 — R620 self-review: 5 ledger c.decision_summary: Owner-requested cost-no-object review of R620 (three independent adversarial reviewers: correctness / SSOT-structure / charter). R620's thesis and its structural move (delete the second command list) survive; its ledger does not. FIVE corrections, append-only (R620 is pushed and frozen). The sharpest is self-inflicted: R620's own synthesis says the store's three rule classes are all INVARIANT-shaped and that no GUARD-shaped primitive exists — and then R620 cited R493, whose every decomposition target is a violation-detector, as refuting the field report's request for a guard. The synthesis refutes the refutation. R493 answered causes-as-gate; the report's 3b asked derives_from-as-guard; they are different questions and the consumer's explicit hedge was right. Second: R620 claimed R527 gap #2 was never refuted and never built while the SAME entry credits the requires predicate as the consumer's missed capability — R559 refuted gap #2 and R568 built it; the true residue is only the STATE-EXPRESSION guard (trust >= 3), which no round owns. Third: the round about unverified hand-maintained counts asserted an unverified hand-maintained count (53, not 52) into a permanent ledger, and the deleted list also named a verb that dispatches to nothing, proving the drift was BIDIRECTIONAL and a one-directional gate insufficient. Fourth: R560 never knowingly reverted R547 — it cites R547 approvingly and calls itself a correction of its own looser restatements; the R546-plus-R560-versus-R547 reading is this author's reconstruction, not any round's finding. Fifth: the round's most valuable output (3a/3b/3c are ONE missing guard direction; the store already has a condition evaluator in the Interval rule class, pointed the wrong way) lived only in gitignored scratch while CLAUDE.md names changelog_entries the SSOT — it is now here. STANDING LESSON reaffirmed at cost: solo self-review missed all five, exactly as at R619 (7 findings). Independent adversarial review is not optional at this bar. The structural debt R620 left (a test gating two hand-maintained lists instead of one derived table, plus the doc surfaces it never swept) is carried to R622-R624, named not silent.

Round 622 — one COMMANDS table: dispatch.decision_summary: R621's structural carry, paid. R620 declared "kill the drift class rather than refresh its instances" and then did neither completely: it deleted ONE hand-maintained command list and gated the survivor with a source-parsing test, and it refreshed the ENGLISH instance of a two-copy README while the consumer is Korean. Three independent reviewers converged on the same verdict — a test is DETECTION, not DERIVATION — and one of them compiled a working proof that the registry objection (four different handler signatures) was unfounded. So: dispatch and help now iterate ONE static COMMANDS table. The 693 lines of parallel logic+data collapse to ~120 lines of types plus declarative data; a verb that dispatches is a verb the help names, by construction, not by assertion. The old gate is deleted outright rather than kept beside the table (no legacy carry) — its source-parser was itself defeatable by ordinary rustfmt output and its len>40 non-vacuity guard was theater against a real count of 81. Safety came from byte-identity, not from review: the pre-refactor --help was captured and the post-refactor output diffs empty against it. The same sweep paid the doc surfaces R620 left: the false SSOT claim R620 itself shipped (describe_schema emits no record types, is store-independent by design, and its own source calls its prose tier "the one part that can drift"), the contradiction R620 created between overview.md and atomic-store.md, README.ko.md's untouched 16-typed-tools and Phase-1-is-deferred, both READMEs' unparseable quickstart TOML and dead verify-generated CI step, and a _vN ban violation living inside two docs. HONEST RESIDUE, named not silent: every one of these was found by a human-style read, not by a gate. The generalization — parse fenced toml through the real Config, check every documented verb against the table — is R623 and is not built. Until it is, this round is one more instance-refresh, better executed.

Round 623 — the doc gate: a documented v.decision_summary: The generalization R620 named and did not build, and R622 carried: a gate for the docs. R620 and R622 fixed real doc defects — a config key dead since Round 400 shipping in five copy-paste blocks ~220 rounds later, both READMEs' quickstart failing to parse, a CI example invoking a verb deleted with GENERATED.md, a _vN-banned identifier living inside two docs — and every one of them was found by a person reading. That is the same posture that let them rot in the first place, so the round is not done until the question is decided by a machine. Two questions about tracked docs are decidable and are now gated: a verb a reader is told to run must dispatch, and a mnemosyne.toml a reader is told to paste must parse through the real loader. The verb oracle is --help, which R622 made a projection of the COMMANDS table, so the gate cannot drift from dispatch. IT IMMEDIATELY EARNED ITSELF: both READMEs documented a CLI commit verb recording design-doc transactions to RocksDB column families — it has never dispatched, and mnemosyne-store is not even a CLI dependency. Three independent adversarial reviewers and two rounds of hand-sweeping missed it; the gate named it in one run. Getting it precise mattered more than getting it built: the first parser read cargo's own --force as a verb, the second cut --help to help, the third mis-paired fence backticks with prose, and the fourth silently MISSED the English commit mention because the inline span wrapped a line while catching the Korean one — an under-detecting verification path, the exact failure this arc condemns. A gate that cries wolf gets ignored and a gate that under-detects is worse than none, so both floors are counted and the non-vacuity is proven by injection.
changes_bullets: new tests/docs_match_reality_smoke.rs: every mnemosyne-cli <verb> a tracked doc tells a reader to run must dispatch, and every fenced mnemosyne.toml must parse through the real mnemosyne_config::parse_config
verb set is read from --help, which R622 made a projection of the COMMANDS table — so the gate's oracle cannot drift from dispatch

scope is INSTRUCTION only: shell-fence lines + inline-code spans. Prose about the tool is excluded (routes through `mnemosyne-cli` mutate API must not read `mutate` as a verb); frozen ledger (docs/.atomic) and claudedocs experiment records are excluded as accurate history, not drift
inline spans are scanned over the whole prose body, not per line — a span wrapping a newline is one span in rendered markdown and a per-line scan silently misses it
config gate checks only blocks carrying [workspace] (the loader requires it); a lone [atomic] override block is a documented fragment, not a paste-whole config

FOUND AND FIXED by the gate, missed by three reviewers and by hand: both READMEs documented `mnemosyne-cli commit` recording transactions to RocksDB column families — no such verb has ever dispatched, and mnemosyne-store is not a CLI dependency at all
verification_bullets: gate is NON-VACUOUS by injection: a planted `mnemosyne-cli totally-fake-verb` line in a tracked doc is caught by file:line, and the gate returns green when reverted

gate is PRECISE: 0 false positives across all tracked instructional markdown. Three parser defects were found and fixed before landing — cargo's own --force/--test flags read as verbs, `mnemosyne-cli --` markers cut `--help` to `help`, and fence backticks mis-paired with prose backticks

the whole-prose span scan was proven load-bearing: the English `commit` mention wraps a line and the per-line scan had MISSED it while catching the Korean one, i.e. the first parser was silently under-detecting
