Round 635 — DEBT-H closed: a misspelled.decision_summary: R635 closes DEBT-H, the gap the CONSUMER filed after it bit them: a canon-order file whose top-level key is misspelled deserialized to an EMPTY order instead of failing loud. The file is named *order*, so writing `order` instead of `edges` is the plausible mistake; with both fields serde(default) it produced order_nodes=0 — no road, every fact incomparable, an interval rule silently dropping to unverifiable so interval_severity=reject did NOT gate — while validate-continuity still printed violations: 0, exit 0. A green gate over a store with no order; they nearly filed a false "canon_to is broken" bug against us because of it. THE FIX IS THE PRECEDENT TWENTY LINES AWAY: deny_unknown_fields on CanonOrderFile, mirroring NarrativeRulesWire (R472/R605), whose own doc says a misspelled key must not silently leave the rules unloaded. The rules file had the defense; the order file — which the same contract says a store NEEDS to be renderable — did not. MY FIRST CUT WAS WRONG AND THE SUITE CAUGHT IT: I copied only half the precedent. The rules wire MODELS `schema` and `comment` so a THIRD unknown key fails loud; I omitted them, and the bare guard REJECTED five legitimate order files (the repo poc corpus carries those tags), turning three CLI smoke tests red. Over-rejection is as fatal as the silent pass it replaces — both tags are now modelled, so the guard bites the typo and passes the real corpus. UNDER R633's third destination this is the canonical case: the skeleton looked fine (facts, sections, rules all green) and play was impossible (no order = no road = nothing comparable); a play-readiness gate must catch that from the skeleton alone, and this one did not. Verified: the consumer's exact repro now fails loud naming the real key; a schema-tagged file, a comment-slotted file, and the REAL consumer store (order_nodes=66) all load clean; a third unknown key still fails loud; load-bearing by neutralization. 959 tests, clippy-D 0, fmt, non-breaking.

Round 636 — the drift claim R631 made an.decision_summary: R636 pays the LAST defect from the owner's cost-no-object session review, and it is the one that stings most: R631 reintroduced the exact anti-pattern R629 had been paid to delete THREE ROUNDS EARLIER. R631 stated the quest object rule TWICE in the published contract — object_shape (prose) and required_object_kind (machine) — and asserted in a doc comment that "the two cannot drift" while binding them with NOTHING; R629's entire content was deleting three comments that claimed a drift-safety the code lacked. The drift is not cosmetic: describe-schema is the authority R620 designated after a consumer concluded seven present capabilities were absent, so prose reading "scalar" while the machine enforces entity teaches a shape the guard rejects — the same misleading-contract mechanism, same surface, same reason. FIXED BY A TEST THAT MAKES THE CLAIM TRUE rather than by softening the comment: quest_object_shape_prose_matches_the_enforced_kind asserts the prose NAMES the enforced kind, and for the None case (completed_by accepts entity OR scalar) that it names BOTH, so that arm is not half-vacuous. Proven by injection both ways: prose flipped to "scalar" fails naming the absent kind; completed_by prose dropping one kind fails the None arm. R631's "STORE boundary … by construction" wording is CORRECTED FORWARD: verified that the malformed fact PERSISTS at write (add-fact accepts it; atomic cannot reject it without learning the reserved quest names, which would invert the crate dependency) and is caught only when a reader runs — so it is a validate-layer gate every quest reader calls at entry, correct and complete for every reader that EXISTS, but a NEW reader that forgets the call is unguarded, and nothing forces it the way R634's tripwire forces a remover to pair with a scan. That limit is now in the code instead of hidden behind "by construction". The session review is fully paid (R634 R630's half-measure, R635 DEBT-H, R636 this). 960 tests, clippy-D 0, fmt.

So it is a validate-layer gate every quest reader calls at entry — correct, complete for every reader that EXISTS, but not by-construction: a NEW quest reader that forgets the call is unguarded, and nothing forces it the way R634's tripwire forces a remover to pair with a scan. verification_bullets: THE PARITY TEST CATCHES REAL DRIFT IN BOTH DIRECTIONS, proven by injection because a passing test is not evidence: flipping `requires` prose to "scalar (the prerequisite quest)" while required_object_kind stays Entity FAILS with "enforced kind `entity` is absent from its published prose"; dropping "scalar" from `completed_by`'s "entity or scalar value" FAILS the None arm with "accepts BOTH kinds, but its prose does not name both". Restored, green.

THE BY-CONSTRUCTION CLAIM WAS TESTED, not assumed: a predicate named `requires` registered with --object-kind scalar plus a scalar-object fact is ACCEPTED and PERSISTED by add-fact (written_bytes non-zero), and only validate-continuity / the quest readers reject it. That is what makes "by construction" false and "a gate every reader calls" true.

Round 637 — the play-readiness gate clas.decision_summary: R637 = the Round 633 COVERAGE round (code 0), the third-destination frontier: reverse-engineer the consumer's knots.py into a CATALOG of play-time problem classes and map each to a gate we HAVE or LACK — measured by INJECTION on a copy of their real 852-fact store, because every play-readiness hole found so far was found by someone STUMBLING on it (the consumer filed DEBT-H; reviewers found R630's two missed classes and R631's drift), never by asking what makes a store un-playable. That question was the round. SEVEN classes catalogued from six named gates plus two unnamed guards; four injected end-to-end. THE HEADLINE: the store has FOUR graphs that can cycle and THREE enforce acyclicity — canon order fail-louds (continuity.rs:145), succession is a first-class ContinuityViolation::SuccessionCycle (:1247/:2443), the branch forest is acyclic by construction (schema.rs:1142) — while the FOURTH, the quest prerequisite graph and the ONLY ONE AN AUTHOR DRAWS FREELY, has no check at all (prereqs declared :4275, written :4293, read :4408, never traversed): an injected six-knot cycle passes the write path, exits 0 on validate-continuity with violations 0, and leaves unresolved_quests empty, while the consumer's own gate FAILS on it. THE HEADLINE, verified first-hand in source: the store has FOUR graphs that can cycle, and THREE of them enforce acyclicity. Canon order fail-louds (`continuity.rs:145`, "a cyclic declaration is no order"). Succession is a first-class violation (`ContinuityViolation::SuccessionCycle`, declared `:1247`, pushed `:2443`). The branch forest is acyclic BY CONSTRUCTION (`schema.rs:1142`, forks immutable after registration). The FOURTH — the quest prerequisite graph, THE ONLY ONE AN AUTHOR DRAWS FREELY — has no check at all: `prereqs` is declared (`:4275`), written (`:4293`), read (`:4408`), and never traversed. So C1 is not a new gate class; it is a MISSING MEMBER OF AN EXISTING FAMILY.

WHY THE GAP EXISTS — the round's real yield, and it is NOT oversight. `validate-continuity`'s contract is to stay GREEN WHILE AUTHORING, and the code says so: a dangling setup is "a report finding, deliberately never a gate reject: a WIP story has dangling setups by definition" (`continuity.rs:2903`), and the quest graph is "Never gated — a reading surface, not a defect detector" (`:4201`). Play-readiness is only meaningful when an author claims DONE, so it CANNOT live inside `validate-continuity` without breaking work-in-progress authoring. It is a SECOND GATE CLASS, and nobody built it because nobody had NAMED it — the consumer named it by building `knots.py` at their own boundary. THE LINE THAT SEPARATES THE TWO: continuity GATES CONTRADICTIONS and REPORTS INCOMPLETENESS. A cycle is a contradiction (never legitimate, not even mid-draft); a quest not yet discharged is incompleteness. By that existing line C1 belongs in `validate-continuity` TODAY, while C2/C4 belong to the unbuilt play-readiness class.

PRECISION SPLITS THE CLASSES, and it is what makes this a catalog instead of a wish-list (Round 624's standing lesson: a gate that cries wolf is ignored, one that under-detects is worse than none). C1 is PRECISE: the healthy baseline holds 0 cycles over 14 `requires` edges / 10 subjects, and the injection yields exactly 1, naming the whole six-knot loop. C2 is PRECISE: `unresolved_quests` is `[]` on the baseline and `['q-knot-3']` on the injection — and Round 569's F4 already documented HOW to read it precisely (`Unknown` = not-on-this-road; orphans listed separately; "read the two together"). C7 is NOT precise and that is why it is not gate-ready: `unknown` CONFLATES "not visible in this world" (legitimate — `q-haewon` in a flee ending) with "nothing discharges it anywhere" (my injection), so BOTH produce the same signal and a naive order gate FIRES FOUR TIMES ON A HEALTHY SHIPPING STORE.

NO BUILD, AND THE LICENSING IS STATED HONESTLY RATHER THAN ASSUMED: C1's fix fits an existing family and Round 633's raised telos, but NO fault has been measured in the wild — the consumer's baseline has zero cycles, so Round 493's measured-fault clause is NOT met and must not be claimed. The next round argues contract-symmetry explicitly or refutes it, under independent adversarial review before any build (the standing five-for-five lesson of this arc).

Its entry key is a BARE `Round 568` with no title, while CLAUDE.md prefix-matches `Round NNN ` WITH A TRAILING SPACE. 96 of 383 entries (25%) carry bare keys, so the documented procedure declares A QUARTER OF THE LEDGER hallucinated — and CLAUDE.md's rule for zero matches is "Do NOT write the citation". Round 620 changed `list_sections` to `list_changelog` and inherited the trailing-space match unexamined. Registered as the next round's target, not deferred.
verification_bullets: METHOD, and the vacuity trap it exists to avoid: every injection went through the MUTATE API (`import-facts` / `retract-fact` / `set-disclosure`), never a hand-edit of the store JSON, and EVERY INJECTION WAS PROVEN LANDED by re-reading the sidecar BEFORE any gate's silence was read — a silent gate over an injection that never landed is exactly the false negative Round 624 named, and it bit twice here in miniature (a missing `--reason` made `retract-fact` and `remove-disclosure` no-ops, and both were caught by the landed-check rather than being written up as "the gate stayed green").

BASELINE FIRST, ON THE COPY: `validate-continuity` reproduces `facts=852 order_nodes=66 conflict_pairs=1 unordered=0 / violations: 0`, exit 0, and the consumer's own `knots.py --check` reports `PASS (하드 0 · 경고 0)` — so every injected FAIL below is attributable to the injection and not to the copy.

THE CONSUMER'S REPOSITORY WAS NEVER MUTATED: all work ran on a scratch copy of `mnemosyne.toml` + `canon-order.json` + the sidecar; `git status` in their repo was clean before and after, and only read-only paths (`knots.py --check`, which writes nothing) ever ran against their tree.

FOUR INJECTIONS x THREE GATES, each landed and each probed: INJ-1 cycle (`q-knot-1 requires q-knot-6`) — import exit 0, fact present, `validate-continuity` exit 0 / violations 0, `unresolved_quests=[]`, `knots.py` FAIL. INJ-2 no-discharger (retract `f-complete-q-knot-3`) — fact gone, `validate-continuity` exit 0 / violations 0, `unresolved_quests=['q-knot-3']`, `knots.py` FAIL. INJ-3 dangling opener (`opened_by` -> `f-mother-is-sunnye`, a one-character typo) — import exit 0, all our surfaces silent, `knots.py` FAIL. INJ-4 undiggable opener (`set-disclosure --mode withhold` on `f-mother-is-sunnyeo`) — override landed as `{"mode":"withhold"}`, all our surfaces silent, `knots.py` FAIL naming the exact consequence.

ROUND 626's GUARD AND ROUND 627's HATCH BOTH WORKED IN THE FIELD, unplanned and on a real store: INJ-2's retract was REFUSED — "fact `f-complete-q-knot-3` carries a disclosure decision under telling `reader` — clear each with `remove_disclosure`" — and following that instruction cleared the decision and let the retract through. The refusal named a hatch that existed and worked; that is the defect Round 627 was paid to fix, verified from the consumer's position rather than from a fixture.

C7's NEGATIVE RESULT IS MEASURED, NOT REASONED: the untouched baseline — a store both gates call green — ALREADY contains 4 done-before-prerequisite pairs (`q-haewon` done in `ending-flee` and `ending-substitute` while `q-apology` and `q-remains` are `unknown` there). The injection adds 5 more (`q-knot-4` done while `q-knot-3` is `unknown`, in every world), and the two sets are INDISTINGUISHABLE BY STATE — both read `unknown`. That is the evidence that the naive form of this check is a false-positive machine and the reason it is catalogued as not-gate-ready instead of proposed as a build.

INDEPENDENT CORROBORATION, source-only and separate from the injections: a second reader over the crates independently reported requires-cycle detection ABSENT (every use of `QUEST_PRED_REQUIRES` enumerated — const `:4013`, a `matches!` at `:4091`, the index arm at `:4290`; no traversal, no visited set, no toposort), `opened_by` at ZERO hits anywhere in `crates/`, `quest_graph` never gated (`:4201`), and the GUARD direction ("if X then unlock") absent by design (a closed three-variant `RuleClass`; every rule arm only pushes violations). Every one of those agrees with what the injections measured from the outside — two methods, one answer.

CITATION HYGIENE RE-RUN CORRECTLY after the procedure defect was found: `Round 442 / 452 / 493 / 546 / 547 / 559 / 560 / 568 / 569 / 589 / 616 / 624 / 625 / 626 / 627 / 631 / 633 / 636` all EXIST in the store (383 entries), `Round 568` confirmed present at list line 315 with a bare `Round 568` key and a body that reads "R568 = the BUILD round for the fact->quest leg". The RESUME memory's claim that R568 built the quest leg is TRUE; the momentary doubt was the broken procedure talking.

THE C1 BUILD IS NAMED BUT UNLICENSED, and the next round that wants it must argue the licensing rather than inherit it from this catalog: the quest prerequisite graph is the only cycle-shaped graph in the store with no acyclicity check, which is contract-symmetry with `SuccessionCycle` / canon-order / the branch forest, but ZERO cycles have been measured in the wild, so Round 493's measured-fault clause is unmet. Independent adversarial review before build (five-for-five this arc: every first design was refuted).

C2 AND C4 ARE THE PLAY-READINESS CLASS AND IT IS UNBUILT AND UNNAMED IN THE SUBSTRATE: they cannot be folded into `validate-continuity` without breaking its WIP-green contract (`continuity.rs:2903`). For C2 the precise signal already exists and Round 569 F4 already documented how to read it (`unresolved_quests` + `Unknown`, together); what is missing is a gate that runs when an author claims DONE. C4's opener predicate is the consumer's by Round 616 decision C, but the SHAPE — a precondition the world can never satisfy — is universal and unowned.

REGISTERED DEBT SSOT REMAINS `Round 632` (DEBT-C re-scoped to typing the reject path, DEBT-D vendor the init skill, DEBT-E single-entry changelog read, DEBT-F overturned and held open, DEBT-G the fact leg with its three preconditions, DEBT-PROSE). This round touched none of them and closes none; DEBT-E is now on the critical path because the citation fix rides it.

METHOD CARRY, cheap and reusable: the injection harness for this catalog is a copy of the consumer's real store plus mutate-API injections, each proven landed before any gate is read. It cost one round and found more than reasoning about the code did — the code-reading pass and the injections agreed, but only the injections produced the false-positive count that disqualified C7.

Round 638 — the citation check stops bei.decision_summary: R638 pays the defect R637 found while running this repo's OWN citation hygiene, and it is Round 620's exact class living inside the text Round 620 rewrote to fix it. THE DEFECT, MEASURED: CLAUDE.md told every agent to prefix-match 'Round NNN ' WITH A TRAILING SPACE against list_changelog's keys, on the stated premise 'Keys are Round NNN — <title>'. That premise is FALSE for 96 of 383 entries (25%) whose keys are a bare 'Round NNN' with no title, so the documented check answers 0 matches for a QUARTER OF THE LEDGER and its own rule for 0 matches is 'hallucinated ... Do NOT write the citation'. R637 hit it live: Round 568, the round that BUILT report-quest-graph and was R637's own subject, reported as hallucinated; the false-negative set also holds Round 400, Round 416-428, Round 500, Round 561-568 and Round 585. R620's own closing paragraph states the principle it then violated — 'A verification path that silently answers does not exist for things that do exist is worse than none: it is exactly how the first playable consumer concluded seven present capabilities were absent' — and it inherited the trailing-space match unexamined, re-committing the defect at a quarter scale instead of a whole one. THE SHARPEST FINDING: the correct resolver ALREADY EXISTED IN PRODUCTION CODE. The code-refs citation gate has always known both key shapes and normalized them, its comment saying so verbatim; the machine was right and the PROSE invented a competing rule and got it wrong. So the fix is DERIVATION, not a corrected ritual — writing the right rule into CLAUDE.md would be a second statement of one semantic bound by nothing, the exact class Round 636 convicted R631 of three rounds ago. The rule is extracted to ONE home (normalize_entry_citation in code_refs.rs; the gate calls it, same code, same behaviour, prefix stays config-driven from [schema].entry_id_prefix), and prose now NAMES A VERB instead of restating a rule. THE VERB PAYS DEBT-E: query --changelog-entry <ID> plus the MCP twin query_changelog_entry — the --inventory twin the decision SSOT never had, whose registered text already pointed at this very procedure. One primitive answers 'does this round exist?' (exit 1 = do not write it) AND returns the decision, so the two-call dance CLAUDE.md deferred is gone. Layered against the real crate graph: mnemosyne-query owns the view, mnemosyne-validate owns the citation rule, mnemosyne-ops composes, CLI and MCP both call the one op — my first cut put resolution in the CLI, which would have forced a second copy into MCP, caught in self-review. Ambiguity FAILS LOUD rather than picking: the resolver reduces an alpha-suffixed key (the Round 474 base-26 column, Round 311aa) to its base number, so a citation resolving to several entries names them and refuses — 0 such keys exist today (measured, not assumed), and the hole is closed before it can open. Two stale carries in the same section deleted per the no-legacy-carry rule: the deferred 'verify_round_citation MCP tool, add only if friction shows' is now BUILT with its condition met twice over, and 'pre-commit gate rejects missing citations (Stage 2)' was listed out-of-scope while it has been shipping for rounds — corrected forward, including the scope limit nobody had written down (the gate scans crates/*/src/ only, so commit messages, ledger prose, CLAUDE.md and memory are uncovered, which is exactly why the writing-moment check still belongs to the agent). PROOF: injecting CLAUDE.md's own rule into the resolver turns 2 of the 4 new pins RED — the procedure is machine-refuted, not merely argued wrong. And the gate caught me committing the very thing this round is about: I wrote Round 638 into 9 code sites BEFORE appending this entry and validate-code-refs rejected all 9, enforcing on its own author the order CLAUDE.md states.

IT IS ROUND 620'S EXACT CLASS INSIDE THE TEXT ROUND 620 WROTE TO FIX IT. It then inherited the trailing-space match unexamined and re-committed the defect one step down, at a quarter scale instead of a whole one.

THE CORRECT RESOLVER ALREADY EXISTED IN PRODUCTION CODE, and this is the round's sharpest finding: `code_refs.rs`'s citation gate has always known BOTH key shapes and normalized them — its comment says so verbatim ("Atomic ledger keys are either short-form (`Round 292`) or long-form (`Round 293 — <title>`); both get normalized"). The machine was right and the prose invented a competing rule and got it wrong. Proven end-to-end before any fix: `validate-code-refs` resolves a citation to bare-key `Round 568` cleanly and rejects `Round 9999` as a `Missing` violation.

THE FIX IS DERIVATION, NOT A CORRECTED RITUAL. Writing the right matching rule into CLAUDE.md would be a SECOND STATEMENT of one semantic bound by nothing — the exact class Round 636 convicted R631 of three rounds ago, and Round 622's line ("a test is DETECTION, not DERIVATION") applied to prose. So: the rule is extracted to ONE home, `normalize_entry_citation(prefix, s)` in `code_refs.rs` (the gate now calls it — same code, same behaviour, one owner, and the prefix stays CONFIG-driven from `[schema].entry_id_prefix`, never a hardcoded "Round "); prose STOPS RESTATING THE RULE and names the verb instead.

THE VERB, WHICH PAYS DEBT-E: `query --changelog-entry <ID>` (+ the MCP twin `query_changelog_entry`) — the `--inventory <ID>` twin the decision SSOT never had. DEBT-E is registered as "reading ONE decision means dumping all 374 and filtering by hand ... I hit this friction MYSELF three times this session and normalized it as my own inconvenience — which is precisely how R620's consumer-facing defects were born", and its own text already points at this procedure. One primitive answers "does this round exist?" (exit 1 = do not write it) AND returns the decision, so the two-call dance CLAUDE.md deferred is gone. Layered so no crate learns another's job: `mnemosyne-query` owns the view projection, `mnemosyne-validate` owns the citation rule, `mnemosyne-ops` composes, and CLI + MCP both call the one op.

AMBIGUITY FAILS LOUD RATHER THAN PICKING ONE, because the resolver reduces an alpha-suffixed key (the Round 474 base-26 column, `Round 311aa`) to its base number: if one citation ever resolves to several entries the read names them and refuses. Today's ledger has 0 suffixed and 0 fractional keys so nothing collides — measured, not assumed — but a silently-arbitrary answer is precisely the class this round exists to kill, so it is closed before it can open. An EXACT key always wins outright, so citing a suffixed key resolves to itself.

TWO STALE CARRIES IN THE SAME SECTION DELETED (the no-legacy-carry rule): "Dedicated `verify_round_citation(n)` MCP tool — add only if the two-call dance shows real friction in practice" is now BUILT, and its own condition was met twice over (DEBT-E's three hits; R637's false negative). "Pre-commit gate that rejects missing/superseded citations (Stage 2)" was listed as out-of-scope while the MISSING half has been shipping for rounds — the hook runs `validate-code-refs` on every commit. Corrected forward to what is actually true, including the scope limit nobody had written down: the gate scans `crates/*/src/` ONLY, so commit messages, ledger prose, CLAUDE.md, and memory are NOT covered — which is exactly why the writing-moment check still belongs to the agent.
verification_bullets: THE PIN IS PROVEN LOAD-BEARING BY INJECTION, in the only way that matters here: CLAUDE.md's own broken rule was INJECTED into the resolver (require a trailing space after the number) and 2 of the 4 new pins went RED — `normalize_entry_citation_reduces_both_stored_key_shapes` and `normalize_entry_citation_does_not_collide_on_a_number_prefix`. So the documented procedure is not merely argued wrong, it is MACHINE-REFUTED: the rule this file taught for rounds cannot pass the test suite. Reverted after the proof.

THE VERB IS CORRECT ON EVERY CASE THE PROSE GOT WRONG, driven on the SHIPPED binary: bare key `Round 568` exit 0 (the 96-entry class), long key `Round 637` exit 0, the full stored key with its title exit 0, `Round 9999` exit 1 naming it hallucinated, `Round 56` exit 1 (it must never latch onto `Round 568` — the same missing-boundary bug in reverse), pre-ledger `Round 60` exit 1 (off-main, correctly unwritable per the existing rule), and a bare `568` exit 1 naming the shape it wants. Seven for seven.

THE REFACTOR IS BEHAVIOUR-PRESERVING ON THE GATE IT TOUCHES, which was the one real risk: `valid_entry_ids` in the code-refs gate now calls the extracted `normalize_entry_citation` instead of an inline copy — same code, same result. Verified by the full suite plus `validate-code-refs` on the real workspace, which reported exactly and only the 9 `Round 638` citations this round's own code introduced (see the next bullet), zero regressions elsewhere.

THE GATE CAUGHT ME COMMITTING THE VERY THING THIS ROUND IS ABOUT, and the order is recorded honestly rather than quietly fixed: I wrote `Round 638` into 9 code sites BEFORE appending this entry, and `validate-code-refs` rejected all 9 as `Missing`. CLAUDE.md states the order plainly ("the round entry must already exist ... before the citation lands") and I had it backwards. The entry landed first, and the citations then resolved — the machine enforcing the discipline on its own author is the best evidence it works.

GREEN, on the whole workspace: 964 tests / 0 failed (was 960 — the 4 new pins), `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` 0, `cargo fmt --all`, `validate-workspace` exit 0 (T1 orphan 0, T3 reject 0, ledger 384, decay 0, commit-ledger drift missing=0). Both surfaces installed with `cargo install --force` (cli + mcp), because R585's lesson is that a tool that is not shipped does not reach — and this one's whole value is being called by the next agent that writes a citation.

LAYERING VERIFIED AGAINST THE CRATE GRAPH, not assumed: `mnemosyne-query` and `mnemosyne-validate` are siblings (neither depends on the other), and `mnemosyne-ops` depends on both — so the composition belongs in ops and nowhere else. Putting the resolution in the CLI (where my first cut had it) would have forced a second copy into MCP, which is the drift this round is paying off; caught in self-review before commit.

Round 640 — the adopter's first surface.decision_summary: R640 pays DEBT-D: the `mnemosyne-init` skill — the surface an ADOPTER MEETS FIRST — is vendored into the repo, put under the gates that already existed, and rewritten to the model that has been true since Round 400. THE REGISTERED FIX WAS FALSE AS WRITTEN, and catching that was the round's first work: DEBT-D says "vendor it in (gates then cover it)", but `.gitignore:5` ignored `.claude/` wholesale, so vendoring alone would have produced an UNTRACKED file — the doc gate's scope is exactly `git ls-files "*.md"`, so the file would have sat in the repo, covered by nothing, while the ledger recorded "the gates now cover it". That is the false-verification class this arc keeps convicting. Git cannot re-include through an excluded parent, so the ignore was re-cut per level (`.claude/*` + `!.claude/skills/` + `.claude/skills/*` + `!.claude/skills/mnemosyne-init/`), verified both ways: the skill is trackable and `settings.local.json` / everything else under `.claude/` stays ignored. THE GATES THEN BIT ON FIRST CONTACT, unprompted — the moment the file was tracked, `documented_configs_all_parse` failed on BOTH config examples (lines 71 and 90) and `documented_verbs_all_dispatch` failed on line 162's `generate-docs`. Non-vacuity needed no injection: the real file WAS the injection. THE DEFECT WAS NOT THREE TYPOS, IT WAS A PRE-R400 ARTIFACT. The skill taught the markdown-doc model that Round 400 deleted: its frontmatter description sold "atomic-store + GENERATED.md design-doc lifecycle", both `mnemosyne.toml` examples were built on `docs` / `default_doc`, and step 4 told the agent to report "round-trip mandatory = X/Y (parser/emitter drift)" — a metric `validate-workspace` does not print (0 hits in its real output), so an agent following the skill would report a number it had to invent. Measured e2e: the config it instructs you to write FAILS TO PARSE (`TOML parse error ... default_doc`), so the adopter hits a wall at step 3. THE HISTORY IS EXACT AND IT INDICTS THE STRUCTURE, NOT THE AUTHOR: Round 400 removed `docs`/`default_doc`; Round 605 F3 then extended `deny_unknown_fields` to every section, "exposing + cleaning R400-era dead config keys (docs/default_doc/output_path) across 13 files" — and this file could not be one of the 13, because it lived outside the repo. R605 turned the skill's config from silently-ignored into a hard load failure and had no way to see it. Round 620 fixed every front door it could reach (CLI usage, MCP onboarding, CLAUDE.md's citation rule, the README); Round 623/624 gated the tracked docs. This was the one surface the whole discoverability arc structurally could not touch. THE REWRITE ROUTES INSTEAD OF RESTATING (Round 638's shape again): the skill now names `describe-schema` as the authority for what the store can hold — "do not enumerate the capabilities from memory, they move" — rather than listing them, which is the exact mechanism by which a consumer once concluded seven present capabilities were absent. It carries a minimal config because an adopter's agent has the skill but NOT our repo, so `docs/GETTING_STARTED.md` (the tracked, gated SSOT for bootstrap, which is itself correct) cannot be pointed at from their machine — a copy that cannot be derived away is instead BOUND BY THE GATE, which is why being tracked is the whole fix. Proven end to end: an adopter following the rewritten skill verbatim now gets a config that parses, `validate-workspace` exit 0, a working `describe-schema`, and a successful `add-section` (sections=1). AND THE ROUND PAID ITS OWN DEBT: the skill's new line explaining that every `mnemosyne-cli <verb>` in it must dispatch TRIPPED THE GATE — `invoked_verb`'s trim strips non-alphanumerics, so the placeholder `<verb>` arrived as a verb literally named `verb`, and the gate reported the doc for describing itself. Rewording the sentence would have been a workaround that left the trap for the next author, so the extractor was fixed to skip `<placeholder>` tokens, pinned with a test whose accept legs are the control against going blind, and proven load-bearing by neutralization (neutralize the guard → the pin AND the live gate both fail). One deliberate leftover, named not silent: the owner's private `~/.claude/skills/mnemosyne-init/SKILL.md` still exists and is now the stale twin of a tracked file — two copies of one thing is the drift class this round exists to kill, but it lives outside the repo in the owner's home directory, so deleting it is theirs to authorize.

Re-cut per level (`.claude/*`, `!.claude/skills/`, `.claude/skills/*`, `!.claude/skills/mnemosyne-init/`) and verified BOTH directions: the skill is trackable, and `settings.local.json` plus everything else under `.claude/` stays ignored.

THE ROUND'S OWN DEBT, PAID IN IT: the skill's new line explaining that every `mnemosyne-cli <verb>` in it must dispatch TRIPPED THE GATE. `invoked_verb` trims non-alphanumerics, so the placeholder `<verb>` arrived as a verb named `verb` and the gate reported the doc for describing itself. It exists — at `docs/GETTING_STARTED.md`; I had looked only at the repo root. Verified before it reached the ledger. It is correct, tracked, gated, and it is where the bootstrap truth lives.

THE EXTRACTOR FIX IS LOAD-BEARING BY NEUTRALIZATION: neutralize the `<placeholder>` guard and BOTH the new pin and the live `documented_verbs_all_dispatch` gate fail (the gate then flags the skill's own line). Restored after the proof.

GREEN: 965 workspace tests / 0 failed (was 964 — the new pin), `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` 0, `cargo fmt --all`, `validate-workspace` exit 0 (T1 orphan 0, T3 reject 0, ledger 386, decay 0, commit-ledger drift missing=0). The gitignore re-cut was verified not to over-reach: `.claude/probe/x.md` and `.claude/settings.local.json` both still resolve as ignored.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: DEBT-D IS CLOSED (registered SSOT = `Round 632`): the adopter's first surface is tracked, gated, and true. REOPEN CONDITION: a second copy of the skill appearing anywhere, or the gitignore negation being collapsed back to a bare `.claude/` — either puts the file back outside the gates' sight, which is the whole defect.

THE OWNER'S CALL, NOT A CARRY: `~/.claude/skills/mnemosyne-init/SKILL.md` is now a stale twin of the tracked file. Two copies of one thing is the drift class; it lives in the owner's home directory, so its deletion needs the owner's word. Until then, which copy an agent loads is unpredictable and one of them is wrong.

REGISTERED DEBT REMAINING (SSOT = `Round 632`): DEBT-C (type the reject path — `AtomicMutateError::Validation(String)` collapses 11 invariants into one variant, the only reason `invariants()` cannot derive), DEBT-F (overturned, held open — no build, R493 unmet), DEBT-G (the fact leg — UNLICENSED, not out-of-charter, per Round 639's correction), DEBT-PROSE (~30 CLI usage / MCP description / atomic "expected one of:" mirrors + ~17 `from_tag` sites ending `_ => None` = a variant that serializes but will not parse back). CLOSED: DEBT-A+B (R630+R634), DEBT-E (R638), DEBT-H (R635), DEBT-D (this round), DEBT-C partial (R629).

NEXT = the Round 637 catalogue's C1, build-or-refute (code-0 first, the R559 pattern, independent adversarial review before any build): the quest prerequisite graph is the only cycle-shaped graph in the store with no acyclicity check, which is contract-symmetry with `SuccessionCycle` / canon-order / the branch forest — but R493's measured-fault clause is UNMET (zero cycles in the wild), so the licensing must be ARGUED or the item REFUTED and closed. Leaving it catalogued-but-unruled is how a carry becomes a carry-forever (R628).

STANDING LESSON, extended: this round's registered fix was FALSE AS WRITTEN, and the falsity was structural (an ignore rule), not a typo. A debt item's stated remedy is a hypothesis from the round that filed it — re-verify the remedy, not just the defect, before executing it.

Round 641 — the disease was live in the.decision_summary: R641 is the round R640 should have been, and the owner's cost-no-object review is why it exists: three independent adversarial reviewers were pointed at my proposed direction, and they killed it and found something worse. MY PROPOSAL WAS REFUTED ON MEASUREMENT, not opinion. I wanted the skill to carry no knowledge — config emitted by a new derived verb, contract from `describe-schema`. A reviewer serialized the real `WorkspaceConfig` and got 65 bytes: neither `[atomic] sidecar_path` nor `[schema]` appears, because `Option`/`None` fields are skipped. A useful emitter therefore needs a hand-written template in Rust — THE SAME COPY, RELOCATED OUT OF THE DOC GATE'S SCOPE (`git ls-files "*.md"`), one round after R640 spent itself dragging that copy inside. R493 was unmet too: the gate already closes the measured fault (proven by injection, and it runs in CI). And `describe-schema` is the STORE authority, not the CONFIG authority — 1 incidental config hit in 181 lines. THE REAL FINDING IS THAT THE DISEASE WAS LIVE AT HEAD, in the very files R640's own skill points agents at, and I VERIFIED ALL OF IT FIRST-HAND: `crates/mnemosyne-mcp/resources/tier-rules.md:15` taught "Workspace default_doc: §N exists in `default_doc`" — a key removed at Round 400, dead in every `src/` file — while `workflow.md:47` in the SAME MCP server says it was removed; two agent-loaded resources contradicting each other, gate green. `docs/GETTING_STARTED.md:176` taught the agent to call `list_sections` and prefix-match `round-NNN--`, which IS the Round 620 defect verbatim, still live in the adopter-facing doc after R638 deleted it from CLAUDE.md — and its premise is false: `list_sections` returns 5 spec sections and has never held a round id. `:192` cited `scripts/install-hooks.sh`, retired at Round 306, while `:152` of the same file says it was retired. AND THE SHARPEST ONE, WHICH THE GATE ITSELF HID: `[code_refs]` — renamed to `[plugins.set_equality_validator]` at Round 306 — sat in GETTING_STARTED's paste-able config block, so an adopter following the "5-minute setup" got `TOML parse error ... [code_refs]` and a dead workspace (verified e2e). It survived 335 rounds because TWO independent vacuity rules each skipped that block: the elision rule fired on `...` inside a COMMENT ("e.g. v1.0.0 / v1.1 ..."), and the fragment rule skipped it for lacking `[workspace]`. Fixing either alone changes nothing — belt and braces, both wrong. THE GATE IS NOW STRUCTURALLY WIDER, not merely patched: the elision test reads CODE through a quote-aware comment stripper (an ellipsis in a value still marks a block illustrative, as in `fetched_sha256 = "..."`), and a fragment starting with a section header is given the `[workspace]` the loader wants and PARSED AS WHAT IT IS instead of being skipped. Coverage went from 8 of 25 toml blocks to 21; the 2 bare-key fragments that remain are undecidable from the block alone (which table owns their keys is unknowable) and are named, not silently dropped. That widening caught 3 more real defects nobody had reported: GETTING_STARTED's `[code_refs]`, and BOTH of SCHEMA_GUIDE's `[[orphan_ledger]]` examples, which omit the REQUIRED `since` field and reject on paste. I NEARLY WEAKENED THE GATE TO SILENCE MY OWN FALSE POSITIVE and measurement stopped me: seeing `doc = "<code-citation>"` I moved to skip `<placeholder>` blocks, then checked — `<code-citation>` is a REAL SENTINEL VALUE by convention (the config's own doc says so; our `mnemosyne.toml` uses `<atomic-changelog>`), so that rule would have silently dropped every orphan_ledger example. The one true placeholder (`epub_sha256 = "<sha256 of the committed .epub>"`) was fixed in the DOC instead, by showing the real shape. NEW GATE, the missing direction: Round 624 gated "every real section is NAMED"; nothing gated the converse, "every section a doc NAMES is real" — and the converse is the direction that catches a renamed table. Now gated, derived from serde, scope already excludes the frozen experiment records by construction, 48 tokens checked, proven by injection (restore `[code_refs]` → it fires, naming file and line). FORWARD-CORRECTIONS to R640, mine, one round old and frozen: "a copy ... is instead BOUND BY THE GATE, which is why being tracked is the whole fix" OVER-CLAIMS — the gate binds a config's parse and a verb's dispatch, and the defect that actually happened (frontmatter selling a deleted artifact, a phantom metric) was caught by me reading, not by any gate; that is the Round 636 shape ("cannot drift", bound by nothing) committed by the round that cited R636. And R640 called `docs/GETTING_STARTED.md` "the tracked, gated SSOT for bootstrap, which is itself correct" — it was not: it carried the Round 620 defect, a dead installer path, and a config that rejects on paste.
changes_bullets: FIVE LIVE DEFECTS IN AGENT- AND ADOPTER-FACING DOCS, all verified first-hand before touching anything: (1) `crates/mnemosyne-mcp/resources/tier-rules.md:15` taught a `default_doc` lookup step for a key removed at Round 400 and dead in every `src/` file — while `workflow.md:47` in the same MCP server says it was removed, so two agent-loaded resources contradicted each other; the step is deleted and the lookup is one line, as it has been since R400. (2) `docs/GETTING_STARTED.md:176` taught `list_sections` + prefix-match `round-NNN--` — the Round 620 defect verbatim, still live after R638 deleted it from CLAUDE.md, and factually false (`list_sections` returns 5 SPEC sections and has never held a round id); replaced by `query_changelog_entry`, the R638 verb. (3) the same passage told the agent to read `decision_status` off a changelog entry — a field that exists only on Sections (R620 verified 366/366); replaced by the prose-supersession search. (4) `:192` cited `scripts/install-hooks.sh`, retired at Round 306, while `:152` of the SAME FILE says it was retired — now `git config core.hooksPath .githooks`. (5) `[code_refs]`, renamed to `[plugins.set_equality_validator]` at Round 306, taught as live config in GETTING_STARTED (a paste-able block + 3 prose sites) and in `docs/RECOVERY_PATTERNS.md:213`.

THE `[code_refs]` DEFECT SURVIVED 335 ROUNDS BECAUSE TWO INDEPENDENT VACUITY RULES EACH SKIPPED ITS BLOCK, and fixing either alone would have changed nothing: the elision rule matched `...` inside a COMMENT ("e.g. v1.0.0 / v1.1 ..."), and the fragment rule skipped the block for lacking `[workspace]`. An adopter following the "5-minute setup" got `TOML parse error ... [code_refs]` and a dead workspace — verified end to end.

THE GATE IS WIDER BY CONSTRUCTION, not patched: the elision test now reads CODE through `toml_code_only`, a quote-aware comment stripper (basic `"` with escapes, literal `'`), so an ellipsis in a VALUE still marks a block illustrative while an ellipsis in prose no longer blinds the gate; and a fragment that starts with a section header is given the `[workspace]` the loader requires and parsed as what it is, rather than skipped. Coverage: 8 of 25 toml blocks → 21. The 2 bare-key fragments still skipped are UNDECIDABLE from the block alone (nothing says which table owns their keys) and are named here rather than dropped in silence.

THE WIDENING IMMEDIATELY CAUGHT 3 MORE REAL DEFECTS NOBODY HAD REPORTED: GETTING_STARTED's `[code_refs]` block, and BOTH `[[orphan_ledger]]` examples in `docs/SCHEMA_GUIDE.md` (:251, :265), which omit the REQUIRED `since` field — an adopter pasting either gets `missing field since`. Fixed in the docs; the gate now covers them.

NEW GATE — THE MISSING DIRECTION (`documented_config_sections_all_exist`): Round 624 gated "every real config section is NAMED by the guides" and nothing gated the converse, "every section a doc NAMES is real" — which is the direction that catches a table renamed out of existence. The real set is DERIVED from `WorkspaceConfig`'s serde shape (shared with R624 via `derived_config_sections`, so the two cannot disagree); scope is the existing `tracked_markdown`, which already excludes the frozen experiment records by construction — so the `[state]` / `[main]` tokens in those logs (disclosure modes and branch ids, not config) are out without a carve-out. 48 tokens checked against a floor of 20.

I NEARLY WEAKENED THE GATE TO SILENCE MY OWN FALSE POSITIVE, and measurement stopped me: seeing `doc = "<code-citation>"` I moved to skip `<placeholder>` blocks — but `<code-citation>` is a REAL SENTINEL VALUE by convention (`mnemosyne-config/src/lib.rs:234` says so; our own `mnemosyne.toml` carries `<atomic-changelog>`), so that rule would have silently dropped every `[[orphan_ledger]]` example. The one genuine placeholder (`epub_sha256 = "<sha256 of the committed .epub>"`, `docs/EPUB_SSOT_RUNBOOK.md:111`) was fixed in the DOC instead — it now shows the real 64-hex shape with the substitution instruction beside it. Fix the doc, never blind the gate.

That is the Round 636 shape — "cannot drift" bound by nothing — committed by the round that cited R636. verification_bullets: THE OWNER'S COST-NO-OBJECT STANDARD WAS APPLIED TO A DECISION, NOT JUST A DIFF: three independent adversarial reviewers, one lens each (YAGNI/licensing, gate coverage, SSOT/distribution). Two REFUTED my proposal, one CONFIRMED the diagnosis while refuting its remedy. My solo answer would have been the emitter — the wrong build, in the wrong crate, for a fault already closed.

EVERY REVIEWER CLAIM I RELAY WAS RE-RUN BY ME — reviewers are not oracles, and this arc has rejected their claims before. Confirmed first-hand: `tier-rules.md:15`'s `default_doc` (and `workflow.md:47` contradicting it, and `default_doc` dead in `src/`); `GETTING_STARTED.md:176`'s prefix-match instruction; `:192`'s `scripts/install-hooks.sh` with `git ls-files scripts/` empty; R624's `guides` array being hand-maintained.

THE `[code_refs]` CHAIN IS PROVEN END TO END, not argued: pasting GETTING_STARTED's block (with the `[workspace]` the doc instructs) → `error: mnemosyne.toml parse failed: TOML parse error at line 16 ... [code_refs]`, exit 1. After the fix, the same paste → exit 0. The adopter went from a dead workspace to a working one.

THE ELISION MEASUREMENT IS EXHAUSTIVE, which is what made the fix precise rather than a guess: across every tracked toml block there are exactly 4 ellipses — 1 in a COMMENT (`GETTING_STARTED.md:45`, the false skip) and 3 in VALUES (`fetched_sha256 = "..."`, `changelog_titles = [...]`, `"abcdef0123...64-hex...0123"`, the legitimate elisions). The comment-aware fix flips exactly 1 block into coverage and leaves the other 3 skipped: 1 true positive, 0 false.

THE FRAGMENT WIDENING WAS MEASURED BEFORE IT WAS WRITTEN: of the 15 blocks lacking `[workspace]`, 13 start with a section header (prepending is correct) and 2 are bare-key fragments (prepending would misfile their keys under `[workspace]` and reject a correct doc). The rule keys on that distinction, so the 2 stay skipped for a stated reason.

BOTH NEW PINS ARE LOAD-BEARING BY NEUTRALIZATION / INJECTION: neutralize the `<placeholder>` guard → the pin AND the live verb gate both fail (R640's fix, re-verified). Restore `[code_refs]` into `RECOVERY_PATTERNS.md` → `documented_config_sections_all_exist` fires naming file, line, and the derived real set; revert → green. `toml_code_only`'s pin carries accept legs (a `#` inside a basic string, a literal string, an escaped quote) as the control against over-stripping.

GREEN: 967 workspace tests / 0 failed (was 966), `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` 0, `cargo fmt --all`, `validate-workspace` exit 0, `validate-code-refs` violations total=0 missing=0, the doc gate 6/6. Citation hygiene: every round cited here verified present via `query --changelog-entry` (R638's verb).

SCOPE HONESTY — WHAT THIS ROUND DID NOT BUILD, and why, both measured by a reviewer rather than asserted by me: a forbidden-token gate derived from `git log --diff-filter=D` (51 deleted paths) produces 48 would-be violations and ~0 true defects, because 8 of the 9 `GENERATED.md` hits in gate scope are the sentences that correctly say it was REMOVED — the separating feature is polarity/tense, which is semantic, and prose lint's ceiling is advisory. It would also miss the phantom metric entirely, which names no deleted file.
carry_forward_bullets: THE RESIDUAL THIS ROUND NAMES AND DOES NOT CLOSE: a doc's MENTAL MODEL is still ungated. A reviewer proved it by experiment — re-introduce "GENERATED.md design-doc lifecycle" prose and a phantom metric into the tracked skill and every gate stays green, `validate-workspace` exits 0, and the real pre-commit hook lets the commit land. The deterministic remedies are measured false (see the verification bullets). The move that DOES work is the one R640 made and R641 repeats: do not lint a transcription, DELETE it — a doc that says "read the metrics off the actual output" and "run `describe-schema`" has nothing to drift. `tier-rules.md` went stale precisely because it transcribed a lookup priority. Unenforced, deliberate, named.

THE SKILL'S DISTRIBUTION IS NOT A ROUND AND IS NOT BROKEN — a reviewer refuted the framing I brought: install is `git clone` + `cargo install --path`, so every adopter already has the tracked file on disk. What is missing is DISCOVERABILITY (`grep -rn "skill" README.md docs/GETTING_STARTED.md` → 0 hits: nothing tells an adopter it exists) and ACTIVATION for a checkout (a symlink `~/.claude/skills/mnemosyne-init` → the tracked dir, verified working by a reviewer running `claude -p` from outside the repo; symlinks are documented first-class, and there is no `skillsPath` config). Writing it up as "solves adopter distribution" would be the R636 over-claim class. Owner's call, one line, not a build.

ROUND 624's `guides` LIST IS STILL HAND-MAINTAINED (`["docs/SCHEMA_GUIDE.md", "crates/mnemosyne-mcp/resources/schema-guide.md"]`) — the R629/R636 convict class living inside an anti-drift gate. NOT fixed here, because the honest question is prior: what DEFINES a schema guide? Deriving the list from `resources.rs`'s table would force every MCP resource to name all 13 sections, which is wrong for `tier-rules.md`. A list that encodes a real editorial scope is not the same defect as a list that mirrors a derivable set — but it deserves the argument, not silence.

THE VACUITY FLOORS ARE THEATRE AND SHOULD BE READ AS SUCH: `checked >= 5` now guards 21 blocks, and `checked >= 20` guards 48 tokens. They catch a parser regression to zero and nothing finer — a block silently dropping out would not move them. R622 deleted exactly this shape ("its `>40` non-vacuity guard was theatre against a real count of 81"); these survive only because no better derived floor is available, and that is a weakness, not a design.

And R640 called `docs/GETTING_STARTED.md` "the tracked, gated SSOT for bootstrap, which is itself correct"; it carried the R620 defect, a dead installer path, and a config that rejects on paste.
publishable_verification_bullets: THE OWNER'S COST-NO-OBJECT STANDARD WAS APPLIED TO A DECISION, NOT JUST A DIFF: three independent adversarial reviewers, one lens each (YAGNI/licensing, gate coverage, SSOT/distribution). Two REFUTED my proposal, one CONFIRMED the diagnosis while refuting its remedy. My solo answer would have been the emitter — the wrong build, in the wrong crate, for a fault already closed.

EVERY REVIEWER CLAIM I RELAY WAS RE-RUN BY ME — reviewers are not oracles, and this arc has rejected their claims before. Confirmed first-hand: `tier-rules.md:15`'s `default_doc` (and `workflow.md:47` contradicting it, and `default_doc` dead in `src/`); `GETTING_STARTED.md:176`'s prefix-match instruction; `:192`'s `scripts/install-hooks.sh` with `git ls-files scripts/` empty; R624's `guides` array being hand-maintained.

THE `[code_refs]` CHAIN IS PROVEN END TO END, not argued: pasting GETTING_STARTED's block (with the `[workspace]` the doc instructs) → `error: mnemosyne.toml parse failed: TOML parse error at line 16 ... [code_refs]`, exit 1. After the fix, the same paste → exit 0. The adopter went from a dead workspace to a working one.

THE ELISION MEASUREMENT IS EXHAUSTIVE, which is what made the fix precise rather than a guess: across every tracked toml block there are exactly 4 ellipses — 1 in a COMMENT (`GETTING_STARTED.md:45`, the false skip) and 3 in VALUES (`fetched_sha256 = "..."`, `changelog_titles = [...]`, `"abcdef0123...64-hex...0123"`, the legitimate elisions). The comment-aware fix flips exactly 1 block into coverage and leaves the other 3 skipped: 1 true positive, 0 false.

THE FRAGMENT WIDENING WAS MEASURED BEFORE IT WAS WRITTEN: of the 15 blocks lacking `[workspace]`, 13 start with a section header (prepending is correct) and 2 are bare-key fragments (prepending would misfile their keys under `[workspace]` and reject a correct doc). The rule keys on that distinction, so the 2 stay skipped for a stated reason.

BOTH NEW PINS ARE LOAD-BEARING BY NEUTRALIZATION / INJECTION: neutralize the `<placeholder>` guard → the pin AND the live verb gate both fail (R640's fix, re-verified). Restore `[code_refs]` into `RECOVERY_PATTERNS.md` → `documented_config_sections_all_exist` fires naming file, line, and the derived real set; revert → green. `toml_code_only`'s pin carries accept legs (a `#` inside a basic string, a literal string, an escaped quote) as the control against over-stripping.

GREEN: 967 workspace tests / 0 failed (was 966), `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` 0, `cargo fmt --all`, `validate-workspace` exit 0, `validate-code-refs` violations total=0 missing=0, the doc gate 6/6. Citation hygiene: every round cited here verified present via `query --changelog-entry` (R638's verb).

SCOPE HONESTY — WHAT THIS ROUND DID NOT BUILD, and why, both measured by a reviewer rather than asserted by me: a forbidden-token gate derived from `git log --diff-filter=D` (51 deleted paths) produces 48 would-be violations and ~0 true defects, because 8 of the 9 `GENERATED.md` hits in gate scope are the sentences that correctly say it was REMOVED — the separating feature is polarity/tense, which is semantic, and prose lint's ceiling is advisory. It would also miss the phantom metric entirely, which names no deleted file.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: THE RESIDUAL THIS ROUND NAMES AND DOES NOT CLOSE: a doc's MENTAL MODEL is still ungated. A reviewer proved it by experiment — re-introduce "GENERATED.md design-doc lifecycle" prose and a phantom metric into the tracked skill and every gate stays green, `validate-workspace` exits 0, and the real pre-commit hook lets the commit land. The deterministic remedies are measured false (see the verification bullets). The move that DOES work is the one R640 made and R641 repeats: do not lint a transcription, DELETE it — a doc that says "read the metrics off the actual output" and "run `describe-schema`" has nothing to drift. `tier-rules.md` went stale precisely because it transcribed a lookup priority. Unenforced, deliberate, named.

THE SKILL'S DISTRIBUTION IS NOT A ROUND AND IS NOT BROKEN — a reviewer refuted the framing I brought: install is `git clone` + `cargo install --path`, so every adopter already has the tracked file on disk. What is missing is DISCOVERABILITY (`grep -rn "skill" README.md docs/GETTING_STARTED.md` → 0 hits: nothing tells an adopter it exists) and ACTIVATION for a checkout (a symlink `~/.claude/skills/mnemosyne-init` → the tracked dir, verified working by a reviewer running `claude -p` from outside the repo; symlinks are documented first-class, and there is no `skillsPath` config). Writing it up as "solves adopter distribution" would be the R636 over-claim class. Owner's call, one line, not a build.

ROUND 624's `guides` LIST IS STILL HAND-MAINTAINED (`["docs/SCHEMA_GUIDE.md", "crates/mnemosyne-mcp/resources/schema-guide.md"]`) — the R629/R636 convict class living inside an anti-drift gate. NOT fixed here, because the honest question is prior: what DEFINES a schema guide? Deriving the list from `resources.rs`'s table would force every MCP resource to name all 13 sections, which is wrong for `tier-rules.md`. A list that encodes a real editorial scope is not the same defect as a list that mirrors a derivable set — but it deserves the argument, not silence.

THE VACUITY FLOORS ARE THEATRE AND SHOULD BE READ AS SUCH: `checked >= 5` now guards 21 blocks, and `checked >= 20` guards 48 tokens. They catch a parser regression to zero and nothing finer — a block silently dropping out would not move them. R622 deleted exactly this shape ("its `>40` non-vacuity guard was theatre against a real count of 81"); these survive only because no better derived floor is available, and that is a weakness, not a design.

REGISTERED DEBT (SSOT = `Round 632`): DEBT-C (type the reject path), DEBT-F (overturned, held open), DEBT-G (the fact leg — UNLICENSED, not out-of-charter per R639), DEBT-PROSE. CLOSED: DEBT-A+B (R630+R634), DEBT-D (R640), DEBT-E (R638), DEBT-H (R635), DEBT-C partial (R629).

NEXT = the Round 637 catalogue's C1, build-or-refute (code-0 first, the R559 pattern, independent adversarial review before any build): the quest prerequisite graph is the only cycle-shaped graph in the store with no acyclicity check — contract-symmetry with `SuccessionCycle` / canon-order / the branch forest, but R493's measured-fault clause is UNMET (zero cycles in the wild). ARGUE the licensing or REFUTE and close it; catalogued-but-unruled is how a carry becomes a carry-forever (R628).

Round 642 — the surface is a hand-copy w.decision_summary: R642 answers the consumer's R639 field-report question ("in a telling where the reader IS the player, what does `state` mean?") at code 0, per the R559 refute-or-design pattern with independent adversarial review BEFORE any build. MY ENTIRE RULING WAS REFUTED — all three claims, by counterfactual experiment, and the reviewers' answer is better than mine. I claimed their `state` was FORCED (an untyped fact cannot take `withhold` or `first_at`, so state/hint/imply with no pin is the only legal way to attach a `surface`). The mechanism is real and precise — the write predicate `mode == Withhold || has_first_at` (`atomic/lib.rs:5210-5212`) is the IDENTICAL boolean to the leak gate's targeting `if !is_withhold && pin.is_none() { continue; }` (`disclosure.rs:214`), so the legal-for-untyped set IS the gate-skipped set — but the ATTRIBUTION is false: they use `withhold` ZERO times in all four plans (`default_mode` is absent, so withhold is already the default and an override is unnecessary — the arm never bound), and at the 5 TYPED player facts, where `first_at` was fully legal, they wrote bare `state` with no pin on all five. Where the invariant did not bind they wrote the identical shape. It was CHOSEN. And the counterfactual kills the causal claim outright: bypass the invariant, author an untyped `withhold`, run the gate — it still errors "un-gateable". Un-gateability is intrinsic to untypedness; the invariant only moves discovery from gate-time to write-time. It is a messenger, not a cause. I claimed the disease was typed coverage (9.7%); that collapsed by my own stated criterion. The leak gate's coverage of their `player` telling is EXACTLY ZERO (`targeted=0`) because targeting is `withhold || first_at` and the plan has neither — TYPING ALL 852 FACTS WOULD MOVE `targeted` FROM 0 TO 0. And `first_at` ("where the reader FIRST LEARNS it") is semantically inapplicable when player agency decides when a fact is learned, at any typing level. Their option (a), a fifth `dug` mode, is refuted for a reason I missed: `imply` ALREADY IS that mode ("realised via an object/environment — the Dark-Souls item-text"). Their option (c) is refuted on a false premise of mine: three of the five telling-consumers (`disclosure_coverage`, `playable_world`, `quest_graph`) take one store and no re-extraction — the MCP contract calls playable-world "Reading surface, never gated", render-free BY DESIGN — and they DID render it (`i18n.py:91-130` builds the game's text from `report-playable-world --telling player`). Their option (b), a telling-level access/agency flag, SURVIVES: mode (how plainly the telling commits) and access (must the audience ACT to get it?) are orthogonal axes that the reader-centric vocabulary conflates. THE FINDING THAT REFRAMES THE ROUND, measured and verified by me: `surface.scene == canon_from` for 1,099 of 1,099 overrides across all four plans, zero exceptions — the field is a BYTE-EXACT HAND-COPY of a value the store already holds, and only 11 carry an `object` (a real carrier). AND WE COMPELLED IT: `continuity.rs:3974` emits no locator without a surface, their engine reads locators exclusively, so dropping one surface makes a climax fact VANISH from the game (737→736 locators) while `report-disclosure-coverage` still calls it `disclosed`. The consumer had to hand-copy `canon_from` 1,099 times or the facts would not exist for their game. By R620's own lesson that is OURS, not their carelessness — and a hand-maintained copy always drifts: theirs drifted twice, 7 facts, found by a person reading. TWO LIVE DEFECTS OF OURS, found independently by both reviewers and reproduced by me: (1) a `withhold` override carrying a surface EMITS A LOCATOR with `mode: "withhold"` — verified, 7 locators for `f-original-sin` at `scene_ordinal: 17` — because there is no mode filter at the emission site; their engine DELETED its withhold filter (commit 17c1340, "Withheld facts emit no locator"), a premise true only because nobody has yet surfaced a withheld fact, so the story's core truth would print onto the game screen through the map seam, past the leak gate. (2) `continuity.rs:3915` documents `locators` as "in walk order"; they are ordered by `canon_from` while each `scene_ordinal` is the SURFACE's — an injected divergence produced an inversion (53→17). Both are latent exactly until someone uses `surface` for the carrier design it exists for. THE DESIGN, licensed by measured fault and NOT built this round: derive the locator scene from `canon_from` when no surface is authored, gated on `mode != withhold`. That collapses 1,099 hand-copies to the 11 real carriers, makes all 7 historical drifts UNREPRESENTABLE rather than detectable (R622's derivation-over-detection), restores `surface` to the sparse override its own doc describes, and closes defect (1) in the same move. REFUTED AS BUILDS, each measured: `surface.object ∈ fact.entities` fires on 4 of 11 and its hits are the MOST correct uses (`ent-bunok`, the store's own designed leaker) — it would forbid the Dark-Souls model the field exists for; `surface == canon_from` as a GATE forbids the design; scene-is-a-section and object-is-registered are already enforced at the write path; off-road-surface is vacuous on this store (all 7 roads span all 66 scenes). The one residual check worth keeping is a REPORT, not a reject: a surface strictly BEFORE its fact's `canon_from` places reader access outside the interval where the claim holds — 0 of 1,099 on the real store, 6 of 7 injected, and honest about the miss (detection there is partly directional luck). It must not gate: `continuity.rs:2903` keeps continuity green while authoring, and a surface is authored before its scene exists. WHAT STAYS OPEN: their actual question. `state`-means-two-things is real (in `reader` the prose TELLS it; in `player` the audience DIGS it), option (b) is the shape of the answer, and no measured fault yet forces the vocabulary change — the judges' E-vs-I disagreement is an authoring decision, which they say themselves. The mechanical defects are licensed now; the vocabulary is not.
changes_bullets: THE RULING, and it is not a vocabulary answer: their `state` was CHOSEN, not forced, and the load-bearing defect is elsewhere. `surface` — the field that drives their game's every door — is documented as "STORED but NOT gated ... craft guidance" while being a MANDATORY hand-copy of `canon_from` (1,099/1,099 across all four plans, zero exceptions, 11 carrying a real `object`). Our API compels the copy: no surface, no locator (`continuity.rs:3974`), and their engine reads locators exclusively — so a missing surface deletes the fact from the game while `report-disclosure-coverage` still reports it `disclosed`.

MY THREE CLAIMS, ALL REFUTED BY EXPERIMENT, recorded because the refutation is the deliverable. (1) "FORCED": they use `withhold` ZERO times in all four plans (the default already withholds, so the arm never bound), and at the 5 TYPED player facts — where `first_at` was fully legal — they wrote the identical bare `state`. (2) "the disease is typed coverage": the leak gate's coverage of `player` is EXACTLY ZERO (`targeted=0`, since targeting is `withhold || first_at`), so typing all 852 facts moves it from 0 to 0; and `first_at` ("where the reader FIRST LEARNS it") is semantically inapplicable when the player's action decides when a fact is learned. (3) "a telling needs a render": three of five telling-consumers take one store with no re-extraction, the MCP contract calls playable-world render-free BY DESIGN, and they DID render it (`i18n.py:91-130`).

WHAT SURVIVES FROM MY RULING, and it is precise: the write predicate `mode == Withhold || has_first_at` (`atomic/lib.rs:5210-5212`) is the IDENTICAL boolean to the leak gate's targeting (`disclosure.rs:214`). The set of decisions the write path lets an untyped fact carry IS the set the gate skips. But the counterfactual refutes the causal reading — bypass the invariant, author an untyped `withhold`, and the gate STILL errors "un-gateable". The invariant is a messenger, not a cause; un-gateability is intrinsic to untypedness.

THEIR THREE OPTIONS, RULED: (a) a fifth `dug` mode — REFUTED, `imply` already IS that mode ("realised via an object/environment — the Dark-Souls item-text"), and the store's own numbers show the idiom split in half (21 `imply` uses across the reader plans with ZERO carriers; 11 carriers exclusively in `player`, which uses `imply` zero times). (c) "maybe it isn't a disclosure plan" — REFUTED on the premise, above. (b) a telling-level ACCESS/AGENCY flag — SURVIVES: mode (how plainly the telling commits) and access (must the audience act to get it?) are orthogonal axes the reader-centric vocabulary conflates. No measured fault forces it yet, so it is not licensed — recorded, not queued.

TWO LIVE DEFECTS, OURS, found independently by BOTH reviewers and reproduced by me: (1) a `withhold` override carrying a surface EMITS A LOCATOR with `mode: "withhold"` (verified: 7 locators for `f-original-sin`, `scene_ordinal: 17`) — no mode filter at the emission site. Their engine DELETED its withhold filter (17c1340, "Withheld facts emit no locator"), a premise true only because nobody has yet surfaced a withheld fact — so the story's core truth would print to the game screen through the map seam, past the leak gate. (2) `continuity.rs:3915` documents `locators` as "in walk order"; they are ordered by `canon_from` while each `scene_ordinal` is the surface's — an injected divergence inverted them (53→17). Both arm the moment `surface` is used for the carrier design it exists for.

THE DESIGN, licensed by measured fault, NOT BUILT THIS ROUND (code 0 by contract): derive the locator scene from `canon_from` when no surface is authored, gated on `mode != withhold`. It collapses 1,099 hand-copies to the 11 real carriers, makes all 7 of their historical drifts UNREPRESENTABLE rather than merely detectable (R622: derivation, not detection), restores `surface` to the sparse override its own doc describes, and closes defect (1) in the same move.

REFUTED AS BUILDS, each measured rather than argued: `surface.object ∈ fact.entities` fires on 4 of 11 and its hits are the MOST correct uses (`ent-bunok` is the store's own designed leaker) — it would forbid the Dark-Souls model the field exists for; `surface == canon_from` as a GATE forbids the design it is meant to protect; scene-is-a-section and object-is-registered are already write-path invariants; off-road-surface is vacuous here (all 7 roads span all 66 scenes). The single residual — a surface strictly BEFORE its fact's `canon_from`, placing reader access outside the interval where the claim holds — is a REPORT, not a reject (`continuity.rs:2903` keeps continuity green while authoring, and a surface is authored before its scene exists): 0/1,099 real, 6/7 injected, and honest that the detection is partly directional luck.
verification_bullets: TWO INDEPENDENT ADVERSARIAL REVIEWERS, BEFORE ANY BUILD (the R559/R613 pattern, and the owner's cost-no-object standard). One was pointed at my ruling and REFUTED all three claims by counterfactual; the other was told to find the strongest DECIDABLE check and measure it. They converged on the same live defect from opposite directions — the withhold-locator leak — which neither was asked about.

I RE-RAN THE TWO CLAIMS THAT REFRAME THE ROUND, because reviewers are not oracles and this arc has rejected their claims before. `surface.scene == canon_from`: 1,099 of 1,099 across all four plans, exactly 11 carrying an `object` — verified on their store at their own HEAD (`974a9dc`). `withhold` + surface: `set-disclosure` accepts it (exit 0) and `report-playable-world --telling player` emits 7 locators for `f-original-sin` carrying `mode: "withhold"`, `scene_ordinal: 17` — verified on a scratch copy.

THE FORCED-MOVE MECHANISM WAS REAL AND I MEASURED IT MYSELF before the reviewers refuted its attribution: on the untyped spine fact `f-victim-had-no-kin`, `--mode withhold` and `--first-at main=d05-saebyeok` are both REJECTED ("has no typed claim") while `state`/`hint`/`imply` are ACCEPTED. The mechanism survives; "therefore they were forced" does not.

REVIEWER SELF-CORRECTION IS RECORDED, not hidden: one corrected its own numbers twice (a first "53/99 off canon order" was its own bug — it ignored the order file's `branches` key; its first injection was invalid because the 7 historical defects live in the `reader` plan, not `player`). Both re-ran with the real `CanonOrder`. The other corrected my prompt's stale `surface.object = 0` to 11 — a number R639 had already measured and my prompt carried stale anyway.

THE COMPULSION IS PROVEN BY DELETION, not argued: dropping ONE surface makes climax fact `f-original-sin` vanish from the game (737 → 736 locators) while `report-disclosure-coverage` still calls it `disclosed`. That is why 1,099 hand-copies exist, and it is our API's doing.
CONSUMER REPO STRICTLY READ-ONLY throughout, by me and by both reviewers: `git status` clean, HEAD unchanged at `974a9dc`, store sha unchanged. Every injection ran on scratch copies.
CODE 0. Nothing built, nothing installed. `validate-workspace` exit 0, ledger 389. Citation hygiene: every round cited verified present via `query --changelog-entry` (R638's verb).
carry_forward_bullets: NEXT = BUILD the derived locator scene (this round's design, licensed by measured fault): emit the locator from `canon_from` when no surface is authored, gated on `mode != withhold`. Two independent measurements license it — 1,099 hand-copies we compelled, and a live withhold-leak through the map seam. Independent adversarial review before it lands: my design has been refuted seven times running this arc, including this round.

THE CONSUMER IS OWED A REPLY and it is an OUTWARD gate (owner's call, do not cross alone): their question is ANSWERED but not as asked — two of their three options are refuted (a: `imply` already is that mode; c: a telling needs no render, and they rendered it anyway), (b) survives unlicensed, and the load-bearing finding is that we compelled 1,099 hand-copies of `canon_from` and shipped them a latent leak. Their diagnosis was better than mine; say so.

WHAT STAYS OPEN, named not silent: `state` really does mean two things (in `reader` the prose TELLS it; in `player` the audience DIGS it), and no measured fault forces the vocabulary change — the judges' E-vs-I disagreement is an authoring decision, as they say themselves. The access/agency axis (option b) is recorded, NOT queued. Revisit when a fault measures it.

It arms the moment a real carrier diverges from its fact's seat. Fix it with the derive build or forward-correct the doc.
