MY THREE CLAIMS, ALL REFUTED BY EXPERIMENT, recorded because the refutation is the deliverable. (1) "FORCED": they use `withhold` ZERO times in all four plans (the default already withholds, so the arm never bound), and at the 5 TYPED player facts — where `first_at` was fully legal — they wrote the identical bare `state`. (2) "the disease is typed coverage": the leak gate's coverage of `player` is EXACTLY ZERO (`targeted=0`, since targeting is `withhold || first_at`), so typing all 852 facts moves it from 0 to 0; and `first_at` ("where the reader FIRST LEARNS it") is semantically inapplicable when the player's action decides when a fact is learned. (3) "a telling needs a render": three of five telling-consumers take one store with no re-extraction, the MCP contract calls playable-world render-free BY DESIGN, and they DID render it (`i18n.py:91-130`).

WHAT SURVIVES FROM MY RULING, and it is precise: the write predicate `mode == Withhold || has_first_at` (`atomic/lib.rs:5210-5212`) is the IDENTICAL boolean to the leak gate's targeting (`disclosure.rs:214`). The set of decisions the write path lets an untyped fact carry IS the set the gate skips. But the counterfactual refutes the causal reading — bypass the invariant, author an untyped `withhold`, and the gate STILL errors "un-gateable". The invariant is a messenger, not a cause; un-gateability is intrinsic to untypedness.

THEIR THREE OPTIONS, RULED: (a) a fifth `dug` mode — REFUTED, `imply` already IS that mode ("realised via an object/environment — the Dark-Souls item-text"), and the store's own numbers show the idiom split in half (21 `imply` uses across the reader plans with ZERO carriers; 11 carriers exclusively in `player`, which uses `imply` zero times). (c) "maybe it isn't a disclosure plan" — REFUTED on the premise, above. (b) a telling-level ACCESS/AGENCY flag — SURVIVES: mode (how plainly the telling commits) and access (must the audience act to get it?) are orthogonal axes the reader-centric vocabulary conflates. No measured fault forces it yet, so it is not licensed — recorded, not queued.

TWO LIVE DEFECTS, OURS, found independently by BOTH reviewers and reproduced by me: (1) a `withhold` override carrying a surface EMITS A LOCATOR with `mode: "withhold"` (verified: 7 locators for `f-original-sin`, `scene_ordinal: 17`) — no mode filter at the emission site. Their engine DELETED its withhold filter (17c1340, "Withheld facts emit no locator"), a premise true only because nobody has yet surfaced a withheld fact — so the story's core truth would print to the game screen through the map seam, past the leak gate. (2) `continuity.rs:3915` documents `locators` as "in walk order"; they are ordered by `canon_from` while each `scene_ordinal` is the surface's — an injected divergence inverted them (53→17). Both arm the moment `surface` is used for the carrier design it exists for.

THE DESIGN, licensed by measured fault, NOT BUILT THIS ROUND (code 0 by contract): derive the locator scene from `canon_from` when no surface is authored, gated on `mode != withhold`. It collapses 1,099 hand-copies to the 11 real carriers, makes all 7 of their historical drifts UNREPRESENTABLE rather than merely detectable (R622: derivation, not detection), restores `surface` to the sparse override its own doc describes, and closes defect (1) in the same move.

REFUTED AS BUILDS, each measured rather than argued: `surface.object ∈ fact.entities` fires on 4 of 11 and its hits are the MOST correct uses (`ent-bunok` is the store's own designed leaker) — it would forbid the Dark-Souls model the field exists for; `surface == canon_from` as a GATE forbids the design it is meant to protect; scene-is-a-section and object-is-registered are already write-path invariants; off-road-surface is vacuous here (all 7 roads span all 66 scenes). The single residual — a surface strictly BEFORE its fact's `canon_from`, placing reader access outside the interval where the claim holds — is a REPORT, not a reject (`continuity.rs:2903` keeps continuity green while authoring, and a surface is authored before its scene exists): 0/1,099 real, 6/7 injected, and honest that the detection is partly directional luck.
publishable_verification_bullets: TWO INDEPENDENT ADVERSARIAL REVIEWERS, BEFORE ANY BUILD (the R559/R613 pattern, and the owner's cost-no-object standard). One was pointed at my ruling and REFUTED all three claims by counterfactual; the other was told to find the strongest DECIDABLE check and measure it. They converged on the same live defect from opposite directions — the withhold-locator leak — which neither was asked about.

I RE-RAN THE TWO CLAIMS THAT REFRAME THE ROUND, because reviewers are not oracles and this arc has rejected their claims before. `surface.scene == canon_from`: 1,099 of 1,099 across all four plans, exactly 11 carrying an `object` — verified on their store at their own HEAD (`974a9dc`). `withhold` + surface: `set-disclosure` accepts it (exit 0) and `report-playable-world --telling player` emits 7 locators for `f-original-sin` carrying `mode: "withhold"`, `scene_ordinal: 17` — verified on a scratch copy.

THE FORCED-MOVE MECHANISM WAS REAL AND I MEASURED IT MYSELF before the reviewers refuted its attribution: on the untyped spine fact `f-victim-had-no-kin`, `--mode withhold` and `--first-at main=d05-saebyeok` are both REJECTED ("has no typed claim") while `state`/`hint`/`imply` are ACCEPTED. The mechanism survives; "therefore they were forced" does not.

REVIEWER SELF-CORRECTION IS RECORDED, not hidden: one corrected its own numbers twice (a first "53/99 off canon order" was its own bug — it ignored the order file's `branches` key; its first injection was invalid because the 7 historical defects live in the `reader` plan, not `player`). Both re-ran with the real `CanonOrder`. The other corrected my prompt's stale `surface.object = 0` to 11 — a number R639 had already measured and my prompt carried stale anyway.

THE COMPULSION IS PROVEN BY DELETION, not argued: dropping ONE surface makes climax fact `f-original-sin` vanish from the game (737 → 736 locators) while `report-disclosure-coverage` still calls it `disclosed`. That is why 1,099 hand-copies exist, and it is our API's doing.
CONSUMER REPO STRICTLY READ-ONLY throughout, by me and by both reviewers: `git status` clean, HEAD unchanged at `974a9dc`, store sha unchanged. Every injection ran on scratch copies.
CODE 0. Nothing built, nothing installed. `validate-workspace` exit 0, ledger 389. Citation hygiene: every round cited verified present via `query --changelog-entry` (R638's verb).
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: NEXT = BUILD the derived locator scene (this round's design, licensed by measured fault): emit the locator from `canon_from` when no surface is authored, gated on `mode != withhold`. Two independent measurements license it — 1,099 hand-copies we compelled, and a live withhold-leak through the map seam. Independent adversarial review before it lands: my design has been refuted seven times running this arc, including this round.

THE CONSUMER IS OWED A REPLY and it is an OUTWARD gate (owner's call, do not cross alone): their question is ANSWERED but not as asked — two of their three options are refuted (a: `imply` already is that mode; c: a telling needs no render, and they rendered it anyway), (b) survives unlicensed, and the load-bearing finding is that we compelled 1,099 hand-copies of `canon_from` and shipped them a latent leak. Their diagnosis was better than mine; say so.

WHAT STAYS OPEN, named not silent: `state` really does mean two things (in `reader` the prose TELLS it; in `player` the audience DIGS it), and no measured fault forces the vocabulary change — the judges' E-vs-I disagreement is an authoring decision, as they say themselves. The access/agency axis (option b) is recorded, NOT queued. Revisit when a fault measures it.

ALSO OPEN (this round's second defect, not fixed): `continuity.rs:3915` documents `locators` as "in walk order" while they are ordered by `canon_from` and each `scene_ordinal` is the surface's — a prose claim its code does not keep, the R636 class. It arms the moment a real carrier diverges from its fact's seat. Fix it with the derive build or forward-correct the doc.

REGISTERED DEBT (SSOT = `Round 632`): DEBT-C (type the reject path), DEBT-F (overturned, held open), DEBT-G (the fact leg — UNLICENSED per R639), DEBT-PROSE. CLOSED: DEBT-A+B (R630+R634), DEBT-D (R640), DEBT-E (R638), DEBT-H (R635).

THE R637 CATALOGUE'S C1 (quest-prerequisite acyclicity) is DEMOTED below this arc, not dropped: R493's measured-fault clause is UNMET there (zero cycles in the wild) while everything in this round is measured. Argue its licensing or refute and close it when the surface work lands.

Round 643 — the map seat derives, and a.decision_summary: R643 BUILDS the design R642 ruled, and it is the smallest change in this arc with the largest reach: the map seat DERIVES from the fact's own `canon_from` when no surface is authored, and a WITHHELD fact never reaches the map at all. Two arms, one emission site (`continuity.rs`), and between them they make an entire defect class unrepresentable rather than merely detectable (R622: derivation, not detection). THE FIRST ARM PAYS A DEBT WE CHARGED A CONSUMER. `surface` is documented as a SPARSE override naming a diegetic carrier, but the emission required it — no surface, no locator — and the first playable consumer's engine reads locators exclusively, so a fact without a surface DID NOT EXIST for their runtime. They therefore hand-copied `canon_from` into `surface.scene` 1,099 times out of 1,099 across four tellings, a byte-exact copy of a value this store already holds, keeping only 11 real carriers among them. It drifted twice (7 facts, found by a person reading). By R620's own lesson that is OURS, not their carelessness: we made a hand-maintained copy the price of existing. PROVEN END TO END on their real store, both directions: delete one surface and the climax fact `f-original-sin` VANISHES from the game under the old binary (0 locators) while `report-disclosure-coverage` still calls it `disclosed`; under the new one it survives at the derived seat `d05-saebyeok`, ordinal 17 — which is byte-identical to what the hand-copy said. So all 1,099 copies are now deletable and nothing moves. THE SECOND ARM CLOSES A LEAK THAT WOULD HAVE PRINTED THE STORY'S CORE TRUTH ONTO THE GAME SCREEN. A `withhold` override carrying a surface emitted a locator stamped `mode: "withhold"` — verified before the fix: 7 locators for `f-original-sin` at ordinal 17 — because the emission had no mode check. The consumer's engine had DELETED its own withhold filter (their commit 17c1340, "Withheld facts emit no locator"), a premise true only because nobody had yet surfaced a withheld fact; the leak gate cannot see this path at all, since it targets `withhold || first_at` and reads re-extracted prose, not the map seam. The mode is the authority on whether the audience gets the fact; the surface only says where. That premise is now true by construction instead of by luck. NON-REGRESSIVE, MEASURED NOT ASSERTED: on their untouched store the new binary emits exactly what the old one did — player 246 → 246, reader 4295 → 4295, zero locators with a missing ordinal, zero stamped withhold — and their own `knots.py` gate still reports PASS on the new binary, `validate-continuity` still exits 0. The change is invisible to them today and unlocks the deletion tomorrow. BOTH ARMS ARE LOAD-BEARING BY NEUTRALIZATION, each killing exactly its own pin and no other: neutralize the withhold filter → `playable_world_keeps_a_withheld_fact_off_the_map` fails; neutralize the derived seat → `playable_world_derives_the_seat_when_no_surface_is_authored` fails; the override control (`..._lets_an_authored_surface_override_the_derived_seat`) survives both, which is what proves the derive arm did not swallow the case the field exists for — a carrier sitting somewhere other than its fact's seat. TWO PROSE CLAIMS CORRECTED FORWARD IN THE SAME CHANGE, both R636-class (a doc asserting what its code does not keep): `MapLocator.scene` said "the authored `surface.scene`" and now states the derive rule and that a `None` ordinal is therefore always an authored surface pointing off this world's road; and `PlayableWorld.locators` said "in walk order" FLAT, which was false exactly when it mattered — the emission walks `canon_from` while a locator's `scene_ordinal` is the SURFACE's, so a carrier away from its seat lands out of order (an injected one inverted 53 → 17). It now says what it is and names the sort for anyone needing encounter order. WHAT THIS DOES NOT DO, named rather than implied: it does not answer the consumer's vocabulary question — `state` still means "the prose tells it" in a reader telling and "the audience digs it" in a player telling, and R642 ruled that unlicensed (no measured fault forces it; the access/agency axis stays recorded). It does not gate anything: a surface before its fact's `canon_from` remains a report-shaped residual, because `continuity.rs:2903` keeps continuity green while authoring and a surface is authored before its scene exists.
changes_bullets: DERIVED SEAT (`continuity.rs`, the `playable_world` emission): when a disclosure carries no `surface`, the locator's seat is now the fact's own `canon_from`. `surface` returns to being the SPARSE override its doc always described — an authored carrier sitting somewhere other than the fact's seat — instead of the mandatory hand-copy the emission had made it. A derived seat is on the world's walk BY CONSTRUCTION (it is the scene the fact begins at), so a `None` `scene_ordinal` is now always an authored surface pointing off-road, which the doc now says.

WITHHOLD NEVER REACHES THE MAP: the emission had no mode check, so a `withhold` override carrying a surface emitted a locator stamped `mode: "withhold"` — 7 of them for `f-original-sin` at ordinal 17, verified before the fix. The consumer's engine reads locators as the game's doors and had DELETED its own withhold filter (their 17c1340, "Withheld facts emit no locator") on a premise that was true only until someone surfaced a withheld fact. The leak gate cannot cover this path — it targets `withhold || first_at` and matches re-extracted prose, not the map seam. Now the premise is true by construction.

THREE PINS, and the third is the control: `playable_world_derives_the_seat_when_no_surface_is_authored` (seat = `canon_from`, ordinal `Some(0)`, no object), `playable_world_keeps_a_withheld_fact_off_the_map` (both with and without a surface — a loop, so neither shape can regress alone), and `playable_world_lets_an_authored_surface_override_the_derived_seat` (an `imply` carrier at another scene still wins, with its object) — the last exists so the derive arm cannot quietly swallow the Dark-Souls case the field was built for.

TWO R636-CLASS PROSE CLAIMS CORRECTED FORWARD, both found by R642's reviewers: `MapLocator.scene` claimed to be "the authored `surface.scene`" (now states the derive rule and what a `None` ordinal implies), and `PlayableWorld.locators` claimed "in walk order" flat — FALSE precisely when it matters, since the emission walks `canon_from` while `scene_ordinal` is the surface's, so a carrier away from its seat lands out of order (an injected one inverted 53 → 17). It now says what it is and names the sort for encounter order.

NOT DONE, and named: the vocabulary question the consumer actually asked (`state` means "told" in a reader telling and "diggable" in a player telling) stays OPEN and UNLICENSED per R642 — no measured fault forces it, and the access/agency axis is recorded, not queued. The residual seat check (a surface strictly BEFORE its fact's `canon_from`, 0/1,099 on the real store and 6/7 injected) stays a REPORT-shaped idea, not a gate: `continuity.rs:2903` keeps continuity green while authoring, and a surface is authored before its scene exists.
verification_bullets: THE POINT, PROVEN BOTH DIRECTIONS ON THEIR REAL STORE (scratch copy; their repo never touched, `git status` clean, HEAD `974a9dc`): delete `f-original-sin`'s surface and under the OLD binary it emits 0 locators — the climax fact VANISHES from the game — while `report-disclosure-coverage` still calls it `disclosed`. Under the NEW binary it survives: 7 locators, seat `d05-saebyeok`, ordinal 17 — BYTE-IDENTICAL to what the hand-copy said. That is the whole thesis: the 1,099 copies are now deletable and nothing moves.

THE LEAK, PROVEN CLOSED: `set-disclosure --telling player --fact f-original-sin --mode withhold --surface d05-saebyeok` is accepted (exit 0, as before — this is a render property, not a store invariant), and `report-playable-world` emitted 7 locators stamped `mode: "withhold"` before the fix and 0 after.

NON-REGRESSIVE, MEASURED AGAINST THE OLD BINARY RATHER THAN ASSERTED: on their untouched store, player 246 → 246 and reader 4295 → 4295 locators, `no_ordinal=0`, `mode=withhold=0`. Their own `knots.py --check` still reports `PASS (하드 0 · 경고 0)` on the new binary and `validate-continuity` still exits 0. Invisible to them today; the unlock is theirs to take.

BOTH ARMS LOAD-BEARING BY NEUTRALIZATION, with no cross-contamination — each kills exactly its own pin: neutralize the withhold filter → only `..._keeps_a_withheld_fact_off_the_map` fails; neutralize the derived seat (restore "no surface, no locator") → only `..._derives_the_seat_when_no_surface_is_authored` fails; the override control passes under both, which is what makes it a control.

GREEN: 970 workspace tests / 0 failed (was 967 — the three new pins), `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` 0, `cargo fmt --all`, `validate-workspace` exit 0. Citation hygiene: every round cited verified present via `query --changelog-entry`.

SCOPE HONESTY: the 1,099 copies are not deleted by this round — they are the consumer's data, in the consumer's repo, and deleting them is theirs. What changed is that keeping them is now a choice rather than the price of existing.
carry_forward_bullets: THE CONSUMER IS OWED A REPLY and it is an OUTWARD gate (owner's call, do not cross alone). It carries: their diagnosis beat mine (R642, said plainly); the `surface` hand-copy was OUR compulsion, not their carelessness — 1,099/1,099, and they can now DELETE every surface whose scene equals its fact's `canon_from`, keeping only their 11 real carriers, with zero change to their game (measured); we shipped them a latent leak (a withheld fact reached the map seam, and their engine had deleted its filter on our contract's shadow) and it is closed; their vocabulary question is real, ruled UNLICENSED, and the access/agency axis is recorded. They must `cargo install --force` to get any of it.

THE VOCABULARY QUESTION STAYS OPEN AND UNLICENSED (R642's ruling, unchanged): `state` means "the prose tells it" in a reader telling and "the audience digs it" in a player telling; option (b) — a telling-level access/agency flag, orthogonal to mode — is the shape of the answer; no measured fault forces it, and the judges' E-vs-I disagreement is an authoring decision, as the consumer says themselves. Revisit when a fault measures it, not before.

THE RESIDUAL SEAT CHECK is designed and deliberately unbuilt: a surface strictly BEFORE its fact's `canon_from` places audience access outside the interval where the claim holds — decidable, semantics-free, 0/1,099 on the real store, 6/7 on injection (and honest that the detection is partly directional luck). It must be a REPORT, not a reject (`continuity.rs:2903`). Build it when a report has somewhere to be read from — `report-authoring-frontier` is the natural home and reads no surface today.

REGISTERED DEBT (SSOT = `Round 632`): DEBT-C (type the reject path), DEBT-F (overturned, held open), DEBT-G (the fact leg — UNLICENSED per R639), DEBT-PROSE. CLOSED: DEBT-A+B (R630+R634), DEBT-D (R640), DEBT-E (R638), DEBT-H (R635).

R637's C1 (quest-prerequisite acyclicity) stays DEMOTED, not dropped: R493's measured-fault clause is UNMET there (zero cycles in the wild) while every item in this arc is measured. Argue its licensing or refute and close it — catalogued-but-unruled is how a carry becomes a carry-forever (R628).

Round 644 — from_tag serializes but woul.decision_summary: R644 pays the code leg of DEBT-PROSE: the from_tag/as_str pair on a manually-labelled enum is TWO hand-written mirrors of serde's own tag list, and only ONE of the two is compiler-forced. `as_str` is an exhaustive `match self`, so a new variant fails the build until its arm is added; `from_tag` is a `&str` match ending `_ => None`, so a new variant SERIALIZES through serde but silently fails to PARSE BACK — nothing forces the arm. That is precisely the class Round 629 convicted for `describe-schema`'s vocabularies, one step downstream: there the missing enumeration silently dropped a variant from the published contract; here the missing from_tag arm silently makes a round-trip lossy. Six enums carry the pair (`ConfirmerKind`, `ConfirmMethod`, `Verdict` in mnemosyne-atomic; `PredicateObjectKind`, `PayoffExpectation`, `DisclosureMode` in mnemosyne-core), all six `#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]` with both `Serialize` and `Deserialize`, so serde is ALREADY the two-way authority and the two match arms are the third and fourth mirrors of it. Measured first, per R493: all six pairs are SYMMETRIC today (every emitted tag parses back), so there is no live defect — this is a TRIPWIRE against a future variant, the R629/R634 shape, not a repair of a broken round-trip. The gap it closes is a real absence: Round 629 already pinned `as_str <-> serde` for THREE of the six (DisclosureMode, PayoffExpectation, PredicateObjectKind, via `as_str_matches_serde` in the describe-schema contract test), but only in the as_str direction and only for the three that reach `describe-schema`; the from_tag direction was pinned for NONE, and the three atomic enums (ConfirmerKind, ConfirmMethod, Verdict) were pinned in NEITHER direction because they are not part of the published schema contract. The fix is DERIVATION, not a second copy (R622): the new pin binds both directions of all six pairs to the ONE variant oracle Round 629 already built, `serde_variants::<T>()` — the serde-derived list captured through the `Deserializer::deserialize_enum(name, variants, visitor)` trait signature, promoted to `pub(crate)` so the pin reaches it instead of standing up a second oracle. For every published tag it asserts `from_tag(tag)` parses AND `as_str(that) == tag`, so a new variant added without its from_tag arm fails here, and a from_tag/as_str that disagrees with serde's spelling fails here — the vocabulary is never re-typed in the test, only round-tripped. Both directions proven load-bearing by injection: adding a `Verdict::Inconclusive` variant with its as_str arm (compiler-forced) but WITHOUT its from_tag arm fails "from_tag rejects the published serde tag `inconclusive`"; misspelling `PredicateObjectKind::Scalar`'s label to `"Scalar"` fails "as_str disagrees with the serde tag `scalar`". The pin is non-vacuous by the same oracle's own emptiness guard (an empty variant list would make it pass over nothing) plus an explicit `!tags.is_empty()` assert. This does NOT remove the hand mirrors — `from_tag` has 31 live call sites and `as_str` its own, so both are real API, not dead code; collapsing them onto `serde_plain`/`variant_from_tag` (the oracle already deserializes a tag to its variant) is a larger change with a caller migration and belongs to its own round. What R644 removes is the SILENCE: the mirrors may stay, but they can no longer drift from serde or from each other without a red test.

Binds BOTH directions (the missing one is from_tag) of all six pairs (`ConfirmerKind`/`ConfirmMethod`/`Verdict` + `PredicateObjectKind`/`PayoffExpectation`/`DisclosureMode`).

Round 645 — DEBT-C ruled: typing the rej.decision_summary: R645 rules DEBT-C at code 0, and the ruling is that DEBT-C's own scoping — Round 629's "type the reject path, then invariants() DERIVES like the other five vocabularies" — is CATEGORY-CONFUSED. Two independent adversarial reviewers refuted my candidate typed-enum design before any build, converging on the same measured verdict from different angles, and I re-ran every load-bearing number. THE PARTITION KILLS THE DERIVE PREMISE. describe-schema publishes 13 named invariants; the write path carries ~150 distinct reject messages through AtomicMutateError::Validation (measured: 141 direct Validation construction sites + 72 Result<_,String> helper rejects funnelled in at the primitive boundary via .map_err(Validation)). The mapping is many-to-one AND two-thirds unmapped: ~30-40 narrative-fact ref-integrity messages fold into the 13 (evidence-provenance alone renders 7 distinct messages; registered-entities 3; pays-off-exists 4), while ~110 messages have NO invariant home at all (the ~10 --reason audit gates, ~13 section field-caps + Round-298 gates + mandatory fields, 8 binding-file path-normalization rules, entry_id/inventory/confirmation/normative_excerpt conformance, and the manifest wrappers that are recursive pass-throughs of an inner error). So the two horns are: a 13-variant enum whose Display cannot reproduce 150 messages (each variant becomes a sub-discriminated field-bag) and leaves ~110 sites on a catch-all String — reintroducing the stringly-typed reject for two-thirds of the surface; or a ~150-variant enum whose serde_variants yields 150 implementation-detail tags that are NOT the 13 curated kebab names, ballooning the contract from 13 teaching guarantees to 150 path-normalization rules and destroying its purpose. Neither is the five-vocabulary shape. THE CATEGORY ERROR, which is the real finding: for disclosure_mode/payoff_expectation/predicate_object_kind/rule_class/quest-predicates, THE ENUM IS THE DOMAIN — a field of that type can hold nothing but a variant, so serde_variants::<T>() is authoritative by construction, the type's inhabitants ARE the value set. There is NO type whose inhabitants are the invariants; the set being enumerated is "the conditions the write path chooses to reject," which lives in imperative control flow (the if cond { return Err } statements), not in any data type. An InvariantViolation enum is a labelling scheme laid over that control flow by hand — its variants are names an author HOPES correspond 1:1 to reject sites, and no compiler law forces either (i) every return Err to carry a distinct variant, or (ii) a variant's prose to match the condition it is attached to. Proven: a reviewer injected a genuinely new write-time reject (a SENTINEL check in build_candidate_fact) — it compiled clean, describe-schema stayed at 13 invariants, and the whole suite stayed green; but under the typed design the same reject could reuse any existing variant and escape identically, because nothing ties a variant's meaning to the if it tests. Typing the reject path would RELOCATE R629's "second mirror the compiler claims to force but doesn't" one level up, at ~150-site cost, while looking MORE authoritative because it is typed. Round 629 fixed the EASY case where the type was the domain; DEBT-C tried to generalize it to a case where the analogy breaks. THE GAP IS REAL BUT NOT R493-LICENSED FOR A BUILD. The reviewer's injection is redundant with the current state: the store already enforces ~150 rejects while publishing 13, and ~110 reject classes are already unpublished with the repo green — that is not a regression to catch, it is the standing shape of a curated contract. invariants() has drifted ZERO times: two deliberate edits in its life (R587 birth, R627 fix), never an accidental omission. The harm is bounded — every unlisted reject is FAIL-LOUD with an actionable message (surprise, not the silent wrong-vocabulary corruption R629 fixed) — and NO production code branches on the reject KIND: ops/lib.rs:424 binds Validation(msg) and wraps it verbatim, atomic_cli.rs:88 maps Validation(_) to "validation" and displays, and a grep for any .contains()/== on a Validation message is empty. So typing has zero measured consumer, and the classification motive (make it derive like the five) is category-confused. Both motives fail. THE RULING: DEBT-C as scoped is REJECTED and RE-SCOPED toward closure, not build. invariants() being hand-maintained is CORRECT for its kind — a curated documentation contract, categorically unlike the five closed-enum vocabularies — and Round 629's "then derive the invariant list" was the mis-generalization. IF drift-catching is later wanted, the honest artifact is NOT a typed enum and NOT sold as derivation: it is a small fail-loud pin giving the ~13 reference-integrity invariants a canonical id and asserting each reaches a live enforcement path (the continuity gate already re-checks these same refs out-of-band per invariant_enforcement, so that mapping has a second home) — ~13 lines, inherently hand-maintained, honest about being a behavioural guard over a curated summary rather than a derivation from a type. That pin is itself unlicensed today (zero measured drift) and is recorded, not built. The R305 field-invariant-parity discipline (two write paths on one field must enforce the same invariant set) is untouched and remains the real structural guard on the reject path; DEBT-C was never about that.
changes_bullets: DEBT-C RULED AT CODE 0 (no code changed): its scoping — Round 629's "type the reject path, then invariants() DERIVES like the other five vocabularies" — is category-confused and REJECTED as specified. Two independent adversarial reviewers refuted the typed-enum design before build; I re-ran every load-bearing number.

THE MEASURED PARTITION that sinks the derive premise: 13 published invariants vs ~150 distinct reject messages (141 direct Validation sites + 72 Result<_,String> helper rejects funnelled in). Many-to-one (evidence-provenance renders 7 messages; registered-entities 3; pays-off-exists 4) AND two-thirds unmapped (~110 messages — --reason gates, section caps, binding-path rules, conformance — have no invariant home). A 13-variant enum can't render 150 messages and leaves ~110 on a String catch-all; a 150-variant enum publishes implementation detail as contract. Neither is the five-vocabulary shape.

THE CATEGORY ERROR (the real finding): the five vocabularies are enums that ARE their domain, so serde_variants::<T>() is authoritative by construction. There is NO type whose inhabitants are the invariants — the enumerated set is "what the write path chooses to reject," which lives in imperative control flow, not a data type. A reviewer's injection (a new SENTINEL reject in build_candidate_fact) compiled clean, left describe-schema at 13, kept the suite green — and the typed design would let the same reject reuse a variant and escape identically, because nothing ties a variant's meaning to the condition it tests. Round 645 — DEBT-C ruled: typing the rej.publishable_decision_summary: R645 rules DEBT-C at code 0, and the ruling is that DEBT-C's own scoping — Round 629's "type the reject path, then invariants() DERIVES like the other five vocabularies" — is CATEGORY-CONFUSED. Two independent adversarial reviewers refuted my candidate typed-enum design before any build, converging on the same measured verdict from different angles, and I re-ran every load-bearing number. THE PARTITION KILLS THE DERIVE PREMISE. describe-schema publishes 13 named invariants; the write path carries ~150 distinct reject messages through AtomicMutateError::Validation (measured: 141 direct Validation construction sites + 72 Result<_,String> helper rejects funnelled in at the primitive boundary via .map_err(Validation)). The mapping is many-to-one AND two-thirds unmapped: ~30-40 narrative-fact ref-integrity messages fold into the 13 (evidence-provenance alone renders 7 distinct messages; registered-entities 3; pays-off-exists 4), while ~110 messages have NO invariant home at all (the ~10 --reason audit gates, ~13 section field-caps + Round-298 gates + mandatory fields, 8 binding-file path-normalization rules, entry_id/inventory/confirmation/normative_excerpt conformance, and the manifest wrappers that are recursive pass-throughs of an inner error). So the two horns are: a 13-variant enum whose Display cannot reproduce 150 messages (each variant becomes a sub-discriminated field-bag) and leaves ~110 sites on a catch-all String — reintroducing the stringly-typed reject for two-thirds of the surface; or a ~150-variant enum whose serde_variants yields 150 implementation-detail tags that are NOT the 13 curated kebab names, ballooning the contract from 13 teaching guarantees to 150 path-normalization rules and destroying its purpose. Neither is the five-vocabulary shape. THE CATEGORY ERROR, which is the real finding: for disclosure_mode/payoff_expectation/predicate_object_kind/rule_class/quest-predicates, THE ENUM IS THE DOMAIN — a field of that type can hold nothing but a variant, so serde_variants::<T>() is authoritative by construction, the type's inhabitants ARE the value set. There is NO type whose inhabitants are the invariants; the set being enumerated is "the conditions the write path chooses to reject," which lives in imperative control flow (the if cond { return Err } statements), not in any data type. An InvariantViolation enum is a labelling scheme laid over that control flow by hand — its variants are names an author HOPES correspond 1:1 to reject sites, and no compiler law forces either (i) every return Err to carry a distinct variant, or (ii) a variant's prose to match the condition it is attached to. Proven: a reviewer injected a genuinely new write-time reject (a SENTINEL check in build_candidate_fact) — it compiled clean, describe-schema stayed at 13 invariants, and the whole suite stayed green; but under the typed design the same reject could reuse any existing variant and escape identically, because nothing ties a variant's meaning to the if it tests. Typing the reject path would RELOCATE R629's "second mirror the compiler claims to force but doesn't" one level up, at ~150-site cost, while looking MORE authoritative because it is typed. Round 629 fixed the EASY case where the type was the domain; DEBT-C tried to generalize it to a case where the analogy breaks. THE GAP IS REAL BUT NOT R493-LICENSED FOR A BUILD. The reviewer's injection is redundant with the current state: the store already enforces ~150 rejects while publishing 13, and ~110 reject classes are already unpublished with the repo green — that is not a regression to catch, it is the standing shape of a curated contract. invariants() has drifted ZERO times: two deliberate edits in its life (R587 birth, R627 fix), never an accidental omission. The harm is bounded — every unlisted reject is FAIL-LOUD with an actionable message (surprise, not the silent wrong-vocabulary corruption R629 fixed) — and NO production code branches on the reject KIND: ops/lib.rs:424 binds Validation(msg) and wraps it verbatim, atomic_cli.rs:88 maps Validation(_) to "validation" and displays, and a grep for any .contains()/== on a Validation message is empty. So typing has zero measured consumer, and the classification motive (make it derive like the five) is category-confused. Both motives fail. THE RULING: DEBT-C as scoped is REJECTED and RE-SCOPED toward closure, not build. invariants() being hand-maintained is CORRECT for its kind — a curated documentation contract, categorically unlike the five closed-enum vocabularies — and Round 629's "then derive the invariant list" was the mis-generalization. IF drift-catching is later wanted, the honest artifact is NOT a typed enum and NOT sold as derivation: it is a small fail-loud pin giving the ~13 reference-integrity invariants a canonical id and asserting each reaches a live enforcement path (the continuity gate already re-checks these same refs out-of-band per invariant_enforcement, so that mapping has a second home) — ~13 lines, inherently hand-maintained, honest about being a behavioural guard over a curated summary rather than a derivation from a type. That pin is itself unlicensed today (zero measured drift) and is recorded, not built. The R305 field-invariant-parity discipline (two write paths on one field must enforce the same invariant set) is untouched and remains the real structural guard on the reject path; DEBT-C was never about that.

Round 646 — C1 refuted: a requires-cycle.decision_summary: R646 rules R637's catalogue item C1 (a quest-prerequisite acyclicity check) at code 0: REFUTE-AND-CLOSE as a validate-continuity gate. An independent adversarial reviewer refuted my candidate BUILD ruling before any build — the eighth first-design refutation of this arc — and the refutation is stronger than my case, because it convicts R637's own framing, which I had inherited. THE CATEGORY ARGUMENT R637 USED IS WRONG FOR requires, and that is the load-bearing finding. R637 said "a cycle is a contradiction (never legitimate, not even mid-draft)" and placed C1 in validate-continuity by the same line that puts SuccessionCycle there. But that intuition is succession's, borrowed without re-deriving it from requires' semantics. A SUCCESSION cycle is truth-corruption: supersedes_in_frame means "the older fact no longer holds", so A supersedes B and B supersedes A makes both facts simultaneously superseded — they sit in the store live-and-latest while silently never holding anywhere (continuity.rs:1244). There is no coherent authoring waypoint where that is valid; it is a genuine contradiction, and continuity gates it. A REQUIRES cycle is well-defined unsatisfiability, verified in source: requires is surfaced ONLY as `prerequisites` metadata on the quest node (continuity.rs:4446), and it does NOT feed quest-state computation — per-world state derives from giving-fact payoff coverage (:4416), and unresolved_quests fires only when a quest has no giving setup at all (:4374). So a requires-cycle where each quest has a discharger produces a completely uncorrupted result: the quests get their normal states and the cycle sits as declared-but-unsatisfiable prerequisite metadata. That is exactly why R637's own injection measured validate-continuity violations 0 AND why my minimal 2-cycle reproduction (q-a requires q-b, q-b requires q-a) exits 0 — nothing is silently false, the store's meaning is simply "these quests are mutually blocked / never openable", which is unsatisfiability, the same genus as a dangling prerequisite that continuity DELIBERATELY tolerates ("a WIP story has dangling setups by definition", :2903). By continuity's OWN stated line — GATES CONTRADICTIONS and REPORTS INCOMPLETENESS — a requires-cycle falls on the REPORT side. R637 put it on the gate side by misclassifying it, and its ruling is internally contradictory: R637 says the play-readiness class (C2 obligation-with-no-discharger, C4 unsatisfiable-precondition) cannot be folded into validate-continuity without breaking the WIP-green contract, yet says C1 belongs in validate-continuity today — the ONLY distinguisher is "cycle = contradiction", and removing it relocates C1 next to C2/C4 in the unbuilt play-readiness class. A requires-cycle ("these quests can't open") is a strict sibling of C2 ("this quest can't be discharged"); gating one while parking the other outside is incoherent. R493 ALREADY RULED THIS EXACT CLASS, and it postdates the sibling precedent: "The only net-new piece (a causal cycle among non-succession facts) is unmeasured, YAGNI" — verbatim in the Round 493 entry. A requires-cycle is a causal cycle among non-succession facts; R493 governs it directly and defers it. The sibling-precedent ground fails too: canon-order and the branch forest are acyclic BY CONSTRUCTION (the mutate API literally cannot express the bad state — continuity.rs:145 "a cyclic declaration is no order"; forks immutable), which is impossible-states-unrepresentable at the type/construction level, not a runtime gate; requires is the opposite, a free-form author-drawn edge the API is designed to let you write (R637's own "the only one an author draws freely"). Making it unrepresentable needs a runtime gate that fires DURING authoring, colliding with the WIP-green contract. And a mid-draft requires-cycle is plausible in a way a mid-draft succession-cycle is not: an author wiring q-a requires q-b, then adding q-b requires q-a while restructuring a mutual dependency, produces a momentary loop = "neither opens yet" = incomplete wiring, not a contradiction. THE RULING: C1 as a validate-continuity acyclicity gate is REFUTED and CLOSED. It is not a contradiction (metadata-only, uncorrupted), it breaks WIP-green (fires during authoring), R493's measured-fault clause is unmet (zero cycles in the sole consumer's 852-fact store; their own knots.py already carries the check, so building it in Mnemosyne unblocks no one), and it fails even by R637's own contradiction-vs-incompleteness line. If anything is ever built it is a REPORT in the play-readiness class — surface a mutually-blocked prerequisite set as an unsatisfiability finding in report-quest-graph, alongside C2/C4 — designed as that named class when an author-claims-DONE gate is actually wanted, never a continuity reject and not as a one-off ahead of the class it belongs to (the scattershot "fix the next thing we tripped over" R633/R637 warned against). One correction to R637's catalogue, forward (R637 is frozen): its C1 row calls the requires-cycle "total silence — write path ACCEPTS, validate-continuity exit 0, report-quest-graph unresolved_quests=[]". The silence is not total: a cyclic quest with no discharger already surfaces in unresolved_quests, and even with dischargers the prerequisites metadata is present in report-quest-graph — what is absent is a NAMING of the cycle qua cycle, which is a report-shaped gap in the unbuilt play-readiness class, not a missing continuity gate.

R637 put it on the gate side by misclassifying it, and its ruling is internally contradictory: R637 says the play-readiness class (C2 obligation-with-no-discharger, C4 unsatisfiable-precondition) cannot be folded into validate-continuity without breaking the WIP-green contract, yet says C1 belongs in validate-continuity today — the ONLY distinguisher is "cycle = contradiction", and removing it relocates C1 next to C2/C4 in the unbuilt play-readiness class. A requires-cycle ("these quests can't open") is a strict sibling of C2 ("this quest can't be discharged"); gating one while parking the other outside is incoherent. R493 ALREADY RULED THIS EXACT CLASS, and it postdates the sibling precedent: "The only net-new piece (a causal cycle among non-succession facts) is unmeasured, YAGNI" — verbatim in the Round 493 entry. A requires-cycle is a causal cycle among non-succession facts; R493 governs it directly and defers it. The sibling-precedent ground fails too: canon-order and the branch forest are acyclic BY CONSTRUCTION (the mutate API literally cannot express the bad state — continuity.rs:145 "a cyclic declaration is no order"; forks immutable), which is impossible-states-unrepresentable at the type/construction level, not a runtime gate; requires is the opposite, a free-form author-drawn edge the API is designed to let you write (R637's own "the only one an author draws freely"). Making it unrepresentable needs a runtime gate that fires DURING authoring, colliding with the WIP-green contract. And a mid-draft requires-cycle is plausible in a way a mid-draft succession-cycle is not: an author wiring q-a requires q-b, then adding q-b requires q-a while restructuring a mutual dependency, produces a momentary loop = "neither opens yet" = incomplete wiring, not a contradiction. THE RULING: C1 as a validate-continuity acyclicity gate is REFUTED and CLOSED. It is not a contradiction (metadata-only, uncorrupted), it breaks WIP-green (fires during authoring), R493's measured-fault clause is unmet (zero cycles in the sole consumer's 852-fact store; their own knots.py already carries the check, so building it in Mnemosyne unblocks no one), and it fails even by R637's own contradiction-vs-incompleteness line. If anything is ever built it is a REPORT in the play-readiness class — surface a mutually-blocked prerequisite set as an unsatisfiability finding in report-quest-graph, alongside C2/C4 — designed as that named class when an author-claims-DONE gate is actually wanted, never a continuity reject and not as a one-off ahead of the class it belongs to (the scattershot "fix the next thing we tripped over" R633/R637 warned against). One correction to R637's catalogue, forward (R637 is frozen): its C1 row calls the requires-cycle "total silence — write path ACCEPTS, validate-continuity exit 0, report-quest-graph unresolved_quests=[]". The silence is not total: a cyclic quest with no discharger already surfaces in unresolved_quests, and even with dischargers the prerequisites metadata is present in report-quest-graph — what is absent is a NAMING of the cycle qua cycle, which is a report-shaped gap in the unbuilt play-readiness class, not a missing continuity gate.

Round 648 — the E/I divergence is craft,.decision_summary: R648 pays what the consumer's R643-reply field report caught, and the payment is TWO forward-corrections plus a refutation of the report's own central claim — not a build. First, the plain debt: their report answered R643 by APPLYING the R643 build (their commits 5f3ac92 pin-bump R627->R643, 7282fe7 "delete the 1,088 surface hand-copies (R643)", 37b0cab drop the inert seat arg) — surface 1099->11, game byte-unchanged, exactly as R643 measured it would be. The derived-seat build reached a real consumer and deleted real hand-copies. Now the corrections. FORWARD-CORRECTION 1 (a factual error in the frozen R642 ledger, and in the reply that carried it): R642 leg-(c) asserted "they DID render it (`i18n.py:91-130` builds the game's text from `report-playable-world --telling player`)." FALSE, verified: i18n.py:74 reads `--telling reader`, and `player` appears ZERO times in that file (grep -c player i18n.py = 0). Every game renderer reads `reader` — knots.py:43, the consumer engine narrative.rs:204, i18n.py:74, and the committed playable-world.json carries telling: reader. Only seat-dump.py:59 reads `player`, and its own docstring calls it "input for authoring the player telling" (it subtracts already-named facts). The (c) refutation SURVIVES on its other two legs (3 of 5 telling-consumers take one store with no re-extraction; the MCP contract is render-free by design), but the "they rendered the PLAYER telling" leg is dead. This is the R643/R644 class one more time: I carried a reviewer's claim into the ledger without re-running it, and the consumer re-ran it and caught me. The larger thing the error hid is a COORDINATE CORRECTION worth more than the error: the consumer's game does not read the player telling AT ALL yet, so everything R625-R643 "measured" was the READER-telling game, never the player-telling game the polarity question lives in. FORWARD-CORRECTION 2 (my R642 §1 overclaim, and the tension the consumer built on it): the reply's (b) row said "your observation is correct" unqualified — that mode (how plainly told) and access (must the audience act) are orthogonal and the reader-centric vocabulary conflates them. The consumer then argued (their §1) that I cannot hold both "(b) is a real conflation" and "the E/I judge divergence is an authoring decision" — if the conflation is real, judges forced to classify along the collapsed axis diverge systematically, so the divergence IS the measured symptom, which licenses the build. THE CAUSAL STEP IS REFUTED by the primary judge data, independently reviewed and re-verified by me: judge E scored days 3-5 (d03-bam..d05-nat), judge I scored days 11-12 (d11-saebyeok..d12-nat) — DISJOINT, not one shared seat. "Same fact, opposite verdict" is impossible from two judges who never looked at the same fact; the "zero overlap" is an artifact of disjoint coverage, not a controlled same-fact split. And both judges SAW the exact axis the consumer says the vocabulary hid from them, named it in plain language, and explicitly handed the threshold to the author: judge E wrote "which of the two criteria is right is not mine to decide but the spine's; I only note it" and even anticipated I's polarity ("if those two are correctly named, my re-statement line is too tight for this layout"); judge I wrote "if the line should be stricter, d11-nat's 4 and d11-jeonyeok's 2 fall off first." A conflated vocabulary that collapsed the axis could not have produced judges who articulate the axis, choose a threshold, and name its cost. The divergence is two judges applying self-anchored craft thresholds to different chapters and correctly deferring the knob to the author — R642's exact claim ("an authoring decision, which they say themselves"), now sourced to the primary text. The consumer's own §1 concession seals it: an access flag would NOT make E and I converge ("access 플래그가 생겨도 우리는 여전히 fact 마다 고른다. 그 잔여는 저작 결정이 맞다") — a divergence that survives the axis is not evidence the axis is missing. So the precise correction to §1 is: the orthogonality is a true STRUCTURAL description of a reader-framed vocabulary (narrative.rs:634 literally says the modes describe what "reaches the READER"), but it does NOT cause the E/I split and it is NOT substrate-licensed. Holding "the conflation is real (structurally)" and "this divergence has a different cause (a craft threshold)" is not a contradiction; the consumer's Claim A equivocates between "observation correct" (structural) and "divergence measures it" (empirical). (b) STAYS UNLICENSED, and R493 is not close to met, three ways verified: the distinction is not inexpressible (narrative.rs:637-639 `Imply` = "realised via an object/environment", `surface.object` names the carrier, and the consumer's 11 real carriers ARE that expression — only the narrow combination "plainly-stated AND must-act" is missing, which is "existing mechanism + more authoring", R493's own unlicensed side); the mode axis is already DECLARED ungated craft (narrative.rs:640-643: "the four modes are an authoring vocabulary", never gated), so a disagreement over where a fact sits on an axis the store refuses to gate is authoring by construction; and every build licensed this arc (R640-R644) fixed a store MALFUNCTION (a config that won't parse, a locator that leaks a withheld fact onto the map seam, a hand-copy the API compelled, a round-trip that would break) whereas the E/I divergence produces NO wrong store output — the store faithfully holds both facts and two humans disagree about a downstream classification the store does not make, does not gate, and (the coordinate correction) nothing reads. Claim B (the inert player telling) STRENGTHENS deferral rather than blocking it: the consumer states in their own words it is not-done-yet ("telling을 갈아 끼우는 것이 우리 다음 일감"), authoring is proceeding not stalled (99 player overrides already written, set-disclosure/remove-disclosure live), and the polarity fault is unmeasurable until wired ("지금은 극성이 어긋나도 아무 데도 안 닿아서 조용하다") — so the polarity fault has never once been in a measured runtime, which is precisely the YAGNI shape R493 defers. THE RULING: (b) is REFUTED as a build and held UNLICENSED; build it if and when the consumer wires the player telling and the polarity actually breaks a runtime, at which point it becomes a measured fault and R493's clause is genuinely met — Claim B moved that day further out, not closer. The debt paid here is the two forward-corrections and the refutation of Claim A's causal step, all owed to the consumer in the next reply, none of it a code change.

Round 649 — the frames refutation we sta.decision_summary: R649 answers the consumer's retraction field report, and the payment is a FORWARD-CORRECTION OF OUR OWN LEDGER plus a three-part answer proven on their store — no build. THE RETRACTION: they withdraw section 2b of their R625 reply, which told us "frames cannot carry dialogue" on the ground that 726 of their 852 facts are `ground-truth`, and they name it the TENTH "Mnemosyne lacks X" that was wrong — this time said about OUR design, to us, and absorbed into our ledger. THEY ARE RIGHT, and every number re-runs to the digit on a scratch copy of their store: 852 facts, 726 ground-truth, 126 belief across 15 CHARACTER frames (frame-jiun 49, frame-village 15, frame-bunok 15, frame-pansu 14, frame-munseon 11), `conflicts_with` 1, `supersedes_in_frame` 10; `f-bunok-guesses-a-lost-name` (frame-bunok) and `f-jongdeuk-son-named` (ground-truth) sit as DISTINCT facts, exactly as Round 430's contract requires; and `grep -c frame engine/src/narrative.rs` is 0 at their cited `6593ebd` and at HEAD `b0df5c5` (working tree already +32 lines, 7 reads — they are wiring it now). OUR FAULT, LOCATED PRECISELY: Round 639's verification bullet wrote "726 of 852 facts are `ground-truth` (their refutation of their own 'frames are the dialogue gate' design premise: CONFIRMED)". The number is true; the CONFIRMED is false. THE LESSON IS SHARPER THAN THE STANDING ONE AND SUPERSEDES ITS FORMULATION: R639 OBEYED "re-run every claim before it lands in the frozen ledger" — the number reproduces exactly. What it did not do is re-derive the INFERENCE the number was offered for. 726/852 is a COVERAGE number; "frames cannot carry dialogue" is a CAPABILITY claim; no coverage number reaches a capability verdict. A verified premise under an unverified conclusion is MORE dangerous than an unverified claim, because the verification bullet makes it look checked. And it was SELF-REFUTING IN ITS OWN SENTENCE: the same bullet records "the next largest is 49" — a frame holding 49 belief facts IS the belief axis being split — and read that as corroboration; meanwhile `describe-schema` already publishes the contract that PREDICTS the number ("frames are sparse and non-privileged: the absence of a fact in a frame is *unrecorded*, never *false*"), so 15% belief coverage is the design's own prediction, not evidence against it. This is ROUND 620 IN THE MIRROR, the consumer's own framing and it is exact: R620's finding was that the documented path told them the substrate did not exist and "the consumer was not careless" — this time WE were the documented path, and R639 held the counter-evidence while stamping the claim. THEIR MISATTRIBUTION CORRECTED (they ask to be told): the round that closed R625's "re-author then re-measure" was R639 RULING 1, not R648 (which never mentions frames), and that closure does NOT stand on their 2b — R620's demand was grounded in typed 9.7% + `surface.object` 0/1000, never frames, and the 638-to-15 re-measure read PROSE, which their own section 6-3 concedes. RULING: DO NOT REOPEN RULING 1; what IS corrected is its cited evidence — R639 wrote "axes 2/5 to 5/5" while their own 2a table counts the FRAME row as done-by-refutation, so the re-authoring is 4/5. A false premise shrank the SCOPE of their re-authoring; it never touched the GROUNDS of the ruling (the 11-judge prose re-measure and the finished-novel argument). THEIR SECTION 4 ANSWERED — "where does the condition for «this answer only comes out if you know that fact» get written?" — and it is THREE questions, two of which are BUILT AND ALREADY AUTHORED BY THEM, both PROVEN BY RUNNING THE VERB rather than asserted (the discipline this round exists to fix): (a) WHICH ANSWER THIS MOUTH GIVES IN THIS WORLD AT THIS POINT = `report-frame-view --frame --branch --at` (Round 432) — RAN IT: frame-bunok at ep-rain holds 11 facts on `weave-ledger` including `f-bunok-can-speak`, and 8 on `ending-silence` including `f-sil-bunok-cannot-speak`, the difference being the three Geumrye-death facts; "the mouth that opens only once the reckoning ends, a different answer per world-line" is not a thing to build, it is a query their store answers today. (b) WHETHER THE ASKER KNOWS X = the SAME verb on the asker's frame — their own registry describes `frame-jiun` as "what Seo Jiun knows/believes at every moment; moving from ignorance to truth", their largest belief frame at 49 — RAN IT: holding 7 at d02-nat, 26 at d08-bam, 36 at ep-rain, the promised movement measurable at any point on any world-line. (c) THE DECLARATION binding (a) to (b) has NO intended place: `opened_by` is THEIRS, not ours, and R639 RULING 3 already conceded our contract invited it (`describe-schema` publishes `completed_by [entity or scalar value]` with `required_object_kind` None); the fact-referencing leg is DEBT-G — UNLICENSED, not out-of-charter (R625 refuted my own design for it before build). R560's LINE ANSWERED EXACTLY AS ASKED: yes, this is the guard-CONDITION-over-world-state slot and it is ours — (b)'s operand is already in it and shipped, (c)'s declaration belongs in it and is not built, and an ACCUMULATING COUNTER does not belong in it (R560's own carve-out, which R625 applied to rule their hand-authored "unanswered >= 3" CORRECT). NO BUILD: (c) stays unlicensed because R493 needs a measured fault and their 16/16 `opened_by` targets resolve with 0 dangling — but the REOPEN CONDITION is concrete for the first time, because they are wiring frames as of this round: if a wired frame gate goes load-bearing on a fact ref with no referential integrity, past what their `knots.py` G4 covers, that is the measured fault. The gap is their wiring, not our substrate, and they are already closing it.
changes_bullets: FORWARD-CORRECTION (Round 639 is frozen, append-only): its verification bullet wrote "726 of 852 facts are `ground-truth` (their refutation of their own 'frames are the dialogue gate' design premise: CONFIRMED)". The NUMBER is true and reproduces exactly; the CONFIRMED is FALSE. 126 belief facts sit across 15 character frames, and a belief and its ground-truth counterpart sit as DISTINCT facts — that is Round 430's design EXECUTING, not failing. Frames carry dialogue fine; their engine reads `frame` zero times. That is wiring, not capability, and the consumer said so first.

THE LESSON, SHARPER THAN THE STANDING ONE: Round 639 OBEYED "re-run every claim before it lands in the frozen ledger" — 726/852 reproduces to the digit. What it did not do is re-derive the INFERENCE the number was offered for. 726/852 is a COVERAGE number; "frames cannot carry dialogue" is a CAPABILITY claim; no coverage number reaches a capability conclusion. A verified premise under an unverified conclusion is MORE dangerous than an unverified claim, because the verification bullet makes it look checked.

SELF-REFUTING INSIDE ITS OWN SENTENCE: the same bullet records "16 frames registered, the next largest is 49". A frame holding 49 belief facts IS the belief axis being split — the counter-evidence was written into the sentence that dismissed it and read as corroboration. And `describe-schema` already publishes the contract that predicts the number: "Frames are sparse and non-privileged: the absence of a fact in a frame is *unrecorded*, never *false* (ground-truth is one frame among many)". 15% belief coverage is the design's own prediction, not evidence against it.

ROUND 620 IN THE MIRROR — the consumer's framing, and it is exact. R620's finding was that the documented onboarding path told them the substrate did not exist, and "the consumer was not careless". This time WE were the documented path: they wrote the refutation into their own audit as a dedicated "disproven premise" section, and our CONFIRMED is what made it safe to keep there. The difference from R620 is that they originated it and we rubber-stamped it — which is worse in one respect, because R639 held the counter-evidence and published it in the same breath.

THE RETRACTION'S OWN MISATTRIBUTION, CORRECTED (they ask to be told): they name R648 as the round that closed R625's "re-author then re-measure" as fulfilled. It was ROUND 639 RULING 1; R648 does not mention frames at all. And they say that closure "stands on our 2b" — it does NOT. R620's demand was grounded in typed 9.7% + `surface.object` 0/1000 (R625 records it verbatim: "R620's other two grounds SURVIVE, so the re-author-then-re-measure order stands"); frames were never one of its grounds, and the 638-to-15 re-measure read PROSE — which their own section 6-3 concedes ("the 638-to-15 the 11 judges measured stood independent of frames").

RULING — DO NOT REOPEN RULING 1. Correct its EVIDENCE line instead: R639 cited "they DID (axes 2/5 to 5/5)", but their own 2a table counts the FRAME row as done-by-refutation ("the letter is true, the conclusion is false ... but see 2b"). So the re-authoring is 4/5, not 5/5: one axis was refused on a false premise. A false premise shrank the SCOPE of the re-authoring; it did not touch the GROUNDS of the ruling, which were the 11-judge prose re-measure and the finished-novel-holds-no-diggable-content argument.

THEIR SECTION 4 ANSWERED, and it is THREE questions, not one — two of the three are BUILT and ALREADY AUTHORED BY THEM. (a) WHICH ANSWER THIS MOUTH GIVES, IN THIS WORLD, AT THIS POINT = `report-frame-view --frame --branch --at` (Round 432, the verb the whole Phase-1A slice was built for). (b) WHETHER THE ASKER KNOWS X = the SAME verb on the asker's frame: their own registry describes `frame-jiun` as "what Seo Jiun knows/believes at every moment; moving from ignorance to truth" — their LARGEST belief frame, 49 facts. (c) THE DECLARATION binding (a) to (b) — "this line is gated on frame-jiun holding f-jongdeuk-son-named" — has NO intended place. `opened_by` is THEIRS, not ours; R639 RULING 3 already CONCEDED that our contract invited it (`describe-schema` publishes `completed_by [entity or scalar value]` with R631's `required_object_kind` = None). The fact-referencing leg is DEBT-G: UNLICENSED, not out-of-charter.

R560's LINE ANSWERED EXACTLY AS ASKED: yes, this is the guard-CONDITION-over-world-state slot, and it is ours. (b)'s operand is ALREADY IN IT AND SHIPPED — `report-frame-view` IS a deterministic world-state query. (c)'s declaration belongs in the same slot and is not built. What does NOT belong there is an ACCUMULATING COUNTER — R560's own carve-out, applied to their 9 measured conds by R625, which ruled their hand-authored "unanswered >= 3" CORRECT. So their instinct is right and their reading of R560 is right.

NO BUILD, and the reopen condition is CONCRETE for the first time: (c) stays UNLICENSED because R493 needs a measured fault and there is none — their 16/16 `opened_by` targets resolve, 0 dangling in the wild (R625 measured it; the store is unchanged since `7282fe7`). They are wiring frames RIGHT NOW (`engine/src/narrative.rs` is +32 lines dirty, `frame` reads 0 to 7). If a wired frame gate goes load-bearing on a fact ref with no referential integrity, past what their own `knots.py` G4 covers, that is the measured fault and (c) becomes licensed.

Round 639 obeyed it exactly and still wrote a false ledger line, because the rule names CLAIMS and the failure was an INFERENCE. Re-run the NUMBER *and* re-derive the CONCLUSION it is offered for. When a bullet is about to say "X: CONFIRMED", ask what X is a conclusion FROM and whether the thing measured can reach it. A coverage number never reaches a capability verdict.

Round 651 — DEBT: R649 proved its thesis.decision_summary: R651 pays the debt R649 shipped in the very section where it was proving its own thesis, and it is the sharpest instance this arc has produced. THE DEFECT: R649 section 3(b) claimed `report-frame-view --frame frame-jiun` answers "does the ASKER know X" and concluded that BOTH operands of "ask plainly get a guess, ask knowing X get the truth" are built and already authored. FALSE, and the consumer refuted it using my own verb. `frame-jiun` is an AUTHORED CANON CURVE: re-measured, it holds 17 at `d05-saebyeok` and 28 at `d09-nat` — and it holds those same counts on `main`, `weave-ledger` AND `ending-silence` alike, so it does not vary with the BRANCH axis, let alone with play. Their player holds 0 to 9 at `d05-saebyeok` depending on how many of the 7 ladder rungs they climb. Different questions; I answered the wrong one. THE IRONY IS THE FINDING: R649's thesis is "re-running the NUMBER is not re-deriving the INFERENCE", and I ran the verb (7/26/36, which the consumer re-ran and confirmed to the digit), read the frame registry's own description ("what Seo Jiun knows/believes at every moment; moving from ignorance to truth"), and inferred "therefore this is the player" — never checking the semantic step. FOURTH INSTANCE IN ONE SESSION, and the pattern is now precise enough to name: THE DISCIPLINE REACHES THE MEASUREMENT AND STOPS AT THE SENTENCE BUILT ON IT. THE COST IS THAT THIS REPO IS PUBLIC and the replies quote the consumer's unpublished novel content and store internals; tracking them publishes that correspondence irreversibly. Alternatives if publication is unacceptable: a private sidecar, or a local-only `git init` under `claudedocs/`. Do not decide this inside a round.

THE SESSION'S FINDING, now demonstrated on the artifact class where it costs most: DISCIPLINE IS NOT A CONTROL. Six of my own claims/practices failed this session (R639's CONFIRMED; "unprocessed"; "unsent"; frame-jiun-is-the-player; the bare `other` quote; and editing a sent file) — ZERO were caught by me, all by the consumer or the owner. R652 said the class is unsolved by discipline alone; R654 is the proof that even a rule I WROTE DOWN TWO TURNS EARLIER does not hold when nothing enforces it. The register (R652) and the banner (here) are patches; the general answer is a mechanism, and for this class the mechanism is version control.

THE OWED REPLY IS `claudedocs/send-r653-reply.md` (OUTWARD gate, owner's word only) — NEW FILE, carrying R651 + R653 + R654: 3(b) conceded with the 17/17/17 measurement; the R560 (b) row moved to pinion's side; their R493 critique GRANTED with the R643 no-victim precedent; (c) re-grounded on MAGNITUDE with a refutable shape; the `other` reading refuted WITH the neighbouring sentence quoted in full; the concession that our bare quote caused their misreading; frames-in-the-ASK-not-the-GROUNDS granted; their thin-frame measurement granted as the reports' real value; the commit-subject seam read MORE against us than they read it; and this round's accident reported to them in the header.

THE CONSUMER-REPORT REGISTER (RESUME live block, built R652+) is now the authority for processing AND send state — it must be updated the moment a send happens, which is exactly what did not happen here. `send-r649-reply` = DELIVERED then MUTATED (banner); `send-r653-reply` = DRAFTED, owed.

REGISTERED DEBT (SSOT = Round 632) unchanged: DEBT-C RULED (R645), DEBT-F (overturned, held open), DEBT-G (UNLICENSED — re-grounded on magnitude by R651), DEBT-PROSE (code leg closed R644). CLOSED: DEBT-A+B (R630+R634), DEBT-D (R640), DEBT-E (R638), DEBT-H (R635).

Round 655 — owner rules: do not track th.decision_summary: R655 records the owner's ruling on the one decision R654 named — and the ruling exposes that R654's premise was my own overclaim. THE RULING: DO NOT git-track `claudedocs/send-*.md`. The sent replies stay gitignored, with no history, no gate, no diff. Recorded as a DECISION, not a deferral; the alternatives R654 listed (private sidecar, local-only `git init`) were not asked for and are not pursued. AND THE RULING IS RIGHT, BECAUSE R654's PREMISE WAS AN OVERCLAIM: R654 says "THE RECORD OF WHAT WENT OUT IS DESTROYED". FALSE — destroyed ON OUR SIDE ONLY. THE COUNTERPARTY HOLDS THE AS-SENT COPY: they received it, and their two field reports quote the load-bearing passages VERBATIM, including the exact section 3(b) sentence I overwrote ("양쪽 피연산자가 둘 다 지어져 있고, 둘 다 당신들이 저작했다") and the section 2 passage their whole `other` rebuttal is built on; the as-sent CLAIMS were independently frozen by `Round 649`. The true loss is narrow and R654 measured it correctly: OUR copy no longer resolves their `§5-1`/`§5-4` citations — recoverable by asking them for the file. THE INFLATED LABEL IS THIS ROUND'S FINDING, and it is R625's lesson repeating with real stakes attached. R625 convicted me of misciting "half-enforced invariant" onto a leg whose parity held, and wrote the rule: "wanting a fix is not evidence that the anti-pattern you named is the one present — AN INFLATED LABEL INFLATES PRIORITY". Here the inflated label priced an OUTWARD, IRREVERSIBLE remedy — publishing a consumer's unpublished novel and store internals to a public repository, permanently, to protect a record the consumer already holds. The cost was real and the benefit near zero, and I could not see it because I had already frozen "destroyed" into an entry. THE CONSENT GATE IS THE MECHANISM THAT WORKED, and this is the one place this session's thesis inverts: R652 ruled the class unsolved by discipline alone, R654 proved even a self-authored rule fails when nothing enforces it, and six of my own claims failed this session with ZERO self-caught (the consumer 5, the owner 1). THE SEVENTH WAS CAUGHT — before it cost anything — because the decision was NAMED AS OWNER-ONLY instead of taken inside a round. When a fix is outward or irreversible, the gate is not ceremony; it is the only control in this session with a save on the board. WHAT DEFENDS THE ARTIFACT NOW, stated without pretending it is a mechanism: discipline (never edit a sent file; forward-correct in a new `send-<round>-reply.md`), the R652 consumer-report register (which must record a send the moment it happens), the ledger's independent freezing of every claim a reply carries, and THE COUNTERPARTY'S OWN COPY — the real backstop, free, and the reason the exposure is small. R654's mutated-after-sending banner on `send-r649-reply.md` stays. NO BUILD, no `.gitignore` change, nothing published, code 0.

REGISTERED DEBT (SSOT = Round 632) unchanged: DEBT-C RULED (R645), DEBT-F (overturned, held open), DEBT-G (UNLICENSED — re-grounded on magnitude by R651), DEBT-PROSE (code leg closed R644). CLOSED: DEBT-A+B (R630+R634), DEBT-D (R640), DEBT-E (R638), DEBT-H (R635).

Round 656 — the hint that told a consume.decision_summary: R656 BUILDS the fix for a defect the consumer found in our own gate, and it is the R627 class one gate over: OUR HINT INSTRUCTED A CONSUMER TO CORRUPT THEIR OWN LEDGER. The commit-to-ledger drift gate reads commit SUBJECTS and resolves each cited round against THIS workspace's ledger, so `missing` has TWO classes needing OPPOSITE fixes and the gate cannot tell them apart: a round of ours that lacks an entry (backfill it) and an UPSTREAM's round a consumer legitimately cites (never backfill it). The old hint named only the R293 backfill flow. The first playable consumer's commit subject read "refactor(store): delete the 1,088 surface hand-copies (R643)" — OUR round, in THEIR repo, whose ledger is Rounds 1-26 of their own authoring — so `R643` is `missing` FOREVER, and following our hint would have written `Round 643` into their ledger: a decision they never made, in an audit trail whose whole value is that its entries are true. They said so in writing and escaped by `filter-branch` instead. AND R377 HAD ALREADY BUILT THE HATCH THE HINT HID — `main.rs:1769-1775` carries R377's own comment, "A multi-workspace consumer whose `(R<n>)` labels are not Mnemosyne changelog rounds sets `warn`/`info`". R627's verdict on R626 was that "the round whose thesis is 'a guard with no escape hatch is a trap' built the hatch and then wrote a sign pointing away from it"; this is that shape exactly, except the sign did not merely mislead — it instructed corruption. THE FIX NAMES BOTH REMEDIES UNCONDITIONALLY AND GUESSES NOTHING: "if R{n} is THIS workspace's round, backfill it" (with `{n}` interpolated, so the suggested command is the one the reader needs, not a `<N>` placeholder); "if it is ANOTHER project's round (an upstream you cite), it is NOT yours to backfill — an entry here would record a decision you never made. This gate reads commit SUBJECTS only: keep the token in the commit body instead, or set `[commit_ledger].severity = warn` (Round 377)." The consumer proposed detecting "out of our ledger's range", and that is REFUSED: an arbitrary threshold is theatre and would be wrong on the first workspace that numbers sparsely. The gate keeps asking the question it can answer and stops pretending to answer the one it cannot. Publishing "SUBJECTS only" is what makes the free fix discoverable — it is what the consumer worked out alone, and without it the only visible door was the destructive one. THE PIN ASSERTS THE REMEDY, NOT THE LABEL (R627's other half): the existing `gate_rejects_missing_round_by_default` asserted only that the gate fires and said NOTHING about the hint, so the defect lived beside a green test. The new pin asserts all five load-bearing properties, and REVERTING TO THE BACKFILL-ONLY WORDING FAILS IT — proven by neutralization (delete the second `println!`: 3 passed / 1 failed at the exact assertion; restore: 4/4), not assumed. LICENSING ARGUED, NOT INHERITED: R493 needs a measured fault and this is measured in the field — a real consumer, a real commit, a remedy that would have corrupted their ledger, and a history rewrite paid to avoid it. R651 corrected my own reading of R493 (a compelled hand-build licenses a fix; a victim was never required), and this is stronger: not a hand-copy we compelled but a LIE we instructed. The consumer filed it as an observation, not a request, and said their discipline covers it — built anyway, because "the consumer worked around it" is exactly what R620 ruled is not grounds. GREEN: 972 tests pass, clippy clean at `-D warnings`, e2e reproduced on their exact case. INSTALL REQUIRED for it to reach anyone (R585): `cargo install --path crates/mnemosyne-cli --force`.
changes_bullets: THE BUILD: the commit-to-ledger drift gate's `reject` hint now names BOTH remedies, because `missing` has two classes that need OPPOSITE fixes and the gate CANNOT tell them apart. It reads commit SUBJECTS and resolves each cited round against THIS workspace's ledger, so an upstream's round number — a consumer citing `R643` of the project they adopted — is `missing` FOREVER. The old hint named only the R293 backfill flow. Round 658 — the owner licensed DEBT-G; t.decision_summary: R658 builds the predicate registry's missing repair half — set_predicate + remove_predicate — because the owner LICENSED DEBT-G and the license does not reach the build: precondition 1 (R625, carried unchanged by R632) is a hard technical blocker, and it is worth closing whether or not the fact leg ever lands. THE HOLE, RE-MEASURED TODAY RATHER THAN RECALLED FROM R625 (30 rounds is long enough for a stale claim): `set_predicate`/`remove_predicate` return zero hits across crates/*/src/; PredicateObjectKind is still {Entity, Scalar}; TypedObject is still {Entity, Value}; stage_registry_entry still rejects a divergent re-declare (lib.rs:4157). So add_predicate could CREATE a registry state no primitive could UNDO — a predicate typed wrong was reachable only through a vN predicate id or a hand-edit of the store, BOTH CLAUDE.md hard bans. A write path that forbids what no path repairs is the shape we charged the disclosure leg with in R625 CARRY-1; it was ours too. THE BUILD: set_predicate is a full replace (PUT) that rejects an absent id (add creates, set mutates — two creators on one registry would be the R295 anti-pattern by construction), reuses build_predicate as the ONE tag-interpretation site (R305 parity), and enforces the re-type invariant — a declaration may only move to a kind EVERY existing use already satisfies, else the registry and its facts disagree and the store round-trips broken. remove_predicate rejects while any typed leg names it (no orphan — the rule R625 CARRY-1 convicted the disclosure leg of missing). object_matches_kind is exhaustive over both enums with NO wildcard arm on purpose: when DEBT-G adds a Fact variant the compiler stops HERE instead of silently answering 'does not conform' (R624's oracle-derived rule; describe-schema's hand-listed [Entity, Scalar] is the counter-example). THE REPO'S OWN GATE FIRED ON ME AND IT WAS RIGHT: R630's every_remover_pairs_with_an_inbound_scan tripwire failed the moment remove_predicate landed unacknowledged — the first real firing of a gate built for exactly this, caught before the commit, not by me. NON-VACUITY PROVEN BY INJECTION, NOT BY GREEN: the parity test was fed an R295-shaped paste-error (setter grows its own looser invariant, unknown tag defaults to Scalar) and FAILED as designed (add=false set=true); restored, green. AND MY OWN E2E LIED TWICE BEFORE I CAUGHT IT: first `echo exit=$?` measured tail rather than the CLI; then a failing add-fact seed made the two most important rejects pass VACUOUSLY (exit 0 with zero uses to block). changes_bullets: BUILT set_predicate (crates/mnemosyne-atomic/src/lib.rs) — re-type or re-describe an EXISTING predicate. Full replace (PUT), never a merge, so nothing is wiped by omission; the CLI makes --description mandatory (unlike add-predicate, which defaults it) because omitting it on an UPDATE path is exactly the silent damage this primitive exists to repair. Absent id REJECTS — add_predicate creates, set_predicate mutates. Tag parsing routes through the existing build_predicate, the ONE interpretation site (R305 parity), so the setter cannot grow its own looser invariant (R295's paste-error class).

THE RE-TYPE INVARIANT, which is what keeps the repair from becoming the damage: a declaration may only move to a kind EVERY existing use already satisfies; otherwise REJECT, naming the offending fact ids (bounded to 5 + a count — a hundred-id list is not a better error). add_predicate enforces the SAME invariant vacuously (a fresh id has no uses, and a use cannot precede its registration because build_typed_claim rejects an unregistered predicate), so this is ONE invariant on two write paths, not two invariants — the CLAUDE.md rule the R295 paste-error broke.

BUILT remove_predicate — rejects while any typed leg names it, listing the users. This is the rule R625 CARRY-1 convicted the disclosure leg of missing (retract-fact ORPHANS a 1000-ref override while set-disclosure REFUSES to create that same state); it is not repeated here. An absent predicate REJECTS rather than no-op'ing: 'remove what is not there' is a caller mistake worth surfacing.

object_matches_kind is EXHAUSTIVE over both enums with NO wildcard arm, deliberately: when DEBT-G adds a Fact variant to either side, this build BREAKS instead of silently answering 'does not conform'. That is R624's oracle-derived rule applied at the seam DEBT-G will actually arrive through — describe-schema's hand-listed [Entity, Scalar] array (a new variant documented into nonexistence) is the counter-example carried since R625.

Acknowledged with its guard named (predicate_uses), and the test's own prose corrected forward: 'Only ONE such primitive exists today' is no longer true, and the comment now records that the tripwire has fired once for real, before a commit.

WIRED the full surface, no half-path: CLI set-predicate / remove-predicate (main.rs COMMANDS + atomic_cli.rs handlers) and MCP set_predicate / remove_predicate (SetPredicateArgs / RemovePredicateArgs).
