The refutation is the deliverable (the R613/R616/R625 pattern), and it costs my own two prior claims. THE LICENSE IS NOT THE ISSUE AND WAS NEVER REFUTED: R493/magnitude was ours to override and the owner overrode it. What died is MY DESIGN, for the second time in this project, and the reviewers proved rather than argued it. THE PAYOFF IS A ROUNDING ERROR, re-measured BY ME on the consumer source: G4 is FIVE LINES (`knots.py:126-130`) in a 189-line file, and the bridge cannot even delete them — G5 immediately below (`:131-138`) iterates the SAME `opens` dict with the SAME nested loop and re-checks the SAME membership itself (`if fid in facts and fid not in diggable`). So a foreign key removes 5 lines and leaves the loop, the dict, and the check standing. WORSE, THE GATE THAT ACTUALLY ENCODES "THE KNOT OPENS" GETS NOTHING: G5's real condition is `fid not in diggable` — withhold / not-on-the-trunk-walk — a DISCLOSURE state, which the fact leg does not model. R643 is the positive control (its build DELETED 1,088 hand-copies and made a class unrepresentable); this deletes five lines and leaves their sibling. MY "THE HARD PART" WAS A PROBLEM I MANUFACTURED: section 4d agonized over migrating the consumer's 16 `Value{fact-id}` objects, tensioning R616's no-per-field-setter rule, because I measured their store as a live database. It is a BUILD ARTIFACT — `build-store.sh:68` is `rm -f .atomic/tide.atomic.json`, one `KO()` helper (`:3913-3917`) emits all 16, the predicate is declared once (`:3836`). Adoption is TWO TOKEN EDITS plus a rebuild; `set_predicate` is never called and `add_predicate` satisfies R658's re-type invariant vacuously — which R658's own doc comment said a day before 4d asked. I measured a generator as if it were a database. THE COMPILER FORCES EVERY READER AND ZERO WRITERS, and reviewer B PROVED it: wiring only the 8 compiler-forced sites + the 1 test-forced `from_tag` arm (41 lines, 4 files) yields cargo build clean, clippy clean, 975/975 GREEN — and the pre-commit hook would PASS that commit — while `--object-kind fact` is ACCEPTED and PERSISTED and `TypedObject::Fact` is UNCONSTRUCTIBLE from every surface. `from_exclusive_args` (`narrative.rs:453`) is a CONSTRUCTOR matching `(Option<String>, Option<String>)`: adding a variant can never break a function that constructs it, so the CLI flag, the MCP arg, and the published JsonSchema are unforced BY CONSTRUCTION, not by oversight. R625 reproduces EXACTLY, one round after R658 was built to prevent it, and R658's `object_matches_kind` does not help because it is a READER too. THE DESIGN REBUILDS R626 AND REVIEWER C PROVED IT BY INJECTION: with the leg half-built, `retract-fact` on a Fact-leg target SUCCEEDS, orphans it, and both gates exit 0 — strictly WORSE than the scalar workaround, which at least has G4 watching. THE HOLE I CITED AS LIVE IS NOT LIVE, and this is the sharpest thing against me: my section 1 quoted R625's "retract-fact on a gate's target SUCCEEDS, leaving the gate dangling, both gates green" as today's state. FALSE, 16/16 — R626 shipped a retract guard on disclosure referrers and every one of the 16 `opened_by` targets carries a disclosure override, so retract REJECTS exit 1 on all of them. I carried a pre-R626 measurement forward as current IN THE SAME SESSION in which I told the owner that a 30-round-old measurement must be re-measured, and my non-vacuity proof for that guard could only ever have passed on a purpose-built fixture with no disclosure plans — the F5 vacuous-pass class, scheduled into a build plan AS EVIDENCE. THE MISSED WRITE AUTHORITY FALSIFIES R616's OWN AFFIRMATION: `import_typing_proposals` (`lib.rs:5650`) writes `store.narrative_facts[id].typed` DIRECTLY (`:5714`), routing neither `build_candidate_fact` nor `validate_and_stamp_fact_refs`, reachable from CLI and MCP. R616 AFFIRMED "facts have no per-field setters; add/import/amend all route the single build_candidate_fact" — that sentence is FALSE at `lib.rs:5714`, and quoting it as precedent is exactly why my enumeration missed the path. THE CHECK HAS NO CORRECT HOME: `build_typed_claim` is PHASE 1 (registries); fact→fact refs live in PHASE 2 (`validate_and_stamp_fact_refs`, against `visible` = store ∪ staged). A phase-1 `contains_key` splits the verdict by caller — add_fact/import_facts see the fact ABSENT and reject a self-ref, amend_fact/import_typing_proposals see it PRESENT and accept — one field, four write authorities, two verdicts on identical input, the CLAUDE.md rule verbatim and R658's parity-failure signature; and it breaks same-manifest refs, legal today and pinned by `import_facts_allows_forward_payoff_ref_within_manifest` (`lib.rs:11558`). NO BUILD. Ledger-corrected forward: R616's AFFIRMED per-field-setter sentence, and my own section-1 citation of a hole R626 closed.
changes_bullets: THE DESIGN IS REFUTED BY THREE INDEPENDENT ADVERSARIAL REVIEWERS BEFORE BUILD (charter/layering, half-wired/wiring, delete-path/invariants), converging from unrelated angles — the R613/R616/R625 pattern where the refutation IS the round's most valuable output. Code 0. THE OWNER'S LICENSE IS NOT OVERTURNED and was never in question: R493/magnitude was OUR rule and the owner's to override. What died is MY DESIGN, and this is the SECOND time this project has killed a fact-leg design of mine before build.

THE PAYOFF IS A ROUNDING ERROR, re-measured BY ME on the consumer source rather than taken from the reviewer: G4 is FIVE LINES (`knots.py:126-130`) in a 189-line file, and the bridge cannot even delete them — G5 (`:131-138`) iterates the SAME `opens` dict with the SAME nested loop and re-checks the SAME membership (`if fid in facts and fid not in diggable`). The foreign key removes five lines and leaves the loop, the dict, and the check standing. R643 (the positive control) DELETED 1,088 hand-copies and made a defect class unrepresentable.

AND THE GATE THAT ACTUALLY ENCODES "THE KNOT OPENS" GETS NOTHING: G5's condition is `fid not in diggable` — withhold / off the trunk walk — a DISCLOSURE state, which no fact→fact foreign key models. The bridge answers "does this id exist", which was never the question that gates the knot. I designed against the check I could build, not the one that carries the meaning.

MY "HARD PART" WAS A PROBLEM I MANUFACTURED BY MEASURING A GENERATOR AS A DATABASE. Section 4d agonized over migrating 16 `Value{fact-id}` objects and tensioned R616's no-per-field-setter rule to do it. The consumer's store is a BUILD ARTIFACT: `build-store.sh:68` = `rm -f .atomic/tide.atomic.json`; one `KO()` helper (`:3913-3917`) emits all 16; the predicate is declared once (`:3836`). Adoption = TWO TOKEN EDITS + rebuild. `set_predicate` is never called; `add_predicate` satisfies R658's re-type invariant vacuously — as R658's OWN doc comment stated a day before 4d asked the question. Delete 4d entirely: with it goes the R616 collision, the bulk-rewrite, the migrate primitive, and proof #4.

"THE COMPILER WILL CATCH IT" IS NOW MEASURED FALSE TWICE (R625, and here at `cf414d6`). The compiler forces every READER of `TypedObject` and ZERO WRITERS, because `from_exclusive_args` (`narrative.rs:453`) is a CONSTRUCTOR matching `(Option<String>, Option<String>)` — a new variant cannot break a function that constructs it. So the CLI flag, the MCP arg field, and the published JsonSchema are unforced BY CONSTRUCTION. R658's `object_matches_kind` does not save this: it is a reader too. THE ONE FORCING FUNCTION THAT WORKED is R624/R629's serde oracle, which caught `from_tag`'s `_ => None`.

THE DESIGN REBUILDS R626, PROVEN BY INJECTION not argued: with the leg half-built, `retract-fact` on a Fact-leg target SUCCEEDS, leaves the referrer pointing at a deleted fact, and BOTH gates exit 0. That is strictly WORSE than the scalar workaround it replaces, which at least has G4 watching it. `inbound_fact_refs` (`lib.rs:5326`) is a hand-listed 3-field scan with no exhaustiveness, so nothing forces the fix.

THE HOLE I CITED AS LIVE IS NOT LIVE, and it convicts me hardest: my section 1 quoted R625's "retract-fact on a gate's target SUCCEEDS, leaving the gate dangling" as TODAY. FALSE, 16/16 — R626 shipped a retract guard on disclosure referrers, all 16 `opened_by` targets carry a disclosure override, and retract REJECTS exit 1 on every one. I carried a pre-R626 measurement forward as current IN THE SAME SESSION where I told the owner a 30-round-old measurement must be re-measured. My non-vacuity proof for that guard could only have passed on a purpose-built fixture with no disclosure plans = the F5 vacuous-pass class, scheduled into a build plan AS EVIDENCE.

R616's AFFIRMATION IS FALSE AND I QUOTED IT AS PRECEDENT — corrected forward here: `import_typing_proposals` (`lib.rs:5650`) writes `store.narrative_facts[id].typed` DIRECTLY (`:5714`), routing neither `build_candidate_fact` nor `validate_and_stamp_fact_refs`, reachable from CLI (`main.rs:615`) and MCP (`mcp/main.rs:1824`). R616 AFFIRMED "facts have no per-field setters; add/import/amend all route the single `build_candidate_fact`". That sentence is false, and quoting it is WHY my enumeration of write authorities missed a fourth.

THE CHECK HAS NO CORRECT HOME, which is the structural reason the design cannot be patched into shape: `build_typed_claim` is PHASE 1 (registries only); fact→fact refs are PHASE 2 (`validate_and_stamp_fact_refs` against `visible` = store ∪ staged). A phase-1 `contains_key` splits the verdict by caller (add/import: fact ABSENT → reject a self-ref; amend/import_typing_proposals: PRESENT → accept) = one field, four authorities, two verdicts on identical input; and it breaks same-manifest fact→fact refs, legal today and pinned by `import_facts_allows_forward_payoff_ref_within_manifest` (`lib.rs:11558`). Phase 2 is the correct home — and `import_typing_proposals` bypasses phase 2 entirely, so no single placement covers all four.

THE CONSUMER'S CHARTER ARGUMENT HAS A SECOND LEG I NEVER ANSWERED. I disposed of it via their R632 §C-1 concession that the GAP-005-C citation was over-extended — but `knots.py:13-18` also claims a PRECEDENT: "관례가 SSOT 이고 검증이 소비자 쪽에 사는 것은 timeline.py 와 같은 자리다." That leg was never withdrawn and this design never engages it. Related and also unasked: `describe-schema` draws the declarative-vs-executable line ("Executable quest lifecycle/guards are SCE/pinion's, not modeled here"), and `opened_by` IS a knowledge-gated quest OPEN guard. I assumed the predicate and debated only its object's type.

WHAT MY SECTION 2 GOT RIGHT, confirmed independently by the reviewer who most wanted to kill it: R616 decision C does NOT block the fact leg — it is scoped to canon-vs-invented ADAPTATION-FIDELITY metadata ("vacuous for an original work") and does not reach a fact→fact foreign key. The asymmetry is also real: a dangling scalar `opened_by` is ACCEPTED exit 0 while the same dangling shape via the ENTITY leg is REJECTED exit 1. The hole exists. It is just five lines wide, and the fix as designed is worse than the hole.
verification_bullets: EVERY LOAD-BEARING REVIEWER CLAIM RE-RUN BY ME before it entered this ledger (R625's discipline: a reviewer is not an oracle either). CONFIRMED with my own eyes on the consumer source, READ-ONLY: `build-store.sh:68` = `rm -f .atomic/tide.atomic.json` (the store is regenerated, so section 4d's migration is unnecessary); `KO()` at `:3913-3917` emits all 16 `opened_by` facts through `--typed-object-value "$2"`; the predicate is declared once at `:3836` with `--object-kind scalar`. Adoption is two token edits.

THE MAGNITUDE RE-MEASURED BY ME, not accepted from the reviewer: `knots.py` is 189 lines; G4 is `:126-130` (five lines); G5 at `:131-138` re-checks `if fid in facts and fid not in diggable` — the SAME membership test over the SAME `opens` dict in the SAME nested loop, so the bridge cannot retire the loop or the check, only the five-line error arm. G5's actual gating condition is `not in diggable` (withhold / off the trunk walk) = a DISCLOSURE state the fact leg does not model.

THE HALF-WIRED-BUT-GREEN CLAIM PROVEN BY THE REVIEWER'S REAL BUILD, and the trap is structural, not sloppiness: 8 compiler-forced sites + 1 test-forced `from_tag` arm = 41 lines across 4 files → `cargo build --workspace` clean, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets` clean, `cargo test --workspace` 975 passed / 0 failed, and the pre-commit hook would PASS it — while `add-predicate --object-kind fact` is ACCEPTED and PERSISTED and `TypedObject::Fact` is unconstructible from CLI, MCP, and the published JsonSchema. Reproduced R625 one round after R658.

THE R626 REBUILD PROVEN BY INJECTION on a throwaway store (consumer untouched): with a post-migration state injected, `retract-fact` on the Fact-leg target SUCCEEDS exit 0, the referrer is left pointing at a deleted fact, and `validate-workspace` + `validate-continuity` BOTH exit 0. The design's own section 1 defect, rebuilt by the design meant to close it.

MY SECTION 1's LIVE-HOLE CITATION FALSIFIED, 16/16, by replaying the real CLI on a scratch copy of the 866-fact consumer store: every `opened_by` target carries a disclosure override, so R626's retract guard REJECTS exit 1 on all 16 ("carries a disclosure decision under telling `player`, `reader`, `reader-substitute`"). R625's measurement was pre-R626 and I carried it forward as current.

THE TWO SECTION-1 BULLETS THAT SURVIVE, re-verified: a dangling scalar `opened_by` = ACCEPTED exit 0; the same dangling shape via the entity leg (`requires`) = REJECTED exit 1. The asymmetry is real and measured; only its magnitude and my fix are refuted.

WORKING TREE VERIFIED CLEAN at `cf414d6` after the reviewers' experiments — `git status --porcelain` empty, `git diff HEAD --stat` empty, and grep confirms ZERO `PredicateObjectKind::Fact` / `TypedObject::Fact` residue in `narrative.rs` or `mnemosyne-atomic/src/lib.rs`. One reviewer transiently carried the half-wired patch in the SHARED tree while another reviewed against it — a real hazard of concurrent reviewers on one worktree, and it is why that reviewer took its baseline from `git show HEAD:` instead. NO `cargo install` ran this round; the consumer's toolchain is where R657's disclosure left it. The consumer repo was READ-ONLY (its tree is dirty from their OWN live session — 217 store insertions and +81 lines of `pred-at` facts in `build-store.sh` — and they are NOT moving on `opened_by`).

Citation hygiene (R255): R452, R493, R613, R616, R625, R626, R629, R636, R643, R657, R658 all verified PRESENT via `query --changelog-entry` before being written. Code 0 — no crate, schema, gate, test, or config touched; `validate-workspace` exit 0.
carry_forward_bullets: DEBT-G STAYS LICENSED AND UNBUILT, and those are not in conflict: the owner licensed it (2026-07-17), and three reviewers refuted the DESIGN, not the license. The reopen question is no longer "may we" but "is there anything worth building" — and the measured answer today is FIVE LINES that cannot fully go (G5 re-checks the same membership), for a gate whose real condition (`not in diggable` = a disclosure state) the bridge does not model. If the owner still wants it after seeing that number, it is theirs to say; this entry exists so the number is on the table before the word.

IF IT IS EVER REOPENED, THE SHAPE IS KNOWN AND NONE OF IT IS THE OLD DESIGN: (1) the check belongs in PHASE 2 (`validate_and_stamp_fact_refs`, which already takes `visible` AND `fact_id`), never in phase-1 `build_typed_claim`; (2) `import_typing_proposals` must be routed through phase 2 FIRST or it stays unguarded under every placement; (3) self-reference must be BANNED as `conflicts_with` (`:3804`) / `supersedes_in_frame` (`:3823`) / `pays_off` (`:3845`) already ban it; (4) the branch invariant must be RULED against the 8/16 cross-branch refs, not left as taste — bare existence would make the fact leg the ONLY branch-blind fact ref in a branch-aware substrate, certifying as sound a gate whose target sits on a world-line the gate cannot see; (5) an out-of-band `validate-continuity` leg is REQUIRED (the substrate's own contract is write-time AND out-of-band re-check; G4 IS the out-of-band check today); (6) NO migration section — the consumer regenerates their store.

⚑ THE INDEPENDENTLY VALUABLE FINDING, worth its own round whether or not DEBT-G ever returns: THE WRITE SURFACE HAS NO FORCING FUNCTION. `from_exclusive_args` (`narrative.rs:453`) constructs `TypedObject` from `(Option<String>, Option<String>)`, so no enum variant can ever break the CLI flag, the MCP arg, or the published JsonSchema. Every "the compiler will catch it" claim about `TypedObject` is false for exactly the half that carries the value. Fix = give the constructor a shape the compiler must destructure, so a new variant breaks the ARG SURFACE and not just the report glosses. This repo has now measured the claim false TWICE (R625, R659).

⚑ LEDGER CORRECTION (forward, per the frozen-ledger discipline): R616's AFFIRMED bullet — "facts have no per-field setters; add/import/amend all route the single `build_candidate_fact` (R305 parity)" — is FALSE. `import_typing_proposals` (`lib.rs:5650`) writes `.typed` directly at `:5714`, routing neither `build_candidate_fact` nor `validate_and_stamp_fact_refs`, and is reachable from CLI and MCP. Any future round reasoning about fact write authorities must count FOUR, not three. I quoted R616's sentence as precedent in this very design, and that is why I missed the fourth.

⚑ LATENT, not live, and it fires the day ANY object variant lands: `describe-schema`'s `predicate_object_kind_values` (`schema.rs:934`) is already serde-DERIVED (R629), so a new variant auto-advertises in the vocabulary — while `typed_object_wire` (`schema.rs:402`) is HAND-WRITTEN prose saying the object is "an INTERNALLY-TAGGED enum with two variants". Adding a variant makes `describe-schema` SELF-CONTRADICT in one run: it tells the agent `object_kind` may be `fact` and never says what JSON to write for it. Worse than R625's "documented into nonexistence", and no oracle can derive that paragraph — it is free prose explaining the deliberate `scalar`-kind/`value`-tag quirk. `manifest_wire_prose_names_every_serde_key` (`schema.rs:1652`) does NOT catch it (the example manifest has no such leg).

MY OWN SCORE THIS SESSION, recorded because the pattern is the point: I have now made THREE claims that felt like memory and skipped the check — (1) "wait for the consumer's branch" (it gates `needs`/G6, not `opened_by`/G4); (2) "R616 decision C blocks" (it does not; the consumer had already conceded the citation); (3) "retract orphans the gate today" (R626 closed it; 16/16 reject). The first two I caught myself by READING R616; the third took a reviewer. Then I over-corrected: after conceding (1) and (2) I told the owner "your instinct is right" — which was ALSO unchecked, and the measured payoff (5 lines, G5 unaffected) is nothing like what that sentence implied. The failure is not caution or boldness; it is asserting either without measuring.

OWED OUTWARD (gate — owner's word only; NOT sent): the consumer's `the-plan-changed` report is still unanswered, and R659 adds to what it owes. Now: the owner's DEBT-G license disclosed; that we designed the bridge they killed and killed it again ourselves, with THEIR licensing test (R657) as the instrument; that their `knots.py:13-18` timeline.py precedent leg was never answered and still stands; and that their store being a regenerated artifact is what made our "hard part" imaginary. They are NOT moving on `opened_by` — their live session is adding `pred-at` facts.

A phase-1 `contains_key` splits the verdict by caller (add/import: fact ABSENT → reject a self-ref; amend/import_typing_proposals: PRESENT → accept) = one field, four authorities, two verdicts on identical input; and it breaks same-manifest fact→fact refs, legal today and pinned by `import_facts_allows_forward_payoff_ref_within_manifest` (`lib.rs:11558`). Phase 2 is the correct home — and `import_typing_proposals` bypasses phase 2 entirely, so no single placement covers all four.

Round 660 — the compiler forces readers.decision_summary: R660 answers the owner's question — "why can't it detect this?" — by building the detector, and the answer is structural, not sloppiness. THE CAUSE: Rust's exhaustiveness only fires on a `match` OVER the type. `TypedObject::from_exclusive_args` (narrative.rs:453) matches `(Option<String>, Option<String>)`, NOT `TypedObject` — it CONSTRUCTS the enum, so its input never changes when a variant is added and its 2x2 match stays exhaustive and green. The arity `2` is a HAND COPY of the variant count, and it is the exact shape R448 consolidated ("both surfaces had hand-rolled copies") without ever DERIVING. So the compiler forces every READER and zero WRITERS, and the write surface (CLI flag, MCP field, published JsonSchema) is where a leg's whole value lives. Tests could not catch it either, because NOTHING DECLARED the requirement: "a Fact variant must be constructible from the args" lived only in my head. THE FIX USES THE TOOL WE ALREADY OWN, pointed at the half we never covered: `serde_variants` (schema.rs:813) has derived the vocabulary for 6 enums since R624/R629 — ALL read-side. The new test asserts EVERY declared `PredicateObjectKind` is SATISFIABLE from the arg surface: the oracle side is serde-derived, the surface side is measured by actually calling the constructor, and their disagreement is the signal. MEASURED, NOT ASSUMED — the oracle CANNOT read `TypedObject`: `serde_variants` captures through `deserialize_enum`, which serde calls only for EXTERNALLY-tagged enums, and `TypedObject` is `#[serde(tag = "kind")]` (internally tagged) so it routes `deserialize_any` and the capture never fires ("this type is not one"). Every type the oracle feeds today is a plain unit enum for exactly that reason; I found this by running it, not by reading it, and the doc comment now records it so the next agent does not re-derive the failure. Pointing it at `PredicateObjectKind` catches the direction that was ACTUALLY unguarded: a bare `TypedObject::Fact` DOES break `build_typed_claim`'s (object, kind) match, but a bare `PredicateObjectKind::Fact` breaks NOTHING — and that is precisely R659's proof (`add-predicate --object-kind fact` ACCEPTED and PERSISTED while no fact could satisfy it, whole suite green). NON-VACUITY PROVEN BY INJECTION, reproducing reviewer B's half-wire end to end: both variants added, all compiler-forced sites wired (build_typed_claim, object_matches_kind, 3 continuity sites, as_str, from_tag) -> `cargo build --workspace` 0 ERRORS, 193 lib tests + every suite GREEN — and the new test FAILS with "object_kind `fact` is DECLARED but NO arg combination can build an object that satisfies it". The forcing chain works in both links: the test's own `conforms` match is exhaustive, so the compiler STOPPED the injection AT the test first (E0004 at schema.rs:1449) before the assert could even run. Injection reverted; only the detector remains (+97 lines, one file). AND THE OWNER'S SECOND QUESTION OPENS SOMETHING BIGGER THAN DEBT-G, measured on the real 866-fact store: `TypedObject::Value` is documented as "an opaque consumer-vocabulary value (`alive`, `undead`) — never enumerated by the substrate", and it is used that way ZERO times out of 24. 16 are SMUGGLED FACT IDS; 8 are Korean PROSE FRAGMENTS ('순녀의 한', '계약 — 지운을 육지로 빼돌려') that appear in NO claim (0/8), i.e. a second, unchecked prose field. The proof it is not a vocabulary is its CARDINALITY: 24 unique values across 24 uses = 1.0, where a vocabulary repeats by definition (`alive` would recur across facts). The scalar leg is a category that does not exist in practice — every real use collapses to an identity or to prose, and both already have homes. Recorded, not built.
changes_bullets: BUILT the detector the owner asked for: `every_declared_object_kind_is_satisfiable_from_the_arg_surface` (crates/mnemosyne-validate/src/schema.rs). The oracle side is DERIVED (`serde_variants::<PredicateObjectKind>()`); the surface side is MEASURED by actually calling `TypedObject::from_exclusive_args` with every arg combination the CLI flags and MCP fields can send; their disagreement is the signal. A hand-list on either side would have been a copy of the very class this kills.

THE CAUSE IS STRUCTURAL AND THE ROUND NAMES IT: Rust's exhaustiveness fires only on a `match` OVER the type. `from_exclusive_args` (`narrative.rs:453`) matches `(Option<String>, Option<String>)` and CONSTRUCTS `TypedObject` — adding a variant cannot break a function that constructs the type, because its input never changed and its 2x2 match is already exhaustive. The arity `2` is a HAND COPY of the variant count. R448 consolidated the two hand-rolled surface copies into this ONE place and never derived it — the duplication died, the hand-list lived.

THE FORCING CHAIN NOW CLOSES, and every link is derived or compiler-forced: (1) add a variant -> the derived oracle grows while the surface does not -> THIS TEST FAILS; (2) the only fix is a new `from_exclusive_args` parameter -> the ARITY changes -> every call site (CLI `atomic_cli.rs`, MCP `mcp/main.rs`) breaks the build; (3) so the author must reach the flag and the arg field to compile at all. The one missing link was (1), and that is what this round adds.

THE ORACLE CANNOT READ `TypedObject`, MEASURED not assumed, and the doc comment records it so the next agent does not re-derive the failure: `serde_variants` captures via `deserialize_enum`, which serde calls only for EXTERNALLY-tagged enums; `TypedObject` is `#[serde(tag = "kind")]` (internally tagged) so it routes `deserialize_any` and the capture never fires, panicking "this type is not one". Every enum the oracle feeds today is a plain unit enum for that reason. I found this by RUNNING it after writing the test against `TypedObject` first.

SO THE ORACLE IS POINTED AT `PredicateObjectKind`, which catches the direction that was actually unguarded. A bare `TypedObject::Fact` DOES break `build_typed_claim`'s (object, kind) match — the compiler has that side. A bare `PredicateObjectKind::Fact` breaks NOTHING, and that is exactly R659's measured defect: `add-predicate --object-kind fact` accepted and persisted, no fact able to satisfy it, suite green.

⚑ THE OWNER'S SECOND QUESTION IS BIGGER THAN DEBT-G AND THE MEASUREMENT BACKS IT: "shouldn't everything be registered?" `TypedObject::Value` is the ONE unregistered identity-shaped slot in a substrate whose frames / branches / entities / predicates / sections / disclosure plans are ALL registered and fail-loud. On the real 866-fact store its documented purpose — "an opaque consumer-vocabulary value (`alive`, `undead`)" — has ZERO of 24 uses. 16 are SMUGGLED FACT IDS (the workaround DEBT-G exists to legitimize); 8 are Korean PROSE FRAGMENTS present in NO claim (0/8), a second prose field that escapes every check. NOT BUILT — recorded as the named next question.

⚑ THE PROOF THAT IT IS NOT A VOCABULARY IS ITS CARDINALITY, and it is the cheapest test in this arc: 24 unique values across 24 uses = 1.0. A vocabulary REPEATS by definition — `alive` would recur across many facts. A slot whose every value appears exactly once is not a closed vocabulary; it is free text. R436 made a typo'd PREDICATE fail loud because rules key off it; a typo'd scalar VALUE is silent today, and that asymmetry is now measured rather than suspected.
verification_bullets: NON-VACUITY PROVEN BY INJECTION, reproducing reviewer B's R659 half-wire end to end rather than trusting the report: added BOTH `TypedObject::Fact { id }` and `PredicateObjectKind::Fact`, then wired every compiler-forced site (`build_typed_claim`, `object_matches_kind`, `continuity.rs` typed_object_key / quest-shape kind map / completed_by actor, `as_str`, `from_tag`, the schema gloss). Result: `cargo build --workspace` 0 ERRORS, 193 lib tests and every integration suite GREEN — and the new test FAILED with "object_kind `fact` is DECLARED but NO arg combination can build an object that satisfies it". R659's green half-wire is now a red test.

THE FORCING CHAIN VERIFIED IN BOTH LINKS, not just the assert: the test's own `conforms` match is exhaustive with no wildcard, so the injection was STOPPED BY THE COMPILER AT THE TEST FIRST (E0004, `schema.rs:1449`, "`(&TypedObject::Fact { .. }, _)` not covered") before the assert could run. Only after filling those arms honestly — what a real author would do — did the assert fire. The test stops you twice: at compile time if you ignore it, at run time if you satisfy it dishonestly.

AND THE DETECTOR CAUGHT A SECOND GAP I DID NOT AIM AT: with `from_tag` left unwired (my injection's first pass), it failed on "from_tag rejects its own serde tag `fact`" — the same defect reviewer B found (the enum grew, the parser did not, the workspace compiled). Redundant with R629's round-trip oracle, and it fired first.

THE ORACLE'S LIMIT MEASURED, NOT ASSUMED: my first version pointed `serde_variants` at `TypedObject` and PANICKED at `schema.rs:862` — "serde reports the variant list for every derived enum (this type is not one)". Cause: the capture hooks `deserialize_enum`, which serde calls only for externally-tagged enums; `#[serde(tag = "kind")]` routes `deserialize_any`. Had I reasoned instead of run, I would have shipped a test that passes for the wrong reason.

THE CONSUMER MEASUREMENT RE-RUN BY ME on the real 866-fact store, READ-ONLY: 86/866 facts carry a typed leg; object shapes = 62 entity / 24 value; of the 24 scalar values, 16 ARE fact ids present in `narrative_facts` and 8 are prose; the 8 appear in NO claim (0/8, checked by substring against each fact's own claim); cardinality is 24 unique / 24 uses = 1.0. Their `build-store.sh` is mid-edit from their own live session — the 8 prose values ride new `pred-guilty-of` / `pred-bound-by` / `pred-cursed` predicates.

GATE CHAIN GREEN with only the detector present: injection reverted from a byte copy and verified gone (`grep` for `PredicateObjectKind::Fact` / `TypedObject::Fact` = 0 in narrative.rs, atomic/lib.rs, continuity.rs); `git diff --stat` = one file, +97; `cargo build --workspace` 0 errors; `cargo test --workspace` all suites pass; `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets` 0; `cargo fmt` clean; `validate-workspace` exit 0.

Citation hygiene (R255): R305, R436, R448, R510, R624, R629, R636, R658, R659 verified PRESENT via `query --changelog-entry` before being written. No `cargo install` ran; the consumer's toolchain is where R657's disclosure left it, and their repository was read-only.
carry_forward_bullets: ⚑⚑ THE NAMED NEXT QUESTION, and it is the owner's, not mine: IS `TypedObject::Value` ITSELF THE DEFECT? Everything else in this substrate is registered and fail-loud (frames, branches, entities, predicates, sections, disclosure plans); the scalar leg is the ONE identity-shaped slot nothing checks, and it is where every problem in this arc came from. Measured on the real store: 0 of 24 uses match its documented purpose; 16 are smuggled fact ids; 8 are prose in no claim; cardinality 1.0 proves it is not a vocabulary. DO NOT BUILD THIS BLIND — it is a schema removal against a live consumer mid-authoring, and it needs its own DESIGN round plus the adversarial review that killed R625 and R659. But it is the first thing in this arc whose evidence is a MEASUREMENT rather than a plan.

IF THE SCALAR LEG GOES, DEBT-G's MAGNITUDE ARGUMENT INVERTS — note this before reusing R659's ruling. R659 killed the fact leg at "it deletes five lines of G4". But if `Value` is removed, the 16 smuggled refs have NO home at all and the fact leg stops being an optimization: the store would have exactly two object shapes, entity and fact, both registered, both checked. R659's magnitude verdict was correct GIVEN the scalar leg stays. It is not a ruling on a store without one.

THE 8 PROSE VALUES ARE A DIFFERENT BUG AND MUST NOT BE "REGISTERED" — registering `'순녀를 갯벌 말뚝에 산 채 제물로'` as an identity would be absurd. They are mini-claims sitting in a typed slot, absent from the `claim` field (0/8), so removing the leg naively DESTROYS information the consumer authored this week. Their home is the claim prose, or a real entity/fact object. Any scalar-leg round must rule on these 8 separately from the 16.

⚑ THE GENERAL RULE THIS ROUND EARNS, worth applying beyond `TypedObject`: THE COMPILER FORCES READERS, NEVER CONSTRUCTORS. Any enum whose value reaches a hand-shaped arg surface (CLI flags, MCP fields, JsonSchema) needs a derived oracle asserting every variant is REACHABLE, because `match`-exhaustiveness structurally cannot see the write path. Audit candidates: every other `from_*_args`-shaped constructor in the workspace. This round covers `TypedObject` only.

⚠ `serde_variants` CANNOT READ INTERNALLY-TAGGED ENUMS (`#[serde(tag = ...)]`) — it hooks `deserialize_enum`, they route `deserialize_any`, and it PANICS "this type is not one" rather than returning empty. That is the right failure (loud, not vacuous), but it means the oracle covers unit enums only. Anyone wanting a variant list for `TypedObject` itself must extend the capture (the `unknown_variant` expected-list is the seam) — do NOT string-parse serde's error message.

CONCURRENT-AGENT HAZARD, learned the hard way this session: three reviewers shared ONE working tree, and one carried a 41-line half-wire in it while another reviewed against it — that reviewer had to take its baseline from `git show HEAD:` to get honest line numbers, and a third opened its report by (correctly) calling my "nothing is built" claim false. If reviewers may edit, give them worktrees (`isolation: "worktree"`) or forbid edits outright. My own injection here ran on the shared tree too, and only the byte-copy revert made it safe.

DEBT-C RULED (R645), DEBT-F (overturned, held open), DEBT-PROSE (code leg closed R644). CLOSED: DEBT-A+B, DEBT-D, DEBT-E, DEBT-H.

publishable_changes_bullets: BUILT the detector the owner asked for: `every_declared_object_kind_is_satisfiable_from_the_arg_surface` (crates/mnemosyne-validate/src/schema.rs). The oracle side is DERIVED (`serde_variants::<PredicateObjectKind>()`); the surface side is MEASURED by actually calling `TypedObject::from_exclusive_args` with every arg combination the CLI flags and MCP fields can send; their disagreement is the signal. A hand-list on either side would have been a copy of the very class this kills.

THE CAUSE IS STRUCTURAL AND THE ROUND NAMES IT: Rust's exhaustiveness fires only on a `match` OVER the type. `from_exclusive_args` (`narrative.rs:453`) matches `(Option<String>, Option<String>)` and CONSTRUCTS `TypedObject` — adding a variant cannot break a function that constructs the type, because its input never changed and its 2x2 match is already exhaustive. The arity `2` is a HAND COPY of the variant count. R448 consolidated the two hand-rolled surface copies into this ONE place and never derived it — the duplication died, the hand-list lived.

THE FORCING CHAIN NOW CLOSES, and every link is derived or compiler-forced: (1) add a variant -> the derived oracle grows while the surface does not -> THIS TEST FAILS; (2) the only fix is a new `from_exclusive_args` parameter -> the ARITY changes -> every call site (CLI `atomic_cli.rs`, MCP `mcp/main.rs`) breaks the build; (3) so the author must reach the flag and the arg field to compile at all. The one missing link was (1), and that is what this round adds.

THE ORACLE CANNOT READ `TypedObject`, MEASURED not assumed, and the doc comment records it so the next agent does not re-derive the failure: `serde_variants` captures via `deserialize_enum`, which serde calls only for EXTERNALLY-tagged enums; `TypedObject` is `#[serde(tag = "kind")]` (internally tagged) so it routes `deserialize_any` and the capture never fires, panicking "this type is not one". Every enum the oracle feeds today is a plain unit enum for that reason. I found this by RUNNING it after writing the test against `TypedObject` first.

SO THE ORACLE IS POINTED AT `PredicateObjectKind`, which catches the direction that was actually unguarded. A bare `TypedObject::Fact` DOES break `build_typed_claim`'s (object, kind) match — the compiler has that side. A bare `PredicateObjectKind::Fact` breaks NOTHING, and that is exactly R659's measured defect: `add-predicate --object-kind fact` accepted and persisted, no fact able to satisfy it, suite green.

⚑ THE OWNER'S SECOND QUESTION IS BIGGER THAN DEBT-G AND THE MEASUREMENT BACKS IT: "shouldn't everything be registered?" `TypedObject::Value` is the ONE unregistered identity-shaped slot in a substrate whose frames / branches / entities / predicates / sections / disclosure plans are ALL registered and fail-loud. On the real 866-fact store its documented purpose — "an opaque consumer-vocabulary value (`alive`, `undead`)" — has ZERO of 24 uses. 16 are SMUGGLED FACT IDS (the workaround DEBT-G exists to legitimize); 8 are Korean PROSE FRAGMENTS present in NO claim (0/8), a second prose field that escapes every check. NOT BUILT — recorded as the named next question.

⚑ THE PROOF THAT IT IS NOT A VOCABULARY IS ITS CARDINALITY, and it is the cheapest test in this arc: 24 unique values across 24 uses = 1.0. A vocabulary REPEATS by definition — `alive` would recur across many facts. A slot whose every value appears exactly once is not a closed vocabulary; it is free text. R436 made a typo'd PREDICATE fail loud because rules key off it; a typo'd scalar VALUE is silent today, and that asymmetry is now measured rather than suspected.

THE FORCING CHAIN VERIFIED IN BOTH LINKS, not just the assert: the test's own `conforms` match is exhaustive with no wildcard, so the injection was STOPPED BY THE COMPILER AT THE TEST FIRST (E0004, `schema.rs:1449`, "`(&TypedObject::Fact { .. }, _)` not covered") before the assert could run. ⚑ THE GENERAL RULE THIS ROUND EARNS, worth applying beyond `TypedObject`: THE COMPILER FORCES READERS, NEVER CONSTRUCTORS. Any enum whose value reaches a hand-shaped arg surface (CLI flags, MCP fields, JsonSchema) needs a derived oracle asserting every variant is REACHABLE, because `match`-exhaustiveness structurally cannot see the write path. ⚠ `serde_variants` CANNOT READ INTERNALLY-TAGGED ENUMS (`#[serde(tag = ...)]`) — it hooks `deserialize_enum`, they route `deserialize_any`, and it PANICS "this type is not one" rather than returning empty. Anyone wanting a variant list for `TypedObject` itself must extend the capture (the `unknown_variant` expected-list is the seam) — do NOT string-parse serde's error message.

verification_bullets: THE RULE ENGINE KEYS OFF OBJECT VALUES BY BARE STRING — verified in source, not recalled: `NarrativeRuleSpec::Transition` / `Exclusive` carry `allowed: [["a","b"]]` (value pairs) and `per: ExclusiveKey::Object`, and `RuleClass` = {Exclusive, Transition, Interval} (`continuity.rs:912`). Interval is the exception and it does NOT key off values: `Interval { right, op, bound }` where `right` is another PREDICATE (`referenced_predicates` includes it), so its arithmetic is over event distance, not over a stored quantity. That is precisely why quantities have no home.

THE SLOT'S OWN CONTRACT IS THE ACCUSATION, quoted verbatim from `narrative.rs:443-445`: "An opaque consumer-vocabulary value (`alive`, `undead`, …). Never enumerated by the substrate." And `Entity{id}`'s contract (`:440-442`) is what forbids the lazy fix: it "must also be a member of the owning fact's `entities` list — the entities list stays THE retrieval key", so vocabulary cannot be modelled as entities without wrecking retrieval.

QUANTITIES HAVE NO MACHINE HOME — taken from R625's measurement rather than re-derived: an 852-fact store holds ZERO numeric typed scalars, and 15 facts whose prose says "three" are ALL untyped, with `f-tide-slips` carrying its count law as PROSE ONLY. So a closed token vocabulary alone cannot reach R476's Band 2 arithmetic fault; a quantity shape is required, and that is why the proposal is four shapes rather than "register everything".

THE REPAIR PATH A CLOSED SET REQUIRES ALREADY EXISTS, and it landed two rounds ago for an unrelated reason: R658's `set-predicate` is how a declared vocabulary is extended mid-authoring. R436 IS THE PRECEDENT AND IT APPLIES VERBATIM ONE LEVEL DOWN, verified in source: predicates are registered because "a typo'd predicate would silently escape its rule". The SAME rule engine keys off object VALUES by bare string — `NarrativeRuleSpec::Transition` / `Exclusive` carry `allowed: [["a","b"]]` and `per: ExclusiveKey::Object` — and NOTHING binds the rule file's value strings to the facts' value strings. publishable_verification_bullets: THE RULE ENGINE KEYS OFF OBJECT VALUES BY BARE STRING — verified in source, not recalled: `NarrativeRuleSpec::Transition` / `Exclusive` carry `allowed: [["a","b"]]` (value pairs) and `per: ExclusiveKey::Object`, and `RuleClass` = {Exclusive, Transition, Interval} (`continuity.rs:912`). Interval is the exception and it does NOT key off values: `Interval { right, op, bound }` where `right` is another PREDICATE (`referenced_predicates` includes it), so its arithmetic is over event distance, not over a stored quantity. That is precisely why quantities have no home.

THE SLOT'S OWN CONTRACT IS THE ACCUSATION, quoted verbatim from `narrative.rs:443-445`: "An opaque consumer-vocabulary value (`alive`, `undead`, …). Never enumerated by the substrate." And `Entity{id}`'s contract (`:440-442`) is what forbids the lazy fix: it "must also be a member of the owning fact's `entities` list — the entities list stays THE retrieval key", so vocabulary cannot be modelled as entities without wrecking retrieval.

QUANTITIES HAVE NO MACHINE HOME — taken from R625's measurement rather than re-derived: an 852-fact store holds ZERO numeric typed scalars, and 15 facts whose prose says "three" are ALL untyped, with `f-tide-slips` carrying its count law as PROSE ONLY. So a closed token vocabulary alone cannot reach R476's Band 2 arithmetic fault; a quantity shape is required, and that is why the proposal is four shapes rather than "register everything".

THE REPAIR PATH A CLOSED SET REQUIRES ALREADY EXISTS, and it landed two rounds ago for an unrelated reason: R658's `set-predicate` is how a declared vocabulary is extended mid-authoring. A closed set without an extension path would be R626's "a guard without an escape hatch is a trap" — that trap is already disarmed.
